title: Postgres connector log position validation logic is flawed [DBZ-9535]
body:
Migrated from [DBZ-9535](https://issues.redhat.com/browse/DBZ-9535)

h1. Bug report

For bug reports, provide this information, please:
h2. What Debezium connector do you use and what version?

Debezium Postgres v3.2.0
h2. What is the connector configuration?
{code:java}
{
            "database.dbname": "mydb",
            "database.hostname": "<HOSTNAME>",
            "database.password": "<PASSWORD>",
            "database.port": "5432",
            "database.user": "postgresadmin",
            "heartbeat.interval.ms": "0",
            "name": "myconnector",
            "publication.name": "dbz_publication",
            "slot.name": "debezium",
            "snapshot.mode": "initial",
            "table.include.list": "public.entry,public.event,public.logbook",
            "tasks.max": "1",
            "topic.prefix": "mydb-prod"
}{code}
h2. What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?

On-premise Postgres v16
h2. What behavior do you expect?

Expected the connector to resume normally after a restart.
h2. What behavior do you see?

The connector failed with the following exception:
{code:java}
io.debezium.DebeziumException: The connector is trying to read change stream starting at PostgresOffsetContext [sourceInfoSchema=Schema{io.debezium.connector.postgresql.Source:STRUCT}, sourceInfo=source_info[server='mydb-prod'db='mydb', lsn=LSN{5/2B1F7590}, txId=81993951, messageType=INSERT, lastCommitLsn=LSN{5/2B1F7590}, timestamp=2025-10-07T05:45:39.078148Z, snapshot=FALSE, schema=, table=], lastSnapshotRecord=false, lastCompletelyProcessedLsn=LSN{5/2B2E2560}, lastCommitLsn=LSN{5/2B1F7590}, streamingStoppingLsn=null, transactionContext=TransactionContext [currentTransactionId=null, perTableEventCount={}, totalEventCount=0], incrementalSnapshotContext=IncrementalSnapshotContext [windowOpened=false, chunkEndPosition=null, dataCollectionsToSnapshot=[], lastEventKeySent=null, maximumKey=null]], but this is no longer available on the server. Reconfigure the connector to use a snapshot mode when needed.
	at io.debezium.connector.common.BaseSourceTask.validateSchemaHistory(BaseSourceTask.java:157){code}
h2. Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?

(Ideally, also verify with latest Alpha/Beta/CR version)

I checked the latest version, and the same code is still present, so the bug exists there as well.
h2. Do you have the connector logs, ideally from start till finish?

(You might be asked later to provide [DEBUG/TRACE|https://debezium.io/documentation/reference/stable/operations/logging.html] level log)
{code:java}
// Stored offsets for the connector
[
 {
  "partition": {
   "server": "mydb-prod"
  },
  "offset": {
   "lsn": 22199280992,
   "lsn_commit": 22198318480,
   "lsn_proc": 22199280992,
   "messageType": "INSERT",
   "ts_usec": 1759815939078148,
   "txId": 81993951
  }
 }
]

// Important Logs
io.debezium.connector.common.BaseSourceTask 
Found previous partition offset PostgresPartition [sourcePartition={server=mydb-prod}]: {lsn_proc=22199280992, messageType=INSERT, lsn_commit=22198318480, lsn=22198318480, txId=81993951, ts_usec=1759815939078148}

io.debezium.connector.postgresql.connection.PostgresConnection 
Slot 'debezium' has restart LSN 'LSN{5/2B2DED98}'

io.debezium.connector.postgresql.connection.PostgresConnection
Obtained valid replication slot ReplicationSlot [active=false, latestFlushedLsn=LSN{5/2B2E2560}, catalogXmin=81993897]

The connector is trying to read change stream starting at PostgresOffsetContext [sourceInfoSchema=Schema{io.debezium.connector.postgresql.Source:STRUCT}, sourceInfo=source_info[server='mydb-prod'db='mydb', lsn=LSN{5/2B1F7590}, txId=81993951, messageType=INSERT, lastCommitLsn=LSN{5/2B1F7590}, timestamp=2025-10-07T05:45:39.078148Z, snapshot=FALSE, schema=, table=], lastSnapshotRecord=false, lastCompletelyProcessedLsn=LSN{5/2B2E2560}, lastCommitLsn=LSN{5/2B1F7590}, streamingStoppingLsn=null, transactionContext=TransactionContext [currentTransactionId=null, perTableEventCount={}, totalEventCount=0], incrementalSnapshotContext=IncrementalSnapshotContext [windowOpened=false, chunkEndPosition=null, dataCollectionsToSnapshot=[], lastEventKeySent=null, maximumKey=null]], but this is no longer available on the server. Reconfigure the connector to use a snapshot mode when needed.{code}
 

*Another issue seen from the logs:*

Even though the {{lsn}} field has the same value as the {{lsn_proc}} field in the stored offsets, the value printed in the logs for {{lsn}} corresponds to {{{}lsn_commit{}}}.
h2. How to reproduce the issue using our [tutorial|https://github.com/debezium/debezium-examples/tree/main/tutorial] deployment?

The reproduction depends on the LSN value assigned as the {{{}restart_lsn{}}}. If, after flushing the latest LSN, the slot’s {{restart_lsn}} is set to a value greater than the last processed commit LSN, the issue will occur.
h2. Proposed Fix

*1. Incorrect log position validation*

While comparing the stored offset LSN with the {{restart_lsn}} of the slot, we use the lsn_commit value ([ref|https://github.com/debezium/debezium/blob/29076fe2936b401982717708c713555c08d24aca/debezium-connector-postgres/src/main/java/io/debezium/connector/postgresql/connection/PostgresConnection.java#L853]). It should be {{lsn_proc}} (lastCompletelyProcessedLsn) value.
{code:java}
T1: BEGIN: 50

T1: COMMIT: 100

T2: BEGIN: 150

   <--- Connector restart with lsn_proc=170, lsn_commit=100 [Flush 170]

T2: COMMIT: 200 

The restart_lsn can very well fall between LSN 100 and 150.{code}
To temporarily mitigate the issue, the connector offsets were updated by setting the {{lsn_commit}} value to match the {{{}lsn_proc{}}}, followed by a connector restart. After this change, the connector resumed normal operation without further errors.

 

*2. Incorrect value being assigned to lsn field*

We assign the value of lsn_commit to the lsn field each time the PostgresOffsetContext is intialized. ([ref1|https://github.com/debezium/debezium/blob/29076fe2936b401982717708c713555c08d24aca/debezium-connector-postgres/src/main/java/io/debezium/connector/postgresql/SourceInfo.java#L158-L160] and [ref2|https://github.com/debezium/debezium/blob/main/debezium-connector-postgres/src/main/java/io/debezium/connector/postgresql/PostgresOffsetContext.java#L58-L59]) 

We can call sourceInfo.updateLastCommit before calling sourceInfo.update to set the correct value for the lsn field.
author: jpechane

project_fields.Title: Postgres connector log position validation logic is flawed [DBZ-9535]

title: Debezium can break when two column names differs only in letter case
body:
I'm getting the exception below for two tables (out of 231 tables). In looking at the resolveColumnsFromStreamTupleData method, it seems that the short numberOfColumns = buffer.getShort(); is longer than the table.columns() which causes this exception and the connector shuts down. I was able get past the problem by adding the two offending tables to the table blacklist. This is all in development and no schema changes are being made to the tables after starting the connector.

The connector is being created by the Strimzi Kafka operator. Also note I'm using an apicurio schema registry and the avro converters.

{code}
ERROR Producer failure (io.debezium.pipeline.ErrorHandler) [debezium-postgresconnector-encdb-change-event-source-coordinator]

title: Foreign Key Constraint Violation When Using Batch Processing in JDBC Sink Connector 2.7.0.Final [DBZ-8922]
body:
Migrated from [DBZ-8922](https://issues.redhat.com/browse/DBZ-8922)

In order to make your issue reports as actionable as possible, please provide the following information, depending on the issue type.
h1. Bug report

For bug reports, provide this information, please:

We are experiencing a foreign key constraint violation when using the Debezium JDBC Sink Connector 2.7.0.Final with batch settings larger than 1. Although the messages in our topic are in the correct order, increasing the batch size seems to introduce an issue during the insert/update operations on the target PostgreSQL 15.4 database.


Observed Behavior:
 * With the connector configured for {{batch.size=1}} and {{{}consumer.override.max.poll.records=1{}}}, all operations proceed correctly without any foreign key issues.
 * When the batch size is increased to {{500}} (along with {{{}consumer.override.max.poll.records=500{}}}), we receive errors such as:

_Caused by: org.postgresql.util.PSQLException: ERROR: insert or update on table "CUsage" violates foreign key constraint "FK_CUsage_AUsage_AUsageId"_
_Detail: Key (AUsageId)=(e9aa119d-15a4-4bcd-9c5d-02183dc3ff4b) is not present in table "AUsage"_


Troubleshooting Attempts:
 * Deferred Foreign Key Constraints:
Configured PostgreSQL to have deferrable constraints (using {{{}DEFERRABLE INITIALLY DEFERRED{}}}), but the error persisted.

_ALTER TABLE "CUsage" DROP CONSTRAINT "FK_CUsage_AUsage_AUsageId";_
_ALTER TABLE "CUsage"_
  _ADD CONSTRAINT "FK_CUsage_AUsage_AUsageId"_
  _FOREIGN KEY ("AUsageId") REFERENCES "AUsage"("Id")_
  _ON DELETE CASCADE_
  _DEFERRABLE INITIALLY DEFERRED;_


 * _Connection Pool Settings:_

Forced a single-connection pool with settings, but the error persisted.:

 ** {{connection.pool.min_size = 1}}

 ** {{connection.pool.max_size = 1}}

 ** {{connection.pool.acquire_increment = 0}}

Questions:
 * Why does the batching lead to a violation of foreign key constraints even when the topic messages are in the proper order?
 * Could there be an issue with how the SQL statements are grouped into transactions when batch processing is enabled?, if yes, how could I set to not have transactions in parallel?
 * Is there a recommended configuration or a workaround that would allow the connector to operate in batch mode without encountering these foreign key violations?, I can not disable foreign key o production environment.
 * Can I somehow use the JDBC connector hibernate.* passthrough properties?

Any insight or guidance on resolving this ordering issue during batched operations in the connector would be greatly appreciated.
h2. What Debezium connector do you use and what version?

Debezium Sync Connector 2.7.0.Final (image: debezium/connect:2.7.3.Final)
h2. What is the connector configuration?

{
    "connector.class": "io.debezium.connector.jdbc.JdbcSinkConnector",
    "connection.password": "******",
    "tasks.max": "1",
    "transforms": "filter",
    "quote.identifiers": "true",
    "consumer.override.max.poll.records": "500",
    "insert.mode": "upsert",
    "table.name.format": "${topic}",
    "primary.key.mode": "record_key",
    "database.time_zone": "UTC",
    "topics": "Topic-Name",
    "batch.size": "500",
    "connection.username": "username",
    "transforms.filter.type": "custom.debezium.connectors.smt.CustomImportFilter",
    "delete.enabled": "true",
    "name": "IMPORT.LOCAL.35adb52b-803b-4ad6-9527-a28080142fe1.83c1243c-3daf-4eb9-a6b5-e5e6b1526c04",
    "primary.key.fields": "Id",
    "auto.create": "true",
    "connection.url": "jdbc:postgresql://192.168.34.123:5432/DatabaseName",
    "table.naming.strategy": "custom.debezium.connectors.smt.CustomTableNamingStrategy"
}
h2. What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?

(E.g. on-premises, with a specific cloud provider, etc.)

on-premises
h2. What behavior do you expect?

I expect the connector to import the messages into the database in the same order as they are in the topic, without batch size 1 where performance is so poor.
h2. What behavior do you see?

The connector seems to transform the messages from the topic into native SQLs which it mixes in a different order or groups into multiple transactions that are run in parallel, thus leading to foreign key errors.
h2. Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?

(Ideally, also verify with latest Alpha/Beta/CR version)

I didn't test
h2. Do you have the connector logs, ideally from start till finish?

(You might be asked later to provide [DEBUG/TRACE|https://debezium.io/documentation/reference/stable/operations/logging.html] level log)

_Caused by: org.postgresql.util.PSQLException: ERROR: insert or update on table "CUsage" violates foreign key constraint "FK_CUsage_AUsage_AUsageId"_
_Detail: Key (AUsageId)=(e9aa119d-15a4-4bcd-9c5d-02183dc3ff4b) is not present in table "AUsage"_
h2. How to reproduce the issue using our [tutorial|https://github.com/debezium/debezium-examples/tree/main/tutorial] deployment?

<Your answer>
h1. Feature request or enhancement

For feature requests or enhancements, provide this information, please:
h2. Which use case/requirement will be addressed by the proposed feature?

<Your answer>
h2. Implementation ideas (optional)

<Your answer>
author: jpechane

title: Add support for plugging in Custom SchemaFactory/Envelope for Envelope Schema [DBZ-8967]
body:
Migrated from [DBZ-8967](https://issues.redhat.com/browse/DBZ-8967)

h1. Feature request or enhancement

For feature requests or enhancements, provide this information, please:
h2. Which use case/requirement will be addressed by the proposed feature?

*Debezium (DBZ)* emits standard CDC events and supports the use of custom SMTs to reshape records into the desired format. While SMTs are powerful, they introduce additional overhead when schema is reconstructed . For instance, the Debezium Server Iceberg sink uses the _unwrap_ SMT to convert CDC records into a structure compatible with Iceberg.

Introducing support for configuring or using a custom {{SchemaFactory}} to define the envelope schema can help reduce latency, especially when the sink does not require the standard CDC format.

 
h2. Implementation ideas (optional)

Add a standard interface like AbstractSourceInfoStructMaker (for source info) at Envelop level.
author: jpechane

title: Postgres: Potential data loss on connector restart [DBZ-9149]
body:
Migrated from [DBZ-9149](https://issues.redhat.com/browse/DBZ-9149)

In order to make your issue reports as actionable as possible, please provide the following information, depending on the issue type.
h1. Bug report

For bug reports, provide this information, please:
h2. What Debezium connector do you use and what version?

3.1.0.Final
h2. What is the connector configuration?

N/A
h2. What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?

(E.g. on-premises, with a specific cloud provider, etc.)

Postgres version 13.
h2. What behavior do you expect?

No data loss on connector restart at any point.
h2. What behavior do you see?

Data loss.
h2. Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?

(Ideally, also verify with latest Alpha/Beta/CR version)

Yes
h2. Do you have the connector logs, ideally from start till finish?

(You might be asked later to provide [DEBUG/TRACE|https://debezium.io/documentation/reference/stable/operations/logging.html] level log)

<Your answer>
h2. How to reproduce the issue using our [tutorial|https://github.com/debezium/debezium-examples/tree/main/tutorial] deployment?
 # Create a connector
 # Begin transaction, Insert multiple records (R1, R2 & R3) and end transaction. Lets say LSNs of R1, R2 and R3 are LSN1, LSN 2, LSN3 and transaction commit LSN is LSN 4
 # Restart the connector when it is processing the records of the transaction created Step 2. Lets LSN1 and LSN2 are processed and persisted offset is lsn = LSN2, lsn_commit = LSN4 and flushed to db i.e flushed db LSN is [LSN4|https://github.com/debezium/debezium/blob/main/debezium-connector-postgres/src/main/java/io/debezium/connector/postgresql/PostgresStreamingChangeEventSource.java#L448] 
 # LSN3 is not processed due to restart.
 # LSN3 is missing after restart.

source sequences of the records

R1 : [LSN4, LSN1] 

R2 : [LSN4, LSN2]

R3 : [LSN4, LSN3]

On Connector restart, database resume from LSN 4 because DB flush LSN is LSN4.
h1. Feature request or enhancement

For feature requests or enhancements, provide this information, please:
h2. Which use case/requirement will be addressed by the proposed feature?

<Your answer>
h2. Implementation ideas (optional)

Use the record LSN as the flushed DB LSN instead of the commit LSN, since the commit LSN is always greater than the LSN of records of the transaction.
author: jpechane

project_fields.Title: Postgres: Potential data loss on connector restart [DBZ-9149]

title: JdbcOffsetBackingStore cannot be enabled when using Debezium Operator [DBZ-9152]
body:
Migrated from [DBZ-9152](https://issues.redhat.com/browse/DBZ-9152)

h1. Bug report
h2. What Debezium connector do you use and what version?

Debezioum Operator Helm chart v 3.1.2

Makes use of Debezium 3.1.2-fina
h2. What is the connector configuration?

The DebeziumServer that is deployed with the operator:
{code:java}
apiVersion: debezium.io/v1alpha1
kind: DebeziumServer
metadata:
  name: foo-server
# https://github.com/debezium/debezium-operator/blob/main/docs/reference.adoc#debezium-operator-schema-reference-debeziumserverspec
spec:
  image: "quay.io/debezium/server:3.1.2-Final"
  quarkus:
    config:
      kubernetes-config.enabled: true
      kubernetes-config.secrets: db-credentials
  runtime:
    api:
      enabled: true
  sink:
    type: pulsar
    config:
		client.serviceUrl: pulsar://pulsar-standalone:6650
  source:
    class: io.debezium.connector.sqlserver.SqlServerConnector
    sourceClass: io.debezium.connector.sqlserver.SqlServerConnector
    offset:
      flush.interval.ms: 60000
l     
	  # https://github.com/debezium/debezium/blob/main/debezium-storage/debezium-storage-jdbc/src/main/java/io/debezium/storage/jdbc/offset/JdbcOffsetBackingStoreConfig.java
      # https://github.com/debezium/debezium/blob/main/debezium-storage/debezium-storage-jdbc/src/main/java/io/debezium/storage/jdbc/JdbcCommonConfig.java
      
	  # << PROBLEM HERE >> 
	  # I should put:
	  # storage: io.debezium.storage.jdbc.offset.JdbcOffsetBackingStore
	  # But then the yaml is not really valid anymore, and below jdbc settings are ignored
	  storage:
        jdbc:
          connection: # <-- btw, no mention of this level in the documentation (https://github.com/debezium/debezium-operator/blob/main/docs/reference.adoc#debezium-operator-schema-reference-jdbcoffsetstore); 
		              # however, if you ommit it, the server complains about missing url etc
            url: jdbc:sqlserver://mssql-latest:1433;sendStringParametersAsUnicode=false;encrypt=false;databaseName=DICCO
            user: '${DB_DDL_USER}'
            password: '${DB_DDL_PASSWORD}'
            wait.retry.delay.ms: 2000
            retry.max.attempts: 20
          offset:
            table:
              name: debezium_offset_table
        config:
    schemaHistory:
      memory: { }
    config:
	  # this doesn't help either; it's simply ignored
      offset.storage: io.debezium.storage.jdbc.offset.JdbcOffsetBackingStore
{code}
Look at the comments I've put in the yaml above.

I cannot 'force' the `io.debezium.storage.jdbc.offset.JdbcOffsetBackingStore` as that yields the yaml invalid. I would have to put:
{code:java}
...
    offset:
      flush.interval.ms: 60000
      storage: io.debezium.storage.jdbc.offset.JdbcOffsetBackingStore
      storage:
        jdbc:
           ....{code}
I tried forcing the setting using the source's 'global' config, but te setting is not picked up.

The `DebeziumServer` deploys, but its output suggests that the `MemoryOffsetBackingStore` :

{"timestamp":"2025-06-17T14:38:19.202099434Z","sequence":768,"loggerClassName":"org.slf4j.impl.Slf4jLogger","loggerName":"io.debezium.connector.common.BaseSourceTask","level":"INFO","message":"Starting SqlServerConnectorTask with configuration:\n   connector.class = io.debezium.connector.sqlserver.SqlServerConnector\n   record.processing.shutdown.timeout.ms = 1000\n   header.converter.header = json\n   record.processing.order = ORDERED\n   topic.prefix = calendar-acl-cdc\n   decimal.handling.mode = double\n   offset.storage.file.filename = \n   {*}offset.storage.pulsar.client.serviceUrl = pulsar://pulsar-standalone:6650{*}\n   record.processing.threads = \n   errors.retry.delay.initial.ms = 300\n   schema.history.internal.pulsar.client.serviceUrl = pulsar://pulsar-standalone:6650\n   value.converter = org.apache.kafka.connect.json.JsonConverter\n   key.converter = org.apache.kafka.connect.json.JsonConverter\n   tenant = connect-evolution\n   header.converter.value = json\n   database.user = dicco_user\n   database.dbname = dicco\n   database.names = dicco\n   offset.storage = {*}org.apache.kafka.connect.storage.MemoryOffsetBackingStore{*}\n   header.converter.key = json\n   key.converter.value = json\n   value.converter.header = json\n   time.precision.mode = connect\n   key.converter.header = json\n   database.server.name = cdc\n   offset.flush.timeout.ms = 5000\n   errors.retry.delay.max.ms = 10000\n   snapshot.isolation.mode = read_committed\n   database.port = 1433\n   value.converter.value = json\n   offset.flush.interval.ms = 60000\n   internal.task.management.timeout.ms = 180000\n   schema.history.internal = io.debezium.relational.history.MemorySchemaHistory\n   record.processing.with.serial.consumer = false\n   errors.max.retries = -1\n   database.hostname = mssql-latest\n   database.password = ********\n   namespace = calendar-acl-cdc\n   name = pulsar\n   task.id = 0\n   table.include.list = ACERTA.GBTEMC01,ACERTA.GBTEMC02,ACERTA.GBTEMC06,ACERTA.GBTEMC03,ACERTA.GBTPAA30,ACERTA.GBTPAA31,ACERTA.GBTPAA32,ACERTA.GBTPAA01,ACERTA.GBTPAA02,ACERTA.GBTPAA10,ACERTA.GBTPAA12,ACERTA.GBTPAA51,ACERTA.GBTOAA01,ACERTA.GBTOAA03,ACERTA.GBTEMR01,ACERTA.GBTEMR02,ACERTA.GBTEMR20,ACERTA.GBTEMR23,ACERTA.GBTACD01,ACERTA.GBTSSR02\n   database.trustServerCertificate = true\n   value.converter.key = json\n   snapshot.mode = schema_only\n   key.converter.key = json\n","threadName":"pool-9-thread-1","threadId":45,"mdc":{},"ndc":"","hostName":"debezium-server-d4448678c-vj2z4","processName":"/usr/lib/jvm/java-21-openjdk-21.0.7.0.6-2.el8.x86_64/bin/java","processId":1}

I don't know why *offset.storage.pulsar.client.serviceUrl* ** is still being set, but I will ignore this :)

Another 'weird' thing is that I don't see any properties being logged that are part of the JDBC-config that I passed in the jdbc.connection.* section. But if I omit this config, the server complains, so I guess it must be there.

I looked at the official IT test [https://github.com/debezium/debezium/blob/cd75ed1c01d2245c93554aad25b09b19938b8dce/debezium-storage/debezium-storage-jdbc/src/test/java/io/debezium/storage/jdbc/JdbcOffsetBackingStoreIT.java#L133]

This IT test does NOT read an actual yaml config file, so there it actually can work. But IMHO, this is conflicting with the configuration that you need in the DebeziumServer CR.
h2. What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?

On-premises kubernetes; MS SQL Server container running in same namespace as debezium. Connection is not a problem, as the DebeziumServer starts streaming to Pulsar. But the offset is kept in memory. 
h2. What behavior do you expect?

io.debezium.storage.jdbc.offset.JdbcOffsetBackingStore should be instantiated and activated.
h2. What behavior do you see?

I see that the *org.apache.kafka.connect.storage.MemoryOffsetBackingStore* is used.
h2. Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?

This is the latest released version.

h2. Logs
Logs in attachment.

title: Strengthen Debezium platform  helm chart [DBZ-9324]
body:
Migrated from [DBZ-9324](https://issues.redhat.com/browse/DBZ-9324)

hello,
i have some suggestions which stem from my try to use this helm chart (with external db)
couple of problems with helm values:
{code:java}
domain:
  url: dbz.my.sv
database:
  enabled: false
  name: debezium
  host: postgresql.infra
  # port: 5432  <----- there is no port support here what if my db is on another port?
  auth:
    existingSecret: debezium-postgresql  #<------- if username and password is set this SHOULD be left empty but it's not possible {code}

and one more important thing i had to go through the code and read the database charts to see that i need to encode my username/password in secret to base64 before using, it's a good idea to append this to the description of the values in the docs.
author: jpechane

title: Enable autoRestart in the KafkaConnector YAML example [DBZ-9185]
body:
Migrated from [DBZ-9185](https://issues.redhat.com/browse/DBZ-9185)

'- [Enhancement request] Enable autoRestart in the KafkaConnector YAML example
 -- When running Debezium with the KafkaConnector on OpenShift, there are cases where Debezium tasks may remain stopped due to some error, and our technical support engineers occasionally receive high-priority inquiries  regarding how to handle such situations.
 -- The KafkaConnector includes an autoRestart setting(1) that automatically restarts tasks if they stop for any reason. I believe this setting should be enabled in many cases to improve Debezium availability. Should we add an example of enabling this setting in the documentation for Debezium running on OpenShift?
 -- If the autoRestart setting(1) is not appropriate, please feel free to reject this ticket.
 -- (1)  Chapter 135. AutoRestart schema reference
 --- [https://docs.redhat.com/en/documentation/red_hat_streams_for_apache_kafka/2.7/html/streams_for_apache_kafka_api_reference/type-AutoRestart-reference]
author: jpechane

title: Failed to Automatically Create debezium_database_history [DBZ-8769]
body:
Migrated from [DBZ-8769](https://issues.redhat.com/browse/DBZ-8769)

When using {*}{*}debezium-storage-jdbc to automatically generate debezium_database_history, an error occurs because the length of the history_data column is too large.
h2. What Debezium connector do you use and what version?

io.debezium:debezium-storage-jdbc:3.0.6
h2. What is the connector configuration?

```

Embedded engine with Spring Boot

@Bean 
fun debeziumFactory(): Configuration { 
return Configuration.create()

.with("schema.history.internal", "io.debezium.storage.jdbc.history.JdbcSchemaHistory")

...

```
h2. What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?

mysql:8.0, using utf8mb4
h2. What behavior do you expect?

Automatically create debezium_database_history table and start application
h2. What behavior do you see?

When running the application, the following error occurs.

```

Column length too big for column 'history_data' (max = 16383); use BLOB or TEXT instead

```
h2. Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?

not confirmed

when using debezium embedded engine with Spring Boot and managing schema history with mysql, an error occurs when starting the application due to the size of the history_data column.

          at java.base/java.util.concurrent.ThreadPoolExecutor$Worker.run(ThreadPoolExecutor.java:642)
          at java.base/java.lang.Thread.run(Thread.java:1583)
 
It breaks on the 'method' line in the select from my table
author: jpechane

project_fields.Title: DDL statement couldn't be parsed. [DBZ-8819]

title: PostgreSQL WAL files not being released despite successful event processing in Debezium PostgreSQL connector [DBZ-8887]

# Set permissions for the new JARs
RUN chown jboss:jboss /debezium/lib/*.jar && \
    chmod 644 /debezium/lib/*.jar

# Copy the template configuration file and entrypoint script
COPY application.properties.template /debezium/config/application.properties.template
COPY entrypoint.sh /debezium/entrypoint.sh# Set permissions

    -XX:+AlwaysPreTouch"

And entrypoint.sh
{code:java}
#!/bin/bash
set -eecho "Starting configuration process..."

if [ -f /debezium/config/application.properties.template ]; then
    echo "Successfully found configuration file. Content preview:"

else
    echo "Error: Could not find file"
    exit 1
fiecho "Current template content (for debugging):"

mv /debezium/config/application.properties.template /debezium/config/application.propertiesecho "Final configuration file content (for debugging):"
cat /debezium/config/application.propertiesecho "Configuration complete. Starting Debezium server..."
bash ./run.sh {code}
 
h2. What behavior do you expect?

I expect the PostgreSQL replication slot to regularly update its {{confirmed_flush_lsn}} value as Debezium processes WAL entries, allowing PostgreSQL to release processed WAL segments and maintain a reasonable WAL size.
h2. What behavior do you see?

The replication slot's {{confirmed_flush_lsn}} is not advancing despite Debezium successfully:
 # Processing records (logs show "records sent during previous...")
 # Correctly updating offsets in Redis
 # Successfully publishing events to RabbitMQ

This results in:
 * WAL files growing to 59 GB despite a small database size (18 MB)
 * Replication slot showing status "extended"
 * Significant lag (59 GB) between {{pg_current_wal_lsn()}} and {{confirmed_flush_lsn}}

{code:java}
SELECT slot_name, confirmed_flush_lsn,
       pg_wal_lsn_diff(pg_current_wal_lsn(), confirmed_flush_lsn) as lag_bytes,
       pg_size_pretty(pg_wal_lsn_diff(pg_current_wal_lsn(), confirmed_flush_lsn)) as lag_size
FROM pg_replication_slots;

Result:
[
  {
    "slot_name": "debezium",
    "confirmed_flush_lsn": "17A/89005C90",
    "lag_bytes": 62864205496,
    "lag_size": "59 GB"
  }
] {code}
Redis offset storage shows transactions being processed:
{code:java}
Field: ["rabbitmq",{"server":"esz_outbox"}]
Value: {"lsn_proc":1645408689392,"messageType":"INSERT","lsn_commit":1625796140176,"lsn":1645408689392,"txId":6787310,"ts_usec":1742484554528465} {code}
But the replication slot's confirmed_flush_lsn remains at "17A/89005C90" indefinitely.

h2. Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?

(Ideally, also verify with latest Alpha/Beta/CR version)

Could not verify, not sure how to quickly test and see it happening :/
h2. Do you have the connector logs, ideally from start till finish?

(You might be asked later to provide [DEBUG/TRACE|https://debezium.io/documentation/reference/stable/operations/logging.html] level log)

Yes, I've attached logs showing the connector startup and processing messages. The logs confirm that records are being successfully processed and sent, but the WAL position acknowledgment isn't happening.

title: JDBC offset store requires PK when connecting with a MySQL cluster
body:
## Bug report

**What Debezium connector do you use and what version?**

We use the MySQL connector (version 3.3.1.Final)

---

**What is the connector configuration?**

Not really relevant for this issue, I left all relevant info out of the config

```
  debezium:
    format:
      schemas:
        enable: false
    source:
      schema:
        history:
          internal:
            ".": io.debezium.storage.jdbc.history.JdbcSchemaHistory
            jdbc:
              connection:
              table:
                ddl: "CREATE TABLE debezium_database_history (`id` VARCHAR(36) NOT NULL, `history_data` BLOB, `history_data_seq` INTEGER, `record_insert_ts` TIMESTAMP NOT NULL, `record_insert_seq` INTEGER NOT NULL, PRIMARY KEY (id))"
            store.only.captured.tables.ddl: true
      poll.interval.ms: 25
      offset:
        flush.interval.ms: 0
        storage:
          ".": io.debezium.storage.jdbc.offset.JdbcOffsetBackingStore
          jdbc:
            connection:
            table:
              ddl: "CREATE TABLE debezium_offset_storage (`id` VARCHAR(36) NOT NULL, `offset_key` VARCHAR(1255), `offset_val` VARCHAR(1255), `record_insert_ts` TIMESTAMP NOT NULL, `record_insert_seq` INTEGER NOT NULL, PRIMARY KEY (id))"
```

---

**What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?**

on-prem, Percona cluster (https://docs.percona.com/percona-server/8.0/release-notes/8.0.41-32.html) with galera. Based on MySQL version 8.0.41. We have 3 nodes. 1 master and 2 slaves (which when no issues occur only replicated the data

---

**What behavior do you expect?**

We are using a Percona cluster with 3 nodes and Apache Pulsar. We notice when switching to the jdbc store, that we needed override the table DDL for both the offset table and history table.

The default is

```
CREATE TABLE debezium_offset_storage (
id VARCHAR(36)      NOT NULL,
offset_key          VARCHAR(1255),
offset_val          VARCHAR(1255),
record_insert_ts    TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,
record_insert_seq   INTEGER NOT NULL)
```

For a cluster, this doesn't work as a Primary key is expected. So we configured the create DDL with the following:
```
CREATE TABLE debezium_offset_storage (
id VARCHAR(36) NOT NULL, 
offset_key VARCHAR(1255), 
offset_val  VARCHAR(1255), 
record_insert_ts TIMESTAMP NOT NULL, 
record_insert_seq INTEGER NOT NULL,
PRIMARY KEY (id))
```
We did exactly the same for the history table.

We were wondering why this is not the default as I assume lots of people run a cluster.

---

**What behavior do you see?**

No primary key for offset table and history table, breaks JDBC offset store. 
Ideally the default or easily configurable without the need to override the whole DDL

---

**Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?**

Yes

---

**Do you have the connector logs, ideally from start till finish?**

 Not relevant

---

**How to reproduce the issue using our [tutorial](https://github.com/debezium/debezium-examples/tree/main/tutorial) deployment?**

Not possible to reproduce with tutorial deployment, we will need a database that runs in cluster mode.
author: ThomasVerhoeven1998

project_fields.Title: JDBC offset store requires PK when connecting with a MySQL cluster

title: Oracle connector can incorrectly build on-demand table schema with optional key fields.
body:
## Bug report

**What Debezium connector do you use and what version?**

Bug is present in current main branch.

---

**What is the connector configuration?**

Config that caused us to run into this issue:

`
database.password=<snip>
database.user=xstrmadmin
database.hostname=db.production.local
database.port=1521
database.dbname=db_special_svc.world
`

Note: We're now correctly setting the service name by setting `database.url`, and have `database.dbname` set to actual db name,  not service name.

Running in Kafka Connect, with:

`
name=mktx.ref.lseg.AdUserSession-debezium-source-connector
    connector.class=io.debezium.connector.oracle.OracleConnector
    tasks.max=1
    errors.tolerance=none
    errors.log.enable=true
    snapshot.mode=initial
    schema.history.internal=io.debezium.storage.file.history.FileSchemaHistory
    schema.history.internal.file.filename=/opt/offsets/schema-history.txt
    schema.history.internal.store.only.captured.tables.ddl=true
    database.connection.adapter=xstream
    topic.prefix=temp-topic-prefix
    decimal.handling.mode=string
    table.include.list=SCHEMA.TABLE1,SCHEMA.TABLE2
    database.out.server.name=MY_OUTBOUND
    ...
`


---

**What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?**

On-prem Oracle RAC v23.6
Debezium running inside Kafka Connect.

---

**What behavior do you expect?**

Originally, we were setting the Oracle service name in `database.dbname`. This meant that the `TableId` of the schemas built during the initial bootstrapping incorrectly started with `DB_SPECIAL_SVC.WORLD.`.

When the first DML event was received by the outbound server it had a source db of `DB.WORLD`. This meant the `table` schema in `LcrEventHandler.dispatchDataChangeEvent` was `null`.

What I would then expect to happen, is that it builds the _correct_ table schema on demand.  There is code to do this.

---

**What behavior do you see?**

We see the following log line, as you'd expect:

```
Obtaining schema for table DB.WORLD.SCHEMA.TABLEA, which should be already loaded, this may signal potential bug in fetching table schemas.
```

and Debezium does build a table schema. Unfortunately, it builds the _wrong_ schema sometimes!

If the table was originally created with DDL where the primary key field(s) were not explicitly marked as `NON NULL` then the field(s) is incorrectly marked as optional.  

I've happens because the on-demand table schema building invoked by `LcrEventHandler` is a completely different code path to the table schema building done during initialisation. This second, much less frequently used, code path takes a different approach and has bugs.

The building of table schema during initialisation is built using  JDBC metadata via jdbcConnection.readSchema():

 1. Uses DatabaseMetaData.getColumns() to read column information
  2. At JdbcConnection.java:1368, sets column optionality based on JDBC metadata:
  column.optional(isNullable(columnMetadata.getInt(11)));
  3. Since Oracle enforces NOT NULL on primary key columns, the JDBC metadata correctly reports the ID column as non-nullable (IS_NULLABLE = 0)

Whereas the building of table schemas if a DML event is received for a table for which no schema is present builds the query by parsing the DDL statements for the table.

  1. Calling getTableMetadataDdl(tableId) at line 175
  2. This uses dbms_metadata.get_ddl('TABLE',?,?) to get the current DDL (OracleConnection.java:334)
  3. The DDL is then parsed using OracleDdlParser via OracleSchemaChangeEventEmitter

Importantly, there are at least two bugs in this DDL parsing approach:
1. If the initial `CREATE TABLE` statement doesn't mark the primary key column(s) as `NOT NULL` but does add a primary key constraint, e.g.

```
  CREATE TABLE foo (
    ID NUMBER,
    CONSTRAINT pk_foo PRIMARY KEY (ID)
  );
```
  Then `CreateTableParserListener` does not take into account that `ID` is implicitly non-null, as its part of a primary key.
2. If the initial `CREATE TABLE` statement doesn't mark the primary key column(s) as `NOT NULL` but does add a primary key constraint latter via an `ALTER TABLE` statement, e.g.

```
  CREATE TABLE foo (
    ID NUMBER
  );

  ALTER TABLE foo ADD  CONSTRAINT pk_foo PRIMARY KEY (ID);
```
  Then `AlterTableParserListener.java` does not take into account that `ID` is implicitly non-null, as its part of a primary key.

It would seem to me that the best fix would be to have LcrEventHandler build the table schema in the same way as the initial bootstrapping.

**NOTE**: The issue I'm raising is that there are two code paths within Debezium to build a table's schema. One used frequently on `initial` / `recovery` which works correctly and a second that is invoked infrequently when a DML event is received for a table not in the schema history.  This second code path has bugs around the optionality of primary key fields: potentially incorrectly marking them as optional.   

So this bug is not about how to configure my setup correctly (I've sorted that), but to highlight this broken code path, which can end up being executed for many reasons, including due to my misconfiguration.


---

**Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?**

_Ideally, also verify with the latest Alpha/Beta/CR version._

`Yes`

---

**Do you have the connector logs, ideally from start till finish?**

_You might be asked later to provide [DEBUG/TRACE](https://debezium.io/documentation/reference/stable/operations/logging.html) level log._

Yes, will add as a comment.

---

**How to reproduce the issue using our [tutorial](https://github.com/debezium/debezium-examples/tree/main/tutorial) deployment?**

Would need:

1. Add a Oracle service
2. Use the service name in `database.dbname`.
3. Create a table with no explicit `NON NULL` primary key
4. do initial load to build table schema
5. insert row into table, causing a DML event on the outbound server.
author: big-andy-coates

project_fields.Title: Oracle connector can incorrectly build on-demand table schema with optional key fields.

title: Oracle numeric default values wrapped in `(` and `)` are mistakenly treated as expressions
body:
## Bug report

**What Debezium connector do you use and what version?**

3.4.0-SNAPSHOT

---

**What is the connector configuration?**

N/A

---

**What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?**

_E.g. on-premises, with a specific cloud provider, etc._

Oracle 21.3.0, mode irrelevant

---

**What behavior do you expect?**

I expect that the `SCOPEID` field would be treated the same as `LNUMID`.
```
"LNUMID" NUMBER(10, 0) DEFAULT 0 NOT NULL ENABLED,
"SCOPEID" NUMBER(10, 0) DEFAULT (0) NOT NULL ENABLED,
```

---

**What behavior do you see?**

While `LNUMID` is emitted with a default value of `0`, the `SCOPEID` field reports that the default value cannot be parsed because Debezium is treating it as an expression.

---

**Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?**

_Ideally, also verify with the latest Alpha/Beta/CR version._

Yes

---

**Do you have the connector logs, ideally from start till finish?**

_You might be asked later to provide [DEBUG/TRACE](https://debezium.io/documentation/reference/stable/operations/logging.html) level log._

```
2025-12-04 07:46:05,458 WARN   Oracle||snapshot  Cannot parse column default value '(0)' to type '2'.  Expression evaluation is not supported.   [io.debezium.connector.oracle.OracleDefaultValueConverter]
```
author: Naros

project_fields.Title: Oracle numeric default values wrapped in `(` and `)` are mistakenly treated as expressions

title: Bump Pulsar Java client version in Debezium Server Pulsar connector
body:
Hi!

The reason I would like to propose this upgrade is because we are seeing some very odd behaviour with our consumers where messages produced by Debezium Server (which seem to be batch messages in an older message format) arrive at our consumers but yield completely broken org.apache.pulsar.client.api.Message instances with no data within (backing byte buffer is completely empty). This sadly leads to data loss because our consumers just can't see the data within. Upgrading the java client version fixes this issue.

We observed this behavior with the 4.0.6.7 version of the Pulsar Broker (managed and patched by an external vendor). And clients that use 4.x.y series Java client libraries.
author: gmiklos-ltg

title: JDBC history store create table DDL fails on MySQL and Oracle databases
body:
## Bug report

**What Debezium connector do you use and what version?**

MySQL connector version 3.3.1.Final

---

**What is the connector configuration?**

Not really relevant for this issue, I left all relevant info out of the config


```
debezium:
    format:
      schemas:
        enable: false
    source:
      schema:
        history:
          internal:
            ".": io.debezium.storage.jdbc.history.JdbcSchemaHistory
            jdbc:
              connection:
              table:
                ddl: "CREATE TABLE debezium_database_history (`id` VARCHAR(36) NOT NULL, `history_data` BLOB, `history_data_seq` INTEGER, `record_insert_ts` TIMESTAMP NOT NULL, `record_insert_seq` INTEGER NOT NULL, PRIMARY KEY (id, history_data_seq))"
            store.only.captured.tables.ddl: true
      poll.interval.ms: 25
      offset:
        flush.interval.ms: 0
        storage:
          ".": io.debezium.storage.jdbc.offset.JdbcOffsetBackingStore
          jdbc:
            connection:
            table:
              ddl: "CREATE TABLE debezium_offset_storage (`id` VARCHAR(36) NOT NULL, `offset_key` VARCHAR(1255), `offset_val` VARCHAR(1255), `record_insert_ts` TIMESTAMP NOT NULL, `record_insert_seq` INTEGER NOT NULL, PRIMARY KEY (id))"
```

Important here is that we changed the datatype of history_data from VARCHAR(65000) to BLOB

---

**What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?**


on-prem, Percona cluster (https://docs.percona.com/percona-server/8.0/release-notes/8.0.41-32.html) with galera. Based on MySQL version 8.0.41. We have 3 nodes. 1 master and 2 slaves (which when no issues occur only replicated the data

---

**What behavior do you expect?**

Create table DDL should work on most well known database like MySQL, sqlLite, Oracle, ... for all typical encodings that are used

---

**What behavior do you see?**

When the default create table DDL is execute the first time:

```
CREATE TABLE debezium_database_history (`id` VARCHAR(36) NOT NULL, `history_data` VARCHAR(65000), `history_data_seq` INTEGER, `record_insert_ts` TIMESTAMP NOT NULL, `record_insert_seq` INTEGER NOT NULL, PRIMARY KEY (id))
```

The database will throw an error: 

`[42000][1074] Column length too big for column 'history_data' (max = 16383); use BLOB or TEXT instead`

We should dynamically determine what kind of database we are using. We could switch to BLOB which is supported by MySQL, Oracle and SQLlite **but not for PostgreSQL** (they use bytea)

---

**Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?**

 yes


**How to reproduce the issue using our [tutorial](https://github.com/debezium/debezium-examples/tree/main/tutorial) deployment?**

No reproducible since we do not use jdbc offset and history store in the tutorial
author: ThomasVerhoeven1998

project_fields.Title: JDBC history store create table DDL fails on MySQL and Oracle databases

title: PostgreSQL Connector: Keep-alive thread logs ERROR on normal shutdown due to InterruptedException
body:
## Bug report

**What Debezium connector do you use and what version?**

Connector: debezium-connector-postgres
Version: 3.3.1.Final

---

**What is the connector configuration?**

Any standard PostgreSQL connector configuration. The issue occurs regardless of specific settings since the keep-alive mechanism is always enabled and cannot be disabled via configuration.

---

**What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?**

Any PostgreSQL version. The issue is not database-specific - it occurs in the connector's internal thread management during shutdown.

---

**What behavior do you expect?**

The connector should shut down cleanly without logging ERROR-level messages. When stopKeepAlive() is called during normal shutdown, the InterruptedException thrown by shutdownNow() interrupting the sleeping keep-alive thread should be handled gracefully and logged at DEBUG level (if at all), since this is expected behavior.

---

**What behavior do you see?**

An ERROR-level log message with full stack trace appears during normal connector shutdown:
```
ERROR Unexpected exception while performing keepalive status update on the replication stream
java.lang.InterruptedException: sleep interrupted
    at java.lang.Thread.sleep(Thread.java:513)
    at io.debezium.util.Metronome$1.pause(Metronome.java:57)
    at io.debezium.connector.postgresql.connection.PostgresReplicationConnection$1.lambda$startKeepAlive$0(PostgresReplicationConnection.java:1007)
    at java.util.concurrent.Executors$RunnableAdapter.call(Executors.java:545)
    at java.util.concurrent.FutureTask.run(FutureTask.java:328)
    at java.util.concurrent.ThreadPoolExecutor.runWorker(ThreadPoolExecutor.java:1095)
    at java.util.concurrent.ThreadPoolExecutor$Worker.run(ThreadPoolExecutor.java:619)
    at java.lang.Thread.run(Thread.java:1447)
```

---

**Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?**

Yes, the issue exists in the current codebase. The problematic code is in PostgresReplicationConnection.java lines 1005-1019 (in the startKeepAlive method).

---

**Do you have the connector logs, ideally from start till finish?**

The ERROR log appears specifically during shutdown when stopKeepAlive() is called, either:
When AsyncEmbeddedEngine.close() is invoked
During reconnection in PostgresStreamingChangeEventSource.execute()
In the finally block of streaming via cleanUpStreamingOnStop()

---

**How to reproduce the issue using our [tutorial](https://github.com/debezium/debezium-examples/tree/main/tutorial) deployment?**

1. Start any PostgreSQL connector using AsyncEmbeddedEngine (or standard embedded engine)
2. Let it run for a moment to establish streaming
3. Call engine.close() to shut down the connector
4. Observe ERROR log with InterruptedException stack trace

Proposed fix:
In PostgresReplicationConnection.java, modify the exception handling in startKeepAlive() to catch InterruptedException separately:
```
catch (InterruptedException ie) {
    // InterruptedException is expected when stopKeepAlive() calls shutdownNow()
    // during normal shutdown - this is not an error condition
    LOGGER.debug("Keep-alive thread interrupted, shutting down");
    Thread.currentThread().interrupt();
    return;
}
catch (Exception exp) {
    LOGGER.error("Unexpected exception while performing keepalive status update on the replication stream", exp);
    return;
}
```
author: hello-maur

project_fields.Title: PostgreSQL Connector: Keep-alive thread logs ERROR on normal shutdown due to InterruptedException

title: Add Commit Signing Check for Pull Requests
body:
In accordance with the [Commonhaus Intellectual Property](https://www.commonhaus.org/policies/ip-policy/) section 2 and 2.1, all contributions to this project must be accompanied by either

- Developer Certificate of Origin (DCO)
- Contributor License Agreement (CLA)

The most straightforward approach would be to mandate that all commits be Signed-Off and treat the Sign-Off as DCO, allowing the contributor to confirm they have the necessary rights to submit the work.

There are two things we need to do.

1. Enable `Settings > General > Require contributors to sign off on web-based commits` in each Debezium repository. This applies only to actions users take within the GH web interface, such as selecting and editing a file and creating a commit/pull request directly in the web UI.
2. Introduce a `pull_request_target` check that runs on pull requests and amends the pull request with information about the requirement to sign off on all commits. I am considering using `1Password/check-signed-commits-action@v1`, but if there is a better option, we can adjust.

In terms of timeline, I was thinking that we would at least add a blog post, mailing list entry, and perhaps something on Zulip about the change this week, and announce that this would go into effect at the start of 2026. This allows everyone to begin setting up their environments without disrupting the current Debezium 3.4 workflow.
author: Naros

project_fields.Title: Add Commit Signing Check for Pull Requests

## Feature request or enhancement

_For feature requests or enhancements, please provide the following information:_

**Which use case/requirement will be addressed by the proposed feature?**

When the Oracle connector outputs "Offset SCN has not advanced in X mining sessions", this can often lead to questions about what transactions this could be related to, etc. To address this concern, the connector should include the active transaction IDs.

Have the buffered implementation provide a mechanism to generate a list of active transactions when the log entry is produced.

title: Update documentation for `reselector` post processor
body:
## Context

The documentation for `reselector` contains deprecated parameters which are not aligned with the code. The actual documentation presents the following:

```txt
  "post.processors": "reselector", 
  "reselector.type": "io.debezium.processors.reselect.ReselectColumnsPostProcessor", 
  "reselector.reselect.columns.include.list": "<schema>.<table>:<column>,<schema>.<table>:<column>", 
  "reselector.reselect.unavailable.values": "true", 
  "reselector.reselect.null.values": "true" 
  "reselector.reselect.use.event.key": "false" 
  "reselector.reselect.error.handling.mode": "WARN"
```

which is now aligned to `transformers` in this way:


```txt
  "post.processors": "reselector", 
  "post.processors.reselector.type": "io.debezium.processors.reselect.ReselectColumnsPostProcessor", 
  "post.processors.reselector.reselect.columns.include.list": "<schema>.<table>:<column>,<schema>.<table>:<column>", 
  "post.processors.reselector.reselect.unavailable.values": "true", 
  "post.processors.reselector.reselect.null.values": "true" 
  "post.processors.reselector.reselect.use.event.key": "false" 
  "post.processors.reselector.reselect.error.handling.mode": "WARN"
```


## Changes

Update the documentation to reflect the code

## References

https://github.com/debezium/debezium/pull/6523

title: Avoid logging Azure Eventhub connection string
body:
## Bug report

**What Debezium connector do you use and what version?**

Debezium Server Operator version `3.2.0` with `image: "quay.io/debezium/server:nightly"`

---

**What is the connector configuration?**

```
apiVersion: debezium.io/v1alpha1
kind: DebeziumServer
metadata:
  name: debezium-server
  namespace: {{ .Values.namespace }}
spec:
  image: "quay.io/debezium/server:nightly"
  quarkus:
    config:
      kubernetes-config.enabled: true
      kubernetes-config.secrets: debezium-secrets
  source:
    class: io.debezium.connector.postgresql.PostgresConnector
    config:
      database.hostname: {{ .Values.database.source.host }}
      database.port: {{ .Values.database.source.port }}
      database.user: {{ .Values.database.source.user }}
      database.password: ${SOURCE_DB_PASSWORD}
      database.dbname: {{ .Values.database.source.name }}
      topic.prefix: {{ .Values.kafka.topicPrefix }}
      plugin.name: pgoutput
    offset:
      jdbc:
        url: jdbc:postgresql://{{ .Values.database.offset.host }}:{{ .Values.database.offset.port }}/{{ .Values.database.offset.name }}
        user: {{ .Values.database.offset.user }}
        password: ${OFFSET_DB_PASSWORD}
        retryDelay: {{ .Values.database.offset.retryDelay }}
        maxRetries: {{ .Values.database.offset.maxRetries }}
    schemaHistory:
      jdbc:
        url: jdbc:postgresql://{{ .Values.database.schema.host }}:{{ .Values.database.schema.port }}/{{ .Values.database.schema.name }}
        user: {{ .Values.database.schema.user }}
        password: ${SCHEMA_DB_PASSWORD}
        retryDelay: {{ .Values.database.schema.retryDelay }}
        maxRetries: {{ .Values.database.schema.maxRetries }}
  sink:
    type: eventhubs
    config:
      connectionstring: ${EVENTHUBS_CONNECTION_STRING}
      hubname: {{ .Values.eventhubs.hubName }}
      maxbatchsize: {{ .Values.eventhubs.maxBatchSize }}
      hashmessagekeyfunction: sha256
  format:
    key:
      type: json
    value:
      type: json
  runtime:
    storage:
      data:
        type: persistent
        claimName: {{ .Values.storage.claimName }}
```

---

**What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?**

Not relevant

---

**What behavior do you expect?**

Debezium Server should avoid logging any sensitive value including the azure event hub connection string

---

**What behavior do you see?**

Currently in the server pod created via the operator it logs out
```
{
  "timestamp": "2025-12-08T22:09:42.580519629Z",
  "sequence": 983,
  "loggerClassName": "org.slf4j.impl.Slf4jLogger",
  "loggerName": "io.debezium.storage.jdbc.JdbcCommonConfig",
  "level": "INFO",
  "message": "Configuration for 'JdbcOffsetBackingStoreConfig' with prefix 'offset.storage.': {jdbc.password=********, eventhubs.connectionstring=Endpoint=sb://eh-namespace.servicebus.windows.net/;SharedAccessKeyName=RootManageSharedAccessKey;SharedAccessK
ey=<REDACTED>, jdbc.user=debezium, jdbc.retry.max.attempts=3, eventhubs.maxbatchsize=262144, jdbc.url=jdbc:postgresql://db-url.postgres.database.azure.com:5432/debezium, jdbc.wait.retry.delay.ms=1000,
 eventhubs.hubname=hubname, eventhubs.hashmessagekeyfunction=sha256, file.filename=}",
  "threadName": "pool-8-thread-1",
  "threadId": 47,
  "mdc": {},
  "ndc": "",
  "hostName": "debezium-server-78d8b64bbb-n5tsr",
  "processName": "/usr/lib/jvm/java-21-openjdk-21.0.9.0.10-1.el8.x86_64/bin/java",
  "processId": 1
}
```

As you can see, it correctly sanitizes the database password field but it is printing out the connection string in plaintext which contains an access key to the eventhub. If the logs were to get leaked (e.g. someone was able to get grafana access), then the root access key would also be leaked.

---

**Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?**

_Ideally, also verify with the latest Alpha/Beta/CR version._

Yes as I am using the nightly image as shown in the helm template above

---

**Do you have the connector logs, ideally from start till finish?**

See previous section with logs

---

**How to reproduce the issue using our [tutorial](https://github.com/debezium/debezium-examples/tree/main/tutorial) deployment?**

https://github.com/debezium/debezium-operator/tree/main/examples/postgres with basic event hub configuration deployed via k8s operator and then run `kubectl logs debezium-server-...  -n debezium | grep "SharedAccessKey" | jq` to get the access key log
author: trevor-inflection

project_fields.Title: Avoid logging Azure Eventhub connection string

---

**What is the connector configuration?**

```
        .with("snapshot.mode", "no_data")
        .with("schema.history.internal", "io.debezium.storage.file.history.FileSchemaHistory")
        .with("schema.history.internal.file.filename", sharedFolder + "/cdc/dbhistory.dat");
```

---

**What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?**

_E.g. on-premises, with a specific cloud provider, etc._

`on-premises. SqlServer 2022`

---

**What behaviour do you expect?**

`Snapshot will finish without error creating the offset file`

---

**What behavior do you see?**

`An IndexOutOfBoundsException for a history table (temporal table) in SQL Server causes Debezium's snapshot to fail. The issue appears to be related to the MSSQL JDBC driver v13.2.1.jre11 (it works forcing the 12.10.2.jre11), where the implementation of _getindexInfo_ has changed. During the snapshot, the following exception blocks the process because the table has neither a primary key (PK) nor a unique constraint (UC). These cannot be created on historical tables.`

---

**Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?**

_Ideally, also verify with the latest Alpha/Beta/CR version._

`Yes`

---

**Do you have the connector logs, ideally from start till finish?**

_You might be asked later to provide [DEBUG/TRACE](https://debezium.io/documentation/reference/stable/operations/logging.html) level log._

```
Caused by: java.lang.IndexOutOfBoundsException: Index -1 out of bounds for length 0
	at java.base/jdk.internal.util.Preconditions.outOfBounds(Preconditions.java:100)
	at java.base/jdk.internal.util.Preconditions.outOfBoundsCheckIndex(Preconditions.java:106)
	at java.base/jdk.internal.util.Preconditions.checkIndex(Preconditions.java:302)
	at java.base/java.util.Objects.checkIndex(Objects.java:365)
	at java.base/java.util.ArrayList.set(ArrayList.java:471)
	at io.debezium.util.Collect.set(Collect.java:287)
	at io.debezium.jdbc.JdbcConnection.readTableUniqueIndices(JdbcConnection.java:1478)
	at io.debezium.jdbc.JdbcConnection.readPrimaryKeyOrUniqueIndexNames(JdbcConnection.java:1487)
	at io.debezium.jdbc.JdbcConnection.readSchema(JdbcConnection.java:1259)
	at io.debezium.connector.sqlserver.SqlServerSnapshotChangeEventSource.readTableStructure(SqlServerSnapshotChangeEventSource.java:209)
	at io.debezium.connector.sqlserver.SqlServerSnapshotChangeEventSource.readTableStructure(SqlServerSnapshotChangeEventSource.java:40)

```

title: Update to latest LTS of Quarkus 3.27.x
body:
It seems that Debezium are affected by [CVE-2025-58057 vulnerability.](https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/cve-2025-58057) 
Netty version 4.1.124.Final and earlier are affected, while Debezium latest version contains Netty version 4.1.118.Final. 

The vulnerability is fixed in version 4.1.125.Final and later, please remediate accordingly.
Thank you very much.

---

**What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?**

_E.g. on-premises, with a specific cloud provider, etc._

`Oracle RAC 19.0.0.0.0`

---

**What behavior do you expect?**

With the correct configuration of the connector for replicating data from Oracle to PostgreSQL via Kafka, we expect:

*   **Stable Data Flow**: Changes from the source Oracle tables are applied to the corresponding PostgreSQL tables continuously and with minimal latency.
*   **High Performance**: Efficient handling of high load and rapid reduction of consumer lag is achieved by leveraging batch processing (`batch.size > 1`) and parallel tasks (`tasks.max > 1`).
*   **Fault Tolerance**: In case of transient network or database errors, the connector should automatically retry operations (`flush.max.retries`) without data loss and resume normal operation once the cause is resolved.
*   **Atomicity and Consistency**: All insert, update, and delete operations must execute safely under concurrent processing conditions without compromising data integrity.


---

**What behavior do you see?**

The actual operation of the connector is characterized by instability and a direct conflict between configuration goals:

*   **Periodic Deadlocks**: Errors with the message `ERROR: deadlock detected` regularly appear in both PostgreSQL and connector logs. This causes individual connector tasks to fail (transition to a `FAILED` state) without automatic recovery.
*   **Stability vs. Performance Conflict**:
    *   **Scenario A (High Performance)**: When using performance-optimized settings (`tasks.max=5`, `batch.size=50`), deadlocks occur frequently, disrupting stability.
    *   **Scenario B (Stability)**: Setting `batch.size=1` stops the deadlock errors, but performance drops to an unacceptable level because each record is processed in a separate transaction.
*   **Ambiguous Role of Parallelism**: The deadlock problem **occurs more frequently** when `tasks.max` is increased but **does not disappear completely** even when `tasks.max=1`. This indicates that resource contention arises not only between the connector's own tasks but also between the connector and other processes in the database (e.g., the main application).
*   **Root Cause (Based on Analysis)**: The problem is linked to the **`INSERT ... ON CONFLICT ... DO UPDATE` (UPSERT)** mechanism that Debezium uses by default to apply changes. When multiple records are processed concurrently—either within a single batch or by different tasks—the order in which rows are locked in PostgreSQL can lead to a classic cyclic wait (deadlock).

I looked into kafka topics. I have a lot of messages that have the same ID, but the LASTDATE changes and they end up in different partitions.

1 partition: ID = 121, LASTDATE: '2025-12-09 16:11:56'
2 partition: ID = 121, LASTDATE: '2025-12-09 16:16:23'

_Ideally, also verify with the latest Alpha/Beta/CR version._

`YES`

title: reorganize the debezium-quarkus repository
body:
## Context

The Debezium Extensions for Quarkus are currently divided into two categories: Runtime and Outbox. Additionally, the number of Runtime extensions is expected to grow as support for new connectors is introduced. A further complication arises from inconsistent naming conventions: the Outbox extension follows the quarkus-debezium pattern, whereas the Runtime extensions use debezium-extensions. These inconsistencies increase the complexity and duration of maintenance activities.

## Proposal

To improve maintainability and consistency, we propose reorganizing the extensions into three primary submodules:

```txt
- extensions
- extensions-core
- testing
- poms
```

Furthermore, all directories should be renamed to reflect only the main scope of the extension (e.g., outbox, mysql), while retaining `debezium` and `quarkus` exclusively within the `artifactId`. This approach will simplify the structure, enhance clarity, and reduce maintenance overhead.
author: kmos

## Feature request or enhancement

_For feature requests or enhancements, please provide the following information:_

**Which use case/requirement will be addressed by the proposed feature?**

As-is, the chart hardcodes `http://` schemas when generating `QUARKUS_HTTP_CORS_ORIGINS` in `conductor-deployment.yaml` and `CONDUCTOR_URL` in `stage-deployment.yaml`. This causes a host of errors when loading the UI over HTTPS (via Traefik, ALB, or similar).

**Implementation ideas (optional)**

Many ways to go about this depending on style philosophy. My first temptation would be to include the scheme in `.Values.domain.url`, but that would break the usage in `conductor-ingress`. Perhaps add scheme as its own value?

## Bug report

Using Debezium Server, tested version 3.3 and 3.4.0-beta1 and using HTTP sink, the headers duplicate every message it sends out.
It works on 3.1 but breaks since 3.2. When using the timescaledb transform, it also has the same issue since at least 3.0

See this REPRO: https://github.com/MV-GH/repro-debezium-server-bug-http-sink-headers

```properties
debezium.format.value=json

```

---

**What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?**

DB pg14, docker compose

**What behavior do you expect?**

Only 1 header, no duplicates

header duplicates

title: Remove unused quarkus.version.extension pom property
body: The latest Quarkus 3.30.5 was released.
author: Naros

project_fields.Title: Remove unused quarkus.version.extension pom property

title: Recovery private redo threads can lead to LogMiner failures
body:
## Bug report

**What Debezium connector do you use and what version?**

3.4.0.Beta1

---

**What is the connector configuration?**

N/A

---

**What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?**

on-prem, Version 19.28.0.0.0

---

**What behavior do you expect?**

Should not fail with `ORA-00310: archived log contains sequence 139983; sequence 139979 required`

---

**What behavior do you see?**

Connector fails `ORA-00310: archived log contains sequence 139983; sequence 139979 required` but when restarted, it often resumes with no issue.

---

**Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?**

_Ideally, also verify with the latest Alpha/Beta/CR version._

Yes

---

**Do you have the connector logs, ideally from start till finish?**

title: Incremental Snapshot Start Lag [DBZ-7256]
body:
Migrated from [DBZ-7256](https://issues.redhat.com/browse/DBZ-7256)

 
h2. What Debezium connector do you use and what version?

https://repo1.maven.org/maven2/io/debezium/debezium-connector-mysql/2.4.0.Final/debezium-connector-mysql-2.4.0.Final-plugin.tar.gz
h2. Relevant configuration?

connector.class    "io.debezium.connector.mysql.MySqlConnector"

incremental.snapshot.allow.schema.changes    "true"

incremental.snapshot.chunk.size    "500000" (we've tried all sorts of different values for this)

signal.enabled.channels "kafka"

read.only    "true"

 
h2. What is the captured database version and mode of depoyment?

{color:#d1d2d3}MySQL 5.7{color}
h2. What behaviour do you expect?

I expect that when a connector reads a signal for an incremental snapshot, and logs... `Requested 'INCREMENTAL' snapshot of data collections [...]`

that soon after we would get the STARTED notification in the notifications topic, and the incremental snapshot would begin.
h2. What behaviour do you see?

We are getting lag anywhere from 15 minutes (consistent) to 3 hours (more extreme) between the time the connector logs that it got the incremental snapshot request, and when the Incremental Snapshot STARTS. We have noticed that the time taken to actually perform the snapshot, between STARTED and COMPLETED, is very reasonable, but the time to START is scaling very poorly.

 
h2. Do you have the connector logs, ideally from start till finish?

Here is the correspondence with the logs and comments from Mario and Jiri. https://groups.google.com/g/debezium/c/5hQUAYQrRI0/m/b__-ERT_AAAJ?utm_medium=email&utm_source=footer
h2. How to reproduce the issue using our [tutorial|https://github.com/debezium/debezium-examples/tree/main/tutorial] deployment?

It is difficult to reproduce in a test env, as this issue seems to be a Production level performance issue. We are taking incremental snapshots on connectors that replicate thousands of tables. (this replication is tried and true, and has been running smoothly in prod for years, its just the Incremental Snapshotting piece that is not scaling).
h1. Feature request or enhancement

For feature requests or enhancements, provide this information, please:
h2. Which use case/requirement will be addressed by the proposed feature?

Faster Start Time for Incremental Snapshotting
author: jpechane

project_fields.Title: Incremental Snapshot Start Lag [DBZ-7256]

title: JDBC sink uses schema's default value when provided and field value is NULL [DBZ-7607]
body:
Migrated from [DBZ-7607](https://issues.redhat.com/browse/DBZ-7607)

If a field's schema defines a default value but the inserted value is null, the JDBC sink connector is binding the default value rather than null.  See zulip conversation - https://debezium.zulipchat.com/#narrow/stream/383534-community-jdbc/topic/postgres.20.20default.20value.20field.20issue/near/424999109
author: jpechane

title: Slow MySQL Data Snapshots on 2.6.1.Final [DBZ-7817]
body:
Migrated from [DBZ-7817](https://issues.redhat.com/browse/DBZ-7817)

My team is trying to upgrade to Debezium 2.6.1.Final from 2.2.1.Final. We currently run connectors for both mySQL 8 and mySQL 5 databases. When testing 2.6.1.Final in our staging env we have found that an initial data snapshot takes much longer than normal with the same configs as 2.2.1.Final. Specifically the Data Snapshot , where Debezium uses a transaction and REPEATABLE_READ to generate the read records from each table. We are using "when_needed" snapshots and "minimal_percona". 

 

Testing on MySQL 5 host with 700,000+ tables:

Current Prod Setup: {*}Debezium 2.2.1.Final + mysql-connector-j-8.0.31{*}: Fully snapshots +700,000 tables in less than 10 hours+

{*}Debezium 2.6.1.Final + mysql-connector-j-8.0.33{*}: Takes +over 3 days+ to snapshot the same MySQL host. (we downgraded the mysql plugin to be compatible)

*Debezium 2.6.1.Final + mysql-connector-j-8.0.31:* Takes +over 3 days+ to snapshot the same MySQL host. (we downgraded the mysql plugin to match prod exactly)

{*}Debezium 2.6.1.Final + mysql-connector-j-8.3.0{*}: Takes +over 3 days+ to snapshot the same MySQL host. 

 

We ran the same tests on a mysql 8 host and did not see any improvements with different driver versions.

We are tentatively concluding that there must be some change in the Debezium code that is significantly slowing down the Data Snapshot as we have held all other variables constant across versions.

We previously ran into an issue where the schema snapshot was taking quite a while on older versions of debezium, but that was fixed, in this case, the schema snapshots are finishing in nearly the same amount of time, but the data snapshot is significantly different.

We have actually not gotten a snapshot to complete on 2.6.1.Final for a large host because the metadata lock is held for so long on the tables we run into issues with the prod hosts and have to cancel them.

The mysql user has the same permissions as prod.
h2. What is the connector configuration?
|snapshot.locking.mode|"minimal_percona"|

|snapshot.mode|"when_needed"|

These 2 were added as a last effort to speed up and track snapshotting but did not make a significant difference.
|snapshot.max.threads|"4"|

|snapshot.tables.order.by.row.count|"descending"|
h2. What is the captured database version and mode of depoyment?

MySQL rds hosts on version 5 and 8
h2. What behavior do you expect?

We expect the data snapshot to complete in the same amount of time as Debezium 2.2.1.Final. 
h2. What behavior do you see?

Data Snapshot taking multiple days, and causing significant lag because the metadata locks are held on the mysql database for so long, and those databases are replicas that start to get blocked and fall behind.
h2. Do you see the same behavior using the latest released Debezium version?

Yes, issue appears on this version.
h2. Do you have the connector logs, ideally from start till finish?

The logs are the same as other versions, its just that the exporting data from table {} logs are running for days.
h2. How to reproduce the issue using our [tutorial|https://github.com/debezium/debezium-examples/tree/main/tutorial] deployment?

You most likely cant which is the issue, we run debezium at a scale that usually finds issues that havent been tested for.
author: jpechane

project_fields.Title: Slow MySQL Data Snapshots on 2.6.1.Final [DBZ-7817]

title: Debezium Postgres connector silent data loss on connector restart [DBZ-8312]
body:
Migrated from [DBZ-8312](https://issues.redhat.com/browse/DBZ-8312)

In order to make your issue reports as actionable as possible, please provide the following information, depending on the issue type.
h1. Bug report

For bug reports, provide this information, please:
h2. What Debezium connector do you use and what version?

Debezium postgres v2.3.5.Final
h2. What is the connector configuration?

        "connector.class": "io.debezium.connector.postgresql.PostgresConnector",

        "snapshot.mode": "never",

        "plugin.name": "pgoutput",

        "producer.override.compression.type": "gzip",

        "producer.override.batch.size": "65536",

        "producer.override.max.request.size": "10485760",

        "producer.override.acks": "all",

        "max.queue.size": "81290",

        "max.batch.size": "20480",

        "heartbeat.interval.ms": "1000",

        "heartbeat.action.query": "<>;", 

        "tombstones.on.delete": "false",

        "decimal.handling.mode": "double",

        "time.precision.mode": "connect",

        "interval.handling.mode": "string",

        "topic.prefix": "<some-prefix>",

     
h2. What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?

postgres-13, AWS RDS
h2. Background

In order to boost confidence in debezium connector's data capture, we use a predictive method by monitoring DB operations on a specific table and verify alignment with the destination topic.

We use a simple a heartbeat table

CREATE TABLE audit_heartbeat ( 

id BIGINT PRIMARY KEY,  ## primary key 

ts_ms BIGINT NOT NULL,   ## – Timestamp in milliseconds

);

 where we insert records at regular intervals (e.g., every 1s) and including it in the connector's {{table.include.list}} ensures these records are captured in Kafka. Monitoring their sequence helps detect gaps or data loss in the capture process.
h2. What behavior do you expect?

On connector restart, Debezium should resume from the last processed LSN, ensuring no events are skipped to prevent data loss.

It's acceptable for Debezium to reprocess events from the 'last processed LSN,' which may result in duplicates, but the primary focus must be on ensuring that no events are lost.
h2. What behavior do you see?

On connector restart, we noticed that sequence 808024 was lost after restart 

{"after":

{"id":"808023","tsMs":"1728097285846"}

,"source":

{"version":"2.3.5.Final","connector":"postgresql","name":"<redacted>","tsMs":"1728097285846","snapshot":"false","db":"<redacted>","schema":"public","table":"audit_heartbeat","txId":"3712864487","lsn":"354650320202872","xmin":"0"}

,"op":"c","tsMs":"1728097286246"}

{"after":

{"id":"808025","tsMs":"1728097287653"}

,"source":

{"version":"2.3.5.Final","connector":"postgresql","name":"<redacted>","tsMs":"1728097287653","snapshot":"false","db":"<redacted>","schema":"public","table":"audit_heartbeat","txId":"3712864501","lsn":"354650320282016","xmin":"0"}

,"op":"c","tsMs":"1728097290303"}
h2. Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?

(Ideally, also verify with latest Alpha/Beta/CR version)

Did not test
h2. Do you have the connector logs, ideally from start till finish?

(You might be asked later to provide [DEBUG/TRACE|https://debezium.io/documentation/reference/stable/operations/logging.html] level log)

Offset topic entry
|{"transaction_id":null,"lsn_proc":354650320202872,"messageType":"INSERT","lsn_commit":354650320199344,"lsn":354650320202872,"txId":3712864487,"ts_usec":1728097285846965}|

Note that 
 * Lsn = 354650320202872 == 1428D/765AEC78 (sequence *_808023)_*

Connector Logs:
|[2024-10-05 03:01:30,047] INFO Starting streaming (io.debezium.pipeline.ChangeEventSourceCoordinator)|
|[2024-10-05 03:01:30,047] INFO Retrieved latest position from stored offset 'LSN\{1428D/765AEC78}' (io.debezium.connector.postgresql.PostgresStreamingChangeEventSource)|

h1. Feature request or enhancement

For feature requests or enhancements, provide this information, please:

Prevent silent data loss
h2. Implementation ideas (optional)
 # Introduce a connector config param to optionally disable `skipMessage` in WalPositionLocator
 # Update resumeFromLsn in WalPositionLocator to ensure that it always exactly resumes from lastChangeLsn even if it means reprocessing previously processed lsn to prevent data loss

title: Debezium connector issue - Connector is not getting connected automatically when postgres database server is getting stop and then start again [DBZ-8322]
body:
Migrated from [DBZ-8322](https://issues.redhat.com/browse/DBZ-8322)

We are using debezium connector to connect postgress database and process the operation to azure function.

When I will stop database server and start again or restart it, connector is loosing connection and then its not connecting automatically, so we are calling api to update connector with same details, but I want to avoid this.

Is there anyway in connector details to say retry and reconnect database?
Atleast retry for next 1 hour when it started getting failed?

title: Debezium sink connector for MongoDB [DBZ-8339]
body:
Migrated from [DBZ-8339](https://issues.redhat.com/browse/DBZ-8339)

h1. Feature request or enhancement

For feature requests or enhancements, provide this information, please:
h2. Which use case/requirement will be addressed by the proposed feature?

Customer is trying extract a relational database, and take it to a non-relational database.
The flow is as follows:
1) Origin (PostgreSQL DB)
2) Extraction( Debezium)
3) Kafka
4) Target (MongoDB and Redis)

h2. Implementation ideas (optional)

discussed with engineering team. Please find below answer

Neither Mongo nor Redis is a JDBC compatible database (JDBC is a RDMS connection "standard" for java)  so unfortunately that will not work. 
Currently RedHat only support MongoDB as a source connector downstream. Sink connector for MongoDB is currently under development in our upstream.
author: jpechane

project_fields.Title: Debezium sink connector for MongoDB [DBZ-8339]
