title: Schema history restricted by 'snapshot.include.collection.list' [DBZ-9717]
body:
Migrated from [DBZ-9717](https://issues.redhat.com/browse/DBZ-9717)

h1. Bug report

For bug reports, provide this information, please:
h2. What Debezium connector do you use and what version?

debezium-connector-oracle-3.0.0
h2. What is the connector configuration?
```
{
"connector.class": "io.debezium.connector.oracle.OracleConnector",
"database.hostname": "oracle",
"database.port": "1521",
"database.user": "c##dbzuser",
"database.password": "dbz",
"database.dbname": "FREE",
"decimal.handling.mode": "double",
"topic.prefix": "CHECKSIGN",
"topic.creation.default.partitions": "5",
"topic.creation.default.replication.factor": "1",
"key.converter": "org.apache.kafka.connect.storage.StringConverter",
"value.converter": "io.confluent.connect.avro.AvroConverter",
"value.converter.schema.registry.url": "http://schemaregistry:8081",
"table.include.list": "BASEADM.STTM_ACCOUNT_CLASS",
"snapshot.max.threads": "1",
"snapshot.locking.mode": "none",
"tombstones.on.delete": "false",
"schema.history.internal.kafka.bootstrap.servers": "broker:29092",
"schema.history.internal.kafka.topic": "schema-changes.checksign",
"snapshot.mode": "initial",
"log.mining.strategy": "redo_log_catalog",
"custom.metric.tags": "table=partner",
"transforms": "Reroute",
"transforms.Reroute.type": "io.debezium.transforms.ByLogicalTableRouter",
"transforms.Reroute.topic.regex": "CHECKSIGN.BASEADM.(.*)",
"transforms.Reroute.topic.replacement": "PUBLIC.STREAMING.FLEX.$1.DEV",
"signal.enabled.channels": "source,kafka",
"signal.data.collection": "FREE.BASEADM_1.DEBEZIUM_SIGNAL",
"signal.kafka.topic": "CHECKSIGN.SIGNALS",
"signal.kafka.bootstrap.servers": "broker:29092",
{color:#FF0000}"schema.history.internal.store.only.captured.tables.ddl":"true",{color}
{color:#FF0000}"snapshot.include.collection.list": "FREE.BASEADM.STTM_ACCOUNT_CLASS",{color}
"incremental.snapshot.chunk.size":"100000"
}
```
h2. What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?

(E.g. on-premises, with a specific cloud provider, etc.)

Tested it on-prem in a dockerized environment. My Oracle image is: 
container-registry.oracle.com/database/free:23.9.0.0
h2. What behavior do you expect?

snapshot.include.collection.list should not restrict the schema of the capturable tables or the schema of signal.data.collection.
h2. What behavior do you see?

if schema.history.internal.store.only.captured.tables.ddl:true, and either table.include.list contains a table with a schema what does not appear in snapshot.include.collection.list for any table or the same for signal.data.collection's schema, then an exception is thrown on connector startup:

io.debezium.DebeziumException: Unable to find relational table model for 'FREE.BASEADM_1.DEBEZIUM_SIGNAL', there may be an issue with your include/exclude list configuration.
h2. Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?

(Ideally, also verify with latest Alpha/Beta/CR version)

Yes, tested it with 
h2. Do you have the connector logs, ideally from start till finish?

(You might be asked later to provide [DEBUG/TRACE|https://debezium.io/documentation/reference/stable/operations/logging.html] level log)

Yes, log file attached
h2. How to reproduce the issue using our [tutorial|https://github.com/debezium/debezium-examples/tree/main/tutorial] deployment?
* set `"schema.history.internal.store.only.captured.tables.ddl":"true"`
* use 2 different schemas in "table.include.list": "BASEADM.STTM_ACCOUNT_CLASS, BASEADM_1.PARTNER"
* only add table(s) from one schema to "snapshot.include.collection.list": "FREE.BASEADM.STTM_ACCOUNT_CLASS"

or

* set `"schema.history.internal.store.only.captured.tables.ddl":"true"`
* let "signal.data.collection" be in a schema, what doesn't appear in `snapshot.include.collection.list`, 
`"signal.data.collection": "FREE.BASEADM_1.DEBEZIUM_SIGNAL"`, `"snapshot.include.collection.list": "FREE.BASEADM.STTM_ACCOUNT_CLASS"`

https://groups.google.com/g/debezium/c/sBqpVyj3j-g
author: jpechane

  "transforms.unwrap.add.headers": "db,op,table,lsn,source.ts_ms",
  "database.port": "2901",
  "schema.history.internal.kafka.recovery.poll.interval.ms": "120000",
  "database.hostname": "***.***.***.247",
  "database.connection.retries": "5",
  "table.include.list": "db.owner.table",
  "key.converter.schema.registry.url": "http://****"
}
h2. What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?

Informix database on-premises
Debezium Informix connector deployed on {*}Kafka Connect (Confluent Platform){*}.
h2. What behavior do you expect?
 * When adding {{column.exclude.list}} for TEXT, BYTE, and BLOB columns,
the connector should {*}skip these columns entirely{*}, exactly like {{column.skip.list}} did in Debezium 3.0.5.

 * Schema and payload should remain consistent.

 * Connector should start normally and stream CDC events for remaining columns.

h2. What behavior do you see?
 * In Debezium {*}3.3.2{*}, {{column.exclude.list}} *does not exclude* TEXT, BYTE, and BLOB columns.

 * Debezium still includes these columns in CDC capture request.

 * The connector fails at CDC capturing with an error code: 83717

 * Same tables work perfectly on Debezium *3.0.5* with {{{}column.skip.list{}}}.

h2. Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?

yes reproduced on 3.3.2 (latest stable)

 
h2. Do you have the connector logs, ideally from start till finish?

(You might be asked later to provide [DEBUG/TRACE|https://debezium.io/documentation/reference/stable/operations/logging.html] level log)

Yes, full logs can be attached, including DEBUG/TRACE on request.
h2. How to reproduce the issue using our [tutorial|https://github.com/debezium/debezium-examples/tree/main/tutorial] deployment?

1- CREATE TABLE "owner".sample_table (
    id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
    name VARCHAR(50),
    description TEXT,
);

2- Configure connector including property:

{{"column.exclude.list": "mydb.owner.sample_table.description"}}

3- Start the connector.
4- Connector fails with error code -83717
h1. Feature request or enhancement
h2. Which use case/requirement will be addressed by the proposed feature?

Users need to ** capture CDC for tables that contain unsupported or large columns (TEXT/BLOB/BYTE) by excluding them.
This functionality existed in Debezium 3.0.5 using {{{}column.skip.list{}}}, and is needed for upgrades.

Without this, CDC becomes impossible for many Informix tables.
h2. Implementation ideas (optional)
 * Ensure {{column.exclude.list}} removes the specified columns from:

 ** schema discovery

 ** emitted record structures

 ** snapshot queries

 ** CDC events

 * Match functionality of previous {{{}column.skip.list{}}}.
author: jpechane

body:
Migrated from [DBZ-9674](https://issues.redhat.com/browse/DBZ-9674)

In preparation for migrating Red Hat documentation sources to DITA, it's necessary to modify some of the AsciiDoc sources in the Debezium community GitHub repository. 
This issue tracks removal of callout markup in code blocks. 
DITA does not include dedicated semantic markup for callouts. Although the tooling that we'll use to convert the AsciiDoc sources to DITA supports callouts, based on competitive analysis, as well as requirements to support accessibility and AI consumption of the content, the Red Hat content services team has decided that we should remove callouts before we migrate content to DITA.

title: Introduce flexible recovery from LSN desynchronisation [DBZ-9688]
body:
Migrated from [DBZ-9688](https://issues.redhat.com/browse/DBZ-9688)

h1. Feature request or enhancement

Introduce a new configuration option to the PostgreSQL connector to allow users to treat the Replication Slot as the durable source of truth.

h2. Which use case/requirement will be addressed by the proposed feature?

When the Debezium PostgreSQL connector starts, it compares the LSN recorded in its offset store with the LSN reported by the replication slot. Currently, if the stored offset is behind the slot ({{{}offset_lsn < slot_lsn{}}}), Debezium enforces a "Fail Fast" policy, crashing with the error: {_}"Saved offset is before replication slot's confirmed lsn"{_}.

While this protects against data loss from re-created slots, it also forces operators to perform a full re-snapshot of the database to recover from benign scenarios where the mismatch is due to a deliberate intervention.

* *Treating the Slot as the Durable Source of Truth* Similar to how Kafka's {{auto.offset.reset}} configuration allows consumers to opt-in to trusting the broker's position when their local state is invalid, Debezium should allow users to opt-in to trusting the PostgreSQL Replication Slot's position. If the connector's offset store is stale, the connector could then "jump ahead" to the Slot's position rather than failing.

*Key Use Cases:*
 * *Respecting Manual Intervention:* If an operator manually advances the slot (via {{{}pg_replication_slot_advance{}}}) to skip corrupted WAL, it should be possible to configure Debezium to respect this change instead of refusing to start. _At [Zalando|https://engineering.zalando.com/], we make use of the ephemeral MemoryOffsetBackingStore store to allow us to do just this._

 * *Recovering from Unmonitored WAL Advancement:* In idle scenarios, [DBZ-9641|https://issues.redhat.com/browse/DBZ-9641] allows users to opt-in to allowing non-monitored events to advance the replication slot beyond the connector's offset. Users who make use of durable OffsetBackingStores that want to use this feature will need a way to accept this new slot position without a hard failure. 

h2. Implementation ideas (optional)

Deprecate the boolean {{slot.seek.to.known.offset.on.start}} and introduce the enum {{{}offset.mismatch.strategy{}}}. This defines behavior during {{startStreaming}} when {{offset_lsn}} and {{slot_lsn}} differ.

*Proposed Enum Values:*
* *{{NO_VALIDATION}} (Default)*
** _Behavior:_ Attempts to stream from stored offset without validation.
May fail with "WAL segment removed" error if WAL is unavailable. Slot recreation is not detected.
** _Rationale:_ Maintains existing default behavior of {{slot.seek.to.known.offset.on.start = false}}. Provides backward compatibility.

* *{{TRUST_OFFSET}}*
** _Behavior:_ Validates slot position. If {{offset_lsn > slot_lsn}}, advances the slot to offset LSN using {{pg_replication_slot_advance()}}. If {{offset_lsn < slot_lsn}}, fails immediately with clear error detecting slot recreation.
** _Rationale:_ Replaces {{slot.seek.to.known.offset.on.start = true}}. Recommended for production to detect slot recreation and prevent silent data inconsistencies.

 * *{{TRUST_SLOT}}*
 ** _Behavior:_ If {{{}offset_lsn < slot_lsn{}}}, advance the *Connector Offset* ("jump ahead") to the slot's LSN. If {{{}offset_lsn > slot_lsn{}}}, fail.
 ** _Rationale:_ Solves the "Hard Reset" problem. Allows recovery from WAL advancement by treating the database slot as the source of truth.

 * *{{TRUST_GREATER_LSN}}*
 ** _Behavior:_ Automatically synchronize to {{{}max(offset_lsn, slot_lsn){}}}.
 ** _Rationale:_ A "self-healing" mode for advanced users who want to recover from _both_ crash-before-flush AND deliberate wal-advance scenarios automatically.

*Code Location:* Minimal changess to logic in {{{}PostgresReplicationConnection#startStreaming{}}}, that determins the starting LSN before the {{createReplicationStream}} call.
author: jpechane

title: Create DevService and scripts for testing Debezium db2 Extension for Quarkus
body:
## Context

Db2 community edition container image doesn't provide CDC abilities. It's necessary to setup and configure the image in order to be CDC ready. Furthermore, the quarkus extension for db2 needs db2 to be debezium-ready in order to have some DevService ready for user and to test it correctly with the test suite for debezium extensions for quarkus.


## Proposal

Create scripts and code to setup-on-fly the image for test containers and dev services


## Reference

- https://github.com/debezium/debezium-connector-db2/tree/main/src/test/docker/db2-cdc-docker
- https://github.com/debezium/debezium-quarkus/blob/b0e274d6533481f7c85008b4a2c6e061c1ecee22/quarkus-debezium-parent/quarkus-debezium-mongodb-parent/deployment/src/main/java/io/quarkus/debezium/mongodb/deployment/MongoDbEngineProcessor.java#L115
author: kmos

project_fields.Title: Create DevService and scripts for testing Debezium db2 Extension for Quarkus

title: Incorrect time unit in retry log message
body:
When the replication stream fails to start, the following WARN message is logged:

> WARN Failed to start replication stream at LSN{0/9D34020}, waiting for PT10S ms and retrying, attempt number 1 over 6
(io.debezium.connector.postgresql.connection.PostgresReplicationConnection:707)


I understand that the intended waiting time is 10 seconds, so this is not a functional problem.
However, PT10S is an ISO-8601 duration format, and appending ms to it (PT10S ms) seems incorrect and potentially confusing.

This is my first contribution to the Debezium project, and I am planning to submit a PR to address this issue.
(I am declaring this in accordance with [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/debezium/debezium/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#making-changes))


## Bug report

**What Debezium connector do you use and what version?**

PostgreSQL connector
main branch(92ffd62f20c495e4c73ead586f4b8495fe5a3e12)

---

**What is the connector configuration?**

> {
>   "name": "ha-test-connector",
>   "config": {
>     "connector.class": "io.debezium.connector.postgresql.PostgresConnector",
>     "database.hostname": "localhost",
>     "database.port": "15432",
>     "database.user": "atorik",
>     "database.password": "xxx",
>     "database.dbname": "postgres",
>     "topic.prefix": "example",
>     "slot.name": "example_slot",
>     "snapshot.mode": "always",
>     "plugin.name": "pgoutput",
>     "publication.autocreate.mode": "disabled",
>     "publication.name": "p1",
>     "provide.transaction.metadata": "true",
>     "producer.max.request.size": "5000000",
>     "producer.buffer.memory": "10000000",
>     "slot.failover": "true",
>     "signal.data.collection": "public.debezium_signals"
>   }
> }

---

**What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?**

on-premises

---

**What behavior do you expect?**

One of the following (but not a mixture of both):

> WARN ... waiting for 10000 ms and retrying, ...
or 
> WARN ... waiting for PT10S and retrying, ...

---

**What behavior do you see?**

> WARN ... waiting for PT10S ms and retrying, ...

---

**Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?**

Yes.

---

**Do you have the connector logs, ideally from start till finish?**

Please let me know if additional logs are required.

---

**How to reproduce the issue using our [tutorial](https://github.com/debezium/debezium-examples/tree/main/tutorial) deployment?**

This message should be logged when creation of the replication stream fails.

In my case, it occurred when connecting to a replication slot that had been synchronized to a standby server before a failover, and then accessed prior to the failover.

title: DB2 - Ability to Set a Maximum Change Timespan to Query
body:
**Which use case/requirement will be addressed by the proposed feature?**

The querying of streaming changes from the database, reading the change table when there are a large number of records in the change table, which in busy databases can trigger query-resource limits such as CPU time, which may kill the query during execution, crashing the connector instance.

---

**Implementation ideas (optional)**

Use similar logic to how IBM does - allow a configuration-controlled join to the IBMSNAP_UOW table, on the COMMITSEQ file.  This table has a timestamp that represents when that unit of work was completed.  On a given query, identify the record in the UOW table that matches the lowest commitseq being queried, then add a configurable number of seconds to add to that, and set it as an upper bound on a left join to this table.  This will result in only returning a limited set of completed work.
author: mhejnas

project_fields.Title: DB2 - Ability to Set a Maximum Change Timespan to Query

title: introduce a factory for `io.debezium.runtime.Debezium`
body:
## Context

In Debezium Extensions for Quarkus, there is an interface `io.debezium.runtime.Debezium` that guides the creation of the engine. Currently, there is only one concrete implementation, `SourceRecordDebezium`, which does not satisfy the need for different engines with different serialization strategies (JSON, Avro, etc.).


## Change

Introduce a factory class that introduce a layer between the creation point of `io.debezium.runtimeDebezium` and the concrete class.
author: kmos

project_fields.Title: introduce a factory for `io.debezium.runtime.Debezium`

title: Debezium issue on connect to IBMi
body:
## Bug report

**What Debezium connector do you use and what version?**

`latest`

title: Restart the connector or Throw an exception if the TCP connection remains ESTABLISHED, but the MySQL session is DEAD, leaving the connection in a stale state indefinitely
body:
Migrated from [DBZ-9755)](https://issues.redhat.com/browse/DBZ-9755)

We suspect this cause of the issue. The TCP connection remains {*}ESTABLISHED{*}, but the MySQL session is {*}DEAD{*}, leaving the connection in a stale state indefinitely.
In this scenario, the connector still shows as {*}RUNNING{*}, which is the problem. 
*Possible causes:* * MySQL process termination
 * Network layer issues
 * MySQL server-side timeouts:
 ** Connection limits reached
 ** Idle connection timeout (MySQL closes inactive sessions)

Debezium Connector → TCP Layer → MySQL Application Layer
1. TCP Handshake (Layer 4)
   ├── SYN packet sent
   ├── SYN-ACK received
   └── ACK sent → TCP ESTABLISHED
2. MySQL Authentication (Layer 7)
   ├── MySQL handshake packet
   ├── Username/password exchange
   └── Authentication OK → MySQL Session Active
3. Binlog Streaming (Layer 7)
   ├── SHOW MASTER STATUS
   ├── COM_BINLOG_DUMP command
   └── Binlog events flowing → CDC Working

{*}One of the Problem Scenario{*}:Debezium Connector
       ↓
TCP Connection: ESTABLISHED   !https://a.slack-edge.com/production-standard-emoji-assets/14.0/google-medium/2705@2x.png!   ← Shows "Connected"
       ↓
MySQL Session: DEAD   !https://a.slack-edge.com/production-standard-emoji-assets/14.0/google-medium/274c@2x.png!          ← Actually disconnected
       ↓
Result: Connector thinks it's working but no data flows 
 * TCP (Layer 4) only knows about network connectivity
 * MySQL (Layer 7) handles actual database sessions

 
h3. Replicating the Issue

To reproduce the issue, I am running a MySQL database in a local Docker container and have completed the local setup to run a MySQL Debezium source Kafka connector (version {*}3.3.2.Final{*}).

I tested the end-to-end flow successfully. When I make changes to a database table, the correct events are pushed to the Kafka topic. I have also configured a heartbeat with a {*}30-second interval{*}, and the heartbeat topic is receiving heartbeat events as expected. Also i configured the heartbeat.action.query to insert current timestamp.

When I pause the MySQL Docker container, the connector does not fail. Instead, the keepalive thread continuously retries the database connection, which can be observed in the Kafka Connect cluster logs. However, the connector task itself does not log any errors or exceptions. Also heartbeat events is stopped.

When I unpause the MySQL Docker container, the keepalive thread restores the connection successfully. This reconnection works correctly in my {*}local PC environment{*}.

However, when the database and the connector are running on {*}different servers{*}, the connection is not restored successfully even when the db is active and able to make new connection. In this scenario, when we restart the connector, it re-establishes the connection and events start flowing again.

The expectation is that, at the connector level, there should be a mechanism to re-establish the connection using the keepalive thread, automatically restart the connector, retry the connection, or at least fail the connector explicitly.
author: jpechane

project_fields.Title: Restart the connector or Throw an exception if the TCP connection remains ESTABLISHED, but the MySQL session is DEAD, leaving the connection in a stale state indefinitely

title: Support to avoid circular fetching for PostgreSQL by pgoutput pulgin. [DBZ-3805]
body:
Migrated from [DBZ-3805](https://issues.redhat.com/browse/DBZ-3805)

# Question

Postgres connector not support capture the data that is just "write" to this db, except for the replicated data. so that If there are two bidirectional copies of DB, Debezium will capture a lot of useless data.

# Solution

As per the specification of the [Logical Replication Message Format](https://www.postgresql.org/docs/12/protocol-logicalrep-message-formats.html), the pgoutput plugin supports the 'Origin' message type to record the data origin name, for instance in the case of multi-master set-ups. So, if we come across this field and find that the source is something we want to avoid copying, we can skip it until we hit 'Commit'.

Another approach is to put the Origin name field in the message body and let the user decide whether the messages are processed or not.
author: jpechane

project_fields.Title: Support to avoid circular fetching for PostgreSQL by pgoutput pulgin. [DBZ-3805]

title: Add callback before saving final offsets on engine close
body:
## Feature request or enhancement

_For feature requests or enhancements, please provide the following information:_

**Which use case/requirement will be addressed by the proposed feature?**

In Debezium 2.7.3, we provided our own implementation of DebeziumEngine.ConnectorCallback#taskStopped where we ensure our data sinks have successfully flushed or uploaded data from the temporary area to the final area before Debezium saves the final offset. If our data sinks fail to push data from temporary area to final area, then we throw an error so Debezium 2.7.3 does not save the final offsets, and next time we start the Debezium engine again, it will resume from the last successful flush.

After we upgraded to Debezium 3.3.2, the callback method calls have changed since we needed to switch from using EmbeddedEngine (removed in Debezium 3.X) to AsyncEmbeddedEngine.

Additional details here: [#community-general > Debezium 3.X Connector Callback @ 💬](https://debezium.zulipchat.com/#narrow/channel/302529-community-general/topic/Debezium.203.2EX.20Connector.20Callback/near/564448760)
---
author: willt1234567

title: Add Header value to Kafka Messages with Transform
body:
## Add Header value to Kafka Messages with Transform

**Which use case/requirement will be addressed by the proposed feature?**

This feature would enable Debezium connectors to enrich change event messages with additional metadata by moving specific fields from the payload into Kafka message headers via a Transform. A common requirement is to propagate distributed tracing information (e.g., `trace_id`) that is persisted in the database. By allowing connectors to add such values to headers, downstream consumers can continue trace propagation seamlessly, ensuring end-to-end observability across systems. This addresses the gap where application-produced events already include tracing headers, but CDC events generated by Debezium currently lose this context.

---

**Implementation ideas (optional)**

- Introduce a new Transform (e.g., `ValueToHeader`) that reads configured fields from the payload (`after.trace_id`) and writes them into Kafka headers.  
- Configuration could look like:  
  ```json
  "transforms": "traceHeader",
  "transforms.traceHeader.type": "io.debezium.transforms.ValueToHeader",
  "transforms.traceHeader.fields": "after.trace_id"
author: talha-inozu

project_fields.Title: Add Header value to Kafka Messages with Transform

title: Cassandra connector return wrong value for DATE columns
body:
### Environment
  - Debezium Connector: Cassandra 5
  - Cassandra Version: 5.0.2
  - Column Type: DATE

  ### Description
  DATE column values are returned as raw shifted integers instead of days since Unix epoch.

  ### Current Behavior
  When inserting `'2025-12-23'` into a DATE column, Debezium returns `-2147463203` instead of `20445` (days since epoch).

  ### Expected Behavior
  Debezium should return days since Unix epoch (1970-01-01) as per Kafka Connect's Date logical type specification.

  ### Root Cause
  Cassandra internally stores DATE values shifted by `Integer.MIN_VALUE` (-2147483648) for byte-order comparability. The connector was returning this raw shifted value without conversion.

  ### Reproduction
  ```sql
  CREATE TABLE test (id int PRIMARY KEY, date_col date);
  INSERT INTO test (id, date_col) VALUES (1, '2025-12-23');
  -- Debezium outputs: -2147463203
  -- Expected output: 20445
author: SreedevT

project_fields.Title: Cassandra connector return wrong value for DATE columns

title: RedisOffsetBackingStore: Race condition causes infinite NullPointerException loop on reconnect leading to unrecoverable state
body:
## Bug report

**What Debezium connector do you use and what version?**

`io.debezium.connector.postgresql.PostgresConnector 3.3.1.Final.jar`

---

**What is the connector configuration?**

We are running it via `debezium-operator` with `DebeziumServer` with the following configuration:
```
source:
  class: io.debezium.connector.postgresql.PostgresConnector
  config:
    database.dbname: XXX
    database.hostname: YYY
    database.password: ZZZ
    database.port: 1234
    database.user: aaa
    decimal.handling.mode: string
    internal.advanced.metrics.enable: true
    key.converter: io.apicurio.registry.utils.converter.ExtJsonConverter
    key.converter.apicurio.registry.auto-register: true
    key.converter.apicurio.registry.find-latest: true
    key.converter.apicurio.registry.global-id: io.apicurio.registry.utils.serde.strategy.GetOrCreateIdStrategy
    key.converter.apicurio.registry.url: http://apicurio.svc.cluster.local:8080/apis/registry/v2
    plugin.name: pgoutput
    post.processors: reselector
    publication.autocreate.mode: filtered
    reselector.reselect.columns.include.list: public.t4:attrs_json
    reselector.reselect.null.values: true
    reselector.reselect.unavailable.values: true
    reselector.reselect.use.event.key: false
    reselector.type: io.debezium.processors.reselect.ReselectColumnsPostProcessor
    schema.include.list: public
    snapshot.include.collection.list: public\.t1,public\.t2,public\.t3
    table.include.list: public\.t1,public\.t2,public\.t3,public\.t4,public\.t5
    tombstones.on.delete: false
    topic.prefix: table
    value.converter: io.apicurio.registry.utils.converter.ExtJsonConverter
    value.converter.apicurio.registry.auto-register: true
    value.converter.apicurio.registry.find-latest: true
    value.converter.apicurio.registry.global-id: io.apicurio.registry.utils.serde.strategy.GetOrCreateIdStrategy
    value.converter.apicurio.registry.url: http://apicurio.svc.cluster.local:8080/apis/registry/v2`
```

Offset is configured as:
```
  offset:
    redis:
      address: dragonfly.svc.cluster.local:6379
      key: debezium:cdc:offsets
```
---

**What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?**

_E.g. on-premises, with a specific cloud provider, etc._

`13.20 AWS RDS`

---

**What behavior do you expect?**

Debezium should handle brief (dozen of seconds) unavailability of Redis (DragonflyDB to be precise) offset store, retry periodically until a new connection is established and resume saving the offset.

---

**What behavior do you see?**

When Redis/Dragonfly is unavailable (dozen of seconds) Debezium tries to reconnect once, then falls into a never-ending loop of NullPointerExceptions when trying to use Redis client, see brief snippet from the logs below.

---

**Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?**

We were not able to check with 3.4.0 yet

---

**Do you have the connector logs, ideally from start till finish?**

Obtained via LogQL: `{somelabel="something"} |="Redis" | json | line_format "ThreadID: {{.threadId}} {{.loggerName}} {{.message}}"`
```shell
2025-12-16 15:53:27.090	
ThreadID: 48 io.debezium.storage.redis.offset.RedisOffsetBackingStore Writing to Redis offset store failed with io.debezium.storage.redis.RedisClientConnectionException: redis.clients.jedis.exceptions.JedisConnectionException: Unexpected end of stream.
	2025-12-16 15:53:27.090	
ThreadID: 48 io.debezium.storage.redis.offset.RedisOffsetBackingStore Will retry
	2025-12-16 15:53:27.090	
ThreadID: 48 io.debezium.storage.redis.offset.RedisOffsetBackingStore Attempting to reconnect to Redis
	2025-12-16 15:53:30.547	
ThreadID: 30 io.debezium.storage.redis.offset.RedisOffsetBackingStore Writing to Redis offset store failed with java.lang.NullPointerException: Cannot invoke "io.debezium.storage.redis.RedisClient.hset(byte[], byte[], byte[])" because "this.client" is null
	2025-12-16 15:53:30.547	
ThreadID: 30 io.debezium.storage.redis.offset.RedisOffsetBackingStore Will retry
	2025-12-16 15:53:32.417	
ThreadID: 30 io.debezium.storage.redis.offset.RedisOffsetBackingStore Writing to Redis offset store failed with java.lang.NullPointerException: Cannot invoke "io.debezium.storage.redis.RedisClient.hset(byte[], byte[], byte[])" because "this.client" is null
```

```Attempting to reconnect to Redis``` happens only once, `java.lang.NullPointerException` repeats until Debezium is restarted.

---

**How to reproduce the issue using our [tutorial](https://github.com/debezium/debezium-examples/tree/main/tutorial) deployment?**

We had multiple occurrences when Redis/Dragonfly offset store was unavailable for dozens of seconds

---

**Additional notes**

It seems that save() is not thread-safe, using logs above as an example:
- Thread 48 catches RedisClientConnectionException → calls connect() → sets client = null
- Thread 30 concurrently tries save() → uses client.hset() → gets NullPointerException
- NullPointerException is not a RedisClientConnectionException, so no reconnect attempt happens
- Thread 30 retries indefinitely with a null client → infinite NPE loop

None of the relevant methods (save(), connect(), closeClient()) are synchronized, nor is the client field declared as volatile.

[The code in question](https://github.com/debezium/debezium/blob/main/debezium-storage/debezium-storage-redis/src/main/java/io/debezium/storage/redis/offset/RedisOffsetBackingStore.java)

We are willing to submit a PR, something in line of making `connect()` synchronised and having a `null` check in `save()` and `load()`. WDYT? 

---
author: mrtworo

project_fields.Title: RedisOffsetBackingStore: Race condition causes infinite NullPointerException loop on reconnect leading to unrecoverable state

title: pg_stat_replication.flush_lsn can move backwards when lsn.flush.mode=connector_and_driver
body:
When `lsn.flush.mode` is set to `connector_and_driver`, I observed cases where `flush_lsn` in `pg_stat_replication` moves backwards.

As far as I understand, `flush_lsn` is supposed to be monotonically increasing, so this behavior does not seem correct.

**What Debezium connector do you use and what version?**

PostgreSQL connector 3.4.0

---

**What is the connector configuration?**

```json
{
  "name": "test-connector",
  "config": {
    "connector.class": "io.debezium.connector.postgresql.PostgresConnector",
    "database.hostname": "localhost",
    "database.port": "15432",
    "database.user": "atorik",
    "database.password": "xxx",
    "database.dbname": "postgres",
    "topic.prefix": "example",
    "slot.name": "example_slot",
    "plugin.name": "pgoutput",
    "publication.autocreate.mode": "disabled",
    "publication.name": "p1",
    "provide.transaction.metadata": "true",
    "offset.mismatch.strategy": "trust_greater_lsn",
    "lsn.flush.mode": "connector_and_driver"
  }
}
```

---

**What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?**

On-premises PostgreSQL 17.7

---

**What behavior do you expect?**

`flush_lsn` in the `pg_stat_replication` view should be monotonically increasing.

---

**What behavior do you see?**

`flush_lsn` in `pg_stat_replication` sometimes decreases to a smaller value.

Example:
```
=# select now(), sent_lsn, write_lsn, flush_lsn, replay_lsn from pg_stat_replication;

              now              |  sent_lsn  | write_lsn  | flush_lsn  | replay_lsn
-------------------------------+------------+------------+------------+------------
 2025-12-31 16:10:04.095346+09 | 0/57C63EE0 | 0/57C63EE0 | 0/57C63EE0 | 0/56637B40
(1 row)

=# select now(), sent_lsn, write_lsn, flush_lsn, replay_lsn from pg_stat_replication;

              now              |  sent_lsn  | write_lsn  | flush_lsn  | replay_lsn
-------------------------------+------------+------------+------------+------------
 2025-12-31 16:10:08.669746+09 | 0/57C63EE0 | 0/57C63EE0 | 0/56637B40 | 0/56637B40
(1 row)

```

---

**Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?**

Yes.

---

**Do you have the connector logs, ideally from start till finish?**

Yes. I believe the same logs can be obtained by following the reproduction steps below. Please let me know if you need the actual log files.

---

**How to reproduce the issue using our [tutorial](https://github.com/debezium/debezium-examples/tree/main/tutorial) deployment?**

The issue can be reproduced by first updating a table that is part of the publication, and then updating tables that are not part of the publication.

In the steps below, `t1` is the table included in the publication, and updates to non-publication tables are generated via pgbench (e.g., updates to pgbench_accounts).

Create `t1` table and the publication:

```
=# create table t1(i int primary key, t text);
=# create publication p1 for table t1;
```

Initialize pgbench tables:

```
$ pgbench postgres -h localhost -U postgres -i
```

Connector configuration:

```
{
  "name": "test-connector",
  "config": {
    "connector.class": "io.debezium.connector.postgresql.PostgresConnector",
    "database.hostname": "postgres",
    "database.port": "5432",
    "database.user": "postgres",
    "database.password": "postgres",
    "database.dbname": "postgres",
    "topic.prefix": "example",
    "slot.name": "example_slot",
    "plugin.name": "pgoutput",
    "publication.autocreate.mode": "disabled",
    "publication.name": "p1",
    "provide.transaction.metadata": "true",
    "lsn.flush.mode": "connector_and_driver"
  }
}
```

Register the connector:

```
$ curl -i -X POST \
  -H "Accept:application/json" \
  -H "Content-Type:application/json" \
  http://localhost:8083/connectors/ \
  -d @docker-register-postgres-lsn.flush.mode.json
```

Update a table included in the publication:

```
=# insert into t1 values (1, 'aa');
```

Generate activity on non-publication tables:

```
$ pgbench -d postgres -T 30 -U postgres
```

After this, if you continuously monitor pg_stat_replication (for example using \watch), you may observe flush_lsn moving backwards.

```
=# select now(), sent_lsn, write_lsn, flush_lsn, replay_lsn from pg_stat_replication;
=# \watch 1
```

Example output:

```
              now              | sent_lsn  | write_lsn | flush_lsn | replay_lsn
-------------------------------+-----------+-----------+-----------+------------
 2025-12-31 07:45:40.702053+00 | 0/524B048 | 0/524B048 | 0/524B048 | 0/37D8B80
(1 row)

              now              | sent_lsn  | write_lsn | flush_lsn | replay_lsn
-------------------------------+-----------+-----------+-----------+------------
 2025-12-31 07:45:41.701846+00 | 0/524B048 | 0/524B048 | 0/37D8B80 | 0/37D8B80
```

After observing for a while, flush_lsn may return to the original value (0/524B080), and then later decrease again to a past value (0/37D8B80).

```
              now              | sent_lsn  | write_lsn | flush_lsn | replay_lsn
-------------------------------+-----------+-----------+-----------+------------
 2025-12-31 07:45:47.701428+00 | 0/524B080 | 0/524B080 | 0/524B080 | 0/37D8B80
```


My understanding of the cause is as follows: 

When `lsn.flush.mode` is set to `connector_and_driver`, `flush_lsn` is updated by two different triggers:

(1) The JDBC driver processes KeepAlive messages and updates `flush_lsn`.

(2) The connector updates `flush_lsn` based on the LSN it observes while processing DML events.

When updates occur only on tables outside the publication, trigger (1) advances `flush_lsn`, but trigger (2) does not.

In this case, it seems that only trigger (1) should be reflected in `pg_stat_replication`. However, in practice, both triggers update `flush_lsn`. When trigger (2) applies, it may set `flush_lsn` to an older value, causing the observed rollback.

During the analysis, I made a small fix that prevents this behavior. 
If it does not cause any inconvenience, I am planning to open a PR.
author: atorik

project_fields.Title: pg_stat_replication.flush_lsn can move backwards when lsn.flush.mode=connector_and_driver

title: JDBC Sink Connector fails to insert JSON data into MariaDB
body:
## Bug report

**What Debezium connector do you use and what version?**

`debezium-connector-jdbc (JDBC Sink Connector), tested on current main branch (3.5.0-SNAPSHOT)`

---

**What is the connector configuration?**

`
   {
     "connector.class": "io.debezium.connector.jdbc.JdbcSinkConnector",
     "connection.url": "jdbc:mariadb://mariadb:3306/db",
     "connection.username": "user",
     "connection.password": "password",
     "insert.mode": "upsert",
     "primary.key.mode": "record_key",
     "schema.evolution": "basic"
  }
`

---

**What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?**

`
  Target database: MariaDB 10.x / 11.x running on Docker.
  (Source database could be any that produces JSON, e.g., Postgres, but the issue is on the Sink side).
`

---

**What behavior do you expect?**

`
The connector should successfully insert records containing JSON fields into MariaDB columns defined as JSON (which are
  alias for LONGTEXT in MariaDB). It should treat them as text/string binding without unsupported casting syntax.

`

---

**What behavior do you see?**

`
  The connector fails with an SQL syntax error. The logs show it attempts to execute an INSERT statement using the MySQL
  syntax CAST(? AS JSON). MariaDB does not support this specific cast syntax in parameterized queries, treating JSON as
  LONGTEXT.
`

---

**Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?**

`
  Yes, this affects the current latest versions as the MariaDB dialect logic inherits directly from MySQL without overriding
  the JSON type handling.
`

---

**Do you have the connector logs, ideally from start till finish?**


`Available upon request`

---

**How to reproduce the issue using our [tutorial](https://github.com/debezium/debezium-examples/tree/main/tutorial) deployment?**

`
   1. Set up a pipeline with any source producing a record with a JSON field.
   2. Configure JDBC Sink Connector pointing to a MariaDB instance.
   3. Observe the connector failing when attempting to write the JSON field.
`
author: abusquets

project_fields.Title: JDBC Sink Connector fails to insert JSON data into MariaDB

title: debezium/server NATS Jetstream sync publishing to have retry loop instead of simply failing with Exception
body:
## Feature request or enhancement

Currently when `debezium-server` publishes to NATS and NATS queue is full due to some limitations like message or byte limits (can happen if NATS set up as a buffer/balancer-queue), Debezium will throw exception and exit.

**Which use case/requirement will be addressed by the proposed feature?**

We can introduce better behavior allowing Debezium to retry instead of quitting with exception. Behavior can be adjusted with options.

This feature intended to be applied only for the sync batch publisher at first as async publisher requires (supposedly) requires more attention for the implementation.

---

**Implementation**

NATS Sync publisher update https://github.com/debezium/debezium-server/pull/246

title: Refactor OracleConnection auto-commit setup as an initial Operation
body:
There are several places in the Oracle codebase where we create a connection and explicitly set its auto-commit to `false`. We should centralize this logic and handle it as an "initialOp" executed when the connection is opened. 

This change not only centralizes the logic but also removes the littered "setAutoCommit(false)" calls across the codebase, shifting to enabling auto-commit only where necessary, if at all. In addition, if a JDBC connection has been idle and dropped, and it reconnects, the right commit mode will be applied automatically.

This is a follow-up to debezium/dbz#1480.
author: Naros

project_fields.Title: Refactor OracleConnection auto-commit setup as an initial Operation

title: Connector on ibmi occasionally asks for a sequence not in the receiver
body:
## Bug report

**What Debezium connector do you use and what version?**

debezium-connector-ibmi

---

**What is the connector configuration?**

can be supplied

---

**What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?**

_E.g. on-premises, with a specific cloud provider, etc._

DB2 on ibmi

---

**What behavior do you expect?**

stream all events

---

**What behavior do you see?**

It occasionally gets stuck saying it can't retrieve a sequence on a given receiver
The sequence number is indeed ouside the range for the receiver when investigated

---

**Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?**

_Ideally, also verify with the latest Alpha/Beta/CR version._

Yes

---

**Do you have the connector logs, ideally from start till finish?**

_You might be asked later to provide [DEBUG/TRACE](https://debezium.io/documentation/reference/stable/operations/logging.html) level log._

Logs can be provided

---

**How to reproduce the issue using our [tutorial](https://github.com/debezium/debezium-examples/tree/main/tutorial) deployment?**

Not easily reproducible
Other clients have reported the same problem

There are two candidates for this:
1/ The end sequence calcuation in ReceiverPagination
2/ The ibmi API returning an incorrect continuation offset

Typically recievers are set to have a high number of entries in them so the complexity in ReceiverPagination to allow the request to span receivers is probably not required. This should simplify the code and should also mean the API is unlikely to return the wrong receiver in the continuation offset.
author: sillencem

title: Do not fail on 'zero-year' dates in MySQL
body:
## Feature request or enhancement

I am using _mysql-binlog-connector-java_ library from your [fork](https://github.com/debezium/mysql-binlog-connector-java).

MySQL allows the storage of 'zero-year' dates such as 0000-07-15. Currently, the _mysql-binlog-connector-java_ library throws an invalidDateAndTimeRepresentation exception when encountering these values.

I would like the library to support these dates by converting them to a Unix timestamp (epoch-based) instead of failing.

---

**Implementation ideas (optional)**

I created a PR: https://github.com/debezium/mysql-binlog-connector-java/pull/15
author: adelajaworowska

title: Postgres connect to snapshot tables in the order defined in table.include.list like other connectors do
body:
## Feature request or enhancement

_For feature requests or enhancements, please provide the following information:_

**Which use case/requirement will be addressed by the proposed feature?**

### Current Behavior
The PostgreSQL connector sorts tables alphabetically during snapshot, ignoring the order specified in `table.include.list` configuration.

### Desired Behavior
- Preserve the order of patterns from `table.include.list`
- Within each pattern match, sort tables alphabetically for predictability

### Use Case
Users want to control the order of table ingestion during snapshots:
- Process small lookup tables first
- Process critical tables before less important ones

---

**Implementation ideas (optional)**

Remove `.sorted()` calls at:
- `debezium-connector-postgres/.../PostgresReplicationConnection.java` line 572
- `debezium-core/.../RelationalSnapshotChangeEventSource.java` line 377

Keep `.sorted()` at:
- `debezium-core/.../RelationalSnapshotChangeEventSource.java` line 321 (sorts within each pattern)

### Affected Components
- `debezium-connector-postgres`
- `debezium-core`

title: Connector cannot handle uncompressed transaction payloads beyond 2GB
body:
## Bug report

**What Debezium connector do you use and what version?**

```
<dependency>
    <groupId>io.debezium</groupId>
    <artifactId>mysql-binlog-connector-java</artifactId>
    <version>0.40.2</version>
</dependency>
```

---

**What is the connector configuration?**

`N/A`

---

**What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?**

_E.g. on-premises, with a specific cloud provider, etc._

`On Premise MYSQL 8.0`

---

**What behavior do you expect?**

`Handle transactions with over 2GB in uncompressed payload.`

---

**What behavior do you see?**

`We see an error while deserializing data, which processing any large transaction over 2GB`

---

**Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?**

_Ideally, also verify with the latest Alpha/Beta/CR version._

`Yes`

title: MySQL: New charset not taken into account
body:
## Bug report

**What Debezium connector do you use and what version?**

`3.4`

---

**What is the connector configuration?**

`N/A`

---

**What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?**

_E.g. on-premises, with a specific cloud provider, etc._

`Azure MySQL Flexible Server 8`

---

**What behavior do you expect?**

`Changing the charset of the table from e.g. latin1 to utf8 should be taken into account.`

---

**What behavior do you see?**

`Debezium continues to encode strings using the old charset, leading in my case to a double utf8 encoding.`

---

**Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?**

_Ideally, also verify with the latest Alpha/Beta/CR version._

`Yes`

---

**Do you have the connector logs, ideally from start till finish?**

_You might be asked later to provide [DEBUG/TRACE](https://debezium.io/documentation/reference/stable/operations/logging.html) level log._

`Extract from the schema history topic`
```json

```

---

**How to reproduce the issue using our [tutorial](https://github.com/debezium/debezium-examples/tree/main/tutorial) deployment?**

`Create a test table with latin1 charset. Perform an initial snapshot. Add some data with accented characters. Alter table to change charset. Add some data with accented characters. Notice the last messages contain garbled text.`

title: Use platform add Destination has error type
body:
When adding a RabbitMQ Destination in the platform, the generated destination type is `rabbitmq_stream`, which causes a bug.

{
  "name": "test",
  "description": "",
  "type": "rabbitmq_stream",
  "schema": "schema123",
  "vaults": [],
  "config": {
    "connection.host": "xxx",
    "connection.port": "xxx",
    "connection.password": "xxx",
    "connection.username": "xxx"
  }
}

According to the official Debezium documentation:
https://debezium.io/documentation/reference/stable/operations/debezium-server.html#_rabbitmq_stream

RabbitMQ Stream still creates a Debezium Server sink, and the sink type must be `rabbitmq` or `rabbitmqstream`.

Expected:
"type": "rabbitmq"

Actual:
"type": "rabbitmq_stream"

Debezium Server version: 3.4.0.Final
author: GuanTing-SMAX

`Connections details are missing in the smart editor.`

---

**Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?**

`yes`

---

title: MySQL connector fails to parse DDL when SRID follows NULL/NOT NULL (DDL statement ParsingException)
body:
## Bug report

**What Debezium connector do you use and what version?**

`3.2.0-Final`

---

**What is the connector configuration?**

```
apiVersion: kafka.strimzi.io/v1beta2
kind: KafkaConnector
metadata:
  name: test-mysql-source-connector
  namespace: kafka-connect
  labels:
    strimzi.io/cluster: kafka-connect-test
spec:
  class: io.debezium.connector.mysql.MySqlConnector
  tasksMax: 1
  config:
    topic.prefix: "cdc-test-mysql"
    snapshot.mode: "no_data"

    # DB connection
    database.hostname: "****"
    database.password: "*****"
    database.user: "****"
    database.server.id: "49999"
    database.port: "****"

    # table whitelist
    table.include.list: "testdb.test"
    
    # schema.history
    schema.history.internal.kafka.bootstrap.servers: "****:9092"
    schema.history.internal.kafka.topic: "cdc-test-mysql-schema-changes"

    # SerDe
    key.converter: "io.confluent.connect.avro.AvroConverter"
    key.converter.schema.registry.url: "http://*****:80"
    value.converter: "io.confluent.connect.avro.AvroConverter"
    value.converter.schema.registry.url: "http://******:80"

  autoRestart:
    enabled: true
    maxRestarts: 3
  state: running 
```

---

**What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?**

_E.g. on-premises, with a specific cloud provider, etc._

`8.0.35-27`

---

**What behavior do you expect?**

I expect the Debezium MySQL connector to successfully parse the ALTER TABLE statement when a spatial column (e.g., POINT) includes the SRID attribute, regardless of its position relative to the nullability constraint (NULL or NOT NULL).

In MySQL 8.0, both of the following syntaxes are valid:

- COLUMN_NAME POINT SRID 4326 NULL
- COLUMN_NAME POINT NULL SRID 4326

However, Debezium should be able to handle the second case without throwing a ParsingException, as it is a standard compliant DDL for MySQL 8.0.

---

**What behavior do you see?**

The connector fails with a ParsingException during binlog processing when it encounters a DDL where SRID follows the NULL or NOT NULL keyword.

Example of failure:
`ALTER TABLE test ADD COLUMN location_2 POINT NULL SRID 4326;`

---

**Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?**

_Ideally, also verify with the latest Alpha/Beta/CR version._

Yes, I have confirmed that this behavior persists in the latest stable release, Debezium 3.4.0.Final. Despite using the most recent version, the ParsingException still occurs when the SRID attribute is placed after the nullability constraint. I have verified that the issue is not resolved in the current latest version.

---

**Do you have the connector logs, ideally from start till finish?**

_You might be asked later to provide [DEBUG/TRACE](https://debezium.io/documentation/reference/stable/operations/logging.html) level log._

```
Caused by: io.debezium.text.ParsingException: DDL statement couldn't be parsed. Please open a Jira issue with the statement ALTER TABLE test ADD COLUMN location_2 POINT  NULL SRID 4326'

mismatched input 'SRID' expecting {<EOF>, '-'}

	at io.debezium.antlr.ParsingErrorListener.syntaxError(ParsingErrorListener.java:43) ~

	at org.antlr.v4.runtime.ProxyErrorListener.syntaxError(ProxyErrorListener.java:41) ~

	at org.antlr.v4.runtime.Parser.notifyErrorListeners(Parser.java:543) ~

	at org.antlr.v4.runtime.DefaultErrorStrategy.reportInputMismatch(DefaultErrorStrategy.java:327) ~

	at org.antlr.v4.runtime.DefaultErrorStrategy.reportError(DefaultErrorStrategy.java:139) ~

	at io.debezium.ddl.parser.mysql.generated.MySqlParser.root(MySqlParser.java:1004) ~

	at io.debezium.connector.mysql.antlr.MySqlAntlrDdlParser.parseTree(MySqlAntlrDdlParser.java:75) ~

	at io.debezium.connector.mysql.antlr.MySqlAntlrDdlParser.parseTree(MySqlAntlrDdlParser.java:48) ~

	at io.debezium.antlr.AntlrDdlParser.parse(AntlrDdlParser.java:73) ~

	at io.debezium.connector.binlog.BinlogDatabaseSchema.parseDdl(BinlogDatabaseSchema.java:311) ~

	at io.debezium.connector.binlog.BinlogDatabaseSchema.parseStreamingDdl(BinlogDatabaseSchema.java:258) ~

	at io.debezium.connector.binlog.BinlogStreamingChangeEventSource.handleQueryEvent(BinlogStreamingChangeEventSource.java:739) ~

	at io.debezium.connector.binlog.BinlogStreamingChangeEventSource.lambda$execute$5(BinlogStreamingChangeEventSource.java:178) ~

	at io.debezium.connector.binlog.BinlogStreamingChangeEventSource.handleEvent(BinlogStreamingChangeEventSource.java:570) ~

	... 6 more
```
author: Minseok-Pak

title: Add conditional snapshot support to Vitess connector
body:
## Feature request or enhancement

**Which use case/requirement will be addressed by the proposed feature?**

Add conditional snapshot support to the Vitess connector, allowing users to snapshot a subset of data rather than the full table.

This is useful for:
- Backfilling data gaps after a service incident without re-ingesting full history
- Snapshotting only recent data for large tables

---

**Implementation ideas (optional)**

Two approaches:

1. **Start copy from a specific PK** (e.g., `id > 1000000`)
   - Use VStream's `table_p_ks` in the VGTID to specify the starting point
   - This would be a Vitess-specific feature

2. **Filter with WHERE clauses** (e.g., `created_at > '2024-01-01'`)
   - VStream already supports this natively; just needs config exposure in the connector
author: tchen-otterai

project_fields.Title: Add conditional snapshot support to Vitess connector

title: Add initial_only snapshot mode support to Vitess connector
body:
## Feature request or enhancement

**Which use case/requirement will be addressed by the proposed feature?**

Add `snapshot.mode=initial_only` support to the Vitess connector, which already exists in other Debezium connectors.

This mode would stop the connector after VStream Copy completes, which is useful for:
- One-time migrations
- Batch extractions where ongoing streaming isn't needed
- Backfilling data gaps after a service incident without keeping the connector running

---

**Implementation ideas (optional)**

Stop the connector after VStream Copy completes, before transitioning to streaming mode.
author: tchen-otterai

project_fields.Title: Add initial_only snapshot mode support to Vitess connector

title: Add when_needed_no_data snapshot mode
body:
## Feature request or enhancement

_For feature requests or enhancements, please provide the following information:_

**Which use case/requirement will be addressed by the proposed feature?**

Hi everyone, I'm using version 3.3.2 on a gke cluster with no_data snapshot mode.
Every so often I get this error in the test environment:

The connector is trying to read change stream starting at SqlServerOffsetContext [sourceInfoSchema=Schema{io.debezium.connector.sqlserver.Source:STRUCT}, sourceInfo=SourceInfo [serverName=885ec0b2-677a-484a-8fd6-88df679ca83e, changeLsn=0002555f:00002950:001b, commitLsn=0002555f:00002950:0035, eventSerialNo=null, snapshot=FALSE, sourceTime=null], snapshotCompleted=false, eventSerialNo=1], but this is no longer available on the server. Reconfigure the connector to use a snapshot mode when needed.', error = 'io.debezium.DebeziumException: The connector is trying to read change stream starting at SqlServerOffsetContext [sourceInfoSchema=Schema{io.debezium.connector.sqlserver.Source:STRUCT}, sourceInfo=SourceInfo [serverName=885ec0b2-677a-484a-8fd6-88df679ca83e, changeLsn=0002555f:00002950:001b, commitLsn=0002555f:00002950:0035, eventSerialNo=null, snapshot=FALSE, sourceTime=null], snapshotCompleted=false, eventSerialNo=1], but this is no longer available on the server. Reconfigure the connector to use a snapshot mode when needed.

I assume it's because SQL Server deletes the LSN from the database at regular intervals, and since there's no data to send for a while, Debezium can't find the LSN and crashes.
To resolve this, I'm deleting the PVC from the cluster to force Debezium to instantiate a new snapshot after the reboot.

---

**Implementation ideas (optional)**

I'm wondering: is it possible to implement the when_needed mode (which I don't use because it would otherwise trigger read events in RabbitMQ for all data in the tables under CDC) with the no_data mode?

This way, the snapshot would be automatically recreated, but without sending any data.

Thank you
author: DanieleVar

title: EventDeserializer composition in mysql-binlog-connector-java library should be fixed
body:
When compatibility mode is set TransactionPayloadEventDataDeserializer() should be aware about it and in deserialize method when it creates new deserializer `EventDeserializer transactionPayloadEventDeserializer = new EventDeserializer();`(url) it should set compatibility mode from top level object.

FYI, the issue was also raised in osheroff repo: [Issue 127](https://github.com/osheroff/mysql-binlog-connector-java/issues/127), [PR 138](https://github.com/osheroff/mysql-binlog-connector-java/pull/138) but never addressed.

I will prepare a PR to debezium repo.
author: adelajaworowska

project_fields.Title: EventDeserializer composition in mysql-binlog-connector-java library should be fixed

title: Reference Guide for Debezium Extensions for Quarkus
body:
## Description

Author and publish an official reference guide on the Quarkus website that explains how to implement Change Data Capture (CDC) using Debezium, with a focus on the Debezium Quarkus Extension. The guide should be aligned with Quarkus documentation conventions.
author: kmos

project_fields.Title: Reference Guide for Debezium Extensions for Quarkus

title: Cache invalidation with Debezium Quarkus Extensions
body:
## Context

Hibernate ORM/JPA’s second-level cache is an effective way to boost performance by storing read-only or rarely updated entities, reducing database calls and improving response times.
Unlike the first-level cache, it’s tied to the session factory (or entity manager factory in JPA), making it shared across transactions and sessions. When cached data changes, the cache must be updated or cleared. If changes occur through Hibernate ORM, this happens automatically.

## Proposal

The Debezium Extensions for Quarkus must be able to automatically evict the item in the second-level cache that are obsolete. The extension should search for all the JPA Entities annotated with `@Cacheable` and manage the evictions following the change events coming from the engine.

reference: https://debezium.io/blog/2018/12/05/automating-cache-invalidation-with-change-data-capture/
author: kmos

project_fields.Title: Cache invalidation with Debezium Quarkus Extensions

title: postgres unnest insert way
body:
# Feature Request: PostgreSQL UNNEST Optimization for Batch Inserts

## Feature request or enhancement

**Which use case/requirement will be addressed by the proposed feature?**

This feature addresses the performance bottleneck in high-throughput data ingestion scenarios where the Debezium JDBC Sink Connector is writing large volumes of data to PostgreSQL databases. 

### Current Limitation
Currently, the connector uses standard JDBC batching which executes individual INSERT statements for each record, even when using `PreparedStatement.executeBatch()`. For example:
```sql
INSERT INTO table VALUES (?, ?);  -- executed N times
```

While JDBC batching reduces network round-trips, PostgreSQL still processes each INSERT as a separate operation, leading to significant overhead in high-volume scenarios (10K+ records/second).

### Use Case Requirements
1. **High-Throughput CDC**: Real-time CDC pipelines from databases like MySQL/Oracle to PostgreSQL requiring millions of records/hour
2. **Initial Snapshots**: Large initial data loads where performance is critical
3. **Bulk Replication**: Syncing large tables with frequent updates
4. **Cost Optimization**: Reducing CPU and I/O on PostgreSQL for the same workload

### Performance Impact
Based on research from TimescaleDB ([Boosting PostgreSQL Insert Performance](https://www.timescale.com/blog/boosting-postgres-insert-performance/)), using UNNEST can provide **5-10x performance improvement** over standard JDBC batching for bulk inserts.

---

## Implementation ideas

### Overview
Implement PostgreSQL-specific batch optimization using the `UNNEST()` function to transform multiple INSERT statements into a single bulk operation.

### Technical Approach

#### 1. Configuration Option
Add a new dialect-specific configuration:
```properties
dialect.postgres.unnest.insert.enabled=false  # Default: disabled for backward compatibility
```

This allows users to opt-in to the optimization without breaking existing deployments.

#### 2. SQL Transformation
Transform standard batched inserts:
```sql
-- Standard JDBC Batch (N separate inserts)
INSERT INTO users (id, name, email) VALUES (?, ?, ?);
INSERT INTO users (id, name, email) VALUES (?, ?, ?);
INSERT INTO users (id, name, email) VALUES (?, ?, ?);
```

Into a single UNNEST operation:
```sql
-- UNNEST Batch (1 operation for N records)
INSERT INTO users (id, name, email) 
SELECT * FROM UNNEST(
  ARRAY[?, ?, ?]::integer[],      -- all id values
  ARRAY[?, ?, ?]::varchar[],      -- all name values  
  ARRAY[?, ?, ?]::varchar[]       -- all email values
) AS t(id, name, email);
```

#### 3. UPSERT Support
For INSERT mode with `insert.mode=upsert`, support ON CONFLICT clause:
```sql
INSERT INTO users (id, name, email) 
SELECT * FROM UNNEST(...) AS t(id, name, email)
ON CONFLICT (id) 
DO UPDATE SET name=EXCLUDED.name, email=EXCLUDED.email;
```

#### 4. Architecture Changes

**a. DatabaseDialect Interface**
Add batch-aware methods:
```java
Optional<String> getBatchInsertStatement(TableDescriptor table, List<JdbcSinkRecord> records);
Optional<String> getBatchUpsertStatement(TableDescriptor table, List<JdbcSinkRecord> records);
```

**b. PostgresDatabaseDialect**
Implement UNNEST SQL generation with:
- Dynamic array sizing based on batch size
- Proper type casting for each column
- ON CONFLICT support for upsert mode

**c. RecordWriter**
Detect UNNEST statements and use column-wise binding instead of row-wise:
```java
// Standard: bind row1, row2, row3
// UNNEST:   bind col1[all rows], col2[all rows], col3[all rows]
```

**d. JdbcChangeEventSink**
Try batch operations first, fall back to standard if not available.

### Benefits

1. **Performance**: 5-10x faster for bulk inserts (based on TimescaleDB benchmarks)
2. **Backward Compatible**: Opt-in via configuration, existing behavior unchanged
3. **Database-Specific**: Only affects PostgreSQL, other databases unaffected
4. **Mode Support**: Works with both INSERT and UPSERT modes
5. **Buffer-Aware**: Leverages existing buffer size configuration

### Limitations & Considerations

1. **PostgreSQL Only**: This optimization is specific to PostgreSQL
2. **INSERT/UPSERT Only**: UPDATE and DELETE operations use standard mode (less critical for performance)
3. **Batch Size Limits**: PostgreSQL has parameter limits (~65K parameters), but typical batch sizes (100-1000) are well within limits
4. **Single Record**: Falls back to standard INSERT for single records (no benefit from UNNEST)
5. **Schema Consistency**: Requires all records in batch to have same schema (already guaranteed by buffer logic)

### Testing Strategy

1. **Unit Tests**:
   - Configuration parsing
   - SQL generation with various column types
   - Batch size variations (2, 10, 100, 1000 records)
   - NULL value handling
   - Fallback scenarios

2. **Integration Tests**:
   - PostgreSQL testcontainers
   - INSERT mode verification
   - UPSERT with ON CONFLICT
   - Mixed data types
   - Performance benchmarking

3. **End-to-End Tests**:
   - CDC pipeline from MySQL → PostgreSQL
   - Snapshot mode with large datasets
   - Error handling and retries

### Migration Path

Users can enable the feature with zero code changes:
```properties
# Add to connector configuration
dialect.postgres.unnest.insert.enabled=true
```

Recommended rollout:
1. Test in staging environment
2. Monitor PostgreSQL CPU/IO metrics
3. Compare throughput before/after
4. Enable in production for high-volume tables first

### Future Enhancements

1. **Batch Size Auto-Tuning**: Dynamically adjust batch size based on performance metrics
2. **Other Databases**: Explore similar optimizations for MySQL (`INSERT INTO ... VALUES (...), (...)`), Oracle, etc.
3. **UPDATE Batching**: Research bulk UPDATE optimizations for PostgreSQL
4. **Metrics**: Add JMX metrics for batch vs. standard mode usage

### References

- [TimescaleDB: Boosting PostgreSQL Insert Performance](https://www.timescale.com/blog/boosting-postgres-insert-performance/)
- [PostgreSQL UNNEST Documentation](https://www.postgresql.org/docs/current/functions-array.html)
- [Debezium JDBC Sink Connector](https://debezium.io/documentation/reference/stable/connectors/jdbc.html)

### Team Discussion

From Slack conversation:
- **Gaurav Miglani**: Proposed implementing UNNEST for PostgreSQL dialect
- **Jiri Pechanec**: Recommended implementing as opt-in config option, can switch to default if successful
- **Approach**: Start with INSERT and UPSERT modes, add config flag for backward compatibility
author: gaurav7261

project_fields.Title: postgres unnest insert way

title: Automate how community committers can update community images
body:
Currently, when a new database image is released, such as the `vitess-lite` image for `debezium-connector-vitess`, this process requires someone from the community who needs it to notify the core team so we can pull and push the image to our `quay.io` repository.

We should explore an automated approach for community-led connector committers to synchronize these images.

One approach is to use an organization token to authenticate a job as the Debezium Builder to push to `quay.io`, ideally using a `workflow_dispatch` style to manually trigger the pull and push. The downside is that GH runners have limited storage, so larger images will not fit; this may require using tooling like `skopeo` to avoid an intermediate pull and tag before pushing to `quay.io`.
author: Naros

project_fields.Title: Automate how community committers can update community images

title: ReselectPostProcessor does not account for CustomConverter(s)
body:
The `ReselectPostProcessor` currently pulls in the `ValueConverterProvider` contract from the connector, which is the connector's specific `JdbcValueConverter` implementation; however, this bypasses the logic used by `TableSchema`.

In `TableSchema`, it uses the converter registry to get the right converter to be used, whether its sourced from the connector's specific `JdbcValueConverter` implementation or a user-supplied `CustomConverter`. This should be what `ReselectPostProcessor` uses.

This will allow Db2, which does not currently support streaming BLOB/CLOB via the `Db2ValueConverters` to work with a custom converter that supports these column types.
author: Naros

project_fields.Title: ReselectPostProcessor does not account for CustomConverter(s)

title: Add support for ORIGIN Message in Postgresql Connector
body:
PostgreSQL's logical replication protocol includes ORIGIN messages that identify the source of transactions in cascaded replication setups. This is particularly important for hub-and-spoke replication topologies where changes may originate from different clusters and need to be tracked to prevent replication loops.

**Current Limitation**: When a Postgresql connector receives an ORIGIN message, it simply acknowledges and skips it without processing.


**Goal:**
1. Process and cache the origin_name and origin_lsn values from the ORIGIN message
2. Inject origin metadata into the source block of each subsequent record in that transaction
3. Maintain transaction scope - the cached origin values apply to all records until the next ORIGIN message or until BEGIN/COMMIT.


Ex:
```
  "payload": {
    "before": null,
    "after": {
      "id": 4,
      "name": "bhdfbubudiffbjfbjfiddbkdf Doe",
      "email": "dbjfnjdbd82bdj@example.com",
      "created_at": 1768209787789429
    },
    "source": {
      "version": "3.5.0-SNAPSHOT",
      "connector": "postgresql",
      "name": "dbserver1",
      "ts_ms": 1768249387789,
      "snapshot": "false",
      "db": "postgres",
      "sequence": "[\"23207504\",\"23207928\"]",
      "ts_us": 1768249387789429,
      "ts_ns": 1768249387789429000,
      "schema": "public",
      "table": "test_table",
      "txId": 756,
      "lsn": 23207928,
      "xmin": null,
      "origin": "origin_1",    ##### New Value 
      "origin_lsn": 74565    ##### New Value 
    },
    "transaction": null,
    "op": "c",
    "ts_ms": 1768249405742,
    "ts_us": 1768249405742575,
    "ts_ns": 1768249405742575600
  }
```
author: shishir2001-yb

project_fields.Title: Add support for ORIGIN Message in Postgresql Connector

title: Postgres connector returns null for duplicated enum types
body:
## Bug report

**What Debezium connector do you use and what version?**
3.4.0.Final

---

**What is the connector configuration?**

```
schema.include.list: public,test
plugin.name: pgoutput
```

---

**What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?**

postgres 16.0

---

**What behavior do you expect?**

when multiple schemas have the same ENUM type name:

Schema 'public' has test_type ENUM (OID X) And Values X, Y
Schema 'test' has test_type ENUM (OID Y)  and Values B, C

A schema refresh is triggered (by adding a new enum value, D)

And we're processing tables having test_type columns, we should get records with values X, Y, B and C, D

---

**What behavior do you see?**
Depending on which enum type is loaded first null values for columns with records X, Y or B, C

And the logs it shows:

Type [oid:35967, name:test_type] is already mapped


---

**Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?**

Yes


**Do you have the connector logs, ideally from start till finish?**

_You might be asked later to provide [DEBUG/TRACE](https://debezium.io/documentation/reference/stable/operations/logging.html) level log._

No

---

**How to reproduce the issue using our [tutorial](https://github.com/debezium/debezium-examples/tree/main/tutorial) deployment?**
author: bakjos

title: vitess-connector: Add connector generation config & state
body:
## Feature request or enhancement

**Which use case/requirement will be addressed by the proposed feature?**

For the vitess-connector [transaction order metadata](https://debezium.io/documentation/reference/stable/connectors/vitess.html#vitess-ordered-transaction-metadata), users may want to trigger a one-off incrementation of the epoch value (e.g., rolling out some change that affects how rank or order may be computed, new version, etc.). One such example is documented in https://github.com/debezium/debezium-connector-vitess/pull/268. Adding this connector config and state management will allow for this

---

**Implementation ideas (optional)**

The idea is that when a connector starts it loads its previous state and compares the state's connector generation to the generation specified in the connector config. If the config generation has been incremented it will trigger the order metadata `epoch` to be incremented as well.

Will put out a PR
author: twthorn
