title: Thread-safety issue in MongoDB connector causes incorrect source.collection metadata during parallel snapshot
body:
**_What Debezium connector do you use and what version?_**
MongoDB Connector, version 3.4.0.Final (also affects 3.5.0-SNAPSHOT )

**_What is the connector configuration?_**
```{
    "connector.class": "io.debezium.connector.mongodb.MongoDbConnector",
    "topic.prefix": "abc.mongo.data",
    "collection.include.list": "[^.]+\\.orders, [^.]+\\.orderDetails, [^.]+\\.orderHistory, ...",
    "database.include.list": "abc_database",
    "snapshot.mode": "initial",
    "snapshot.max.threads": "4",
    "field.name.adjustment.mode": "avro"
}
```

Key configuration: snapshot.max.threads > 1 (any value greater than 1 triggers the bug)

**_What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?_**
MongoDB 7.x , cloud deployment.

**_What behavior do you expect?_**
Each change event should have source.collection matching the actual MongoDB collection the document belongs to.

For example, a document from the orders collection should have:
```
"source": {
  "collection": "orders",
  "db": "abc_database",
  ...
}
```

**_What behavior do you see?_**
Documents have incorrect source.collection values. A document verified to belong to the orders collection (by checking MongoDB _id) appears with wrong metadata:
The document lands in the correct topic (...orders), and the _id confirms it's from the orders collection, but the source.collection metadata is incorrectly set to another collection being snapshotted in parallel.

**_Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?_**
Yes, the bug exists in the current main branch (3.5.0-SNAPSHOT). 

**_Do you have the connector logs, ideally from start till finish?_**
Logs can be provided upon request. The issue occurs during initial snapshot phase with multiple threads.

**_How to reproduce the issue using our [tutorial](https://github.com/debezium/debezium-examples/tree/main/tutorial) deployment?_**

1. Create a MongoDB database with multiple collections (e.g., orders, orderDetails, orderHistory)
2. Insert documents into each collection
3. Configure the MongoDB connector with:
4. Start the connector
5. Consume messages from any topic and inspect the source.collection field
6. Cross-reference with the document _id in MongoDB to verify mismatch
7. Workaround: Set snapshot.max.threads to 1

**_Root Cause (from code analysis):_**
Race condition in **_MongoDbSnapshotChangeEventSource.java_**

1. Multiple snapshot threads share a single **_MongoDbOffsetContext_** (and its SourceInfo)
2. SourceInfo is explicitly _@NotThreadSafe_ with a mutable collectionId field
3. Each thread calls **_snapshotContext.offset.readEvent(collectionId, ...)_** which overwrites the shared collectionId
4. When another thread reads collectionId to build the source struct, it may get the wrong value

_**Affected code:**_

- _MongoDbSnapshotChangeEventSource.getChangeRecordEmitter()_ - modifies shared state then returns emitter
- SourceInfo.onEvent() - sets this.collectionId without synchronization
author: dyaneshvarun

project_fields.Title: Thread-safety issue in MongoDB connector causes incorrect source.collection metadata during parallel snapshot

title: Verify archive log entry has been deleted on exception
body:
## Feature request or enhancement

_For feature requests or enhancements, please provide the following information:_

**Which use case/requirement will be addressed by the proposed feature?**

During the log gathering process, after all log fetch retries are exhausted, Debezium will throw a `LogFileNotFoundException` stating that none of the log files contain the specified SCN. This can be confusing without the other details from prior fetches to determine whether the log was purged or is still in transit to `V$ARCHIVED_LOG`.

We should be able to use the data in `V$ARCHIVED_LOG` to throw a different message if the log has been purged.

---

**Implementation ideas (optional)**

When Debezium would otherwise throw this exception, Debezium should execute one additional query to obtain all archive logs for the thread, regardless of deletion status, and verify whether a log entry exists that contains the SCN. If a log entry exists and its status is `D` (deleted), we can provide a clearer message that we cannot proceed because the log has been deleted.
author: Naros

project_fields.Title: Verify archive log entry has been deleted on exception

title: Invalid incremental snapshot leave Connectors stuck; stop-snapshot ineffective and requires manual offset modification
body:
## Bug report

Triggering an incremental snapshot with-for example-an invalid surrogate key column causes the Debezium connector to enter a stuck state. Although the snapshot does not continue and throws a warning, invalid incremental snapshot metadata is persisted to offsets and never cleared. Subsequent snapshot requests do not execute, and `stop-snapshot` does not resolve the issue.

The connector can only be recovered by deleting offsets (full reset) or manually editing the offset to remove snapshot metadata, or reverting to an older offset.

Scenarios such as invalid `additional-conditions` i.e. filter columns or values that don't exist, seem to throw an error and Connector doesn't become stuck, but not sure if there are other scenarios where the same thing can happen.

**What Debezium connector do you use and what version?**

All of them, versions' 3.0.7.Final, 3.2.2.Final and 3.2.4.Final.

---

**What is the connector configuration?**

Can provide an example on request. I don't think this matters though as it seems to be an inherent problem with `stop-snapshot`, or our expectation that it should recover a Connector from an invalid snapshot signal.

---

**What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?**

N/A

---

**What behavior do you expect?**

- Snapshot initialization failures should not persist incremental snapshot metadata to offsets.
- Subsequent snapshot requests should execute normally.
- `stop-snapshot` should fully clear snapshot state.

---

**What behavior do you see?**

- Invalid snapshot signal:
  - Logs a warning
  - Writes incremental snapshot metadata to offsets
- Connector becomes stuck:
  - Subsequent snapshots never execute
  - `stop-snapshot` does not clear snapshot state
- Resetting to an older offset frequently fails with:
```txt 
The connector is trying to read change stream starting at ... but this is no longer available on the server.
```

This often forces a full connector reset and snapshot.

There is a workaround, by manually modifying the connector offset.

1. Read the latest committed offset from the offset topic
2. Remove all incremental snapshot fields (incremental_snapshot_*)
3. Produce the cleaned offset back to the offset topic
4. Restart the connector
5. This restores normal operation without apparent data loss.

---

**Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?**

Not tested.

---

**Do you have the connector logs, ideally from start till finish?**

Yes, at `INFO` or above level onwards. Available on request.

---

**How to reproduce the issue using our [tutorial](https://github.com/debezium/debezium-examples/tree/main/tutorial) deployment?**

1. Start a Debezium connector in streaming mode.
2. Submit an incremental snapshot signal with an invalid surrogate key-for example:
```
{
  "data-collections": ["public.customers"],
  "type": "INCREMENTAL",
  "surrogate-key": "totally_bad_key_name"
}
```
3. Observe the warning in the connector logs:
```
WARN Action execute-snapshot failed...
IllegalArgumentException: Surrogate key "totally_bad_key_name" doesn't exist
```
4. Submit a new incremental snapshot signal using:
  - a valid surrogate key, or
  - no surrogate key (PK fallback).
5. Observe that the snapshot never starts.
6. Submit a `stop-snapshot` signal.
7. Observe that:
  - the `stop-snapshot` signal is logged
  - incremental snapshot metadata remains in offsets
  - subsequent snapshot requests do nothing
author: JonathonO

project_fields.Title: Invalid incremental snapshot leave Connectors stuck; stop-snapshot ineffective and requires manual offset modification

title: Avoid overfetching of data for multi-tenant use cases
body:
## Bug report

**What Debezium connector do you use and what version?**

3.4.0.Final

---

**What is the connector configuration?**

n/a

---

**What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?**

PG 18

---

**What behavior do you expect?**

Fast connector start-up when having large numbers of identical schemas

---

**What behavior do you see?**

We're observing very long start-up times (hours) for the Postgres connectors for multi-tenant scenarios, where there are hundreds or thousands of schemas, each with the same set of schemas. The reason is that column metadata is fetched purely by table name, which means that when retrieving column metadata for table schema1.foo, the result set contains the foo metadata for all tables with that name across all schemas.

---

**Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?**

Yes.
---

**Do you have the connector logs, ideally from start till finish?**

n/a

---

**How to reproduce the issue using our [tutorial](https://github.com/debezium/debezium-examples/tree/main/tutorial) deployment?**

n/a
author: gunnarmorling

project_fields.Title: Avoid overfetching of data for multi-tenant use cases

title: Bug in TransactionPayloadIntegrationTest in mysql-binlog-connector-java repo
body:
When I am running `TransactionPayloadIntegrationTest` using: 
`MYSQL_VERSION=8.0 mvn test -Dtest=TransactionPayloadIntegrationTest` command
it fails with message:
_Should have captured TRANSACTION_PAYLOAD event expected [true] but found [false]_

I added custom event listener to the test, that captures uncompressed TRANSACTION_PAYLOAD events and we can perform some validation on it. 

Alternative way would be modifying`onEvent()` method from `CapturingEventListener` to store also an original event of  TRANSACTION_PAYLOAD type. But this would causes some kind of a duplication (TRANSACTION_PAYLOAD event and all uncompressed events)

I created a PR with the fix: [PR 20](https://github.com/debezium/mysql-binlog-connector-java/pull/20)
Please let me know WDYT.
author: adelajaworowska

title: Null pointer exception and failed to capture incremental snapshot for newly added table in Postgres CDC Source Connector
body:
## Bug report

**What Debezium connector do you use and what version?**

`3.0.8`

---

**What is the connector configuration?**

Standard postgres configs, some configs to highlight

```
"database.dbname": "test_database",
"publication.autocreate.mode": "filtered",
"publication.name": "d1234",
"signal.data.collection": "public.debezium_signal_table",
"table.include.list": "public.*"
```
---
**What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?**

Both onprem and on cloud


---

**What behavior do you expect?**

Initially, postgres database had 2 tables, 1 signal table  `debezium_signal_table` and one `normal_table_1`
Connector was started with above configs.
Then, a new table  `normal_table_2` was created which matched regex, and few sample rows were added , and table was manually added to publication via ALTER publication. Then incremental snapshot was executed via entry in signal table as
```
INSERT INTO debezium_signal_table (id, type, data) 
VALUES (
    'adhoc-snap-002',                                           -- unique id
    'execute-snapshot',                                         -- type must be 'execute-snapshot'
    '{"data-collections": ["public.normal_table_2"], "type": "incremental"}'  -- JSON data
);
```

The connector should capture the snapshot of `normal_table_2`.

```
	at java.base/java.util.concurrent.ThreadPoolExecutor$Worker.run(ThreadPoolExecutor.java:642)
	at java.base/java.lang.Thread.run(Thread.java:1583)

message: Failed to retrieve schema for public.normal_table_2
```


---

**Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?**

Code flow is same in latest main branch, so should exists.

---

**Do you have the connector logs, ideally from start till finish?**

Have pasted the relevant log above, if needed, will share detailed log.

Have described above

title: bug: AbstractHistoryRecordComparator incorrectly evaluate to true every time.
body:
## Bug report

**What Debezium connector do you use and what version?**

MySql connector. Debezium v3.2.4.

---

**What behavior do you expect?**

When recovering schema history from my customized `AbstractFileBasedSchemaHistory`, HistoryRecord are not correctly filtered out by position. i.e. [isPositionAtOrBefore](https://github.com/debezium/debezium/blob/4159397bd40dd5d0c8efe2b97ba61ba8eb220b58/debezium-connector-mysql/src/main/java/io/debezium/connector/mysql/strategy/AbstractHistoryRecordComparator.java#L48) incorrectly evaluate to true every time. 

---

**Root Cause**

The root cause is the inconsistent usage of field names [ts_sec](https://github.com/debezium/debezium/blob/4159397bd40dd5d0c8efe2b97ba61ba8eb220b58/debezium-connector-mysql/src/main/java/io/debezium/connector/mysql/MySqlOffsetContext.java#L102) and [ts_ms](https://github.com/debezium/debezium/blob/4159397bd40dd5d0c8efe2b97ba61ba8eb220b58/debezium-connector-mysql/src/main/java/io/debezium/connector/mysql/strategy/AbstractHistoryRecordComparator.java#L104). It causes [this comparison](https://github.com/debezium/debezium/blob/4159397bd40dd5d0c8efe2b97ba61ba8eb220b58/debezium-connector-mysql/src/main/java/io/debezium/connector/mysql/strategy/AbstractHistoryRecordComparator.java#L106) to incorrectly evaluate to true every time, because ts_ms field is never set.
author: zwang28

title: MySQL CDC fails due to TIMESTAMP schema drift (Zoned->Nano)
body:
MySQL CDC fails after running snapshot for newly added database: identical table produces NanoTimestamp schema and UPDATE events cause NULL for NOT NULL DEFAULT TIMESTAMP columns

**Environment**

- Debezium version: 3.2.2-Final
- Connector: MySQL source connector
- MySQL: 8.x
- binlog_format: ROW
- binlog_row_image: FULL

**Connector configuration (relevant parts)**
```
{   "schema.history.internal.store.only.captured.databases.ddl": "true",
    "schema.history.internal.store.only.captured.tables.ddl": "true",
    "snapshot.mode": "schema_only",
...
}
```
**Table schema**
Same table definition exists in multiple MySQL databases.

```
CREATE TABLE orders (   
  id BIGINT PRIMARY KEY,
  CreatedAt TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  UpdatedAt TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  amount DECIMAL(10,2) 
);
```
Application UPDATE statements do not include CreatedAt or UpdatedAt, relying on MySQL defaults.

**Observed behavior**

1. **Initial connector startup (existing databases)**

    During initial snapshot, Debezium discovers table schema via INFORMATION_SCHEMA and produces the following schema for timestamp columns:
    
```
  {
       "name": "CreatedAt",
       "type": {
           "type": "string",
           "connect.version": 1,
           "connect.default": "1970-01-01T00:00:00Z",
           "connect.name": "io.debezium.time.ZonedTimestamp"
        },
        "default": "1970-01-01T00:00:00Z"
  }
```
    Snapshot and CDC work as expected.

2. **Adding a new database / table**
    A new database containing the same table schema is added to the connector include list.
    
    After updating the include list, a snapshot is triggered for the newly added database/table.
    
    During this snapshot, Debezium generates a different schema for the same timestamp columns:
    
    ```
    {
      "name": "CreatedAt",
      "type": {
        "type": "long",
        "connect.version": 1,
        "connect.name": "io.debezium.time.NanoTimestamp"
      }
    }
    ```
    No default value is present.

3. **CDC UPDATE events**
    When an UPDATE occurs that does not modify CreatedAt / UpdatedAt, the connector fails with:
    ```
    org.apache.kafka.connect.errors.DataException: Invalid value: null used for required field: "CreatedAt", schema type: INT64
    ```
**Questions:**
Why does schema generation differ (ZonedTimestamp vs NanoTimestamp) for identical table definitions when snapshots are run at different times?

Is there a recommended configuration to ensure consistent schema generation across snapshots and CDC?

**Expected behavior**
Identical table schemas should result in identical Connect schemas.

CDC should not fail at runtime for tables that snapshot successfully.
author: wrehman-skap

project_fields.Title: MySQL CDC fails due to TIMESTAMP schema drift (Zoned->Nano)

title: Allow mining session lower bound to advance when time threshold reached
body:
## Feature request or enhancement_

**Which use case/requirement will be addressed by the proposed feature?**

From 3.2.4 onwards when using the Oracle Connector with Logminer in buffered mode the connector will use the start scn of the oldest transaction in the buffer to determine the lower bound of the mining session.  This prevents certain edge cases that lead to unsupported transactions/missed commits, however, in environments with long-running transactions it can lead to very wide session boundaries that take hours to complete.  This is especially an issue where the long-running transactions are on tables that aren't being tracked.

By having a time threshold my hope is that we can balance increase risk of edge cases with too-large session windows.

---

**Implementation ideas (optional)**

I'm currently working on a PR that will advance the lower bound based on a configurable threshold.  First calculate the age of the transaction that is setting the lower bound and if exceeds the threshold move the lower bound to the start scn of the next transaction in the buffer (if there is one).  I'm hoping to be able to submit something soon that can be looked at.
author: nathan-smit-1

project_fields.Title: Allow mining session lower bound to advance when time threshold reached

title: Incorrect LSNs emitted by Postgres connector
body:
I am observing incorrect LSNs in data change events as well as transaction metadata events with the Debezium connector for Postgres, version 3.4.0.Final.

Consider the following two transactions:

```sql
--TX1
BEGIN;
-- content doesn't matter
COMMIT;

--TX 2
BEGIN;

INSERT INTO orders (order_date, purchaser, shipping_address)
   VALUES (CURRENT_DATE, 1002, '456 Oak Avenue, Chicago, IL 60601') RETURNING id;

INSERT INTO order_lines (order_id, product_id, quantity, price)
   VALUES
     (10006, 101, 1, 19.99),
     (10006, 102, 3, 49.99),
     (10006, 103, 2, 29.99);

COMMIT;
```

When fetching the change events via the PG SQL interface, this is what you get:

```sql
SELECT
     lsn,
     (lsn - '0/0')::bigint AS lsn_long,
     xid,
     data
 FROM pg_logical_slot_peek_changes('demo_slot', NULL, NULL);
+-----------+----------+-----+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| lsn       | lsn_long | xid | data                                                                                                                                                               |
|-----------+----------+-----+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| 0/2B8FBE0 | 45677536 | 806 | BEGIN 806                                                                                                                                                          |
| ...       | ...      | ... | ...                                                                                                                                                                |
| 0/2B900F0 | 45678832 | 806 | COMMIT 806                                                                                                                                                         |
| 0/2B90230 | 45679152 | 807 | BEGIN 807                                                                                                                                                          |
| 0/2B90298 | 45679256 | 807 | table inventory.orders: INSERT: id[integer]:10006 order_date[date]:'2026-01-21' purchaser[integer]:1002 shipping_address[text]:'456 Oak Avenue, Chicago, IL 60601' |
| 0/2B907F8 | 45680632 | 807 | table inventory.order_lines: INSERT: id[integer]:100012 order_id[integer]:10006 product_id[integer]:101 quantity[integer]:1 price[numeric]:19.99                   |
| 0/2B90C28 | 45681704 | 807 | table inventory.order_lines: INSERT: id[integer]:100013 order_id[integer]:10006 product_id[integer]:102 quantity[integer]:3 price[numeric]:49.99                   |
| 0/2B90CB8 | 45681848 | 807 | table inventory.order_lines: INSERT: id[integer]:100014 order_id[integer]:10006 product_id[integer]:103 quantity[integer]:2 price[numeric]:29.99                   |
| 0/2B91170 | 45683056 | 807 | COMMIT 807                                                                                                                                                         |
+-----------+----------+-----+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
```

Whereas, what Debezium, incorrectly, exposes is the following.

The change event for the first INSERT in the tx2:

```json
{
  "before": null,
  "after": {
    "id": 10006,
    "order_date": 20474,
    "purchaser": 1002,
    "shipping_address": "456 Oak Avenue, Chicago, IL 60601"
  },
  "source": {
    "version": "3.4.0.Final",
    "connector": "postgresql",
    "name": "dbserver1",
    "ts_ms": 1768990664620,
    "snapshot": "false",
    "db": "postgres",
    "sequence": "[\"45678832\",\"45679256\"]",
    "ts_us": 1768990664620149,
    "ts_ns": 1768990664620149000,
    "schema": "inventory",
    "table": "orders",
    "txId": 807,
    "lsn": 45679256,
    "xmin": null
  },
  "transaction": {
    "id": "807:45679256",
    "total_order": 1,
    "data_collection_order": 1
  },
  "op": "c",
  "ts_ms": 1768990665096,
  "ts_us": 1768990665096167,
  "ts_ns": 1768990665096167588
}
```

Specifically,

* `source.sequence` is incorrect; it contains the commit LSN of the *previous* transaction (45678832) and the event LSN, whereas it should contain the commit LSN of the *current* transaction (45683056) and the event LSN
* `transaction.id` is incorrect; it contains the event LSN (45679256), whereas it should contain the commit LSN (45683056)

The transaction metadata BEGIN event is the following:

```json
{
  "id": "807:45679256"
}
{
  "status": "BEGIN",
  "id": "807:45679256",
  "event_count": null,
  "data_collections": null,
  "ts_ms": 1768990664620
}
```

Specifically, the id (in both key and value) contains the LSN of the first event in the transaction (45679256), whereas it should be the commit LSN (45683056, which is what identifies transactions in monotonically increasing order, i.e. the order of transaction execution in the WAL).

In order to fix this issue, the commit LSN can be obtained from the transaction BEGIN event sent by Postgres, which contains that LSN in its `final_lsn` field. That LSN can be stored in the processing context and be assigned to all events from the current transaction. Currently, we're [solely logging the final LSN](https://github.com/debezium/debezium/blob/main/debezium-connector-postgres/src/main/java/io/debezium/connector/postgresql/connection/pgoutput/PgOutputMessageDecoder.java#L258) value but don't propagate it at all.
author: gunnarmorling

project_fields.Title: Incorrect LSNs emitted by Postgres connector

title: Expose transaction metadata events for snapshot transactions
body: Currently, we only expose transaction metadata events (BEGIN, END; the latter with event counts per collection type) for transactions captured from the transaction log. The idea is to also expose this for snapshot transactions, which will allow downstream stream processors to implement Debezium change events with transactional semantics and consistency, no matter whether it's events received from the log, or whether it's snapshot events.
author: gunnarmorling

title: Log mining lower boundary does not update until a log switch
body:
In DBZ-9680, we made a conscious decision to limit changing the lower mining boundary until a log switch occurs. The goal was to minimize LogMiner thrashing and allow it to reuse what it could from the prior mining pass.

However, in practice, this does not seem to help and, in fact, leads to poorer performance. To address this, we should resume updating the lower boundary on each mining pass.
author: Naros

project_fields.Title: Log mining lower boundary does not update until a log switch

title: Missleading error message when loss of database connectivity happens with mysql >= 8.4.X
body: ## Bug report **What Debezium connector do you use and what version?** Debezium-connector-mysql version 3.3.2.Final. Bundle was downloaded from https://debezium.io/releases/3.3/ This bundle has been baked in a kafka-3.6.1 docker image that is then deployed in a kafka-connect cluster via the strimzi operator. --- **What is the connector configuration?** config: database.user: [REDACTED] database.password: [REDACTED] database.server.id: 11 schema.history.internal.kafka.bootstrap.servers: ilot-cluster-kafka-bootstrap:9092 schema.history.internal.kafka.topic: schemahistory.my-connector database.port: [REDACTED] database.hostname: [REDACTED] database.include.list: my_db table.include.list: my_db.outbox topic.prefix: my.prefix transforms: outbox transforms.outbox.type: io.debezium.transforms.outbox.EventRouter transforms.outbox.route.by.field: topic transforms.outbox.table.field.event.id: kafka_key transforms.outbox.table.field.event.key: kafka_key transforms.outbox.table.field.event.payload.id: kafka_key transforms.outbox.table.field.event.payload: payload transforms.outbox.table.field.event.timestamp : dt transforms.outbox.route.topic.replacement: my.prefix.${routedByValue} transforms.outbox.table.expand.json.payload: false transforms.outbox.table.fields.additional.placement: tp:header:__TypeId__ key.converter: org.apache.kafka.connect.storage.StringConverter key.converter.schemas.enable: false value.converter: org.apache.kafka.connect.storage.StringConverter value.converter.schemas.enable: false event.deserialization.failure.handling.mode: ignore database.history.skip.unparseable.ddl: true schema.history.internal.skip.unparseable.ddl: true inconsistent.schema.handling.mode: skip database.history.store.only.monitored.tables.ddl: true use.nongraceful.disconnect: true snapshot.mode: no_data --- **What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?** On-premises InnoDB mysql cluster, version 8.4.5 running on cluster mode with 2 replicaSets. The connectors connect to the innodb clusters through multiple mysql-routers that have been deployed in kubernetes as a replicaSet. --- **What is the source of the issue?** The source of this seems to be the constructor of the MysqlConnection class (https://github.com/debezium/debezium/blob/v3.3.2.Final/debezium-connector-mysql/src/main/java/io/debezium/connector/mysql/jdbc/MySqlConnection.java) The logic implemented tries to execute the Mysql.Version>=8.4.0 expected binlog command, `SHOW BINARY LOG STATUS` , and if that errors out it falls back to the Mysql.Version<8.4.0 version, `SHOW MASTER STATUS`. So the MysqlConnection instance created will have the variable binaryLogStatusStatement set to 'SHOW MASTER STATUS'. From this point any additional GTID related function execution will always fail with the error message mentioned above, like the functions isGtidModeEnabled() or knownGtidSet(), even if the network connectivity issue was established again, since this sql command will never be successfully executed on a mysql >=8.4.0. --- **What behavior do you see?** Upon facing issues related to the database connectivity from the cluster due to new members joining the cluster and adding a huge load due to replication of a high volume, the routers lost connectivity for some time. Based on the logs we have captured, my estimation is 1 minute. The connector then reported that the issue that happened was due to the sql command `SHOW MASTER STATUS` failing to execute successfully. --- **What behavior do you expect?** The connector should remain in a failed state but the error message should be accurate. An error message such as: - Driver did not receive packets for a long time - Database connection lost - Cannot establish connection to the database or basically any other of the related and common network/database errors that should have been emitted. This means that the constructor should have failed instantiation and should have thrown the correct error message instead of setting the wrong binaryLogStatusStatement value. --- **Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?** This has only been tested on 3.1.1.Final and 3.3.2.Final. --- **Do you have the connector logs, ideally from start till finish?** N/A --- **How to reproduce the issue using our [tutorial](https://github.com/debezium/debezium-examples/tree/main/tutorial) deployment?** I did not have the chance to try and reproduce the error in a test environment but this has happened multiple days in the past few days
author: sancroth

project_fields.Title: Missleading error message when loss of database connectivity happens with mysql >= 8.4.X

title: Hyphens in topic name resulting in hyphens in Avro schema namespace causing issues with deserialization
body:
## Bug report

**What Debezium connector do you use and what version?**

MySQL connector (3.4.0.Final) and JDBC sink connector (3.0.4.Final).

---

**What is the connector configuration?**

MySQL Connector relevant config:

Kafka Connect Cluster relevant config:

# Apicurio Registry configuration

---

**What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?**

_E.g. on-premises, with a specific cloud provider, etc._

On prem, version unknown, should be irrelevant in this case.

---

**What behavior do you expect?**

Messages should be successfully deserialized using Avro serdes as according to what is stored in schema registry.

When a hyphen is used in the topic prefix, or when any hyphen is in the topic name, that seems to end up in the avro namespace?  I believe this is not allowed under the Avro specification and so Debezium should catch this and prevent/warn of it.

---

**What behavior do you see?**

I get:
org.apache.avro.SchemaParseException: Namespace part "mysql-cdc" is invalid: Illegal character in: mysql-cdc
In other words, the messages created by the debezium source connector are proving to not be deserializable by the sink connector because the avro schema was created with a hyphen in the namespace.
---

**Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?**



---

**Do you have the connector logs, ideally from start till finish?**

No

---

**How to reproduce the issue using our [tutorial](https://github.com/debezium/debezium-examples/tree/main/tutorial) deployment?**

Set topic prefix to something with a hyphen in it and setup Avro serdes with an apicurio registry.  You should see the schema registered with a namespace containing hyphens making it impossible to deserialize when using the jdbc sink connector.
author: CamFromStar

project_fields.Title: Hyphens in topic name resulting in hyphens in Avro schema namespace causing issues with deserialization

title: State leaks from aborted incremental snapshot to the next
body:
## Bug report

**What Debezium connector do you use and what version?**

debezium-connector-mysql 3.4.0

---

**What is the connector configuration?**

Not relevant

---

**What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?**

Running local mysql in docker/podman; mysql:8.0.26

---

**What behavior do you expect?**

After I start an incremental snapshot of all tables (with datacollection `.*`), then abort that incremental snapshot - I expect that I can start another incremental snapshot on a single table.

---

**What behavior do you see?**

After "immediately" aborting the snapshot of "all tables" and starting a new incremental snapshot of a single table, parts of the state from the first snapshot leaks into the next snapshot execution.

What I've seen is that io.debezium.pipeline.source.snapshot.incremental.AbstractIncrementalSnapshotChangeEventSource#addDataCollectionNamesToSnapshot is called with a signalPayload where `signalPayload.offsetContext.incrementalSnapshotContext` contains e.g. `chunkEndPosition`, `lastEventKeySent`, `maximumKey` etc from the first table in the previous snapshot execution. As a result the new snapshot execution resumes the snapshot from the maximumKey from another snapshot of _another table_, which is obviously not correct.

Also I observed that `total_rows_scanned` in the TABLE_SCAN_COMPLETED notification includes rows from the previous aborted snapshot.

---

**Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?**

_Ideally, also verify with the latest Alpha/Beta/CR version._

Yes, I have seen it in 3.1.x and the same behaviour is in 3.4.0 which is the latest version.

---

**Do you have the connector logs, ideally from start till finish?**

_You might be asked later to provide [DEBUG/TRACE](https://debezium.io/documentation/reference/stable/operations/logging.html) level log._

No, but I can provide DEBUG/TRACE logs on request.

---

**How to reproduce the issue using our [tutorial](https://github.com/debezium/debezium-examples/tree/main/tutorial) deployment?**

Idk, I have built my own rest api to trigger snapshots, but it is just a thin wrapper around the JmxSignalChannel from debezium. I saw some mentioning of a feature to send signals from debezium-ui in recent versions, but I have never used debezium-ui so not sure if this issue can be reproduced from there.
author: tommyk-gears

title: Stuck transaction when using CTE query with Oracle connector
body:
## Bug report

**What Debezium connector do you use and what version?**

Oracle connector 3.4.0.Final

---

**What is the connector configuration?**

```
"lob.enabled": "false",
"schema.history.internal.kafka.bootstrap.servers": "broker:29092",
"schema.history.internal.kafka.topic": "local.debezium.schema-changes",
"schema.history.internal.store.only.captured.tables.ddl": "true",
"log.mining.strategy": "hybrid",
"log.mining.query.filter.mode": "in",
"table.include.list": "C##DBZUSER.TEST",
"internal.log.mining.use.cte.query": "true"
```

---

**What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?**

Oracle 23ai Free running locally in Docker

---

**What behavior do you expect?**

Committed transactions emit CDC events.

---

**What behavior do you see?**

When inserting a row and waiting a bit to commit the transaction gets stuck in Debezium. 

Steps to reproduce:

1.  Start the Oracle Debezium connector against a configured Oracle database with internal.log.mining.use.cte.query = true
2. Insert a row into a table that is being mined. Do not commit.
3. Wait ~10 seconds and then commit

I've attached debug logs for when this happens. From what I can tell this is happening because the transaction ends up getting filtered out as the start scn for the batch increases. This is what `V$LOGMNR_CONTENTS` looks like for the transaction:

```
SCN      | COMMIT SCN | TIMESTAMP           | XID              | OPERATION | SQL_REDO                                                           |
---------|------------|---------------------|------------------|-----------|--------------------------------------------------------------------|
4750591  |            | 2026-01-23 22:24:23 | 08001C00EB030000 | START     | set transaction read write                                         |
4750591  |            | 2026-01-23 22:24:23 | 08001C00EB030000 | INSERT    | insert into "C##DBZUSER"."TEST"("ID","DATA") values ('1','test')   |
4750621  | 4750621    | 2026-01-23 22:24:30 | 08001C00EB030000 | COMMIT    | commit                                                             |
```

From the logs we can see Debezium initially queries SCN [4750493, 4750597] and sees the insert. The next batch queries SCN [4750591, 4750597]. Since the CTE is using `SCN > ? AND SCN <= ?` it will no longer see XID 08001C00EB030000. 

I attempted to fix this by having the CTE use the full SCN range of the mining session and it seems to aleviate the problem. 

---

**Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?**

`Yes.`

---

**Do you have the connector logs, ideally from start till finish?**

Attached

[debezium_debug.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/24832002/debezium_debug.log)
author: aofisherzg

project_fields.Title: Stuck transaction when using CTE query with Oracle connector

title: Implicit nullability in DDL (ALTER TABLE ... CHANGE ...) not respected by MySQL connector
body:
## Bug report

**What Debezium connector do you use and what version?**

MySQL Connector version ```2.4.0.Alpha1``` (also verified on ```3.4.0.Final```).

---

**What is the connector configuration?**

```json
{
  "connector.class": "io.debezium.connector.mysql.MySqlConnector",
  "snapshot.locking.mode": "none",
  "converters": "boolean",
  "snapshot.tables.order.by.row.count": "descending",
  "boolean.type": "io.debezium.connector.mysql.converters.TinyIntOneToBooleanConverter",
  "producer.override.max.request.size": "10485760",
  "max.batch.size": "500000",
  "snapshot.mode": "when_needed",
  "snapshot.delay.ms": "300000",
  "tombstones.on.delete": "false",
  "producer.override.max_block_ms": "60000",
  "snapshot.max.threads": "32",
  "database.connectionTimeZone": "UTC",
  "producer.override.buffer.memory": "409600000",
  "producer.override.linger.ms": "100",
  "max.queue.size": "1000000",
  "tasks.max": "1",
  "include.schema.comments": "false",
  "producer.override.acks": "all",
  "database.port": "3306",
  "topic.creation.enabled": "true",
}
```

---

**What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?**

Database is MySQL 8.0, connector is deployed with Kafka Connect ```3.6.0``` and AWS MSK.

---

**What behavior do you expect?**

When a column is altered from ```NOT NULL``` to ```NULL``` via an ```ALTER TABLE ... CHANGE``` statement, Debezium should recognize an implicit transition to a nullable state, even if the explicit ```NULL``` keyword is omitted. In MySQL, omitting ```NOT NULL``` implies ```NULL``` (and some ORMs like Rails ActiveRecord will do this). The resulting Kafka Connect schema should mark the field as ```optional: true``` and emit ```null``` values as ```null```.

---

**What behavior do you see?**

If the DDL uses implicit nullability (e.g., ```ALTER TABLE table1 CHANGE col col DATE DEFAULT NULL```), the Debezium Antlr parser fails to update the internal schema state. The field remains ```NOT NULL``` in Debezium's memory. When a ```NULL``` value is encountered in the binlog, the connector falls back to the logical type default (0), resulting in incorrect data in Kafka.

This seemingly occurs because ```ColumnDefinitionParserListener.java``` only updates the ```columnEditor``` if an explicit ```NULL``` or ```NOT NULL``` token is parsed. In ```exitColumnDefinition```, the logic:

```java
if (optionalColumn.get() != null) {
    columnEditor.optional(optionalColumn.get().booleanValue());
}
```

fails to account for the fact that a ```CHANGE``` operation should default to ```optional: true``` if no constraint is provided.

---

**Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?**

Yes. Verified by reproducing on ```3.4.0.Final```. Also by inspecting the current ```main``` branch logic in ```io.debezium.connector.mysql.antlr.listener.ColumnDefinitionParserListener```. The ```AtomicReference<Boolean> optionalColumn``` is never set if the Antlr walker doesn't hit a ```nullColumnConstraint``` token, causing the ```columnEditor``` to retain its stale state from the previous version of the table.

---

**Do you have the connector logs, ideally from start till finish?**

(N/A - This is not logged, just a logic error in the DDL parser).

---

**How to reproduce the issue using our [tutorial] deployment?**

1. Create a table with a non-nullable column: 
   ```CREATE TABLE test (id INT PRIMARY KEY, start_date DATE NOT NULL);```
2. Ensure Debezium has captured this schema (field will be non-optional).
3. Alter the column to be nullable using ActiveRecord-style (implicit) syntax: 
   ```ALTER TABLE test CHANGE start_date start_date DATE DEFAULT NULL;```
4. Insert a record with a null value: 
   ```INSERT INTO test (id, start_date) VALUES (1, NULL);```
5. Check the Kafka record: 
   - Value will be ```0``` (mapped to ```1970-01-01```) instead of ```null```.
   - Schema will still show ```"optional": false```.
6. Contrast: Running ```ALTER TABLE test CHANGE start_date start_date DATE NULL DEFAULT NULL;``` (explicit ```NULL```) correctly updates the schema.
author: duncan-prince

project_fields.Title: Implicit nullability in DDL (ALTER TABLE ... CHANGE ...) not respected by MySQL connector

title: SqlServerMetricsIT tests are unstable
body:
When we updated to a later JUnit, several methods in `AbstractMetricsTest` were changed to package-private, and `SqlServerMetricsIT` had originally overridden them; however, this led JUnit to run the abstract implementation during testing, causing incorrect results and test failures.

The methods in `AbstractMetricsTest` should have their visibility changed back and the `SqlServerMetricsIT` methods marked as overrides so that Junit correctly executes the right flavors of the tests.
author: Naros

project_fields.Title: SqlServerMetricsIT tests are unstable

## Feature request or enhancement

Add heartbeat feature in Debezium for the Informix connector. We also have a ticket (TS021250785) requesting a workaround to prevent de-synchronization.

_For feature requests or enhancements, please provide the following information:_

**Which use case/requirement will be addressed by the proposed feature?**
The reason why this feature would be useful is that the connectors are not always connected to the database and we run in a de-synchronization issue that breaks data continuity. As other flows fill up the CDC logs, the logs then get deleted, between our connections.

Thank you,

title: TLS/mTLS support for RabbitMQ Stream sink (rabbitmqstream)
body:
## Bug report

**What Debezium connector do you use and what version?**

Debezium Server 3.4.0 with rabbitmqstream sink

---

**What is the connector configuration?**
```
    #   connection.host: rabbitmq-host
    #   connection.port: "5551"
    #   connection.username: debezium
    #   connection.password: ${rabbitmq_password}
    #   connection.ssl.enabled: "true"
    #   connection.ssl.rootCert: /debezium/external/rabbitmq-tls/ca.crt
    #   connection.ssl.certFile: /debezium/external/rabbitmq-tls/tls.crt
    #   connection.ssl.keyFile: /debezium/external/rabbitmq-tls/tls.key
```
---

**What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?**

PostgreSQL 17, deployed on Kubernetes. RabbitMQ 4.2.0 cluster with mTLS enabled on the Stream protocol (port 5551).


---

**What behavior do you expect?**

I expect to be able to configure TLS/mTLS for the rabbitmqstream sink, similar to how the rabbitmq (AMQP) sink supports TLS via ConnectionFactoryConfigurator with PEM

---

**What behavior do you see?**

There is no way to configure TLS/mTLS for the `rabbitmqstream` sink. The `connection.*` passthrough properties do not seem to support TLS configuration for the Stream client.

When connecting to a TLS-enabled RabbitMQ Stream port, the connection fails with:
```
com.rabbitmq.stream.impl.ConnectionStreamException: Connection is closed
```

RabbitMQ logs show:
```
{tls_alert,{unexpected_message,"TLS server: In state hello at tls_record.erl:532 generated SERVER ALERT: Fatal - Unexpected Message\n {unsupported_record_type,0}"}}
```

This indicates the client is sending plaintext to a TLS port.

---

**Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?**

Yes, tested with Debezium Server 3.4.0.Final.

---

**Do you have the connector logs, ideally from start till finish?**

_You might be asked later to provide [DEBUG/TRACE](https://debezium.io/documentation/reference/stable/operations/logging.html) level log._

```
{"level":"WARN","message":"The parameters connection.host and connection.port are deprecated, please use rabbitmqstream.host and rabbitmqstream.port moving forward."}
{"level":"INFO","message":"Using connection to rabbitmq.example.com:5551"}
{"level":"ERROR","message":"Failed to start application","exception":{"exceptionType":"io.debezium.DebeziumException","message":"com.rabbitmq.stream.impl.ConnectionStreamException: Connection is closed"}}
```
---

**How to reproduce the issue using our [tutorial](https://github.com/debezium/debezium-examples/tree/main/tutorial) deployment?**

Deploy RabbitMQ with TLS/mTLS enabled on the Stream protocol (port 5551)
Configure Debezium Server with rabbitmqstream sink pointing to the TLS port
Attempt to start Debezium Server
Connection fails because there is no way to configure TLS for the Stream client

**Feature request**
Could you please add TLS/mTLS support for the rabbitmqstream sink? The RabbitMQ Stream Java Client supports TLS via EnvironmentBuilder.tls().sslContext(...) with Netty's SslContextBuilder.
Ideally, the configuration would support PEM files directly, similar to the AMQP sink:
propertiesdebezium.sink.rabbitmqstream.tls.enabled=true
debezium.sink.rabbitmqstream.tls.trust.certificate.path=/path/to/ca.crt
debezium.sink.rabbitmqstream.tls.client.certificate.path=/path/to/client.crt
debezium.sink.rabbitmqstream.tls.client.key.path=/path/to/client.key
debezium.sink.rabbitmqstream.tls.hostname.verification=true
Thank you!
author: younessof2m

project_fields.Title: TLS/mTLS support for RabbitMQ Stream sink (rabbitmqstream)

title: Optimize memory in debezium connectors keeping only relevant schemas in memory
body:
## Feature request or enhancement
 Debezium connectors load ALL table schemas into memory during recovery and streaming, even for tables not in `table.include.list`. For databases with millions of tables and lesser include tables (e.g., 3M tables), this wastes ~3-15GB of memory (15KB per table schema).

We can improve on memory, by persisting only the schema which are part of include list in memory.

More discussion on this is available on [#community-mysql-mariadb > Memory Optimisation for having schema in memory](https://debezium.zulipchat.com/#narrow/channel/348104-community-mysql-mariadb/topic/Memory.20Optimisation.20for.20having.20schema.20in.20memory/with/571951631)
_For feature requests or enhancements, please provide the following information:_

**Which use case/requirement will be addressed by the proposed feature?**

Memory optimisation of connect pod.

---

**Implementation ideas (optional)**

Filter schemas at the memory level while preserving full schema history in the storage backend.
Continue persisting ALL schemas to history topic (unchanged), but only load included tables into memory.
author: yashisrivastava-confluent

project_fields.Title: Optimize memory in debezium connectors keeping only relevant schemas in memory

title: Investigate compatibility with DBMS_REDEFINITION
body:
The `DBMS_REDEFINITION` PL/SQL package is used by DBAs to reorganize tables on disk for performance. It does so by creating a temporary table, copying data from the base table while keeping it synchronized with current actions on the base table, and ultimately replacing the base table with the redefined one.

The key concern is the final table replacement, since the interim table's object identity is retained, meaning that a table would randomly move from being OBJECT_ID 12345 to 24567. For `redo_log_catalog` and `online_catalog` mining strategies, this is not an issue since object identity isn't used; however, for `hybrid`, this could be a concern.

The key question is how DBMS_REDEFINITION renames the table; does it use `ALTER TABLE <old> RENAME TO <new>` or does it use some other internal or undocumented way to rename the table? 

If it uses DDL statements, we already handle this scenario; however, if it uses another approach (including a currently unsupported DDL syntax), the table object identity would be out of sync, which would be problematic.

We should investigate how the use of DBSM_REDEFINITION affects captured tables.

See [#community-oracle > Debezium Oracle  interaction  with DBMS_REDEFINITION](https://debezium.zulipchat.com/#narrow/channel/348250-community-oracle/topic/Debezium.20Oracle.20.20interaction.20.20with.20DBMS_REDEFINITION/with/572142449)
author: Naros

project_fields.Title: Investigate compatibility with DBMS_REDEFINITION

title: Document and/or rework Redo Thread inconsistency log messages
body:
The following log message, or similar inconsistent messages, often prompt community questions about why this happens. We often have to reexplain this repeatedly and provide ways for users to customize the behavior.
```
Redo Thread X is inconsistent; an archive log with sequence 12345 is not available
```

At a minimum, we should add a section to the documentation (either in the FAQ or as a standalone section) that discusses these log messages and explains why they occur. We should document the expected configuration options that can be tuned to tweak the behavior for environments that require it.

Secondly, we should consider removing the stack trace exception at the INFO level. The stack trace is often what catches users' attention, and I believe it causes more confusion than it helps. The only thing we should consider adjusting is whether to include the redo thread status (open or closed), which is provided by the stack trace but not by the log message. From there, we could include the stack trace at the DEBUG level.
author: Naros

project_fields.Title: Document and/or rework Redo Thread inconsistency log messages

title: PostgreSQL connector requires full schema discovery even with snapshot.mode=no_data, causing severe DB load in large-schema environments
body:
## Bug report

**What Debezium connector do you use and what version?**

`version 3.4.1`

We are observing a significant behavioral regression in the PostgreSQL connector compared to Debezium 1.5, related to schema discovery and replication slot activation when using snapshot.mode=no_data.


Debezium PostgreSQL Connector: 3.4.1.Final

PostgreSQL: Logical replication using pgoutput

Kafka Connect (distributed mode)

Database size: 900+ schemas, thousands of tables

Connector configured with:

snapshot.mode = no_data

Narrow table.include.list (single table)

Fresh offset, config, and status topics

Observed Behavior

Even with snapshot.mode=no_data and a very limited table include list, the connector performs extensive schema discovery across the database at startup:

Repeated catalog scans via PostgresSnapshotChangeEventSource

Reads structure of hundreds of schemas unrelated to the included tables

Causes noticeable CPU and IO spikes on the database

Replication slot remains inactive (active = false) for a long time until schema discovery completes

Example log lines:

Reading structure of schema 'company_id_403' of catalog 'fareye_mobi'
Reading structure of schema 'company_id_1208' of catalog 'fareye_mobi'
...

During this time:

WAL streaming has not started

Slot is created but inactive

Connector appears “running” but is not consuming changes

Expected Behavior

With snapshot.mode=no_data:

No data snapshot should occur

Schema discovery should be strictly limited to:

Included schemas/tables

Or deferred until WAL events require schema resolution

Replication slot should become active quickly and begin streaming WAL

This was effectively the behavior in Debezium 1.5, where CDC could proceed without full upfront schema knowledge.

Why this is a problem

In large multi-tenant Postgres databases (hundreds of schemas):

Full schema discovery is extremely expensive

Causes production DB load spikes

Makes connector restarts operationally risky

Makes snapshot.mode=no_data impractical despite being documented as “no snapshot”

While we understand Debezium has improved correctness guarantees since 1.5, the current behavior makes it impossible to run CDC efficiently in large-schema environments where only a small subset of tables is required.

Question / Request

Is this full schema discovery intentional and unavoidable in 3.x?

If intentional, can documentation clarify that snapshot.mode=no_data still performs full schema loading?

If not intentional, can schema discovery be:

Strictly scoped to table.include.list

Or deferred until WAL decoding requires it?

Is there a recommended configuration or feature flag to reduce startup catalog scanning in large databases?

**What is the connector configuration?**

`        - name: CONNECT_KEY_CONVERTER
          value: io.confluent.connect.avro.AvroConverter
        - name: CONNECT_VALUE_CONVERTER
          value: io.confluent.connect.avro.AvroConverter
        - name: CONNECT_KEY_CONVERTER_SCHEMAS_ENABLE
          value: "true"
        - name: CONNECT_VALUE_CONVERTER_SCHEMAS_ENABLE
          value: "true"
        - name: CONNECT_VALUE_CONVERTER_SCHEMA_REGISTRY_URL
          value: http://schema-registry-qa:8081
        - name: CONNECT_KEY_CONVERTER_SCHEMA_REGISTRY_URL
          value: http://schema-registry-qa:8081
        - name: CONNECT_REST_ADVERTISED_HOST_NAME
          valueFrom:
            fieldRef:
              apiVersion: v1
              fieldPath: status.podIP
        - name: CONNECT_PLUGIN_PATH
          value: /usr/share/java,/usr/share/confluent-hub-components


      - command:
        - /bin/sh
        - -c
        - "echo \"Starting configuration sidecar for qa-auto-route...\"\n\n# Wait
          for the main container's API to be ready\nuntil curl -s -f http://localhost:8083/connectors
          > /dev/null; do\n  echo \"Waiting for Kafka Connect API at localhost:8083...\"\n
          \ sleep 10\ndone\n\necho \"API is ready. Resolving database password...\"\nPASSWORD_KEY_NAME=\"qa_auto_route_secret\"\nDB_PASSWORD=$(eval
          echo \"\\$$PASSWORD_KEY_NAME\")\n\necho \"Applying configuration for qa-auto-route...\"\n#
          Using PUT for idempotency (handles both create and update)\ncurl -s -X PUT
          \\\n  -H \"Content-Type: application/json\" \\\n  -d \"{\n    \\\"connector.class\\\":
          \\\"io.debezium.connector.postgresql.PostgresConnector\\\",\n    \\\"database.user\\\":
          \\\"debezium_appuser\\\",\n    \\\"database.dbname\\\": \\\"fareye_mobi\\\",\n
          \   \\\"slot.name\\\": \\\"idp_qa_auto_route\\\",\n    \\\"time.precision.mode\\\":
          \\\"connect\\\",\n    \\\"database.server.name\\\": \\\"qa\\\",\n    \\\"heartbeat.interval.ms\\\":

          \   \\\"topic.prefix\\\": \\\"qa\\\",\n    \\\"slot.drop_on_stop\\\": \\\"false\\\",\n

**What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?**

Database-version: 16.11

I am deploying it via kubernetes

---

**What behavior do you expect?**

`I am expected connector and slot will be up within seconds`

title: Debezium Platform: Add support to generate the Debezium server config from platform pipeline.
body:
## Feature request or enhancement

_Add support use the platform UI also to generate the Debezium server config from the data pipeline configured in the debezium platform_

**Which use case/requirement will be addressed by the proposed feature?**

`Currently, we already support directly importing a Debezium Server configuration into the Platform UI to create a data pipeline. 
We can extend this by allowing users to take an existing pipeline configured in the Debezium Platform and deploy it on bare metal, by providing an option either in the final step of pipeline creation or afterwards to export a Debezium Server configuration that can be used directly with Debezium Server.
`
author: indraraj

project_fields.Title: Debezium Platform: Add support to generate the Debezium server config from platform pipeline.

title: Debezium platform: Make the password field to mask the user entered text
body: ## Bug report In the connector configuration details, hide the user-entered text in the password fields. Also, check this issue in the other places, e.g. transform and connections
author: indraraj

project_fields.Title: Debezium platform: Make the password field to mask the user entered text

title: Oracle Hybrid Strategy is not compatible with column filtering
body:
## Bug report

When setting `column.include.list` or `column.exclude.list`, the relational `Table` model is constructed with only the columns that match the given filter. However, when a column is omitted, and the Oracle Hybrid strategy attempts to resolve a column ordinal to a name, this fails if that given column is omitted.

"COL 10" = HEXTORAW('c10333') and "COL 11" = HEXTORAW('c103') and "COL 12" = HEXTORAW('c10c') and "COL 13" = HEXTORAW('c3031217') and "COL 14" IS NULL and "COL 15" = HEXTORAW('534d49524e4f5641') and "COL 16" = HEXTORAW('787e020818361b') and "COL 17" IS NULL and "COL 18" IS NULL and "COL 19" = HEXTORAW('80') and "COL 20" = HEXTORAW('c10333') and "COL 21" = HEXTORAW('c503010f3925') and "COL 22" = HEXTORAW('80') and "COL 23" IS NULL and "COL 24" = HEXTORAW('53') and "COL 25" = HEXTORAW('4132394432303133303736383338353945303533453030343035304136364339') and "COL 26" = HEXTORAW('c106') and "COL 27" = HEXTORAW('c106') and "COL 28" = HEXTORAW('01') and "COL 29" = HEXTORAW('7064');'
```
        at io.debezium.connector.oracle.logminer.parser.LogMinerDmlParser.parseUpdate(LogMinerDmlParser.java:173) ~[debezium-connector-oracle-3.3.1.Final.jar:3.3.1.Final]
        at io.debezium.connector.oracle.logminer.parser.LogMinerDmlParser.parse(LogMinerDmlParser.java:82) ~[debezium-connector-oracle-3.3.1.Final.jar:3.3.1.Final]
        at io.debezium.connector.oracle.logminer.AbstractLogMinerStreamingChangeEventSource.parseDmlStatement(AbstractLogMinerStreamingChangeEventSource.java:1432) ~[debezium-connector-oracle-3.3.1.Final.jar:3.3.1.Final]
        ... 16 more
Caused by: io.debezium.DebeziumException: Failed to find column COL 29 in table column position cache
        at io.debezium.connector.oracle.logminer.parser.LogMinerColumnResolverDmlParser.getColumnIndexByName(LogMinerColumnResolverDmlParser.java:45) ~[debezium-connector-oracle-3.3.1.Final.jar:3.3.1.Final]
        at io.debezium.connector.oracle.logminer.parser.LogMinerDmlParser.parseSetClause(LogMinerDmlParser.java:526) ~[debezium-connector-oracle-3.3.1.Final.jar:3.3.1.Final]
        at io.debezium.connector.oracle.logminer.parser.LogMinerDmlParser.parseUpdate(LogMinerDmlParser.java:148) ~[debezium-connector-oracle-3.3.1.Final.jar:3.3.1.Final]
        at io.debezium.connector.oracle.logminer.parser.LogMinerDmlParser.parse(LogMinerDmlParser.java:82) ~[debezium-connector-oracle-3.3.1.Final.jar:3.3.1.Final]
        at io.debezium.connector.oracle.logminer.AbstractLogMinerStreamingChangeEventSource.parseDmlStatement(AbstractLogMinerStreamingChangeEventSource.java:1432) ~[debezium-connector-oracle-3.3.1.Final.jar:3.3.1.Final]
        ... 16 more
```

## Workaround

The current workaround is to move column filtering into a transformation, so that the relational model retains all columns and works as expected with the `hybrid` mining strategy.

## Implementation Ideas

There are two possible ideas to address the shortcomings.

1. In the Oracle connector, identify if a column with the specified _ordinal_ is defined. If it isn't defined, we should omit handling of that column in the SQL parsing. This keeps the logic centralized in the Oracle connector; however, it will be highly dependent on being able to properly map columns by ordinal when columns are missing. This will need to be tested.
2. We instead rethink what the `Table` model represents, and we move the complex logic of omitting columns to the `TableSchemaBuilder` where we build the schema and value mappers. This would allow the `Table` to hold any number of columns, including virtual or hidden columns, despite them being ignored and not part of the event. But this is a much larger sweeping change that makes more sense for a major update than in a minor update.
author: Naros

project_fields.Title: Oracle Hybrid Strategy is not compatible with column filtering

title: Allow incremental snapshot using physical row identifier ROWID for Oracle [DBZ-9582]
body:
Migrated from [DBZ-9582](https://issues.redhat.com/browse/DBZ-9582)

Relates to this conversation:  https://debezium.zulipchat.com/#narrow/channel/348250-community-oracle/topic/ROWID.20based.20snapshot.20for.20oracle/with/546225699

Currently Incremental snapshots primarily make use of the primary key or alternately a surrogate-key if the table has one.  It is currently not possible to pass a pseudo column like ROWID as the surrogate key due to the way that getQueryColumns works.  The goal of this request is to allow connectors that support this to use the physical row identifier instead of the table's primary key.

The primary benefit here is where tables have multiple columns forming the primary key.  In oracle the generated query doesn't hit the index which causes incremental snapshots to be very slow.
author: jpechane

project_fields.Title: Allow incremental snapshot using physical row identifier ROWID for Oracle [DBZ-9582]

title: Debezium returns null for unchanged PostGIS geometry column with REPLICA IDENTITY DEFAULT
body:
## Bug report

**What Debezium connector do you use and what version?**

PostgreSQL connector
version:3.4.0.Final

---

**What is the connector configuration?**

connector.class=io.debezium.connector.postgresql.PostgresConnector
plugin.name=pgoutput
publication.autocreate.mode=filtered
schema.name.adjustment.mode=avro_unicode
schema.history.internal.store.only.captured.tables.ddl=true
snapshot.mode=no_data
offset.storage=io.debezium.storage.jdbc.offset.JdbcOffsetBackingStore
binary.handling.mode=bytes
interval.handling.mode=string
lob.enabled=true
decimal.handling.mode=precise
errors.log.include.messages=true
provide.transaction.metadata=true
max.batch.size=10240
max.queue.size=20480

---

**What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?**

Version : PostgreSQL 17.7
on-premises

---

**What behavior do you expect?**

I tested a simple PostGIS table with a primary key and REPLICA IDENTITY DEFAULT:

CREATE TABLE test.postgis (    
 id          INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,    
 status_code CHAR(5),    
 geom        geometry(LINESTRING, 4326) );

I then ran an update that does not modify the PostGIS (TOAST) column:

UPDATE test.postgis SET status_code = 'UPD01';

The Debezium update event I received looks like this:

"payload": { 
  "op": "u", 
  "after": {    
 "id": 1,    
 "geom": null,   
  "status_code": "UPD01"   }, 
  "before": null }

I expected the configured unavailable placeholder (for example __debezium_unavailable_value) when the TOAST column is unchanged.
---

**What behavior do you see?**

What I found interesting is that for scalar TOAST types (like text, jsonb, bytea) I usually see the configured unavailable placeholder (for example __debezium_unavailable_value) when the TOAST column is unchanged.

However, for the PostGIS geometry column, I see geom: null instead of the unavailable placeholder.

---

**Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?**

_Ideally, also verify with the latest Alpha/Beta/CR version._

yes

---

**Do you have the connector logs, ideally from start till finish?**

_You might be asked later to provide [DEBUG/TRACE](https://debezium.io/documentation/reference/stable/operations/logging.html) level log._

sure

---

**How to reproduce the issue using our [tutorial](https://github.com/debezium/debezium-examples/tree/main/tutorial) deployment?**

create a table something like below  having POSTGIS datatypes

CREATE TABLE test.postgis (    
 id          INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,    
 status_code CHAR(5),    
 geom        geometry(LINESTRING, 4326) );


then try to update on any row changing other than toast columns as below

UPDATE test.postgis SET status_code = 'UPD01';

then observe the event (TOAST COLUMNS IN PAYLOAD) from debezium.
author: Vineesha114

title: Automatic Detection and Snapshotting of new tables on connector startup
body:
# Description
Currently, when users add new tables to the `table.include.list` regex pattern, they could need to manually trigger an incremental snapshot to capture the initial data. This enhancement proposes automating this process by tracking which tables have been snapshotted and intelligently detecting new tables on connector startup.

# Proposed Solution
Introduce a connector initialization hook that:

Receives connector config, connection, and offset information at startup and automatically detects tables matching the regex that haven't been snapshotted yet
Queues snapshot signals (blocking or incremental) for execution during startup
Separates snapshot state tracking from offset storage (similar to schema history topic approach)

This provides extensibility for both blocking and incremental snapshot strategies while keeping the codebase flexible for future enhancements.

# Suggested Subtasks

## Phase 1
- [ ] Design Debezium state storage mechanism - Define a separate store for Debezium state to contain information about the state of Debezium that are not strict related to the source processing. Initially it will be used to store snapshot metadata (similar to schema history topic) to avoid bloating the offset state. In future we can think to move other things currently in the offset in this dedicated store. 
## Phase 2
- [ ] Implement startup initialization hook - Create the connector initialization method that receives config, connection, and offset/Debezium state data
- [ ]  Implement blocking snapshot strategy - Auto-detect new tables on startup and queue blocking snapshots
- [ ] Add per-table snapshot tracking - Track which tables have been snapshotted to enable accurate diff detection on startup
## Phase 3
- [ ]  Implement incremental snapshot strategy - Enable parallel streaming with incremental snapshotting for new tables
author: mfvitale

project_fields.Title: Automatic Detection and Snapshotting of new tables on connector startup

title: flush_lsn may not advance with lsn.flush.mode=connector_and_driver when only unmonitored tables are updated
body:
## Bug report

When `lsn.flush.mode` is set to `connector_and_driver` and `provide.transaction.metadata` is set to `false` (default), I observed a case where flush_lsn in pg_stat_replication and confirmed_flush_lsn in pg_replication_slots do not advance even when there is ongoing WAL-generating activity.

**What Debezium connector do you use and what version?**

PostgreSQL connector 3.4.1

---

**What is the connector configuration?**

```json
{
  "name": "test-connector",
  "config": {
    "connector.class": "io.debezium.connector.postgresql.PostgresConnector",
    "database.hostname": "localhost",
    "database.port": "15432",
    "database.user": "atorik",
    "database.password": "xxx",
    "database.dbname": "postgres",
    "topic.prefix": "example",
    "slot.name": "example_slot",
    "plugin.name": "pgoutput",
    "publication.autocreate.mode": "disabled",
    "publication.name": "p1",
    "lsn.flush.mode":"connector_and_driver"
  }
}
```

Note that when `provide.transaction.metadata`: `true` was added to the configuration, this problem does NOT occur.

---

**What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?**

on-premises

---

**What behavior do you expect?**

Even if WAL activity occurs on tables that are not part of the publication (i.e., not captured by CDC), both:

- flush_lsn in pg_stat_replication, and
- confirmed_flush_lsn in pg_replication_slots

should continue to advance.

---

**What behavior do you see?**

Even when WAL activity occurs on tables that are not part of the publication, both:

- flush_lsn in pg_stat_replication, and
- confirmed_flush_lsn in pg_replication_slots

remain unchanged and do not advance.

---

**Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?**

Yes.

---

**Do you have the connector logs, ideally from start till finish?**

Yes. I believe the same logs can be obtained by following the reproduction steps below. Please let me know if you need the actual log files.

---

**How to reproduce the issue using our [tutorial](https://github.com/debezium/debezium-examples/tree/main/tutorial) deployment?**

The issue can be reproduced by:

- Updating a table that is part of the publication, and then
- Generating WAL activity on tables that are not part of the publication.

If only non-publication tables are updated (without first updating a publication table), the issue does NOT occur.

In the steps below:

- `t1` is included in the publication.
- Non-publication WAL activity is generated via pgbench (e.g., updates to pgbench_accounts).

Create `t1` table and the publication:

```
=# create table t1(i int primary key, t text);
=# create publication p1 for table t1;
```

Initialize pgbench tables:

```
$ pgbench postgres -h localhost -U postgres -i
```

Connector configuration:

```
{
  "name": "test-connector",
  "config": {
    "connector.class": "io.debezium.connector.postgresql.PostgresConnector",
    "database.hostname": "postgres",
    "database.port": "5432",
    "database.user": "postgres",
    "database.password": "postgres",
    "database.dbname": "postgres",
    "topic.prefix": "example",
    "slot.name": "example_slot",
    "plugin.name": "pgoutput",
    "publication.autocreate.mode": "disabled",
    "publication.name": "p1",
    "lsn.flush.mode": "connector_and_driver"
  }
}
```

Register the connector:

```
$ curl -i -X POST \
  -H "Accept:application/json" \
  -H "Content-Type:application/json" \
  http://localhost:8083/connectors/ \
  -d @docker-non-proceed-lsn.json
```

Update a table included in the publication:

```
=# insert into t1 values (1, 'aaa');
```

At this point, flush_lsn and confirmed_flush_lsn are 0/37DABB0:

```
=# select now(), sent_lsn, write_lsn, flush_lsn, replay_lsn from pg_stat_replication;

              now              | sent_lsn  | write_lsn | flush_lsn | replay_lsn
-------------------------------+-----------+-----------+-----------+------------
 2026-02-12 07:25:30.583864+00 | 0/37DACF8 | 0/37DACF8 | 0/37DABB0 | 0/37DABB0


=# select now(), confirmed_flush_lsn from pg_replication_slots;

              now              | confirmed_flush_lsn
-------------------------------+---------------------
 2026-02-12 07:25:23.319627+00 | 0/37DABB0
```


Generate activity on non-publication tables:

```
$ pgbench -d postgres -T 30 -U postgres
```

At this point, both flush_lsn and confirmed_flush_lsn remain unchanged:

```
              now              | confirmed_flush_lsn
-------------------------------+---------------------
 2026-02-12 07:29:58.925503+00 | 0/37DABB0
(1 row)

              now              | confirmed_flush_lsn
-------------------------------+---------------------
 2026-02-12 07:30:45.896185+00 | 0/37DABB0
 ```

---
**Cause Analysis**

The direct cause appears to be the condition for updating `lastFlushedLSN` in the keepalive message handling of the JDBC driver:

```java
  private boolean processKeepAliveMessage(ByteBuffer buffer) {

    // if the client has confirmed flush of last XLogData msg and KeepAlive shows ServerLSN is still
    // advancing, we can safely advance FlushLSN to ServerLSN
    if (automaticFlush && explicitlyFlushedLSN.asLong() >= startOfLastMessageLSN.asLong()
        && lastServerLSN.asLong() > explicitlyFlushedLSN.asLong()
        && lastServerLSN.asLong() > lastFlushedLSN.asLong()) {
      lastFlushedLSN = lastServerLSN;
```

When the issue occurs, debugging shows something like:

```
explicitlyFlushedLSN: 97663152 (5D238B0)
startOfLastMessageLSN: 97663328 (5D23960)
```

The condition `explicitlyFlushedLSN.asLong() >= startOfLastMessageLSN.asLong()` is not satisfied.

This might be wrong, but from my investigation:

- The JDBC driver updates `startOfLastMessageLSN` even for transaction messages such as COMMIT
(see [processXLogData()](https://github.com/atorik/pgjdbc/blob/c07721af972eee4f10873b1a23b3811336454436/pgjdbc/src/main/java/org/postgresql/core/v3/replication/V3PGReplicationStream.java#L267))
- On the other hand, `explicitlyFlushedLSN` is derived from commitOffset(), which uses
PostgresOffsetContext.LAST_COMPLETELY_PROCESSED_LSN_KEY.

When `provide.transaction.metadata` = `false`, transaction metadata events are not emitted, and therefore the LSN used to update `explicitlyFlushedLSN` does not advance in certain scenarios.

---
**Possible approaches?**

One idea I considered was modifying commitOffset() to use effectiveOffset.lastCompletelyProcessedLsn() instead of PostgresOffsetContext.LAST_COMPLETELY_PROCESSED_LSN_KEY.

However, this would acknowledge LSNs to PostgreSQL that may not yet have been fully transferred to Kafka, potentially causing data loss. Therefore, I believe this approach is unsafe.

At the moment, the only workaround I can think of is:

When `lsn.flush.mode` = `connector_and_driver`, require `provide.transaction.metadata` = `true`, and fail fast otherwise. If this direction is acceptable, I am willing to prepare a PR.

What do you think?
author: atorik

project_fields.Title: flush_lsn may not advance with lsn.flush.mode=connector_and_driver when only unmonitored tables are updated

## Bug report

**What Debezium connector do you use and what version?**

`3.3.2.Final`

---

**What is the connector configuration?**

```
apiVersion: kafka.strimzi.io/v1beta2
kind: KafkaConnect
metadata:

  config:

    # -1 means it will use the default replication factor configured in the broker
    config.storage.replication.factor: -1
    offset.storage.replication.factor: -1

```

`Oracle 19`

`The process might fail, but it must restart itself, not just remain "broken" and waiting for a restart`

**What behavior do you see?**

```
The tasks crashes and it not restarted, leading to a database that is past its redo log retention and unrecoverable by debezium.
```

**Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?**

_Ideally, also verify with the latest Alpha/Beta/CR version._

`Were not able to test this`

---

**Do you have the connector logs, ideally from start till finish?**

_You might be asked later to provide [DEBUG/TRACE](https://debezium.io/documentation/reference/stable/operations/logging.html) level log._

```
2025-11-13 20:34:05,917 ERROR [debezium-poc-oracle-cintmcn1-dbintmbr2|task-0] LogMiner session stopped due to an error. (io.debezium.connector.oracle.logminer.AbstractLogMinerStreamingChangeEventSource) [debezium-oracleconnector-server1-change-event-source-coordinator]
2025-11-13 20:34:05,921 ERROR [debezium-poc-oracle-cintmcn1-dbintmbr2|task-0] Producer failure (io.debezium.pipeline.ErrorHandler) [debezium-oracleconnector-server1-change-event-source-coordinator]
2025-11-13 20:34:18,132 ERROR [debezium-poc-oracle-cintmcn1-dbintmbr2|task-0] WorkerSourceTask{id=debezium-poc-oracle-cintmcn1-dbintmbr2-0} Task threw an uncaught and unrecoverable exception. Task is being killed and will not recover until manually restarted (org.apache.kafka.connect.runtime.WorkerTask) [task-thread-debezium-poc-oracle-cintmcn1-dbintmbr2-0]
```

---

**How to reproduce the issue using our [tutorial](https://github.com/debezium/debezium-examples/tree/main/tutorial) deployment?**

`The issue just occurs on its own.  After a few days, the tasks crashes and that is that.  The Database was a test DB and there was not a lot of activity on the DB, maybe this is an important piece of the puzzle.`
