title: Event loss when Kafka producer fails (e.g. delivery.timeout.ms exceeded)
body:
## Bug report

**What Debezium connector do you use and what version?**

Cassandra connector `3.4.1.Final`

---

**What is the connector configuration?**

```properties
connector.name=dbz_test_connector
commit.log.relocation.dir=/opt/debezium/relocation
commit.log.real.time.processing.enabled=true
commit.log.error.reprocessing.enabled=true
http.port=8000
cassandra.config=/etc/cassandra/cassandra.yaml
cassandra.hosts=host.docker.internal
cassandra.port=9042
cassandra.driver.config.file=/opt/debezium/conf/cassandra-driver.conf
kafka.producer.bootstrap.servers=kafka:9092
kafka.producer.retries=3
kafka.producer.retry.backoff.ms=1000
kafka.producer.delivery.timeout.ms=120000
kafka.producer.enable.idempotence=false
topic.prefix=dbz_test
key.converter=org.apache.kafka.connect.json.JsonConverter
key.converter.schemas.enable=false
value.converter=org.apache.kafka.connect.json.JsonConverter
value.converter.schemas.enable=false
offset.backing.store.dir=/opt/debezium/offsets
snapshot.consistency=ONE
snapshot.mode=NEVER
latest.commit.log.only=true
```

---

**What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?**

`4.1.9` Cassandra. Local deployment using docker image `cassandra:4.1.9`

---

**What behavior do you expect?**

When the Kafka producer fails to deliver a record (for example, when `delivery.timeout.ms` is exceeded or any other producer error occurs and the callback receives an exception), the connector should not lose the corresponding commit log entry.

In particular, failed records should be preserved and made eligible for reprocessing when `commit.log.error.reprocessing.enabled=true`, ensuring that temporary Kafka outages do not result in permanent data loss.

---

**What behavior do you see?**

When the Kafka producer fails to deliver a record (for example, when `delivery.timeout.ms` is exceeded), the producer callback receives an exception, but the corresponding commit log file is not moved to the error/reprocessing directory.

As a result:
- The failed commit log file is not preserved for retry.
- It is not reprocessed, even when `commit.log.error.reprocessing.enabled=true`.
- Events are permanently lost.

After Kafka becomes available again, event production resumes, but the records that failed during the outage are missing from the Kafka topic. Debezium restart also won't help.

---

**Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?**

Didn't try `3.5.0.alpha` but all previous versions have the same issue.

---

**Do you have the connector logs, ideally from start till finish?**

Yes. I have full connector logs from start to finish. 

For investigation purposes, I also added additional logging to the source code and built a custom JAR to better understand the failure path.

[cassandra-dbz.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25362212/cassandra-dbz.log)

---

**How to reproduce the issue using our [tutorial](https://github.com/debezium/debezium-examples/tree/main/tutorial) deployment?**

I did not use the official tutorial setup directly. However, my local environment is very similar to it. Therefore, the reproduction steps are essentially the same as described below.

1. Create a test table in Cassandra with a `bigint` primary key (e.g., `id`) and enable CDC on that table.
2. Implement a simple script that continuously inserts rows into this table with strictly increasing sequential `id` values (1, 2, 3, …).
3. Start Kafka and the Debezium Cassandra connector.
4. Start the insert script and verify:
   - Records are successfully written to Cassandra.
   - Corresponding change events are produced to the Kafka topic.
5. Stop Kafka for a period longer than `delivery.timeout.ms` (e.g., `docker stop <kafka-container>`).
6. Wait until the producer starts failing with delivery timeout errors.
7. Start Kafka again.
8. Observe that event production resumes.
9. Stop the insert script.
10. Read all messages from the Kafka topic and compare the list of `id` values with those present in the Cassandra table.

I can share the docker-compose configuration for my local setup (as well as the Dockerfile used to build the Debezium image), along with the scripts I used to insert data into Cassandra and to compare the data between Kafka and Cassandra.

Please let me know if providing these artifacts would help with investigation or reproduction.

---

**Additional Info**

Here is the link to community thread [#community-cassandra > Records with exceptions in KafkaRecordEmitter.callback()](https://debezium.zulipchat.com/#narrow/channel/348251-community-cassandra/topic/Records.20with.20exceptions.20in.20KafkaRecordEmitter.2Ecallback.28.29/with/567548285)

Based on my investigation of the source code, the issue appears to be related to `KafkaRecordEmitter`.

When a record is sent via `KafkaRecordEmitter.emit(Record record)`, the result is handled asynchronously in `KafkaRecordEmitter.callback(Record record, Exception exception)`. If the callback receives a non-null exception, the corresponding commit log file is not added to `erroneousCommitLogs`.

However, simply adding failed records to `erroneousCommitLogs` inside the callback may not fully solve the problem. Since the producer callback is asynchronous, it seems possible for an `EOFEvent` to be processed before the callback is executed. In that case, the commit log file may already be moved to the archive directory instead of the error directory. Because of this race condition, the solution likely needs to be more robust than just updating the callback logic
author: arturalbov

project_fields.Title: Event loss when Kafka producer fails (e.g. delivery.timeout.ms exceeded)

title: Quarkus compatibility mode for external Source Connectors
body:
## Context

Debezium Extensions for Quarkus help the creation of native build for source connectors. The limit of this approach is that you have to instrument the connectors one-by-one depending on peculiarities of the source. This can be a great limitation for debezium server which should use the quarkus code to instrument a correct datapipeline for source connectors that doesn't have their quarkus implementation.

## Proposal

We should have a compatibility mode to integrate in Debezium Server the connectors that don't have a Quarkus implementation. The drawback would be that for these extensions, we don't provide a native build.

title: Documentation for Debezium Hibernate Cache
body:
## Context

Debezium’s Quarkus extensions now introduce support for automatic invalidation of Hibernate’s second‑level cache when database changes occur outside the application. This feature is based on the mechanism described in the official Debezium blog post on CDC‑driven cache invalidation and demonstrated in the cache-invalidation example.

## What needs to be documented
Add a new documentation section under `Integrations` explaining how to enable and use Hibernate 2nd‑level cache invalidation within a Quarkus application. Users need clear guidance on how to adopt the new feature in Quarkus applications, ensuring Hibernate’s 2nd‑level cache stays consistent when external database modifications occur. This reduces stale reads and removes the need for manual cache clearing, implementing a fully automated, CDC‑driven invalidation flow.
author: kmos

project_fields.Title: Documentation for Debezium Hibernate Cache

title: Debounce self-originated Hibernate cache invalidation events in Debezium Hibernate
body:
## Context

When the application writes to the DB, it often triggers both:

1. a local cache invalidation (because the app knows it changed the entity), and
2. a CDC event consumed by the same app via Debezium, which can cause a second, redundant invalidation.

This duplication increases cache churn and can cause thundering-herd effects under write-heavy workloads.

## Proposal

We need a mechanism to debounce invalidation events that are self-originated (i.e., changes the same application instance just performed), while still honoring invalidations coming from other nodes/instances.
author: kmos

project_fields.Title: Debounce self-originated Hibernate cache invalidation events in Debezium Hibernate

title: Add JMX metrics for CDC directory size and utilization in Cassandra connector
body:
## Feature request or enhancement

**Which use case/requirement will be addressed by the proposed feature?**

In production environments using Cassandra CDC, it is important to monitor the size and utilization of the CDC directory in order to prevent operational issues.

When the CDC directory reaches its configured limit:
- If `cdc_block_writes=true`, Cassandra blocks writes.
- If `cdc_block_writes=false`, CDC events may be dropped.

Both scenarios are critical from an operational perspective.

At the moment, I could not find an easily consumable built-in Cassandra metric that exposes CDC directory size or utilization in a way that can be directly used for monitoring and alerting.

Since the Debezium Cassandra connector directly processes commit logs from this directory, exposing a metric such as:
- Total CDC directory size
- Number of commit log files
- Percentage of configured CDC space limit (if available)

would significantly improve observability and operational safety.

Ideally, such a metric would be exposed directly by Cassandra itself. However, at the moment I could not find an easily consumable built-in metric for CDC directory utilization.

Given that the Debezium Cassandra connector directly depends on the CDC directory for correct operation, exposing this information at the connector level could still provide significant operational value, especially for early detection of backlogs or capacity issues.

Even if Cassandra eventually provides such a metric natively, having visibility from the connector perspective may still be useful for monitoring pipeline health end-to-end.

If you think this feature would be valuable, I would be glad to prepare a pull request with an implementation proposal.
author: arturalbov

project_fields.Title: Add JMX metrics for CDC directory size and utilization in Cassandra connector

title: Adding new SMT to concatenate key with provided delimiter
body:
## Feature request or enhancement

### Which use case/requirement will be addressed by the proposed feature?

In CDC pipelines using Debezium with **compacted topics**, key byte stability is critical.

Currently, Debezium emits structured keys (`Struct`) reflecting the database primary key. When using Avro or JSON converters, the final serialized key bytes may change due to:

* Schema evolution
* Field reordering
* Converter behavior

Kafka compares **raw key bytes**, not logical equality. If serialized bytes change:

* Compaction may fail to remove older records
* Tombstones may not match previous records
* Downstream upsert consumers may produce duplicates
* Partition routing may become unstable

We experienced this in production when using Avro keys with compacted topics.

---

## Proposed enhancement

Provide a Debezium SMT that:

* Concatenates selected key fields (typically PK fields)
* Produces a deterministic `String` key
* Uses a configurable delimiter
* Ensures stable field order

Example:

Database PK:

```sql
PRIMARY KEY (currency, isin, stock_exchange)
```

Structured key → transformed key:

```
CHF|CH1234567890|SIX
```

This ensures:

* Byte-level determinism
* Converter independence
* Stable partitioning
* Safe compaction behavior

---

## Implementation ideas (optional)

Config example:

```properties
transforms=keyConcat
transforms.keyConcat.type=io.debezium.transforms.KeyConcatenation
transforms.keyConcat.fields=currency,isin,stock_exchange
transforms.keyConcat.delimiter=|
```

Options:

* `fields` (default: all key fields in schema order)
* `delimiter` (default: `|`)
* `null.handling` (fail / empty / literal)

Output key schema: `Schema.STRING_SCHEMA`.

We have implemented internally but would be happy to contribute to dbz open source if people are interested.
author: yingkun-bai

project_fields.Title: Adding new SMT to concatenate key with provided delimiter

title: CockroachDB connector enhancements and security fixes
body:
Production hardening of the CockroachDB connector after MVP1. Covers security, reliability, type system, and configuration improvements.

 ## Changes

 **Security fixes:**
 - Fix SQL injection in CockroachDBConnection and CockroachDBStreamingChangeEventSource via parameterized queries
 - Remove credential exposure from INFO-level logs in CockroachDBConnector
 - Remove changefeed payload leakage from logs in streaming source and ChangefeedSchemaParser

 **NPE hardening:**
 - Fix 46+ null-safety risks across 10 source files with null guards, safe defaults, and proper validation

 **Type system:**
 - Rewrite CockroachDBValueConverterProvider with 30+ CockroachDB type mappings
 - Add pgvector-compatible VECTOR type support (mapped to io.debezium.data.DoubleVector)

 **Error handling:**
 - Rewrite CockroachDBErrorHandler to extend Debezium's ErrorHandler with CockroachDB-specific transient error classification (40001 serialization, 40P01
  deadlock, 08xxx connection)

 **Configuration:**
 - Add 6 new config properties: connection validation timeout, skip permission check, Kafka consumer group/poll/offset settings
 - Fix connection.retry.delay.ms default (100ms -> 1000ms)
 - Remove default from changefeed.sink.uri (required field)
 - Add config validators for changefeed envelope and sink type

 **Infrastructure:**
 - Parameterize CockroachDB version across IT tests and docker-compose (default v26.1.0, override via -Dcockroachdb.version)
 - Replace unbounded dedup cache with bounded LRU in streaming source
 - Add VECTOR type streaming integration test

 **Tests:**
 - New: CockroachDBErrorHandlerTest (7 tests), CockroachDBValueConverterProviderTest (20 tests), vector streaming IT
 - All 91 unit tests pass, format/import validation passes
author: viragtripathi

project_fields.Title: CockroachDB connector enhancements and security fixes

title: Add snapshot support using CockroachDB changefeed initial_scan
body:
Add snapshot support so the connector can backfill existing table data using CockroachDB'\''s native changefeed `initial_scan` option, rather than a separate JDBC-based snapshot phase.

## Background

CockroachDB changefeeds have a built-in `initial_scan` option that handles backfilling existing rows natively:
- `initial_scan='\''yes'\''` (default): Full table scan at changefeed creation, then stream changes
- `initial_scan='\''no'\''`: Skip scan, stream only ongoing changes
- `initial_scan='\''only'\''`: Scan all rows, then terminate the changefeed job with `succeeded` status

This is fundamentally different from PostgreSQL, which uses JDBC `SELECT` + `pg_export_snapshot()` + WAL replication slots. CockroachDB has none of those
mechanisms.

## Current Behavior

`CockroachDBSnapshotChangeEventSource` returns `SnapshotResult.skipped()` unconditionally. The changefeed is always created without `initial_scan`
configuration, so pre-existing rows may or may not be captured depending on CockroachDB defaults.

## Proposed Implementation

### Supported Snapshot Modes

| Mode | Changefeed Option | Behavior |
|---|---|---|
| `initial` (default) | `initial_scan='\''yes'\''` | Backfill existing rows on first start (no prior offset), then stream. If offset exists, resume with
`cursor=<offset>` |
| `always` | `initial_scan='\''yes'\''` | Drop/recreate changefeed with `initial_scan='\''yes'\''` on every connector restart |
| `no_data` / `never` | `initial_scan='\''no'\''` or `cursor=now()` | Skip backfill, stream only ongoing changes |
| `initial_only` | `initial_scan='\''only'\''` | Backfill all rows, then connector stops (changefeed terminates with `succeeded`) |
| `when_needed` | Conditional | Check if offset exists and is valid; if not, behave like `initial`; otherwise resume streaming |
| `configuration_based` | Debezium SPI | Delegate to `snapshot.mode.configuration.based.*` properties |
| `custom` | User SPI | Delegate to user-provided snapshotter class |

### Removed Mode
- **`exported`**: Remove from `SnapshotMode` enum. This is PostgreSQL-specific (`pg_export_snapshot()`) with no CockroachDB equivalent.

### Key Implementation Details

1. **Changefeed creation**: Pass `initial_scan` option based on `snapshot.mode` when building the `CREATE CHANGEFEED` statement in
`CockroachDBStreamingChangeEventSource`
2. **Offset handling**: When resuming, use `cursor=<resolved_timestamp>` from stored offset to avoid gaps/duplicates
3. **Event operation type**: Initial scan events should have `op=r` (read); streaming events use `op=c/u/d`. CockroachDB enriched changefeed envelope does not distinguish these -- the connector needs to track whether initial scan is still in progress (before first resolved timestamp)
4. **`CockroachDBSnapshotChangeEventSource`**: Remains a lightweight coordinator that configures the changefeed options; the actual data flows through the existing Kafka consumer path since CockroachDB'\''s initial scan emits to the same sink
5. **GC TTL awareness**: Document that `initial_scan` + `cursor` must be within `gc.ttlseconds` window. CockroachDB changefeeds automatically create protected timestamp records to prevent GC during the scan

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `snapshot.mode=initial` backfills existing rows on first start, resumes from offset on restart
- [ ] `snapshot.mode=always` backfills on every restart
- [ ] `snapshot.mode=no_data` skips backfill entirely
- [ ] `snapshot.mode=initial_only` backfills then stops the connector
- [ ] `snapshot.mode=when_needed` conditionally backfills based on offset state
- [ ] Remove `exported` from SnapshotMode enum (not applicable to CockroachDB)
- [ ] Initial scan events marked with `op=r`, streaming events with `op=c/u/d`
- [ ] No gaps or duplicates between initial scan and streaming phases
- [ ] Unit and integration tests covering each snapshot mode
- [ ] Works with CockroachDB Cloud

## References
- [CockroachDB CREATE CHANGEFEED docs](https://www.cockroachlabs.com/docs/stable/create-changefeed.html#initial-scan)
- [CockroachDB AS OF SYSTEM TIME](https://www.cockroachlabs.com/docs/stable/as-of-system-time)
author: viragtripathi

project_fields.Title: Add snapshot support using CockroachDB changefeed initial_scan

title: CockroachDB connector: Multi-table concurrent changefeed support
body:
## Summary

Support multiple tables in a single connector instance using a single CockroachDB changefeed job.

## Current Behavior

The connector iterates tables **sequentially** in a `for` loop and creates **one changefeed per table**, each with its own KafkaConsumer and consumer group. In practice, only the last table's consumer loop runs actively -- earlier tables have changefeeds created but their Kafka topics are not consumed concurrently.

## CockroachDB Native Capability

CockroachDB natively supports multi-table changefeeds:
```sql
CREATE CHANGEFEED FOR table1, table2, table3 
  INTO 'kafka://broker:9092' 
  WITH format='json', envelope='enriched';
```
Events are automatically routed to per-table Kafka topics. CockroachDB recommends <=80 changefeed jobs per cluster, so a single multi-table changefeed is preferred.

## Proposed Implementation

1. Create a single `CREATE CHANGEFEED FOR table1, table2, ..., tableN` statement covering all configured tables
2. Subscribe to all per-table Kafka topics in a single KafkaConsumer (or use topic regex pattern)
3. Route consumed events to the correct `TableId` based on the Kafka topic name or the `source.table_name` in the enriched envelope
4. Share one resolved timestamp / offset across all tables from the single changefeed job

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Single changefeed job created for all configured tables
- [ ] Events from all tables consumed concurrently
- [ ] Correct topic routing per table
- [ ] Offset tracking works across all tables
- [ ] Unit and integration tests with multiple tables
- [ ] Performance improvement over per-table changefeed approach
author: viragtripathi

project_fields.Title: CockroachDB connector: Multi-table concurrent changefeed support

title: CockroachDB connector: Schema evolution detection (DDL changes without restart)
body:
## Summary

Automatically detect and adapt to DDL changes (ALTER TABLE ADD/DROP/RENAME COLUMN, etc.) without requiring a connector restart.

## Current Behavior

- `CockroachDBSchema.initialize()` queries `information_schema` **once at startup** and never refreshes
- If `ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN` is executed while the connector runs, the changefeed JSON payload includes the new column but the connector **silently drops it** because the column is absent from the registered schema
- If `ALTER TABLE DROP COLUMN` is executed, the connector may fail or produce incorrect values
- README explicitly states: "No schema change detection: DDL changes are not automatically detected. Restart the connector after schema changes."

## CockroachDB Native Capability

CockroachDB changefeeds **handle schema changes natively**:
- When `ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN` is executed, the changefeed performs a **backfill** -- it re-emits all existing rows with the new schema
- With `enriched_properties = 'schema'`, the changefeed includes the table schema in each event, providing a built-in mechanism to detect schema changes
- Events after the schema change automatically include new columns in the JSON payload

## Proposed Implementation

1. **Detect schema changes**: When processing changefeed events, compare incoming JSON field count/names against the registered `Table` column list. If fields don't match, trigger a schema refresh
2. **Schema refresh**: Re-query `information_schema.columns` and `information_schema.key_column_usage` for the affected table
3. **Update Debezium registry**: Call `tables().overwriteTable(newTable)` and `refreshSchema(tableId)` to rebuild the `TableSchema`
4. **Alternative approach**: Use `enriched_properties = 'schema'` in the changefeed to get schema metadata embedded in each event, avoiding round-trips to CockroachDB for schema discovery

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN` detected and new column values emitted without restart
- [ ] `ALTER TABLE DROP COLUMN` handled gracefully (null values for removed columns during transition)
- [ ] `ALTER TABLE RENAME COLUMN` detected and reflected in emitted events
- [ ] Schema change emits appropriate schema change events to the schema change topic
- [ ] No data loss during schema transition
- [ ] Unit and integration tests for DDL scenarios
author: viragtripathi

project_fields.Title: CockroachDB connector: Schema evolution detection (DDL changes without restart)

title: CockroachDB connector: Signal-based incremental snapshots
body:
## Summary

Implement signal-based incremental snapshots so users can re-snapshot specific tables (or subsets of rows) on demand without restarting the connector or interrupting streaming.

## Current Behavior

- `CockroachDBChangeEventSourceFactory.getIncrementalSnapshotChangeEventSource()` returns `Optional.empty()`
- No signaling table infrastructure exists
- Full re-snapshot requires a connector restart with `snapshot.mode=always`

## CockroachDB Native Capability

- `AS OF SYSTEM TIME` allows **consistent reads** at a specific timestamp without locks, ideal for chunk-based snapshot reads
- CockroachDB's serializable isolation guarantees consistency
- Changefeed resolved timestamps can serve as watermarks for deduplication between snapshot and streaming events

## Proposed Implementation

1. Implement `CockroachDBIncrementalSnapshotChangeEventSource` extending Debezium's `AbstractIncrementalSnapshotChangeEventSource<CockroachDBPartition, TableId>`
2. Support signal delivery via:
   - Signaling table (standard Debezium approach)
   - Kafka signal channel (`signal.kafka.topic`)
3. For each chunk: `SELECT * FROM table AS OF SYSTEM TIME '<ts>' WHERE pk > ? ORDER BY pk LIMIT <chunk_size>`
4. Emit snapshot events with `op=r` through the dispatcher
5. Use Debezium's existing watermark-based deduplication to handle overlap between snapshot and streaming events
6. Return the implementation from `getIncrementalSnapshotChangeEventSource()`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Incremental snapshot triggered via signaling table or Kafka signal
- [ ] Chunk-based reads using `AS OF SYSTEM TIME` for consistency
- [ ] No data loss or duplication between snapshot and streaming events
- [ ] Supports table-level and row-level (with additional-condition) snapshot signals
- [ ] Progress tracked and resumable after connector restart
- [ ] Unit and integration tests

## Dependencies

- Benefits from schema evolution detection (schema must be current for snapshot reads)
author: viragtripathi

project_fields.Title: CockroachDB connector: Signal-based incremental snapshots

title: CockroachDB connector: Heartbeat support using resolved timestamps
body:
## Summary

Emit heartbeat events to advance connector offsets during idle periods, using CockroachDB's native resolved timestamps.

## Current Behavior

- Zero heartbeat implementation in the connector
- CockroachDB changefeed resolved timestamps are received but **silently discarded** with only a debug log:
  ```java
  if (jsonNode.has("resolved")) {
      LOGGER.debug("Received resolved timestamp: {}", jsonNode.get("resolved").asText());
      return;
  }
  ```
- During idle periods (no data changes), connector offsets do not advance, which can cause issues with:
  - Kafka Connect offset commit lag
  - Monitoring systems reporting the connector as stuck
  - CockroachDB protected timestamp accumulation

## CockroachDB Native Capability

CockroachDB changefeeds emit **resolved timestamps** at configurable intervals:
```sql
CREATE CHANGEFEED FOR ... WITH resolved = '10s';
```
A resolved timestamp means all changes up to that timestamp have been emitted. This is functionally identical to a heartbeat.

## Proposed Implementation

### Phase 1: Enable Debezium built-in heartbeat
- Debezium core provides `HeartbeatImpl` which emits heartbeat `SourceRecord`s to `__debezium-heartbeat.<server_name>` topic
- Configured via `heartbeat.interval.ms` (already inherited from `CommonConnectorConfig`)
- Call `dispatcher.dispatchServerHeartbeatEvent()` at appropriate intervals during the Kafka consumer poll loop

### Phase 2: Convert resolved timestamps to offset-advancing heartbeats
- When a resolved timestamp is received, instead of discarding it:
  1. Parse the timestamp
  2. Update the `CockroachDBOffsetContext` with the resolved timestamp
  3. Dispatch a heartbeat event to advance offsets
- This ensures offsets advance even with no data changes

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `heartbeat.interval.ms` config property controls heartbeat frequency
- [ ] Heartbeat events emitted to `__debezium-heartbeat.<server_name>` topic
- [ ] CockroachDB resolved timestamps advance connector offsets
- [ ] Offsets advance during idle periods (no data changes)
- [ ] Unit and integration tests
author: viragtripathi

project_fields.Title: CockroachDB connector: Heartbeat support using resolved timestamps

title: CockroachDB connector: Non-Kafka sink support (webhook, Pub/Sub, cloud storage)
body:
## Summary

Support CockroachDB changefeed sink types beyond Kafka: webhook, Google Cloud Pub/Sub, and cloud storage (S3/GCS/Azure).

## Current Behavior

- The connector **only supports Kafka** as the intermediate changefeed sink
- `consumeChangefeedEvents()` has a switch that throws `IllegalArgumentException` for any sink type other than `"kafka"`
- Config fields already enumerate planned types: `changefeed.sink.type` mentions `webhook`, `pubsub`, `cloudstorage`
- Validation rejects anything other than `"kafka"`
- No abstraction layer/interface for sink consumers -- just the `consumeFromKafkaTopic()` method

## CockroachDB Native Capability

CockroachDB supports multiple sink types natively:
- **Webhook**: `INTO 'webhook-https://endpoint:port/path'` -- posts JSON batches to HTTP endpoint
- **Google Cloud Pub/Sub**: `INTO 'gcpubsub://projects/project/topics/topic'`
- **Cloud Storage**: `INTO 's3://bucket/path?AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID=...'` -- writes JSON/Avro/Parquet files
- **Azure**: `INTO 'azure://container/path?AZURE_ACCOUNT_NAME=...'`

## Proposed Implementation

### 1. Create a `ChangefeedConsumer` interface
```java
public interface ChangefeedConsumer {
    void consume(TableId table, CockroachDBOffsetContext offset, ChangeEventSourceContext context);
}
```

### 2. Implement per-sink consumers

**Webhook consumer**:
- Start an embedded HTTP server to receive webhook POSTs from CockroachDB
- Parse JSON batches and dispatch events via existing `processChangefeedEvent()`
- Config: endpoint port, TLS settings

**Cloud storage consumer**:
- Poll S3/GCS/Azure for new changefeed files at configurable intervals
- Parse JSON/ndjson files, dispatch events
- Track processed files to avoid reprocessing
- Config: bucket, path prefix, poll interval, credentials

**Pub/Sub consumer**:
- Use Google Cloud Pub/Sub client library to subscribe and consume messages
- Acknowledge messages after successful dispatch
- Config: project, subscription, credentials

### 3. Registration
- Register implementations in `consumeChangefeedEvents()` switch
- Update `validateChangefeedSinkType()` to accept new types
- Add sink-specific config properties with validation

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `ChangefeedConsumer` interface abstraction
- [ ] Webhook sink: receive and process HTTP-delivered changefeed events
- [ ] Cloud storage sink: poll and process file-based changefeed events
- [ ] Pub/Sub sink: subscribe and process Pub/Sub-delivered changefeed events
- [ ] Sink-specific configuration properties with validation
- [ ] Unit and integration tests per sink type
- [ ] Documentation for each sink type setup
author: viragtripathi

project_fields.Title: CockroachDB connector: Non-Kafka sink support (webhook, Pub/Sub, cloud storage)

title: Add CockroachDB connector documentation to debezium.io
body:
Add comprehensive documentation for the Debezium CockroachDB connector to the official documentation site.

The documentation covers:
- Overview and architecture (two-stage Kafka pipeline with enriched changefeeds)
- Snapshot support via CockroachDB native initial_scan
- Streaming changes with resolved timestamp offset tracking
- Data change events with full JSON examples
- Data type mappings (25+ CockroachDB types including VECTOR)

title: Manipulating LOB columns using DBMS_LOB package fails using XStream
body:
## Bug Report

While manipulating an LOB column using the following PL/SQL script:
```sql
                DECLARE
                    l_clob CLOB;
                    l_buf VARCHAR2(32767);
                    p_times INTEGER := 5;
                BEGIN
                    SELECT data INTO l_clob
                      FROM DEBEZIUM.APPS_HEARTBEAT_XSTREAM_CUSTOM
                     WHERE ID = 1
                       FOR UPDATE;
                    
                    FOR i IN 1..p_times LOOP
                        l_buf := RPAD('append-' || i || '-', 5000, 'y');
                        DBMS_LOB.WRITEAPPEND(l_clob, LENGTH(l_buf), l_buf);
                    END LOOP;
                    
                    COMMIT;
                END;
```

This causes the XStream adapter to fail with 
```
Caused by: oracle.streams.StreamsException: ORA-26824: user-defined XStream callback error
XStreamOut DeliverChunk: exception thrown in processChunk callback.
```

The root cause is
```
Caused by: java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: Received event of unexpected command type: DefaultRowLCR:
Header:
source database name: ORCLPDB1
command type: LOB WRITE
object owner: DEBEZIUM
object name: APPS_HEARTBEAT_XSTREAM_CUSTOM
tag: []
position: [0 0 0 0 0 4d ffffffdb 72 0 0 0 1 0 0 0 1 0 0 0 0 0 4d ffffffdb 60 0 0 0 4 0 0 0 1 2]
transaction id: 10.12.2242
source time: 2026-02-20 12:57:04
scn: null
commit scn: null
lcr flags:
Statement LCR: false
IDKeyColumnsOnly LCR: false
Sequence LCR: false
Attribute[2]
(0): ROOT_NAME : ORCLCDB
(1): ROW_ID : AAAR4/AAMAAAAWUAAA
Old column values (0):
New column values (2):
(0): DefaultColumnValue(csid:0, tde:false, type:0, name:ID, data:1.0)
(1): DefaultChunkColumnValue(offset:1, size:0, chunkType:1, emptyChunk:false, lastChunk:true, xmlDiff:false, endOfRow:false, charSetId:0, DefaultColumnValue(csid:0, tde:false, type:1, name:DATA, data:null))

	at io.debezium.connector.oracle.xstream.XStreamChangeRecordEmitter.getOperation(XStreamChangeRecordEmitter.java:51)
	at io.debezium.relational.RelationalChangeRecordEmitter.emitChangeRecords(RelationalChangeRecordEmitter.java:43)
	at io.debezium.pipeline.EventDispatcher.dispatchDataChangeEvent(EventDispatcher.java:299)
	... 36 more
```

The current LCR handler does not expect to receive `LOB_WRITE` row changes, and expects LOB changes to be provided only via the chunk handler API. This leads to the change record emitter being unable to resolve the operation associated with `LOB_WRITE`.
author: Naros

project_fields.Title: Manipulating LOB columns using DBMS_LOB package fails using XStream

title: CockroachDB connector: Add configurable changefeed strategy (single vs per-table)
body:
## Summary

Add a `cockroachdb.changefeed.strategy` configuration option that lets users choose between:

- **`single`** (current default): One `CREATE CHANGEFEED FOR table1, table2, ...` covering all tables. Single KafkaConsumer subscribes to all per-table topics. Recommended by CockroachDB for clusters with many tables (~80 job limit).

- **`per-table`**: One changefeed per table, each with its own KafkaConsumer. Better for:
  - **Parallelism**: Changefeed jobs can run on different cluster nodes
  - **Isolation**: A failure in one table's changefeed doesn't affect others
  - **Per-table tuning**: Different `resolved` intervals, cursors, or options per table
  - **High-throughput tables**: Isolate hot tables from low-throughput ones

## Proposed Config

| Option | Default | Description |
|--------|---------|-------------|
| `cockroachdb.changefeed.strategy` | `single` | Changefeed creation strategy. `single` creates one multi-table changefeed (recommended for large table counts). `per-table` creates a separate changefeed per table (better parallelism and isolation). |

## Implementation Notes

- `single` strategy is already implemented ([PR #25](https://github.com/debezium/debezium-connector-cockroachdb/pull/25))
- `per-table` strategy would restore the original per-table loop but with the improved topic naming (`full_table_name` + `topic_prefix`) and `kafka.bootstrap.servers` config from PR #25
- Each per-table changefeed would get its own KafkaConsumer with a table-specific consumer group ID
- The README and debezium docs should be updated to document both strategies and when to use each

## Related

- Follow-up from [debezium-connector-cockroachdb#25](https://github.com/debezium/debezium-connector-cockroachdb/pull/25) (multi-table concurrent changefeed support)
- CockroachDB changefeed recommendations: https://www.cockroachlabs.com/docs/stable/create-and-configure-changefeeds#recommendations
author: viragtripathi

project_fields.Title: CockroachDB connector: Add configurable changefeed strategy (single vs per-table)

title: Incremental snapshot fails for tables without prior DML events in PostgreSQL
body:
## Bug report

**What Debezium connector do you use and what version?**

Postgres, version 3.1.3

**What is the connector configuration?**

config used to replicate the bug with local pg instance: 
```
{
  "name": "dbz-4903-test-connector",
  "config": {
    "connector.class": "io.debezium.connector.postgresql.PostgresConnector",
    "tasks.max": "1",
    "database.hostname": "postgres",
    "database.port": "5432",
    "database.user": "<user>",
    "database.password": "<password>",
    "database.dbname": "testdb",
    "database.server.name": "dbz4903test",
    "topic.prefix": "dbz4903test",
    "plugin.name": "pgoutput",
    "publication.name": "dbz_4903_publication",
    "publication.autocreate.mode": "filtered",
    "table.include.list": "public.dbz_4903_test,public.debezium_signal",
    "snapshot.mode": "no_data",
    "slot.name": "dbz_4903_slot",
    "signal.data.collection": "public.debezium_signal",
    "notification.enabled.channels": "log",
    "schema.history.internal.kafka.bootstrap.servers": "kafka:9092",
    "schema.history.internal.kafka.topic": "schema-changes.dbz4903test"
  }
}
```

---

**What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?**

AWS RDS postgres

**What behavior do you expect?**

The fix for DBZ-4903 ([PR #5962](https://github.com/debezium/debezium/pull/5962)) added automatic schema retrieval when a table isn't in memory during incremental snapshots. This works for MySQL and Oracle but doesn't work for Postgres.

1. Connector starts with `snapshot.mode=no_data`
2. No initial snapshot is taken
3. Ad-hoc snapshot can be triggered successfully via signals table
4. Records are captured during the ad-hoc snapshot

**What behavior do you see?**

Following a connector restart, an incremental snapshot fails if a DML event doesn't occur prior to the snapshot starting. 

The behavior observed:
1. Connector starts with `snapshot.mode=no_data`
2. No initial snapshot is taken
3. Ad-hoc snapshot triggered via signals table fails with warning: "Schema not found for table"

---

**Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?**

Yes

**Do you have the connector logs, ideally from start till finish?**

_You might be asked later to provide [DEBUG/TRACE](https://debezium.io/documentation/reference/stable/operations/logging.html) level log._

`Schema retrieval failed due to an exception for public.my_table.`

---

**How to reproduce the issue using our [tutorial](https://github.com/debezium/debezium-examples/tree/main/tutorial) deployment?**

`<Your answer>`

title: JDBC Sink: Add CockroachDBDatabaseDialectProvider to support Hibernate CockroachDialect
body:
# 🚀 Feature Request

## Problem Description

The Debezium JDBC sink currently requires the following configuration when writing to CockroachDB:

```
hibernate.dialect=org.hibernate.dialect.PostgreSQLDialect
```

Hibernate 6.x provides a first-class dialect for CockroachDB:

```
org.hibernate.dialect.CockroachDialect
```

However, when `CockroachDialect` is used, the JDBC sink's `DatabaseDialectResolver` does not recognize it as PostgreSQL-compatible.

Because `CockroachDialect` extends `Dialect` directly (not `PostgreSQLDialect`), the resolver falls back to:

```
GeneralDatabaseDialect
```

`GeneralDatabaseDialect`:

* Does not support upsert
* Does not generate `INSERT ... ON CONFLICT ... DO UPDATE`

CockroachDB fully supports PostgreSQL-style upsert syntax, so this fallback results in missing upsert capability unless users manually override the dialect to `PostgreSQLDialect`.

---

## Proposed Change

Add a `CockroachDBDatabaseDialectProvider` to the JDBC sink that:

* Detects `org.hibernate.dialect.CockroachDialect`
* Maps it to a Cockroach-specific dialect implementation
* Or internally reuses `PostgresDatabaseDialect`
* Enables proper generation of `INSERT ... ON CONFLICT ... DO UPDATE`

This would allow users to use:

```
hibernate.dialect=org.hibernate.dialect.CockroachDialect
```

without losing upsert support.

---

## Expected Behavior

When using the Hibernate `CockroachDialect`, the Debezium JDBC sink should:

* Automatically resolve to a dialect that supports upsert
* Generate PostgreSQL-compatible `ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE` statements
* Avoid falling back to `GeneralDatabaseDialect`
* Remove the need for manual dialect overrides

---

## Alternatives Considered

Users can currently work around the issue by forcing:

```
hibernate.dialect=org.hibernate.dialect.PostgreSQLDialect
```

However, this:

* Is unintuitive
* Hides CockroachDB-specific optimizations
* Breaks principle-of-least-surprise behavior
* Prevents future CockroachDB-specific enhancements in the sink

---

## Additional Context

The JDBC sink requires hibernate.dialect=org.hibernate.dialect.PostgreSQLDialect. While CockroachDB has a first-class [CockroachDialect](https://docs.hibernate.org/orm/6.3/javadocs/org/hibernate/dialect/CockroachDialect.html) in Hibernate 6.x ([CockroachDB + Hibernate docs](https://www.cockroachlabs.com/docs/stable/build-a-java-app-with-cockroachdb-hibernate)), the Debezium JDBC sink's DatabaseDialectResolver does not yet have a CockroachDB-specific DatabaseDialectProvider. Since CockroachDialect extends Dialect directly (not PostgreSQLDialect), the resolver falls back to GeneralDatabaseDialect which lacks upsert support. Using PostgreSQLDialect correctly maps to the Debezium PostgresDatabaseDialect, which generates the INSERT ... ON CONFLICT ... DO UPDATE syntax that CockroachDB supports. Adding a CockroachDBDatabaseDialectProvider to the Debezium JDBC sink is a natural follow-up enhancement.
author: viragtripathi

project_fields.Title: JDBC Sink: Add CockroachDBDatabaseDialectProvider to support Hibernate CockroachDialect

title: A rolled back transaction mined in two steps sometimes leads to partial transaction id [DBZ-9686]
body:
Migrated from [DBZ-9686](https://issues.redhat.com/browse/DBZ-9686)

h1. Bug report
Relates to this conversation:  https://debezium.zulipchat.com/#narrow/channel/348250-community-oracle/topic/OldestScn.20not.20advancing.20in.203.2E2.2E1/near/556764402

In 3.2.3 and potentially all earlier versions DBZ seems to not be able to deal with an unusual way that Oracle handles splitting a transaction across two queries/sessions.

Here are two transactions mined in one step.  Note that both rollbacks are accounted for
 !image-2025-11-17-14-16-03-332.png|thumbnail! 

However, if split across two steps at a specific point Oracle will generate a partial transaction id as shown below and DBZ seems to then not be able to match the overall rollback to the transaction that's in the buffer:

 !image-2025-11-17-14-17-33-918.png|thumbnail! 

I've attached also the full range that would have been mined in each fetch query for the test case I did

h2. What Debezium connector do you use and what version?
I have reproduced on 3.2.0 and also observed on 3.0.8

h2. What is the connector configuration?
Hybrid, with "IN" otherwise largely defaults

h2. What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?
Oracle 19C

h2. What behavior do you expect?
DBZ should be able to walk these back to the correct transactions in the buffer similar to partial rollbacks

h2. What behavior do you see?
DBZ misses the rollback and so low watermark never advances

h2. Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?
NO, but caveat:  The reason this doesn't occur in the latest version is that it is no longer possible in recent versions to mine a transaction in two steps as the eldest open transaction  is the low watermark for the session.  This solves a lot of issues however in some of our environments it makes mining infeasible where there are large transaction volumes and long-running transactions.

h2. Do you have the connector logs, ideally from start till finish?
Yes have attached the log which has a customisation to print out the fetch ranges as info messages.
author: jpechane

project_fields.Title: A rolled back transaction mined in two steps sometimes leads to partial transaction id [DBZ-9686]

4. Start the Postgres example

6. Create the connector
7. See the warning in the logs

I would propose that the warning should only be logged for missing select statements, when the table in question is not the signal data collection one. This seems like a trivial change to me. I would be willing to create a PR for this.

## Bug report

**What Debezium connector do you use and what version?**

`debezium-connector-mongodb-3.4.1.Final-plugin.tar.gz`

---

**What is the connector configuration?**

```
{
  "name": "dbz-mongo-json-cdc-src",
  "config": {
    "buffer.memory": 16777216,
    "capture.mode": "change_streams_update_full",
    "capture.scope": "collection",
    "collection.include.list": "database.coll1,database.coll2,database.coll3,database.coll4,database.coll5,database.coll6,database.coll7",
    "connector.class": "io.debezium.connector.mongodb.MongoDbConnector",
    "key.converter": "org.apache.kafka.connect.json.JsonConverter",
    "key.converter.schemas.enable": false,
    "max.record.size": 16777216,
    "message.max.bytes": 16777216,
    "mongodb.connection.string": "mongodb+srv://REDACTED.mongodb.net/?tls=true&authSource=%24external&authMechanism=MONGODB-AWS",
    "mongodb.members.auto.discover": true,
    "mongodb.ssl.enabled": true,
    "producer.override.max.request.size": 16777216,
    "publish.full.document.only": true,
    "signal.enabled.channels": "kafka",
    "signal.kafka.bootstrap.servers": "REDACTED.confluent.cloud:9092",
    "signal.kafka.topic": "vkc.cdc.mongodb.json.v1._signal",
    "snapshot.mode": "initial",
    "tasks.max": 1,
    "topic.creation.default.partitions": "3",
    "topic.creation.default.replication.factor": "3",
    "topic.creation.enable": true,
    "topic.prefix": "vkc.cdc.mongodb.json.v1",
    "value.converter": "org.apache.kafka.connect.json.JsonConverter",
    "value.converter.schemas.enable": false
  }
}
```

---

**What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?**

MongoDB Atlas, version 8.0.19. The cluster in question has two shards currently.

---

**What behavior do you expect?**

Expected to be able to take snapshots from the indicated MongoDB collections.

---

**What behavior do you see?**

Instead we get the following, seemingly fatal, error:

```
[2026-02-23 16:11:43,637] ERROR Error while attempting to Setting resume token: No value present (io.debezium.connector.mongodb.connection.MongoDbConnections:52)
java.util.NoSuchElementException: No value present
```

---

**Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?**

Yes, as we are running the latest released version already. We have not tried any pre-release versions as of yet.

---

**Do you have the connector logs, ideally from start till finish?**

Yes, but only at `INFO` level at the moment. If necessary, I can get `DEBUG` or `TRACE` level logs.

---

**How to reproduce the issue using our [tutorial](https://github.com/debezium/debezium-examples/tree/main/tutorial) deployment?**

Neither the MongoDB cluster nor the Kafka broker in question would be available for use with this.

title: `commit.log.error.reprocessing.enabled=true` silently skips all mutations from error commit logs once newer segments have been processed
body:
## Bug report

This is a follow up issue to debezium/dbz#1610. It turned out that fix of debezium/dbz#1610 solved only half of the problem.

**What Debezium connector do you use and what version?**

Cassandra Connector `3.4.1.Final` and latest `origin/main`

---

**What is the connector configuration?**

```properties
connector.name=dbz_test_connector
commit.log.relocation.dir=/opt/debezium/relocation
commit.log.real.time.processing.enabled=true
commit.log.error.reprocessing.enabled=true
http.port=8000
cassandra.config=/etc/cassandra/cassandra.yaml
cassandra.hosts=host.docker.internal
cassandra.port=9042
cassandra.driver.config.file=/opt/debezium/conf/cassandra-driver.conf
kafka.producer.bootstrap.servers=kafka:9092
kafka.producer.retries=3
kafka.producer.retry.backoff.ms=1000
kafka.producer.delivery.timeout.ms=120000
kafka.producer.enable.idempotence=false
topic.prefix=dbz_test
key.converter=org.apache.kafka.connect.json.JsonConverter
key.converter.schemas.enable=false
value.converter=org.apache.kafka.connect.json.JsonConverter
value.converter.schemas.enable=false
offset.backing.store.dir=/opt/debezium/offsets
snapshot.consistency=ONE
snapshot.mode=NEVER
```

---

**What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?**

`4.1.9` Cassandra. Local deployment using docker image `cassandra:4.1.9`

---

**What behavior do you expect?**

When `commit.log.error.reprocessing.enabled=true` and error commit log files are moved back to the CDC directory (via `CommitLogTransfer.getErrorCommitLogFiles()`), the connector should reprocess all mutations from those files that were never successfully emitted to Kafka.

---

**What behavior do you see?**

All mutations in the reprocessed error file are silently skipped with the log message:
```
Mutation at CommitLog-7-<segment>.log:<position> for table <keyspace>.<table> already processed, skipping...
```

**Root cause**: `FileOffsetWriter` tracks a single per-table offset representing the globally latest processed position. `CassandraXCommitLogReadHandlerImpl.handleMutation()` compares each mutation's position against this recorded offset using `OffsetPosition.compareTo()`, which sorts by commit log segment ID extracted from the filename first, then by byte position. Once any newer segment advances the per-table offset past the error file's segment ID, every mutation in that error file satisfies `currentOffset <= recordedOffset` and is considered already processed — even mutations that were never emitted to Kafka.

Scenario that triggers it:
  1. `CommitLog-7-100.log` fails mid-processing → moved to error/
  2. Processing continues normally; `CommitLog-7-200.log` finishes → per-table offset is now `CommitLog-7-200.log:<position>`
  3. `CommitLog-7-100.log` is moved back to CDC dir for reprocessing
  4. Every mutation in `CommitLog-7-100.log` is skipped — data is permanently lost with no warning

In practice this means `errorCommitLogReprocessEnabled` is only effective in a very narrow window where no newer commit logs have been processed for the affected table since the failure. In any realistic production environment the feature does not work.

---

**Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?**

Yes

---

**Do you have the connector logs, ideally from start till finish?**

No

---

**How to reproduce the issue using our [tutorial](https://github.com/debezium/debezium-examples/tree/main/tutorial) deployment?**

1. Configure the connector with `commit.log.error.reprocessing.enabled=true` and a custom `CommitLogTransfer` implementation that moves files between `error/` and `CDC` dir
  2. Reproduce all steps from debezium/dbz#1610
  3. Restart connector (that should move files back from `error/` to `CDC` dir)
  4. Error files are processed by changes never reach Kafka

---

**Fix Proposal**

Proposed fix:

- Add a `reprocessingCommitLogs: Set<String>` to the connector context. 
- `CommitLogTransfer` — change `getErrorCommitLogFiles()` to return `Collection<String>` of moved filenames
- `CommitLogProcessor` + `CommitLogIdxProcessor` — populate the set from `getErrorCommitLogFiles()` return value
- `Cassandra3/4/5CommitLogReadHandlerImpl` — bypass `isOffsetProcessed` check when file is in the set
- `QueueProcessor` — remove file from set on successful archive

Basically, `isOffsetProcessed` would check if the file is present in the `reprocessingCommitLogs` set and, if it is, allow mutations to flow through.

I will gladly add those changes if you agree with them.
author: arturalbov

project_fields.Title: `commit.log.error.reprocessing.enabled=true` silently skips all mutations from error commit logs once newer segments have been processed

title: Unable to parse ROW ARCHIVAL DDL and corresponding DML with hidden ORA_ARCHIVE_STATE column
body:
## Bug report

**What Debezium connector do you use and what version?**

- **Connector:** Debezium Oracle connector on Kafka Connect
- **Version:** `3.3.2.Final`
- **Library:** `https://repo1.maven.org/maven2/io/debezium/debezium-connector-oracle/3.3.2.Final/debezium-connector-oracle-3.3.2.Final-plugin.tar.gz`

---

**What is the connector configuration?**

```json
{
        "connector.type": "source.debezium.oracle",
        "database.dbname": "mydatabase_name",
        "database.hostname": "mydatabase_hostname",
        "database.user": "mydatabase_user",
        "internal.kafka.bootstrap.servers": "my_kafka_brokers",
        "log.mining.archive.log.hours": "0",
        "log.mining.skip.missing.logs": "true",
        "schema.include.list": "MY_SCHEMA",
        "table.include.list": "MY_SCHEMA.MY_TABLE",
        "topic.prefix": "my-topic",
        "schema.history.internal.skip.unparseable.ddl": "true"
      }
```

**Remark:** the issue exists with `schema.history.internal.skip.unparseable.ddl=true` and without it, but behaves slightly differently.

---

**What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?**

The issue occurs with:
- 
- `Oracle 19` running on AWS EC2 RHeL in CDB/PDB
- `Oracle 19` running as AWS RDS

---

## Behaviour 1 - DDL issue

**Scenario**

- An Oracle table MY_SCHEMA.MY_TABLE is part of the include list for Debezium.
- Initial snapshot has been performed successfully
- Changes have been captured successfully through CDC, post initial snapshot
- The table is altered to enable `ROW ARCHIVAL`

**What behavior do you expect?**

**DDL**
It must be possible for Debezium to gracefully handle ROW ARCHIVAL DDL such as:

```sql
ALTER TABLE MY_SCHEMA.MY_TABLE ROW ARCHIVAL;
```

---

**What behavior do you see?**

The DDL `ALTER TABLE MY_SCHEMA.MY_TABLE ROW ARCHIVAL;` causes an error in the connector.

When `schema.history.internal.skip.unparseable.ddl` is omitted (default value applies), the error `io.debezium.text.ParsingException: DDL statement couldn't be parsed. Please open a Jira issue with the statement 'ALTER TABLE MY_SCHEMA.MY_TABLE ROW ARCHIVAL;'
no viable alternative at input 'ROW ARCHIVAL'` occurs and the connector stops. When the `schema.history.internal.skip.unparseable.ddl=true`, then the error occurs as well but is logged as a warning and the connector continues.

<details>

Here is the stack trace with `schema.history.internal.skip.unparseable.ddl=true`

```text
[2026-02-26 07:23:42,212] WARN [source-connector-local-oracle|task-0] Ignoring unparsable DDL statement 'ALTER TABLE MY_SCHEMA.MY_TABLE ROW ARCHIVAL;': (io.debezium.connector.oracle.OracleSchemaChangeEventEmitter:107)
io.debezium.text.ParsingException: DDL statement couldn't be parsed. Please open a Jira issue with the statement 'ALTER TABLE MY_SCHEMA.MY_TABLE ROW ARCHIVAL;'
no viable alternative at input 'ROW ARCHIVAL'
        at io.debezium.antlr.ParsingErrorListener.syntaxError(ParsingErrorListener.java:43)
        at org.antlr.v4.runtime.ProxyErrorListener.syntaxError(ProxyErrorListener.java:41)
        at org.antlr.v4.runtime.Parser.notifyErrorListeners(Parser.java:543)
        at org.antlr.v4.runtime.DefaultErrorStrategy.reportNoViableAlternative(DefaultErrorStrategy.java:310)
        at org.antlr.v4.runtime.DefaultErrorStrategy.reportError(DefaultErrorStrategy.java:136)
        at io.debezium.ddl.parser.oracle.generated.PlSqlParser.alter_table_properties_1(PlSqlParser.java)
        at io.debezium.ddl.parser.oracle.generated.PlSqlParser.alter_table_properties(PlSqlParser.java)
        at io.debezium.ddl.parser.oracle.generated.PlSqlParser.alter_table(PlSqlParser.java)
        at io.debezium.ddl.parser.oracle.generated.PlSqlParser.unit_statement(PlSqlParser.java:2525)
        at io.debezium.ddl.parser.oracle.generated.PlSqlParser.sql_script(PlSqlParser.java:2143)
        at io.debezium.connector.oracle.antlr.OracleDdlParser.parseTree(OracleDdlParser.java:76)
        at io.debezium.connector.oracle.antlr.OracleDdlParser.parseTree(OracleDdlParser.java:33)
        at io.debezium.antlr.AntlrDdlParser.parse(AntlrDdlParser.java:74)
        at io.debezium.connector.oracle.antlr.OracleDdlParser.parse(OracleDdlParser.java:71)
        at io.debezium.connector.oracle.OracleSchemaChangeEventEmitter.emitSchemaChangeEvent(OracleSchemaChangeEventEmitter.java:103)
        at io.debezium.pipeline.EventDispatcher.dispatchSchemaChangeEvent(EventDispatcher.java:417)                                                                                                                                                                                  at io.debezium.connector.oracle.logminer.AbstractLogMinerStreamingChangeEventSource.dispatchSchemaChangeEventInternal(AbstractLogMinerStreamingChangeEventSource.java:1276)
        at io.debezium.connector.oracle.logminer.buffered.BufferedLogMinerStreamingChangeEventSource.handleSchemaChangeEvent(BufferedLogMinerStreamingChangeEventSource.java:580)
        at io.debezium.connector.oracle.logminer.AbstractLogMinerStreamingChangeEventSource.processEvent(AbstractLogMinerStreamingChangeEventSource.java:480)
        at io.debezium.connector.oracle.logminer.AbstractLogMinerStreamingChangeEventSource.executeAndProcessQuery(AbstractLogMinerStreamingChangeEventSource.java:404)
        at io.debezium.connector.oracle.logminer.buffered.BufferedLogMinerStreamingChangeEventSource.process(BufferedLogMinerStreamingChangeEventSource.java:244)
        at io.debezium.connector.oracle.logminer.buffered.BufferedLogMinerStreamingChangeEventSource.executeLogMiningStreaming(BufferedLogMinerStreamingChangeEventSource.java:157)
        at io.debezium.connector.oracle.logminer.AbstractLogMinerStreamingChangeEventSource.execute(AbstractLogMinerStreamingChangeEventSource.java:212)
        at io.debezium.connector.oracle.logminer.AbstractLogMinerStreamingChangeEventSource.execute(AbstractLogMinerStreamingChangeEventSource.java:88)
        at io.debezium.pipeline.ChangeEventSourceCoordinator.streamEvents(ChangeEventSourceCoordinator.java:329)
        at io.debezium.pipeline.ChangeEventSourceCoordinator.executeChangeEventSources(ChangeEventSourceCoordinator.java:207)
        at io.debezium.pipeline.ChangeEventSourceCoordinator.lambda$start$0(ChangeEventSourceCoordinator.java:147)                                                                                                                                                                   at java.base/java.util.concurrent.Executors$RunnableAdapter.call(Executors.java:539)
        at java.base/java.util.concurrent.FutureTask.run(FutureTask.java:264)
        at java.base/java.util.concurrent.ThreadPoolExecutor.runWorker(ThreadPoolExecutor.java:1136)
        at java.base/java.util.concurrent.ThreadPoolExecutor$Worker.run(ThreadPoolExecutor.java:635)
        at java.base/java.lang.Thread.run(Thread.java:840)
Caused by: org.antlr.v4.runtime.NoViableAltException
        at org.antlr.v4.runtime.atn.ParserATNSimulator.noViableAlt(ParserATNSimulator.java:2028)
        at org.antlr.v4.runtime.atn.ParserATNSimulator.execATN(ParserATNSimulator.java:467)
        at org.antlr.v4.runtime.atn.ParserATNSimulator.adaptivePredict(ParserATNSimulator.java:393)
        ... 27 more
```

</details>


## Behaviour 2 - DML issue

**Scenario**

- An Oracle table MY_SCHEMA.MY_TABLE is part of the include list for Debezium and `ROW ARCHIVAL` is already active on the table.
- Initial snapshot has been performed successfully
- DML changes are initiated on the table (e.g., `INSERT` in the table)

---

**What behavior do you expect?**

It must be possible for Debezium to seamlessly capture changes on a table where `ROW ARCHIVAL` is enabled.

---

**What behavior do you see?**

The Debezium connector is unable to parse the DML, error `DML statement couldn't be parsed. [...] Caused by: java.lang.ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException: Index 8 out of bounds for length 8` is raised.

<details>

```text
[2026-02-26 07:23:42,216] ERROR [source-connector-local-oracle|task-0] LogMiner session stopped due to an error. (io.debezium.connector.oracle.logminer.AbstractLogMinerStreamingChangeEventSource:215)
io.debezium.connector.oracle.logminer.parser.DmlParserException: DML statement couldn't be parsed. Please open a Jira issue with the statement 'insert into "MY_SCHEMA"."MY_TABLE"("LAST_NAME","FIRST_NAME","AGE","ADDRESS","EMAIL","UPDATED_AT","USER_ID","MY_SECRETS","ORA_ARCHIVE_STATE") values ('Test','Name','31','Some address','someemail@example.com',TO_TIMESTAMP('2026-02-26 06:54:51.683680000'),'22282',NULL,'0');'.
        at io.debezium.connector.oracle.logminer.AbstractLogMinerStreamingChangeEventSource.parseDmlStatement(AbstractLogMinerStreamingChangeEventSource.java:1451)
        at io.debezium.connector.oracle.logminer.AbstractLogMinerStreamingChangeEventSource.dispatchDataChangeEventInternal(AbstractLogMinerStreamingChangeEventSource.java:1313)

        at io.debezium.connector.oracle.logminer.buffered.BufferedLogMinerStreamingChangeEventSource.process(BufferedLogMinerStreamingChangeEventSource.java:244)
        at io.debezium.connector.oracle.logminer.buffered.BufferedLogMinerStreamingChangeEventSource.executeLogMiningStreaming(BufferedLogMinerStreamingChangeEventSource.java:157)
        at io.debezium.connector.oracle.logminer.AbstractLogMinerStreamingChangeEventSource.execute(AbstractLogMinerStreamingChangeEventSource.java:212)
        at io.debezium.connector.oracle.logminer.AbstractLogMinerStreamingChangeEventSource.execute(AbstractLogMinerStreamingChangeEventSource.java:88)
        at io.debezium.pipeline.ChangeEventSourceCoordinator.streamEvents(ChangeEventSourceCoordinator.java:329)

Caused by: io.debezium.connector.oracle.logminer.parser.DmlParserException: Failed to parse insert DML: 'insert into "MY_SCHEMA"."MY_TABLE"("LAST_NAME","FIRST_NAME","AGE","ADDRESS","EMAIL","UPDATED_AT","USER_ID","MY_SECRETS","ORA_ARCHIVE_STATE") values ('Test','Name','31','Some address','someemail@example.com',TO_TIMESTAMP('2026-02-26 06:54:51.683680000'),'22282',NULL,'0');'
```

title: Switch Oracle version resolution logic to use DatabaseMetadata
body:
## Feature request or enhancement

_For feature requests or enhancements, please provide the following information:_

**Which use case/requirement will be addressed by the proposed feature?**

Currently, the Oracle connector resolves the database version by inspecting and parsing the database banner. We should check to see whether or not the JDBC `DatabaseMetadata` can be used instead and is equally reliable to resolving the right major/minor versions.
author: Naros

project_fields.Title: Switch Oracle version resolution logic to use DatabaseMetadata

title: Update `maven.compiler.testRelease` and `debezium.java.specific.target` to Java 25
body:
In order to use Java 25 for JUnit tests and the "specific" modules (Server, Operator, Outbox and Quarkus extension) update `maven.compiler.testRelease` and `debezium.java.specific.target` to Java 25.

Mind to enable Java 25 in:

- build-infrastructure (containers and environments)
- debezium/container-images
- test-infrastructure / GH Actions and Maven Docker plugin
- Testcontainers container images

title: Remove hard line breaks throughout [DBZ-9675]
body:
Migrated from [DBZ-9675](https://issues.redhat.com/browse/DBZ-9675)

h3. Use of hard line breaks

The current docs use hard line breaks extensively, especially in the description entries in properties tables and description lists. Although they serve a useful purpose, generally speaking, the rendering that they effect can be preserved through other means. 

Alternate markup, such as the use of list continuations vs. hard breaks can accomplish a similar, yet "legal" result for these entries. These look similar, because they both use the {{+}} character, but continuations, which just tie together paragraphs within a block don't place a space before the character. In some cases, it might be possible to dispense with the extra markup altogether. For example:

{quote}{{{}property name{}}}:: 
Description

Default value::: VALUE
option1::: blah blah blah
option2::: blah blah blah

property name 2::
Description
Default value::: VALUE
option1::: blah blah blah
option2::: blah blah blah
{quote}

This renders correctly as:

{quote}*{{property name}}*
   Description
   *Default value* 
      VALUE
   *option1*
      blah blah blah
   *option2*
      blah blah blah 
{quote}

{quote}*property name 2*
   Description
   *Default value* 
     VALUE
{quote}
{quote}   *option1*
      blah blah blah
   *option2*
      blah blah blah
{quote}
author: jpechane

project_fields.Title: Remove hard line breaks throughout [DBZ-9675]

title: JDBC Sink Connector errors when "-infinity" provided for timestamptz column if not first row in batch
body:
## Bug report

**What Debezium connector do you use and what version?**

`debezium-connector-jdbc 3.4.1.Final`
`Issue seen while ingesting messages from both the 1.9.7 and 3.4.1 versions of debezium-connector-postgres`

---

**What is the connector configuration?**

```
{
      "connector.class": "io.debezium.connector.jdbc.JdbcSinkConnector",
      "connection.url": "jdbc:postgresql:hostname:5432/database",
      "connection.username": "user",
      "connection.password": "password",
      "errors.log.enable": "true",
      "errors.log.include.messages": "true",
      "errors.retry.timeout": "-1",
      "delete.enabled": "true",
      "insert.mode": "upsert",
      "primary.key.mode": "record_key",
      "quote.identifiers": "true",
      "schema.evolution": "basic",
      "tasks.max": "1",
      "topics": "tableTypes-upgrade,table-upgrade",
      "use.time.zone": "America/Chicago",
      "key.converter": "io.confluent.connect.avro.AvroConverter",
      "key.converter.schema.registry.url": "http://schema-registry",
      "value.converter": "io.confluent.connect.avro.AvroConverter",
      "value.converter.schema.registry.url": "http://schema-registry",
      "value.converter.use.latest.version": "true"
    }
```

---

**What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?**

`postgres 14.20 deployed in AWS RDS` for both source and target database

---

**What behavior do you expect?**

`When adding a timestamptz value of -infinity that it gets added to the target database even if the record is not the first entry in the batch`

---

**What behavior do you see?**

When adding a timestamptz value of "-infinity", it works fine if it is the only record in the batch or the first record in the batch and gets inserted into the target db as "-infinity". When adding it as the second record in a batch where the first record has a valid timestamp, instead the connector errors out with the following error.
`Caused by: org.postgresql.util.PSQLException: ERROR: column "DateTimeUtc" is of type timestamp with time zone but expression is of type character varying`

---

**Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?**

_Ideally, also verify with the latest Alpha/Beta/CR version._

`Yes on 3.4.1.Final`

---

**Do you have the connector logs, ideally from start till finish?**

_You might be asked later to provide [DEBUG/TRACE](https://debezium.io/documentation/reference/stable/operations/logging.html) level log._

Heavily redacted to remove extra columns, table names and any other identifying information 
[jdbcConnectError.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25691389/jdbcConnectError.log)

---

**How to reproduce the issue using our [tutorial](https://github.com/debezium/debezium-examples/tree/main/tutorial) deployment?**

1. Add a table with a column of type timestamptz to the postgres database
2. Add 2 rows to the table. First row has a normal timestamp. Second row has "-infinity".
3. Hook up table to kafka with debezium postgres connector
4. Hook up kafka topic to postgres database with debezium jdbc sink connector
5. Should see error if 2nd record is not the first in the batch

Problem is due to https://github.com/debezium/debezium/blob/main/debezium-connector-jdbc/src/main/java/io/debezium/connector/jdbc/field/JdbcFieldDescriptor.java#L35 where it caches the query binding for a field based on the first record read. The query binding is different if the first record read is a normal timestamp vs the special infinity values https://github.com/debezium/debezium/blob/main/debezium-connector-jdbc/src/main/java/io/debezium/connector/jdbc/dialect/postgres/ZonedTimestampType.java#L29. This makes batches that have infinity or -infinity as not the first record fail since the binding is cached to not do the cast.
author: braeden-soetaert-veriforce

project_fields.Title: JDBC Sink Connector errors when "-infinity" provided for timestamptz column if not first row in batch

title: Add OpenTelemetry support to server Docker image
body:
## Feature request or enhancement

_For feature requests or enhancements, please provide the following information:_

**Which use case/requirement will be addressed by the proposed feature?**

Add built-in OpenTelemetry (OTEL) support to the Debezium server Docker image, similar to what already exists in the connect base image:
https://github.com/debezium/container-images/blob/main/connect-base/3.5/Dockerfile#L6

This would allow users running Debezium Server to easily enable OTEL-based observability (traces, metrics, logs) without having to build and maintain a custom Docker image.

Today, OTEL instrumentation is available in the connect base image but not in the server base image. This creates an inconsistency between the two base images and increases operational complexity for teams that:

- Standardize on OpenTelemetry for observability
- Run Debezium Server (instead of Kafka Connect)

Adding OTEL support to the server base image would:

- Reduce the need for custom image extensions
- Simplify integration with OTEL collectors
- Improve out-of-the-box observability for Debezium Server deployments

---

**Implementation ideas (optional)**

- Mirror the approach used in the connect base image Dockerfile (e.g., adding the OTEL Java agent and related configuration).
- Ensure the solution remains optional and configurable so that users can enable or disable OTEL without impacting existing deployments.
author: tomflenner

project_fields.Title: Add OpenTelemetry support to server Docker image

title: Allow non-critical metadata columns to be removed from Logminer query
body:
**Which use case/requirement will be addressed by the proposed feature?**

Relates broadly to discussion [here](https://debezium.zulipchat.com/#narrow/channel/348250-community-oracle/topic/Oracle.20Heap.20Footprint)

As part of the heap footprint changes it was made possible to drop one of the metadata used in the oracle connector by setting log.mining.buffer.track.rs_id=false.  Based on the feedback I think it would be useful to add similar configurations for other metadata columns that are non-critical.  In particular, I have been looking at these:

- CLIENT_ID
- USERNAME
- COMMIT_TIMESTAMP
- START_TIMESTAMP 

The first three in particular I've found to consistently improve performance by a small amount (about 3%) when removed (even when there is no output for the query) and there is likely no real downside to having users able to toggle these on/off.
author: nathan-smit-1

project_fields.Title: Allow non-critical metadata columns to be removed from Logminer query

title: Optimize platform-conductor Docker image size
body:
## Problem

  The current `platform-conductor` Docker image is unnecessarily large at **~603 MB** due to inefficient layering in the Dockerfile build process.

  ### Root Cause

  The Dockerfile at `platform-conductor/3.5/Dockerfile` uses multiple sequential `RUN` commands that create duplicate layers:

  - Layer 5 (~145 MB): Downloads the tarball via `curl`
  - Layer 6 (~145 MB): Extracts tarball with `tar` and removes it with `rm`
  - Layer 7 (~145 MB): Changes ownership with `chown`

 Each `RUN` command creates a new layer in Docker. When a file is deleted in a later layer, it doesn't reduce the image size because the file still exists in the earlier layer. This causes the ~145 MB tarball to be effectively stored three times.

  ## Proposed Solution

  Use a **multi-stage build** approach:

  1. **Builder stage**: Download and extract the application tarball in a minimal image
  2. **Final stage**: Copy only the extracted application files from the builder to the runtime image (openjdk-21)

  This ensures the tarball exists only in the discarded builder stage and never makes it into the final image layers.

  ## Expected Benefits

  - **Size reduction:** From ~603 MB to ~300 MB (~50% reduction)
  - Faster image pulls and reduced registry storage costs
  - Cleaner layer structure with better caching
  - No functionality changes or breaking changes

## Files to Modify
- https://github.com/debezium/container-images/blob/main/platform-conductor/3.5/Dockerfile
- https://github.com/debezium/container-images/blob/main/platform-conductor/snapshot/Dockerfile
author: mfvitale

project_fields.Title: Optimize platform-conductor Docker image size

title: Improve CI / Jenkins Pipeline for Oracle 23 & 26
body:
Are discussion with @AlvarVG, there are a few things we need to tackle in this issue:

**Allow `test.sh` for TFT to work for all Oracle 23 versions.**
Right now the script the is limited to executing specific steps when the `$ORACLE_VERSION` is `23.3.0.0`. This prevents these steps from running for Oracle 26ai. The criteria should be changed to use `23*` for matching.

**Decouple docker build step with Oracle version profiles**
The Jenkins pipelines are presently designed to spin up Oracle images for Oracle 19, 21, and 23. The new `oracle-26` profile automatically spins up the image to be more helpful for the upstream contributor community, but this creates a rigid scenario for Jenkins where we would need to make significant changes.

For now, we've adjusted the pipeline to just use `oracle-23` profile, but ideally it should use `oracle-26` in the event we increase the driver version or other 26-specific things. So we should consider how to decouple this in a way where for Jenkins, we can explicitly have the Maven command not start a container when using the `oracle-26` profile, while also being easier for upstream contributors to run Oracle tests themselves before submitting pull requests.
author: Naros

project_fields.Title: Improve CI / Jenkins Pipeline for Oracle 23 & 26

The idea is to setup a job that runs nightly/weekly that for each maven repo uploaded artifacts, we compute the total size.  If the total size of the artifacts are a specific X% (70, 80, or 90) of the 1GB limit, we should raise an alert/email (or preferrably a zulip webhook) that notifies the team that we're approaching size concerns and that we should take a look.
