title: PostgreSQL pgvector sparsevec parsing fails on empty vectors (e.g., {}/5), incorrectly emitting null
body:
## Bug report

**What Debezium connector do you use and what version?**

PostgreSQL Connector (via Debezium Engine)
Version: 3.3.2.Final

---

**What is the connector configuration?**

Standard PostgreSQL connector configuration. No special transformations are currently applied to the vector column.

```json
{
  "connector.class": "io.debezium.connector.postgresql.PostgresConnector",
  "plugin.name": "pgoutput",
  "database.hostname": "<YOUR_DB_HOST>",
  "database.port": "5432",
  "database.user": "<YOUR_USER>",
  "database.password": "<YOUR_PASSWORD>",
  "database.dbname": "<YOUR_DB_NAME>",
  "database.server.name": "dbserver1",
  "table.include.list": "public.vector_test"
}

```

---

**What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?**

* Database: PostgreSQL 12, 13, 14, 15, 16, 17 (with `pgvector` extension enabled)

---

**What behavior do you expect?**

When PostgreSQL stores an empty `sparsevec` (a vector where all elements are zero), its native string representation is `{}/<dimensions>` (e.g., `{}/5`).

Debezium should recognize `{}/5` as a valid `pgvector` format. It should successfully parse the dimensions (5) and emit the corresponding `io.debezium.data.SparseVector` struct containing:

* `dimensions`: 5
* `vector`: `{}` (an empty map, since there are no non-zero elements).

---

**What behavior do you see?**

The Debezium `pgvector` parser assumes a sparse vector must contain at least one non-zero element. When it encounters `{}/5`, it treats the value as malformed, logs a warning, and attempts to default the field to `null`.

**Crucially, if the database column is defined as `NOT NULL`, this causes a fatal crash.** Because the Kafka Connect schema correctly marks the field as required, Debezium's attempt to insert a `null` throws a `DataException`, completely halting the connector. (If the column is nullable, it simply results in silent data loss downstream).

---

**Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?**

Yes, observed on 3.3.2.Final. The regex/parsing logic in `io.debezium.data.vector.Vectors` does not appear to handle the empty map syntax.

---

**Do you have the connector logs, ideally from start till finish?**

```log
2026-03-05 09:27:10,084 WARN  [io.debezium.data.vector.Vectors] (pool-17-thread-1) Cannot convert sparse vector {}/5, expected format is {i1:x,i2:y,i3:z,...}/dimensions

Failed to properly convert data value for 'public.vector_test.csparsevec' of type sparsevec: org.apache.kafka.connect.errors.DataException: Invalid value: null used for required field: "csparsevec", schema type: STRUCT

```

---

**How to reproduce the issue using our tutorial deployment?**

Run the following SQL commands on the source PostgreSQL database:

```sql
-- 1. Ensure extension is enabled
CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS vector;

-- 2. Create a table with a REQUIRED sparsevec column
CREATE TABLE vector_test (
    id serial PRIMARY KEY,
    cSparsevec sparsevec(5) NOT NULL
);

-- 3. Insert a valid empty sparse vector (representing all zeros across 5 dimensions)
INSERT INTO vector_test (cSparsevec) VALUES ('{}/5');

```

Start the Debezium connector. Observe that the connector logs a parsing warning and immediately crashes with a `DataException` because it attempts to pass a `null` into a required schema field.
author: mly-zju

project_fields.Title: PostgreSQL pgvector sparsevec parsing fails on empty vectors (e.g., {}/5), incorrectly emitting null

title: MariaDB failover restart can trigger "Encountered change event ... whose schema isn't known to this connector" despite non-empty schema history
body:
## Bug report

**What Debezium connector do you use and what version?**

`io.debezium.connector.mariadb.MariaDbConnector`

Tested with 3.2.0.Final and 3.3.2.Final (same behavior reproducible in both).

---

**What is the connector configuration?**

Main relevant config:

```json
{
  "connector.class": "io.debezium.connector.mariadb.MariaDbConnector",
  "tasks.max": "1",
  "topic.prefix": "db_prefix",

  "database.hostname": "maxscale",
  "database.port": "4006",
  "database.user": "dbz",
  "database.password": "****",
  "database.server.id": "100003",
  "database.ssl.mode": "disable",

  "database.include.list": "my_db",
  "table.include.list": "my_db.table1,my_db.table2,my_db.table3",

  "schema.history.internal.kafka.bootstrap.servers": "kafka:9092",
  "schema.history.internal.kafka.topic": "schema-changes.db_prefix",
  "schema.history.internal.store.only.captured.databases.ddl": "false",
  "schema.history.internal.store.only.captured.tables.ddl": "false",
  "schema.history.internal.skip.unparseable.ddl": "true",

  "snapshot.mode": "no_data",
  "snapshot.locking.mode": "none",
  "include.schema.changes": "true",
  "inconsistent.schema.handling.mode": "fail",

  "heartbeat.interval.ms": "60000",
  "connect.keep.alive.interval.ms": "10000",
  "read.only": "true",
  "errors.log.enable": "true"
}
```

Kafka Connect worker setting used during testing: `offset.flush.interval.ms=10000` (also tested default).

---

**What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?**

_E.g. on-premises, with a specific cloud provider, etc._

MariaDB 10.11.x, GTID replication, primary/replica failover controlled by MaxScale.

Deployment mode: containerized lab (docker compose), reproducing production-like topology (connector -> MaxScale -> MariaDB cluster).

---

**What behavior do you expect?**

After failover and connector task restart, connector should recover schema from schema history and continue streaming captured table events without manual recovery snapshot.

---

**What behavior do you see?**

Intermittently after failover + task restart, connector starts with warning:

`After applying the include/exclude list filters, no changes will be captured. Please check your configuration!`

Then it fails immediately on the first TABLE_MAP event for a captured table:

`Encountered change event ... for table my_db.table1 whose schema isn't known to this connector`

Example failure offset: `{file=mysql-bin.000012, pos=106676, gtids=0-303-1457, server_id=303, event=1}`

Schema history topic is not empty and contains DROP/CREATE for my_db.table1 before the failure timestamp.

---

**Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?**

_Ideally, also verify with the latest Alpha/Beta/CR version._

Yes. Reproduced on **3.2.0.Final** and **3.3.2.Final**.

Not yet verified on Alpha/Beta/CR.

---

**Do you have the connector logs, ideally from start till finish?**

_You might be asked later to provide [DEBUG/TRACE](https://debezium.io/documentation/reference/stable/operations/logging.html) level log._

Yes. Full startup-to-failure logs are available.

Representative sequence:
1. `Finished database schema history recovery of 8 change(s) ...`
2. `Starting streaming`
3. `WARN ... no changes will be captured`
4. `Connected to binlog ...`
5. `ERROR ... TABLE_MAP ... table1 ... schema isn't known ...`
6. Task transitions to **FAILED**.

---

**How to reproduce the issue using our [tutorial](https://github.com/debezium/debezium-examples/tree/main/tutorial) deployment?**

Repro steps in a MariaDB primary/replica + MaxScale setup:
1. Start connector with `snapshot.mode=no_data` and config above.
2. Keep continuous writes in captured table (`my_db.table1`).
3. Trigger master failover.
4. Wait until connector reconnects and streaming resumes.
5. Restart connector task (`POST /connectors/<name>/tasks/0/restart`).
6. In failing runs, startup shows warning (`no changes will be captured`) and fails immediately on first TABLE_MAP for table1.

`Workaround: run one cycle with snapshot.mode=recovery, then switch back to no_data; streaming resumes.`
author: disc

project_fields.Title: MariaDB failover restart can trigger "Encountered change event ... whose schema isn't known to this connector" despite non-empty schema history

title: [Debezium Operator Helm chart] Conflicting `app.kubernetes.io/managed-by` label
body:
`debezium-operator` helm charts hardcodes label `app.kubernetes.io/managed-by: quarkus` to resources deployed by chart.

It's problematic, as it goes against Helm best practices. https://docs.helm.sh/docs/chart_best_practices/labels/#standard-labels. It's expected for label `app.kubernetes.io/managed-by` to be set as `{{ .Release.Service }}`. Or just `Helm` if you intend to hardcode it, as this is what effectively a result of rendering the templates. Or just drop it, `helm install` can add it automatically.

With custom label value we see probalematic behaviour when kustomizing and diffing the chart, somewhere during the pipeline expected label `app.kubernetes.io/managed-by: Helm` appears and this triggers a perpetual diff between planned and actual state of labels.
author: z0rc

project_fields.Title: [Debezium Operator Helm chart] Conflicting `app.kubernetes.io/managed-by` label

title: Refactor duplicated validator logic for include/exclude filter properties
body:
Follow-up from the investigation in DBZ-3324.

### Background

During the investigation of DBZ-3324, I reviewed the validation logic related to include/exclude filter properties.

Although the original issue about duplicate warnings is no longer applicable (since the legacy `*_WHITELIST` and `*_BLACKLIST` properties have been removed), I observed that several validator methods still contain very similar validation logic.

### Problem

Multiple validator methods perform nearly identical validation checks for include/exclude configuration combinations, which results in duplicated code across the validation logic.

Additionally, the method `validateTableBlacklist()` appears to no longer be used.

### Proposed Improvement

Refactor the duplicated validator logic into a shared helper method that can be reused across the existing validators.

This would:

* Reduce code duplication
* Improve maintainability
* Simplify future updates to validation logic
author: Binayak490-cyber

project_fields.Title: Refactor duplicated validator logic for include/exclude filter properties

title: Unable to parse DML for CDC after initial snapshot on a table with ROW ARCHIVAL enabled
body:
## Bug report

**What Debezium connector do you use and what version?**

- **Connector:** Debezium Oracle connector on Kafka Connect
- **Version:** `3.4.2.Final`
- **Library:** `https://repo1.maven.org/maven2/io/debezium/debezium-connector-oracle/3.4.2.Final/debezium-connector-oracle-3.4.2.Final-plugin.tar.gz`

**Remark:** this ticket is a follow-up to #1650 that fixed the first issue on DDL parsing, but does not prevent the issue on DML parsing.

---

**What is the connector configuration?**

```json
{
        "connector.type": "source.debezium.oracle",
        "database.dbname": "mydatabase_name",
        "database.hostname": "mydatabase_hostname",
        "database.user": "mydatabase_user",
        "internal.kafka.bootstrap.servers": "my_kafka_brokers",
        "log.mining.archive.log.hours": "0",
        "log.mining.skip.missing.logs": "true",
        "schema.include.list": "MY_SCHEMA",
        "table.include.list": "MY_SCHEMA.MY_TABLE",
        "topic.prefix": "my-topic",
        "schema.history.internal.skip.unparseable.ddl": "true"
      }
```

**Remark:** the issue exists with `schema.history.internal.skip.unparseable.ddl=true` and without it, but behaves slightly differently.

---

**What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?**

The issue occurs with:
- 
- `Oracle 19` running on AWS EC2 RHeL in CDB/PDB
- `Oracle 19` running as AWS RDS

## DML issue

**Scenario**

- An Oracle table MY_SCHEMA.MY_TABLE is part of the include list for Debezium and `ROW ARCHIVAL` is already active on the table.
- Initial snapshot has been performed successfully
- Topic `schema-changes` show the DDL with `ROW ARCHIVAL` but does not see the corresponding column `ORA_ARCHIVE_STATE`.
- DML changes are initiated on the table (e.g., `INSERT` or `UPDATE`in the table)

---

**What behavior do you expect?**

It must be possible for Debezium to seamlessly capture changes on a table where `ROW ARCHIVAL` is enabled.

title: [Debezium Server] Support Exactly-Once Semantics (EOS) via Kafka Transactions for Kafka Sink
body:
## Feature request or enhancement

**Which use case/requirement will be addressed by the proposed feature?**

Hello guys,

Currently, when running Debezium Server standalone with the `kafka` sink and using Kafka for offset storage (`offset.storage=org.apache.kafka.connect.storage.KafkaOffsetBackingStore`), we are strictly limited to **At-Least-Once** delivery semantics. 

Under the hood, producing the CDC data records and flushing the offsets to the `debezium-offsets` topic happen independently. If the server crashes after sending a batch of data but before the background thread flushes the offsets (based on `offset.flush.interval.ms`), the server restarts, re-reads the old offset, and we end up with duplicate records in our destination topics. 

While downstream consumers can be built to be idempotent, this isn't always possible. Kafka Connect already solved this with Exactly-Once Semantics (EOS) for source connectors (KIP-618). I believe we really need a similar capability in Debezium Server to utilize Kafka's Transactional Producer, ensuring that both data records and source offsets are committed atomically in a single Kafka transaction.

---

**Implementation ideas (optional)**

I definitely need to investigate the codebase more and think through the edge cases, but here is my initial idea on how we could tackle this.

To achieve a single transaction, both data and offset records *must* be sent using the exact same `KafkaProducer` instance within the same `beginTransaction()` and `commitTransaction()` block. However, `DebeziumEngine` inherently decouples the `ChangeConsumer` (data routing) from the `OffsetBackingStore` (offset management).

**My initial thought:**
What if we unify these two roles when EOS is enabled? We could create a class (e.g., `KafkaExactlyOnceChangeConsumer`) that implements **both** the `ChangeConsumer` and `OffsetBackingStore` interfaces. 

1. When configured, Debezium Server instantiates this single object.
2. It injects this exact same instance into the `DebeziumEngine.Builder` for both `.notifying(...)` and `.offsetStore(...)`.
3. This object holds a single, transactional `KafkaProducer`.
4. In the `handleBatch` method, we start a transaction, send the data records, manually extract the latest offset from the batch, send the offset record to the offset topic using the *same* producer, and finally commit the transaction.
5. The default `flush()` method from the `OffsetBackingStore` interface would simply become a No-Op to bypass the engine's background flusher.

**How to expose this:**
We have two ways to introduce this to the users:
1. **A new config flag:** Something like `debezium.sink.kafka.exactly.once.enabled=true` which alters the behavior of the existing Kafka sink.
2. **A completely new Sink:** Introduce a new sink type entirely (e.g., `debezium.sink.type=kafka-eos`) so we don't mess with the logic and stability of the current, standard Kafka sink.

**Architectural Concerns (The dark side of this idea):**
I have to admit, from an Object-Oriented Programming (OOP) and Design Pattern perspective, this approach has some drawbacks:
*   **Violates Single Responsibility Principle (SRP):** We are forcing one class to handle data routing/batching *and* manage low-level offset state storage.
*   **High Coupling:** It feels a bit like a "God Object" anti-pattern or a hack around the engine's beautifully decoupled architecture just to share a producer context. 
*   It slightly misuses the engine API by making the standard offset flushing mechanism a No-Op.

Despite the OOP drawbacks, it seems like the least invasive way to get a shared transactional boundary without completely rewriting how `DebeziumEngine` handles state. 

I'll be happy to hear your feedback on this approach, and whether this kind of EOS support for Debezium Server has been discussed before.
author: samssh

project_fields.Title: [Debezium Server] Support Exactly-Once Semantics (EOS) via Kafka Transactions for Kafka Sink

title: Make mysql images with arm64/aarch64
body:
## Current behavior

Several mysql Docker images used by the integration tests in Debezium are **not compatible with ARM64/AArch64 architectures**.

When running the build on ARM-based systems (for example Apple Silicon), the build fails while attempting to start the test containers.

Example error:
`[ERROR] DOCKER> I/O Error [Unable to create container for [debezium/mysql-server-test-database] :
{"message":"image with reference debezium/mysql-server-test-database:latest was found but does not provide the specified platform (linux/arm64)"}]`


As a result, the integration tests cannot run successfully on ARM-based environments.

---

## Expected behavior

The build and integration tests should **run successfully on ARM64/AArch64 architectures** without requiring additional external configuration or manual platform overrides.

---

## Additional information

It may be worth investigating whether the build can be made architecture-aware .

One possible approach could be to use the `os-maven-plugin` to detect the host architecture during the build and expose normalized properties (e.g. `os.detected.arch`). These properties could then be used in the Docker build configuration to select or build images compatible with the detected architecture.
author: vsantonastaso

project_fields.Title: Make mysql images with arm64/aarch64

title: Snapshot pause signal is not saved to connector offset [DBZ-8384]
body:
Migrated from [DBZ-8384](https://issues.redhat.com/browse/DBZ-8384)

h1. Bug report
h2. What Debezium connector do you use and what version?

MySQL, 3.0.

Bug present in earlier versions as well.
h2. What behavior do you expect?

When the incremental snapshot pause signal is sent for a connector via Kafka, it should be saved to the connector offsets so that when the connector restarts it doesn't forget that the snapshots are paused. 

Furthermore, I (as the operator) should be able to pause snapshots even when they're not running. Effectively putting them on standby until I'm ready to run snapshots again.

The docs describing how to send the signal are misleading:

>The {{data}} field is ignored.

While the field is ignored, it still needs to be provided otherwise it causes a NullPointerException in the `KafkaSignalChannel` when it goes to `
data.toString()`, and the thread silently dies.
 
Finally, if the signal were properly saved, I would expect the `resume` signal to remove the flag that indicates that snapshots are paused.
h2. What behavior do you see?

Nothing is saved in the connector offsets to indicate that the snapshot is paused. Restarting a connector will restart any paused snapshots.  
h2. Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?

Yes
author: jpechane

project_fields.Title: Snapshot pause signal is not saved to connector offset [DBZ-8384]

title: Add support for PostgreSQL Domain [DBZ-961]
body:
Migrated from [DBZ-961](https://issues.redhat.com/browse/DBZ-961)

When a column is defined using a postgres DOMAIN the underlying type is not being outputted from either wal2json plugin or protobuff. Instead the domain name is given as the type, which causes Debezium to skip processing this field when include.unknown.datatypes is set as false.

Example:

Given schema:
{code:sql}
create domain sf_id as varchar(18);

create table payment
(
 id serial not null constraint payment_pkey primary key,
 salesforce_id sf_id,
);
{code}

Debezium will complain that sf_id is an unknown type, and filter out the column.

*Expected:* Debezium will treat this column as varchar(18)

Solution can come either from plugins side, to allow the option to produce the underlying type instead of domain name, or from postgresql-connector - to get domain to underlying type mapping from DB.

Feature request created for wal2json: https://github.com/eulerto/wal2json/issues/85
Google group thread: https://groups.google.com/forum/#!topic/debezium/6Kp7HMfWLPY
author: jpechane

project_fields.Title: Add support for PostgreSQL Domain [DBZ-961]

title: Oracle OLR adapter: BINARY_FLOAT whole-number values converted to null
body:
## Bug report

**What Debezium connector do you use and what version?**

Oracle connector, Debezium Server 3.0

---

**What is the connector configuration?**

```properties
debezium.source.connector.class=io.debezium.connector.oracle.OracleConnector
debezium.source.database.connection.adapter=olr
debezium.source.openlogreplicator.source=XE
debezium.source.openlogreplicator.host=olr
debezium.source.openlogreplicator.port=5000
debezium.source.database.hostname=oracle
debezium.source.database.port=1521
debezium.source.database.user=c##dbzuser
debezium.source.database.password=******
debezium.source.database.dbname=XE
debezium.source.database.pdb.name=XEPDB1
debezium.source.schema.include.list=OLR_TEST
debezium.source.snapshot.mode=no_data
```

---

**What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?**

Oracle XE 21c, on-premises (Docker container, `gvenzl/oracle-xe:21.3.0-slim-faststart`).

---

**What behavior do you expect?**

`BINARY_FLOAT` columns should contain the correct float value (e.g., `256.0`).

---

**What behavior do you see?**

`BINARY_FLOAT` columns are `null` in change events when the OLR JSON value is a whole number (e.g., `0`, `1`, `256`). Fractional values like `3.14` work correctly.

OLR sends correct values in its JSON payload (e.g., `{"COL_FLOAT": 256}`), but Debezium logs show:

```
[ERROR] Failed to properly convert data value for 'XEPDB1.OLR_TEST.TEST_NUMBERS.COL_FLOAT' of type BINARY_FLOAT
  DataException: Invalid Java object for schema with type FLOAT32: class java.lang.Double for field: "COL_FLOAT"
```

---

**Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?**

Yes. The test suite also acknowledges it via `@SkipWhenAdapterNameIs(value = AdapterName.OLR, reason = "BINARY_FLOAT not supported")` in `OraclePrimaryKeyLobReselectIT.java:100`.

---

**Do you have the connector logs, ideally from start till finish?**

Relevant log excerpt (Debezium Server 3.0, OLR adapter):

```
[ERROR] Failed to properly convert data value for 'XEPDB1.OLR_TEST.TEST_NUMBERS.COL_FLOAT' of type BINARY_FLOAT
  org.apache.kafka.connect.errors.DataException: Invalid Java object for schema with type FLOAT32: class java.lang.Double for field: "COL_FLOAT"
    at org.apache.kafka.connect.data.ConnectSchema.validateValue(ConnectSchema.java:246)
    at org.apache.kafka.connect.data.Struct.put(Struct.java:216)
    at io.debezium.relational.TableSchemaBuilder.lambda$createValueGenerator$5(TableSchemaBuilder.java:328)
    at io.debezium.relational.TableSchema.valueFromColumnData(TableSchema.java:141)
    at io.debezium.relational.RelationalChangeRecordEmitter.emitCreateRecord(RelationalChangeRecordEmitter.java:71)
```

This error occurs for every `BINARY_FLOAT` column where OLR sends a whole-number JSON value (e.g., `256` instead of `256.0`). Fractional values (e.g., `3.14`) do not trigger this error because Jackson deserializes them as `Double`, which is handled by `OracleValueConverters.convertFloat()`.

---

**How to reproduce the issue using our [tutorial](https://github.com/debezium/debezium-examples/tree/main/tutorial) deployment?**

1. Create a table with a BINARY_FLOAT column:
```sql
CREATE TABLE TEST_FLOAT (
    id        NUMBER PRIMARY KEY,
    col_float BINARY_FLOAT
);
ALTER TABLE TEST_FLOAT ADD SUPPLEMENTAL LOG DATA (ALL) COLUMNS;
INSERT INTO TEST_FLOAT VALUES (1, 256.0);
INSERT INTO TEST_FLOAT VALUES (2, 3.14);
COMMIT;
```

2. Run Debezium Oracle connector with `database.connection.adapter=olr`
3. Observe that `col_float` is `null` for row 1 (value `256` — whole number) but correct for row 2 (value `3.14` — fractional)
author: rophy

project_fields.Title: Oracle OLR adapter: BINARY_FLOAT whole-number values converted to null

title: Oracle OLR adapter: NUMBER values with >15 significant digits lose precision
body:
## Bug report

**What Debezium connector do you use and what version?**

Oracle connector, Debezium Server 3.0

---

**What is the connector configuration?**

```properties
debezium.source.connector.class=io.debezium.connector.oracle.OracleConnector
debezium.source.database.connection.adapter=olr
debezium.source.openlogreplicator.source=XE
debezium.source.openlogreplicator.host=olr
debezium.source.openlogreplicator.port=5000
debezium.source.database.hostname=oracle
debezium.source.database.port=1521
debezium.source.database.user=c##dbzuser
debezium.source.database.password=******
debezium.source.database.dbname=XE
debezium.source.database.pdb.name=XEPDB1
debezium.source.schema.include.list=OLR_TEST
debezium.source.snapshot.mode=no_data
```

---

**What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?**

Oracle XE 21c, on-premises (Docker container, `gvenzl/oracle-xe:21.3.0-slim-faststart`).

---

**What behavior do you expect?**

Oracle `NUMBER` values should be captured with full precision (up to 38 significant digits), matching the LogMiner adapter behavior.

For example, `NUMBER(20,10)` value `9999999999.9999999999` should produce Kafka Connect Decimal with unscaled value `99999999999999999999`.

---

**What behavior do you see?**

`NUMBER` values with more than ~15 significant digits are silently rounded. For example:

- Input: `9999999999.9999999999` (20 significant digits)
- Expected Kafka Connect Decimal (base64): `BWvHXi1jD///` (unscaled `99999999999999999999`)
- Actual Kafka Connect Decimal (base64): `BWvHXi1jEAAA` (unscaled `100000000000000000000`)

The LogMiner adapter produces the correct value. This is specific to the OLR adapter.

---

**Root cause**

`OlrNetworkClient` uses Jackson `ObjectMapper` with default settings to deserialize the OLR JSON payload. The [`Values`](https://github.com/debezium/debezium/blob/main/debezium-connector-oracle/src/main/java/io/debezium/connector/oracle/olr/client/payloads/Values.java#L28) class uses `@JsonAnySetter` with `Object` type, so Jackson applies its default heuristic and parses decimal JSON numbers as `Double` (~15-17 significant digits). Precision is lost before the value converter runs.

This is the same category of issue as [dbz#1679](https://github.com/debezium/dbz/issues/1679) — Jackson's type guessing produces an unexpected Java type for OLR column values. Other Oracle adapters (LogMiner, Xstream) receive values as already-typed Java objects from JDBC, so Jackson is not involved.

OLR itself outputs exact decimal values — its [`parseNumber()`](https://github.com/bersler/OpenLogReplicator/blob/master/src/builder/Builder.h#L422) decodes Oracle's BCD format byte-by-byte into exact decimal strings. The precision is lost only during Jackson deserialization.

---

**Proposed fix**

Enable `DeserializationFeature.USE_BIG_DECIMAL_FOR_FLOATS` on the `ObjectMapper` in `OlrNetworkClient`, so Jackson parses decimal JSON numbers as `BigDecimal` instead of `Double`. This aligns the OLR adapter with all other Debezium adapters, which deliver `BigDecimal` for high-precision numeric columns via JDBC.

Since this also changes BINARY_FLOAT/BINARY_DOUBLE parsing from `Double` to `BigDecimal`, add `BigDecimal` handling in `OpenLogReplicatorValueConverter.convertFloat()`/`convertDouble()` (similar to the `Integer`/`Long` handling in [PR #7146](https://github.com/debezium/debezium/pull/7146)).

---

**Steps to reproduce**

```sql
CREATE TABLE TEST_PRECISION (
    id          NUMBER PRIMARY KEY,
    col_number  NUMBER,
    col_decimal NUMBER(20,10)
);
ALTER TABLE TEST_PRECISION ADD SUPPLEMENTAL LOG DATA (ALL) COLUMNS;
INSERT INTO TEST_PRECISION VALUES (1, 99999999999999999999999999999999999999, 9999999999.9999999999);
COMMIT;
```

Compare OLR adapter output against LogMiner adapter output — `col_decimal` and `col_number` values will differ due to precision loss.
author: rophy

project_fields.Title: Oracle OLR adapter: NUMBER values with >15 significant digits lose precision

title: PgOutputMessageDecoder corrupts multi-byte UTF-8 table/column names during CDC streaming
body:
## Bug report

**What Debezium connector do you use and what version?**

PostgreSQL Connector (using Debezium Engine) 
Version: 3.3.2.Final

---

**What is the connector configuration?**

```
{
  "connector.class": "io.debezium.connector.postgresql.PostgresConnector",
  "plugin.name": "pgoutput",
  "database.hostname": "<DB_HOST>",
  "database.port": "5432",
  "database.user": "<USER>",
  "database.password": "<PASSWORD>",
  "database.dbname": "<DB_NAME>",
  "topic.prefix": "tutorial",
  "table.include.list": "\"テストスキーマ\".\"ユーザーテーブル\""
}

```

---

**What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?**

On-prem
Database: PostgreSQL 12, 13, 14, 15, 16, 17

---

**What behavior do you expect?**

Any multi-byte UTF-8 table and column names should be correctly decoded during CDC streaming, matching the identifiers captured during the snapshot phase.

---

**What behavior do you see?**

When a PostgreSQL table or column names contains multi-byte UTF-8 characters (e.g., Chinese, Japanese), the CDC streaming phase of the Debezium PostgreSQL connector fails to correctly decode these identifiers. The `readString()` method in `PgOutputMessageDecoder` produces corrupted strings for any identifier containing multi-byte UTF-8 characters. For example, the table name `test_表` (where `表` is encoded as 3 bytes: `0xE8 0xA1 0xA8` in UTF-8) would be decoded as 3 separate garbage characters instead of the single character `表`. Note that the initial snapshot phase works correctly because it uses standard JDBC `ResultSetMetaData` to correctly read the multi-byte table/column names.

The root cause is in the `readString()` method of `PgOutputMessageDecoder.java`, which reads bytes from the pgoutput protocol's `ByteBuffer` and casts each byte individually to `char` via `(char) b`. This works for single-byte ASCII characters but corrupts any multi-byte UTF-8 sequence, since each byte of a multi-byte character is independently cast to a separate `char`. I have applied the fix locally by properly encoding the complete byte stream rather than encoding each individual byte and it worked fine.

---

**Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?**

I have observed in 3.3.2.Final, though it should apply to pre-existing/later versions (as I don’t see a related fix in place in the release notes).

---

**Do you have the connector logs, ideally from start till finish?**

Below are the related log chunks illustrating the PgOutputMessageDecoder failing to properly encode the multi-byte column names.

```
2026-03-09T11:07:31,170 INFO   Postgres||streaming  Starting streaming   [io.debezium.pipeline.ChangeEventSourceCoordinator]

2026-03-09T11:07:31,170 INFO   Postgres||streaming  Retrieved latest position from stored offset 'LSN{0/20D80D0}'   [io.debezium.connector.postgresql.PostgresStreamingChangeEventSource]

2026-03-09T11:07:31,170 INFO   Postgres||streaming  Looking for WAL restart position for last commit LSN 'null' and last change LSN 'LSN{0/20D80D0}'   [io.debezium.connector.postgresql.connection.WalPositionLocator]

2026-03-09T11:07:31,170 INFO   Postgres||streaming  Initializing PgOutput logical decoder publication   [io.debezium.connector.postgresql.connection.PostgresReplicationConnection]

2026-03-09T11:07:31,181 INFO   Postgres||streaming  Obtained valid replication slot ReplicationSlot [active=false, latestFlushedLsn=LSN{0/20D80D0}, catalogXmin=773]   [io.debezium.connector.postgresql.connection.PostgresConnection]

2026-03-09T11:07:31,181 INFO   Postgres||streaming  Requested thread factory for component JdbcConnection, id = JdbcConnection named = jdbc-connection-close   [io.debezium.util.Threads]

2026-03-09T11:07:31,181 INFO   Postgres||streaming  Creating thread debezium-jdbcconnection-JdbcConnection-jdbc-connection-close   [io.debezium.util.Threads]

2026-03-09T11:07:31,182 INFO   ||  Connection gracefully closed   [io.debezium.jdbc.JdbcConnection]

2026-03-09T11:07:31,197 INFO   Postgres||streaming  Requested thread factory for component PostgresConnector, id = tutorial named = keep-alive   [io.debezium.util.Threads]

2026-03-09T11:07:31,197 INFO   Postgres||streaming  Creating thread debezium-postgresconnector-tutorial-keep-alive   [io.debezium.util.Threads]

2026-03-09T11:07:31,198 INFO   Postgres||streaming  Processing messages   [io.debezium.connector.postgresql.PostgresStreamingChangeEventSource]

2026-03-09T11:08:31,470 INFO   ||  No previous offsets found   [io.debezium.connector.common.BaseSourceTask]

2026-03-09T11:08:46,162 WARN   Postgres||streaming  Primary keys are not defined for table '￣ﾃﾦ￣ﾃﾼ￣ﾂﾶ￣ﾃﾼ￣ﾃﾆ￣ﾃﾼ￣ﾃﾖ￣ﾃﾫ', defaulting to unique indices   [io.debezium.connector.postgresql.connection.pgoutput.PgOutputMessageDecoder]

2026-03-09T11:08:46,165 WARN   Postgres||streaming  Column '￨ﾭﾘ￥ﾈﾥ￥ﾭﾐ' optionality could not be determined, defaulting to true   [io.debezium.connector.postgresql.connection.pgoutput.PgOutputMessageDecoder]

2026-03-09T11:08:46,166 WARN   Postgres||streaming  Column '￦ﾰﾏ￥ﾐﾍ' optionality could not be determined, defaulting to true   [io.debezium.connector.postgresql.connection.pgoutput.PgOutputMessageDecoder]

2026-03-09T11:08:46,168 WARN   Postgres||streaming  Column '￩ﾃﾽ￥ﾸﾂ￥ﾐﾍ' optionality could not be determined, defaulting to true   [io.debezium.connector.postgresql.connection.pgoutput.PgOutputMessageDecoder]

2026-03-09T11:08:46,168 WARN   Postgres||streaming  Column '￣ﾂﾹ￣ﾃﾆ￣ﾃﾼ￣ﾂ﾿￣ﾂﾹ_￰ﾟﾚﾀ' optionality could not be determined, defaulting to true   [io.debezium.connector.postgresql.connection.pgoutput.PgOutputMessageDecoder]
```

---

**How to reproduce the issue using our [tutorial](https://github.com/debezium/debezium-examples/tree/main/tutorial) deployment?**

Run the following SQL commands on the source PostgreSQL database:
```
-- 1. Create a schema with a multi-byte name (Katakana for 'test_schema')
CREATE SCHEMA "テストスキーマ";

-- 2. Create a table with multi-byte names and columns
-- We include an emoji to test the 4-byte UTF-8 extreme alongside 3-byte Japanese characters.
CREATE TABLE "テストスキーマ"."ユーザーテーブル" (
    "識別子" serial PRIMARY KEY,          -- Multi-byte column (Identifier)
    "氏名" varchar(100) NOT NULL,        -- Multi-byte column (Full Name)
    "都市名" varchar(50),                -- Multi-byte column (City Name)
    "ステータス_🚀" varchar(20)          -- Multi-byte column with Emoji (Status)
);

INSERT INTO "テストスキーマ"."ユーザーテーブル" ("氏名", "都市名", "ステータス_🚀") VALUES
    ('山田太郎', '東京', 'アクティブ ✅'),          -- Fully multi-byte (Taro Yamada, Tokyo, Active)
    ('佐藤花子', '大阪', '非アクティブ ❌'),        -- Fully multi-byte (Hanako Sato, Osaka, Inactive)
    ('John Doe', '京都', '保留中 ⏳');            -- Mixed ASCII and multi-byte (Kyoto, Pending)
```

title: PostgreSQL: Connector startup is very slow with many custom types and network latency
body:
PostgreSQL: Connector startup is very slow with many custom types and network latency

## Bug report

**What Debezium connector do you use and what version?**

3.4.2

---

**What is the connector configuration?**

Please refer to the configuration used in the reproduction procedure below.

---

**What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?**

on-premise

**What behavior do you expect?**

Debezium should start within a few seconds and begin replication.

---

**What behavior do you see?**

Starting Debezium takes more than 30 minutes.
More specifically, the processing of the type registry takes a very long time.

The following log messages appear repeatedly:

```
[2026-03-09 18:58:53,340] TRACE [test-connector|task-0] Type OID '13676' not cached, attempting to lookup from database. (io.debezium.connector.postgresql.TypeRegistry:453)
[2026-03-09 18:58:53,343] TRACE [test-connector|task-0] Type OID '13679' not cached, attempting to lookup from database. (io.debezium.connector.postgresql.TypeRegistry:453)
[2026-03-09 18:58:53,344] TRACE [test-connector|task-0] Type OID '13681' not cached, attempting to lookup from database. (io.debezium.connector.postgresql.TypeRegistry:453)
```

---

**Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?**

Yes. I have confirmed that the same problem occurs with the current main branch HEAD.

---

**Do you have the connector logs, ideally from start till finish?**

Yes. I believe the same logs can be obtained by following the reproduction steps below. Please let me know if you need the actual log files.

---

**Cause**

Based on my investigation, the cause appears to be the following.

The assumptions are:

1) A very large number of custom types exist, especially array types (e.g., ~30,000 or more).

2) There is network latency between Debezium and PostgreSQL (for example, 20 ms between executing a SELECT 1 and receiving the result, even when the connection is already established).

When the [SQL_TYPES](https://github.com/debezium/debezium/blob/22f42e7763d62b1d6adef6da05b75db2186fd951/debezium-connector-postgres/src/main/java/io/debezium/connector/postgresql/TypeRegistry.java#L73) query is executed, the results are typically returned in the order:

```
 base type -> array type
```

For example, if we create custom types like newtype1, newtype2, the results of SQL_TYPES are normally returned in this order (Note that in pg_type array types start with _):

```
  newtype1
  _newtype1
  newtype2
  _newtype2
```

However, since SQL_TYPES does not specify ORDER BY, the result order is not guaranteed.

In our environment, likely due to the large number of rows in pg_type, the execution plan used a MERGE JOIN, and the results were sorted by OID.
In PostgreSQL, the OID of an array type seems to be smaller than the OID of its base type. As a result, the query returned array types first:

```
  _newtype1
  newtype1
  _newtype2
  newtype2
```

Because of this, Debezium attempts to register the array type first.
However, since the base type has not yet been registered, [resolveUnknownType()](https://github.com/debezium/debezium/blob/22f42e7763d62b1d6adef6da05b75db2186fd951/debezium-connector-postgres/src/main/java/io/debezium/connector/postgresql/TypeRegistry.java#L205) is called.

resolveUnknownType() queries PostgreSQL using [SQL_OID_LOOKUP](https://github.com/debezium/debezium/blob/22f42e7763d62b1d6adef6da05b75db2186fd951/debezium-connector-postgres/src/main/java/io/debezium/connector/postgresql/TypeRegistry.java#L81), which looks up a type by OID.
This SQL is executed once for each array type.

Under assumption 1), the number of executions becomes very large, and combined with the network latency described in 2), each query takes longer. As a result, the connector takes a very long time to start.


**How to reproduce the issue using our [tutorial](https://github.com/debezium/debezium-examples/tree/main/tutorial) deployment?**

The following steps reproduce a situation where MERGE JOIN is used for the SQL_TYPES execution plan, causing many SQL_OID_LOOKUP queries to be executed.

This procedure does not introduce network latency or a large number of custom types, so the connector will still start quickly. However, I think this is sufficient to demonstrate where the problem occurs.


```
postgres=# alter system set enable_seqscan to off;
postgres=# alter system set enable_hashjoin to off;
postgres=# alter system set log_statement TO 'all';
postgres=# select pg_reload_conf();
postgres=# create publication p1;
```

Connector configuration:

```
% cat docker-min-no-table.json
{
  "name": "test-connector",
  "config": {
    "connector.class": "io.debezium.connector.postgresql.PostgresConnector",
    "database.hostname": "postgres",
    "database.port": "5432",
    "database.user": "postgres",
    "database.password": "postgres",
    "database.dbname": "postgres",
    "topic.prefix": "example",
    "slot.name": "example_slot",
    "plugin.name": "pgoutput",
    "publication.autocreate.mode": "disabled",
    "publication.name": "p1"
  }
}
```

Create the connector:

```
% curl -i -X POST \
  -H "Accept:application/json" \
  -H "Content-Type:application/json" \
  http://localhost:8083/connectors/ \
  -d @docker-min-no-table.json
```

From the PostgreSQL logs, you can see that SQL_OID_LOOKUP queries are executed many times:

```
2026-03-09 11:52:20.136 UTC [54] LOG:  execute S_1: SELECT t.oid AS oid, ...
2026-03-09 11:52:20.136 UTC [54] DETAIL:  Parameters: $1 = '16823'
2026-03-09 11:52:20.136 UTC [54] LOG:  execute S_1: SELECT t.oid AS oid, ...
2026-03-09 11:52:20.136 UTC [54] DETAIL:  Parameters: $1 = '17115'
```
---

**Related discussion**

A related problem involving a large number of types was also discussed in #1400 .

That discussion focuses on problems when types with the same name exist in different schemas.
However, the reporter also mentioned that *"the connector gets stuck during startup"*.

It is possible that the same behavior described in this issue was also occurring there.

From what I observed, the message *"the connector gets stuck during startup"* itself does not necessarily indicate a special delay.

---




Issuing a large number of `SQL_OID_LOOKUP` queries in this situation seems unnecessary.
I am planning to submit a PR to improve this problem.
author: atorik

title: Quarkus Extension support for Debezium Server configuration conventions
body:
## Context

As part of the re‑engineering of Debezium Server for native execution, it is necessary to make the Quarkus extension compatible with the configuration conventions used by Debezium Server. In particular, the Quarkus extension currently uses the prefix `quarkus.debezium.*`, while Debezium Server follows the canonical `debezium.*` convention.
author: kmos

project_fields.Title: Quarkus Extension support for Debezium Server configuration conventions

title: Add runnable Python example demonstrating Debezium Connect-mode CDC event extraction
body:
## Summary

This issue tracks adding a runnable Python example demonstrating how Debezium CDC events can be consumed using **Connect mode (EngineFormat.CONNECT)** without JSON serialization/deserialization.

Most Python integrations with Debezium currently rely on JSON serialization of events. This example demonstrates consuming Debezium events directly from Kafka Connect structures.

This can help developers building Python integrations for AI systems, data pipelines, or frameworks such as LangChain/LangGraph.

## Planned Example

The PR will include a runnable example that demonstrates:
- Running Debezium in Connect mode
- Receiving raw `SourceRecord` objects in Python
- Converting the event structure into Python dictionaries
- Demonstrating validated CDC event models

The example will include instructions for running it locally.

## Context

This work follows the discussion in the Debezium community chat:

[#community-gsoc > Kodukulla - PyDebeziumAI](https://debezium.zulipchat.com/#narrow/channel/573881-community-gsoc/topic/Kodukulla.20-.20PyDebeziumAI/with/577819893)
author: KMohnishM

project_fields.Title: Add runnable Python example demonstrating Debezium Connect-mode CDC event extraction

title: Deprecate "redo_log_catalog" Oracle mining strategy
body:
**Maintenance burden**: Supporting multiple mining modes requires every change to be fully tested. This isn't always the case, given the complexities that redo log catalog implies with the volume of archive logs created over the course of the test suite.

**Behavioral differences**: The mode relies on Oracle's redo log for catalog/dictionary information rather than the online catalog. This can lead to subtle differences and nuances that require performing specific tasks, such as a data dictionary build step, at carefully selected points in the mining process. This adds a level of complexity that differs from the `online_catalog` mode, making introducing code changes unnecessarily complex and often completely different because of how the modes operate.

**Performance**: Using the redo log as the catalog source forces Oracle to do additional work to reconstruct the dictionary state from the redo log stream. This can increase the session startup time, memory consumption, and increase the frequency and size of archive logs.

**Testing**: Given that we're already only testing this mode in limited capacity and can't run the entire test suite with the mode turned on, this creates the potential for behavior gaps, undiagnosed regressions, etc.

So, deprecating this in Debezium 3.x and removing this mining mode, most likely in Debezium 4.0, reduces the connector's overall behavior footprint, removes a constant pain point with multi-connector deployment, and eliminates performance, behavior, and testing concerns mentioned above.
author: Naros

project_fields.Title: Deprecate "redo_log_catalog" Oracle mining strategy

title: Add PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md to guide contributors on DCO sign-off and issue linking
body:
Currently contributors may encounter CI failures due to missing DCO sign-offs
or incorrect issue linking syntax when submitting pull requests.

During a recent PR discussion it was suggested that adding a
.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md could help guide contributors before
submission.

The template could include reminders to:

• Sign all commits using the -s flag to satisfy the DCO check
• Use the correct "Fixes debezium/dbz#<issue-number>" syntax
• Ensure commits follow the project's contribution guidelines

Using HTML comments (<!-- -->) would allow the instructions to be visible
in the PR editor but not appear in the final submitted PR description.

This change would help reduce common CI failures and improve the
contribution workflow.

Context: discussion in PR debezium/debezium#7161
author: div-dev123

project_fields.Title: Add PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md to guide contributors on DCO sign-off and issue linking

title: add net_write_timeout and net_read_timeout configuration options
body:
## Feature request or enhancement

_For feature requests or enhancements, please provide the following information:_

**Which use case/requirement will be addressed by the proposed feature?**

When consuming binlog events with BinaryLogClient, if the event processing in the EventListener is slow (e.g., due to large data volume, complex business logic, or downstream system latency), the MySQL server's net_write_timeout/net_read_timeout (default 60s) may expire before the client finishes consuming data from the TCP buffer. This causes the server to close the binlog dump connection, resulting in an unexpected EOFException on the client side.

---

**Implementation ideas (optional)**

add net_write_timeout and net_read_timeout configuration options
author: Hisoka-X

project_fields.Title: add net_write_timeout and net_read_timeout configuration options

title: Support nested field creation into payload in SMTs
body:
## Feature request or enhancement

STMs that support nested fields to bring some value into the payload does not support the creation of new fields. (Initial discussion https://github.com/debezium/dbz/issues/1669)

Taking in example the `HeaderToValue` SMT

```json
"transforms.moveHeadersToValue.type":"io.debezium.transforms.HeaderToValue",
"transforms.moveHeadersToValue.headers":"some_header",
"transforms.moveHeadersToValue.fields":"from_header.some_header",
"transforms.moveHeadersToValue.operation":"copy",
```

If the field `from_header` does not exists in the payload with https://github.com/debezium/debezium/pull/7177 it throws an error. 
We want instead support the creation of this new field.

Starting SMT could be `HeaderToValue` but other SMTs should already using a common utility `io.debezium.transforms.ConnectRecordUtil#makeNewSchema` so a check should be also done on other SMTs.
author: mfvitale

project_fields.Title: Support nested field creation into payload in SMTs

title: Fix CI test issue in `MongoDbConnectorDatabaseRestrictedIT#shouldFailInGuardRailValidationWithoutPermissions`
body:
The fix for #425 in https://github.com/debezium/debezium/pull/7144 introduced a CI test failure in `io.debezium.connector.mongodb.MongoDbConnectorDatabaseRestrictedIT#shouldFailInGuardRailValidationWithoutPermissions` because the Guardrail check relied on the invalid user/pass combination triggering the Guardrail previously which is now already catched in `MongoDbConnector#validate` (see PR #7144).

I am not sure if with this added check in `#validate`  now makes this test obsolete or what is the best way to test this scenario?

On a quick first look it seems there is no code to actually validate authentication with Guardrail properties. So it already relied on a failing connection (in `Connector#start`). I know what this test wants to express, but it is hard to construct a scenario like this now with the new check in `Connector#validate`. Wdyt?

FYI @gmarav05
author: rk3rn3r

project_fields.Title: Fix CI test issue in `MongoDbConnectorDatabaseRestrictedIT#shouldFailInGuardRailValidationWithoutPermissions`

**What Debezium connector do you use and what version?**

Informix

---

**What is the connector configuration?**

```{
  "name":"inventory-connector-informix1773415451967",
  "config":{

    "database.user":"informix",

    "predicates.TopicPredicate.pattern":"inventory_connector_informix1773415451967.*\\.customers",
    "task.max":"1",
    "transforms.route.language":"jsr223.groovy",
    "transforms":"route,unwrap",
    "schema.history.internal.kafka.bootstrap.servers":"tc-TFkU5NvH:9092",
    "database.port":"9088",
    "transforms.route.type":"io.debezium.transforms.ContentBasedRouter",
    "predicates.TopicPredicate.type":"org.apache.kafka.connect.transforms.predicates.TopicNameMatches",
    "topic.prefix":"inventory_connector_informix1773415451967",
    "transforms.route.topic.expression":"value.op == 'u' ? 'inventory_connector_informix1773415451967.u.customers' : null",
    "predicates":"TopicPredicate",
    "schema.history.internal.kafka.topic":"schema-changes.inventory",
    "database.hostname":"tc-H6nqdQkK",
    "database.password":"in4mix",
    "transforms.unwrap.drop.tombstones":"false",
    "name":"inventory-connector-informix1773415451967",
    "transforms.unwrap.add.fields":"table,lsn",
    "transforms.unwrap.type":"io.debezium.transforms.ExtractNewRecordState"
  },
  "tasks":[
    {
      "connector":"inventory-connector-informix1773415451967",
      "task":0
    }
  ],
  "type":"source"
}
```

---

**What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?**

Running on docker containers.
quay.io/rh_integration/dbz-informix:14 

---

**What behavior do you expect?**

Whenever I run a DELETE against kafka connect i expect that the connector grafecully gets deleted/undeployed and i would like to be able to spin the same connector just right after. 

---

**What behavior do you see?**

When I run the DELETE request I do see in the kafka connect logs that the connector is not being properly closed, and left some stuff that does not allow to re-create the same connector (with another transformation).

Logs like this one:

```
WARN  [task-thread-inventory-connector-informix1773412709131-0:ChangeEventSourceCoordinator@392] - Coordinator didn't stop in the expected time, shutting down executor now
ERROR [StartAndStopExecutor-connect-172.19.0.3:8083-4:Worker@1074] - Graceful stop of task inventory-connector-informix1773412709131-0 failed.
WARN  [task-thread-inventory-connector-informix1773412709131-0:JmxUtils@55] - Unable to register metrics as an old set with the same name: 'debezium.informix_server:type=connector-metrics,context=schema-history,server=inventory_connector_informix1773412709131' exists, retrying in PT5S (attempt 1 out of 12)
```

---

**Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?**

This has been tested with version 3.4.1.CR1. 

---

**Do you have the connector logs, ideally from start till finish?**

_You might be asked later to provide [DEBUG/TRACE](https://debezium.io/documentation/reference/stable/operations/logging.html) level log._

Debezium test suite logs + kafka connect logs are present in this file (only the affected time)

[informix-report.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25975231/informix-report.log)

---

**How to reproduce the issue using our [tutorial](https://github.com/debezium/debezium-examples/tree/main/tutorial) deployment?**

This is not coming from the tutorial, it is coming from running the test suite you have in place for docker.

title: Remainder review comments on Ingres Connector initial code drop
body:
Per request when we merged https://github.com/debezium/debezium-connector-ingres/pull/3#top, here are the remaining review comments to explore and resolve

1. https://github.com/debezium/debezium-connector-ingres/pull/3#discussion_r2698537645 Here it's mentioned that there is a much larger list of options to pull from. Is there a list to reference? Informix has a couple added options, not all of them looked applicable to Ingres.
2.  https://github.com/debezium/debezium-connector-ingres/pull/3#discussion_r2708073188 A question of do we need to handle RELATION records. After looking into this, yes, Ingres should be able to send them mid-transaction so that needs to be addressed.
3. https://github.com/debezium/debezium-connector-ingres/pull/3#discussion_r2708087517 asking about the cache comment. Ingres does work as Informix works, so we have to cache records until the end of the transaction.
author: brian-hughes

title: Fix MongoDB connector crash loop when snapshot is interrupted
body:
## Bug report

**What Debezium connector do you use and what version?**
MongoDb connector - 3.4.0.Final

---

**What is the connector configuration?**

`<Your answer>`

---

**What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?**

_E.g. on-premises, with a specific cloud provider, etc._

`<Your answer>`

---

**What behavior do you expect?**
When connector is stopped mid-snapshot. When connector is restarted, it fails.

`<Your answer>`
Bug 1:
MongoDbOffsetContext.Loader.load() did not call sourceInfo.startInitialSnapshot() when deserializing an offset with initsync=true. This caused isInitialSnapshotRunning() to return false, making the connector attempt resume token validation instead of recognizing an incomplete snapshot. Since the position was never loaded, the resume token is null, isValidResumeToken(null) returns false without contacting MongoDB, and the connector enters a permanent crash loop with a misleading 'offset no longer available' error.

Bug 2: 
The error-reporting path in MongoDbConnectorTask.validate() calls offset.getSourceInfo() which invokes MongoDbSourceInfoStructMaker.struct(). When collectionId is null (as it is for an incomplete snapshot offset),
Kafka Connect throws DataException on the required 'collection' field. This crash in the error path masked the real offset validation error.

---

**What behavior do you see?**

`<Your answer>`

---

**Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?**

_Ideally, also verify with the latest Alpha/Beta/CR version._

`<Your answer>`

---

**Do you have the connector logs, ideally from start till finish?**

_You might be asked later to provide [DEBUG/TRACE](https://debezium.io/documentation/reference/stable/operations/logging.html) level log._

`<Your answer>`
Last recorded offset is no longer available on the server.

WorkerSourceTask{id=my-connector-0} Task threw an uncaught and unrecoverable exception. Task is being killed and will not recover until manually restarted Exception:org.apache.kafka.connect.errors.DataException: Invalid value: null used for required field: "collection", schema type: STRING
	at org.apache.kafka.connect.data.ConnectSchema.validateValue(ConnectSchema.java:220)
	at org.apache.kafka.connect.data.Struct.put(Struct.java:216)
	at org.apache.kafka.connect.data.Struct.put(Struct.java:203)
	at io.debezium.connector.mongodb.MongoDbSourceInfoStructMaker.struct(MongoDbSourceInfoStructMaker.java:40)
	at io.debezium.connector.mongodb.MongoDbSourceInfoStructMaker.struct(MongoDbSourceInfoStructMaker.java:14)
	at io.debezium.connector.AbstractSourceInfo.struct(AbstractSourceInfo.java:79)
	at io.debezium.pipeline.CommonOffsetContext.getSourceInfo(CommonOffsetContext.java:58)
	at io.debezium.connector.mongodb.MongoDbConnectorTask.validate(MongoDbConnectorTask.java:345)
	at io.debezium.connector.mongodb.MongoDbConnectorTask.start(MongoDbConnectorTask.java:156)
	at io.debezium.connector.common.BaseSourceTask.start(BaseSourceTask.java:267)
	at org.apache.kafka.connect.runtime.AbstractWorkerSourceTask.initializeAndStart(AbstractWorkerSourceTask.java:280)
	at org.apache.kafka.connect.runtime.WorkerTask.doRun(WorkerTask.java:202)
	at org.apache.kafka.connect.runtime.WorkerTask.run(WorkerTask.java:259)
	at org.apache.kafka.connect.runtime.AbstractWorkerSourceTask.run(AbstractWorkerSourceTask.java:77)
	at org.apache.kafka.connect.runtime.isolation.Plugins.lambda$withClassLoader$1(Plugins.java:236)

title: Add explicit heartbeat.topic.name configuration to allow shared heartbeat topic across connector
body:
## Feature request or enhancement

**Which use case/requirement will be addressed by the proposed feature?**

When running a large number of Debezium source connectors (e.g., 300+), each connector creates its own heartbeat topic based on the pattern {topic.heartbeat.prefix}.{topic.prefix}. This results in
  hundreds of single-partition Kafka topics that serve no differentiated purpose, since the heartbeat topic is purely write-only — the source connector never reads from it.

  Currently, there is no configuration to specify an explicit, full heartbeat topic name. The only available option is topic.heartbeat.prefix, which merely changes the prefix prepended to topic.prefix.

 To consolidate heartbeat events into a single shared topic, users must either implement a custom `SchemaTopicNamingStrategy` (overriding heartbeatTopic()) or use a Kafka transformation like RegexRouter to
  reroute events — both of which add unnecessary complexity for a straightforward use case.
  The MongoDB Kafka Connector already supports this via a `heartbeat.topic.name`
  (​https://www.mongodb.com/docs/kafka-connector/current/source-connector/configuration-properties/all-properties/#std-label-source-configuration-all-properties​) configuration property, providing a simpler
  user experience.

---

**Implementation ideas (optional)**

Introduce a new optional configuration property (e.g., heartbeat.topic.name) that allows users to specify the full heartbeat topic name explicitly.
  • Default behavior (backward-compatible): If the property is empty or unset, fall back to the existing behavior of {topic.heartbeat.prefix}.{topic.prefix}.
  • When specified: Use the provided value as the exact heartbeat topic name, bypassing the prefix-based naming.

  This would be a minimal, non-breaking change that gives users direct control over heartbeat topic naming without requiring custom strategies or transformations.
author: gaurav7261

project_fields.Title: Add explicit heartbeat.topic.name configuration to allow shared heartbeat topic across connector

title: Allow to run specific MySQL ITs with a given database [DBZ-4831]
body:
Migrated from [DBZ-4831](https://issues.redhat.com/browse/DBZ-4831)

Currently it is not possible to run a single MySQL IT for a given database config (e.g. with SSL, GTIDs, etc.):

{code}
mvn verify -pl :debezium-connector-mysql -Dit.test=MySqlUnsignedIntegerIT -Pssl 
-DskipTests=true
{code}

The reason being that the database configuration (container image start) is extracted to separate Maven build profiles, but the actual test execution configuration (system properties e.g. to enable SSL) is done in multiple Failsafe plug-in executions within the "assembly" profile.

To do:

* Move those Failsafe executions into the corresponding profiles, i.e. one will have a matching set-up of started container and Failsafe config when activating a specific profile, allowing the mvn invocation above to succeed
* On CI, have a job (or adjust an existing one) which builds with all the profiles enabled, so to keep testing with all the different DB set-ups, as this won't be the case any longer when solely enabling the "assembly" profile

title: Add JMH microbenchmarks for CockroachDB connector
body:
## Feature request

### Which use case/requirement will be addressed by the proposed feature?

The CockroachDB connector lacks performance benchmarks to measure and track the throughput of critical code paths, detect regressions across releases, and validate performance characteristics.

### A brief description of the proposed feature

Add JMH-based microbenchmarks following the `debezium-microbenchmark` module pattern.

**Benchmarks:**

1. **Changefeed JSON event parsing** -- enriched JSON parsing throughput (hot path on every CDC event)
2. **Offset context serialization/deserialization** -- `Loader.load()` including `IncrementalSnapshotContext` restoration
3. **Schema refresh / evolution detection** -- table schema refresh from `information_schema`
4. **End-to-end streaming throughput** -- JMH-orchestrated integration benchmark using Testcontainers with configurable row counts via `@Param`

**Implementation:**

- Add `jmh-core`, `jmh-generator-annprocess` dependencies (versions from `debezium-parent`)
- Add `maven-shade-plugin` to produce standalone benchmark jar
- Benchmark classes under `src/main/java/io/debezium/performance/cockroachdb/`
- `@Setup(Level.Trial)` for container lifecycle, `@Benchmark` for measured regions

### References

- `debezium-microbenchmark`: https://github.com/debezium/debezium/tree/main/debezium-microbenchmark
- CockroachDB connector: https://github.com/debezium/debezium-connector-cockroachdb
author: viragtripathi

project_fields.Title: Add JMH microbenchmarks for CockroachDB connector

title: Cache parsed schemas to avoid per-event SchemaBuilder/Struct rebuild
body:
## Feature request

### Which use case/requirement will be addressed by the proposed feature?

JMH benchmarks (debezium/dbz#1711) show the full changefeed parsing pipeline (JSON deserialization + SchemaBuilder + Struct conversion) costs 3-4x raw Jackson `readTree` alone. For a medium-sized event (8 fields), raw JSON parsing takes ~1.3 us while the full pipeline takes ~4.2 us. The overhead comes from rebuilding Kafka Connect `Schema` and `Struct` objects on every event, even when the table schema has not changed.

### A brief description of the proposed feature

Cache the parsed `Schema` objects per table and reuse them across events when the schema has not changed. The existing `hasSchemaChanged()` detection (which costs only ~150 ns for a 15-column table) already determines whether a schema change occurred, so the cache invalidation signal is already in place.

**Benchmark data (JMH, 2 forks, 5 warmup + 5 measurement iterations):**

```
Benchmark                                               (payloadSize)  Mode  Cnt  Score   Error  Units
ChangefeedJsonParsingBenchmark.jsonDeserializationOnly          small  avgt   10  0.266 ± 0.005  us/op
ChangefeedJsonParsingBenchmark.jsonDeserializationOnly         medium  avgt   10  1.335 ± 0.011  us/op
ChangefeedJsonParsingBenchmark.parseEnrichedChangefeedEvent     small  avgt   10  1.104 ± 0.015  us/op
ChangefeedJsonParsingBenchmark.parseEnrichedChangefeedEvent    medium  avgt   10  4.191 ± 0.070  us/op
```

Caching would bring the full pipeline closer to the raw JSON cost for the common case (no schema change).

### References

- Benchmark issue: https://github.com/debezium/dbz/issues/1711
- CockroachDB connector: https://github.com/debezium/debezium-connector-cockroachdb
author: viragtripathi

project_fields.Title: Cache parsed schemas to avoid per-event SchemaBuilder/Struct rebuild

title: Add configurable way to scale the Oracle LogMiner batch size window.
body:
## Feature request or enhancement

### Motivation

In Debezium 1.x, 2.x, and earlier versions of Debezium 3.x, the connector used a mining window algorithm that, when it had fallen significantly behind, would use the current database write position and perform a single large pass over the archive and redo logs to catch up. However, this proved problematic in environments that were handling massive data loads or had a high volume of activity, as the connector could never catch up. 

As a solution in Debezium 3.2, we adjusted the mining logic so that the batch window scales linearly rather than with a single large jump. This algorithm works by always extending the batch window size `log.mining.batch.size.max`. For customers with a high volume of activity, this approach often worked better and helped maintain sustained ingestion of changes, but there were a few issues.

## Implementation

The idea is to introduce a new `log.mining.batch.size.window.scale` option that gives the user control over exactly how the connector scales the mining window when it falls behind and reaches `log.mining.batch.size.max`. The window scale can be set to one of three values

**`linear`**
This will be the default mode, and is the current behavior in Debezium 3.4 and 3.5. It works by checking if the batch size needs to be extended when we continue to remain a `log.mining.batch.size`, and takes the previous value and adds `log.mining.batch.size.max` on each step, growing the mining window's maximum range by a linear scale.

**`current_write`**
This is the old behavior from Debezium 1.x, 2.x, and early 3.x days. It works by determining if the connector needs to make a large leap to catch up, potentially enqueuing dozens of archive logs at once. For systems with small bursts of activity, this allows them to use the legacy behavior, which may be faster on smaller systems than `linear`.

**`exponential`**
This is a new mode that works almost identically to `linear`; however, the increment occurs exponentially. This provides a stable middle ground between the two extremes, which aligns with the `linear` approach during low-activity periods and trends toward `current_write` during high-activity periods.
author: Naros

project_fields.Title: Add configurable way to scale the Oracle LogMiner batch size window.

title: Integrate debezium-connector-ingres in Java Quality Outreach, PR and Push GitHub Workflows
body:
### Description

The newly added Ingres connector (`debezium-connector-ingres`) is currently not included in the main CI/CD workflows that run on pull requests and pushes to protected branches. This means that changes to core Debezium code may introduce regressions in the Ingres connector that won't be detected until much later.

### Current Behavior

The connector is currently integrated in:
- ✅ Nightly build size check workflow (`nightly-build-size-check.yml`)

But is missing from:
- ❌ PR workflow (`debezium-workflow-pr.yml`)
- ❌ Push workflow (`debezium-workflow-push.yml`)
- ❌ Java Quality Outreach (`jdk-outreach-workflow.yml`)


### Expected Behavior

After integrating the Ingres connector into the PR and Push workflows:

1. **On Pull Request Creation/Update:**
   - The Ingres connector is automatically built and tested against the PR changes
   - Test failures in the Ingres connector block the PR from being merged
   - Contributors can see Ingres build status alongside other connector builds

2. **On Push to Protected Branches:**
   - Every push to `main`, `1.*`, `2.*`, `3.*`, or `4.*` branches triggers an Ingres build
   - The workflow checks out the matching branch from the Ingres repository (e.g., `main` for `main`, `2.x` for `2.x`)
   - Build failures are immediately visible in the workflow run

### Additional Notes

- The Ingres connector action already supports the `only-build: 'true'` parameter which is used by the nightly workflow to skip tests and checkstyle for faster size checking
- The connector follows the same pattern as other external connectors (Db2, IBMi, Informix, etc.)
author: vsantonastaso

project_fields.Title: Integrate debezium-connector-ingres in Java Quality Outreach, PR and Push GitHub Workflows

title: Create dedicated SMT to convert MongoDB events into relational-style structs
body:
## Description:

Reference: Issue #594 

Currently, unlike relational connectors that emit before and after states as nested `Struct` objects, MongoDB events emit them as simple JSON strings. To enable a shared behavior pipeline for both relational and non-relational events, we need to extract the before/after struct conversion logic into its own dedicated Single Message Transform (SMT).

Requirements for the new SMT:

- Relational Event Formatting: The SMT should focus solely on parsing the MongoDB sequence and converting it into a structured format where before and after are nested Struct objects. This will allow MongoDB events to seamlessly feed into the existing `ExtractChangedRecordState` SMT.

- Schema Mapping: The new SMT must allow users to define the final target schema. This will make it possible to normalize per-collection documents into a pre-defined schema (when configured) and effectively solve the missing field values problem.
author: div-dev123

project_fields.Title: Create dedicated SMT to convert MongoDB events into relational-style structs

title: SQL Server connector with initial_only snapshot mode gets stuck in infinite retry loop when database name is invalid
body:
## Bug report
When a non-existent database name is configured with initial_only snapshot mode, the SQL Server connector gets stuck in an infinite retry loop during task reconfiguration. retrieveRealDatabaseName() throws IllegalStateException: Could not retrieve exactly one database name, which the Connect runtime
  (DistributedHerder.reconfigureConnectorTasksWithExponentialBackoffRetries) catches and retries indefinitely. The connector never starts a task and never reports FAILED status.

**What Debezium connector do you use and what version?**

`SqlServer, v3.2.5`

---

**What is the connector configuration?**

```
{
"connector.class" : "io.debezium.connector.sqlserver.SqlServerConnector",  
        "database.hostname" : "******",  
        "database.port" : "1433",  
        "database.user" : "******",  
        "database.password" : "******",   
        "database.names" : "bgoyal_test, one123",  
        "topic.prefix" : "test111",  
        "tasks.max" : "1",  
        "schema.history.internal.kafka.bootstrap.servers" : "localhost:9092", 
        "schema.history.internal.kafka.topic": "history11"  ,
     "table.include.list":"dbo.type_time",
     "signal.data.collection": "bgoyal_test.dbo.dbz_signaling",
    "key.converter":"io.confluent.connect.avro.AvroConverter",
    "value.converter":"io.confluent.connect.avro.AvroConverter",
    "key.converter.schema.registry.url": "http://localhost:8081",
    "value.converter.schema.registry.url": "http://localhost:8081",
    "confluent.topic.bootstrap.servers": "localhost:9092",
    "snapshot.mode": "initial_only",
    "database.encrypt":"false",
      "internal.log.position.check.enable":"false"
}
```

---

**What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?**

_E.g. on-premises, with a specific cloud provider, etc._

`on-prem`

---

**What behavior do you expect?**

`The task should fail gracefully with a meaningful and actionable user-message regarding non-existent database.`

---

**What behavior do you see?**

`The task keeps on retrying with below exception`
```

 ERROR [DistributedHerder-connect-127.0.0.1:8083-1] [Worker clientId=connect-127.0.0.1:8083, groupId=connect-cluster] Failed to reconfigure connector's tasks (test3), retrying after backoff. (org.apache.kafka.connect.runtime.distributed.DistributedHerder:2292)
Error: java.lang.IllegalStateException: Could not retrieve exactly one database name
	at io.debezium.jdbc.JdbcConnection.lambda$singleResultMapper$10(Jdbc
```

---

**Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?**

_Ideally, also verify with the latest Alpha/Beta/CR version._

`Haven't test but should be same as the issue is triggerd due to [KAFKA-14746](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/KAFKA-14746)`

---

**Do you have the connector logs, ideally from start till finish?**

_You might be asked later to provide [DEBUG/TRACE](https://debezium.io/documentation/reference/stable/operations/logging.html) level log._

```

 ERROR [DistributedHerder-connect-127.0.0.1:8083-1] [Worker clientId=connect-127.0.0.1:8083, groupId=connect-cluster] Failed to reconfigure connector's tasks (test3), retrying after backoff. (org.apache.kafka.connect.runtime.distributed.DistributedHerder:2292)
Error: java.lang.IllegalStateException: Could not retrieve exactly one database name
	at io.debezium.jdbc.JdbcConnection.lambda$singleResultMapper$10(Jdbc
```

---

**How to reproduce the issue using our [tutorial](https://github.com/debezium/debezium-examples/tree/main/tutorial) deployment?**

`Start a sqlserver connector with dummy db name and snapshot.mode=initial_only`

title: MDC context missing in snapshot worker threads and JdbcConnection close thread
body:
After the recent MDC logging improvements, gaps were observed during test suite runs where MDC context is missing from log output.

Two cases were identified where MDC context is not properly propagated across threads:

1. In `RelationalSnapshotChangeEventSource`, the methods `createDataEventsForTableCallable` and `createDataEventsForChunkedTableCallable` use the 3-argument variant of `LoggingContext.forConnector()`, which does not include `taskId` and `partition`. As a result, snapshot worker thread logs contain empty MDC sections (||).

2. In `JdbcConnection.doClose()`, `Threads.runWithTimeout()` spawns a new thread. Since MDC in SLF4J is thread-local, the logging context is not inherited by the new thread, causing log entries within that thread to miss MDC information.

Example logs showing missing MDC context:

2026-03-19 05:11:22,153 INFO   ||  Exporting data from table 'ORCLPDB1.DEBEZIUM.NUMBER_NEGATIVE_SCALE'
2026-03-19 05:11:22,161 INFO   ||        Finished exporting 1 records for table 'ORCLPDB1.DEBEZIUM.NUMBER_NEGATIVE_SCALE'

2026-03-19 05:16:33,930 INFO   ||  Connection gracefully closed

Expected behavior:
Full MDC context should be present in all log lines.
author: Binayak490-cyber

project_fields.Title: MDC context missing in snapshot worker threads and JdbcConnection close thread

title: Duplicate END records of a transaction
body:
## Bug report

**What Debezium connector do you use and what version?**

Debezium MongoDB CDC Source, I was using 3.0.8 and planning to move to 3.2.5, The bug is there in both of them.

---

**What is the connector configuration?**

Apologies, but I missed the exact configuration, 
For this issue only config which is important is `"provide.transaction.metadata": "true"`

```
{
    "connector.class": "io.debezium.connector.v2.mongodb.MongoDbConnector",
    "authentication.method": "io.debezium.connector.v2.mongodb.connection.DefaultMongoDbAuthProvider",
    "mongodb.connection.string": "${MONGO_URI}",
    "mongodb.user": "${MONGO_USER}",
    "mongodb.password": "${MONGO_PASSWORD}",
    "mongodb.ssl.enabled": "true",
    "topic.prefix": "test-mongo",
    "output.data.format": "JSON",
    "output.key.format": "JSON",
    "capture.mode": "change_streams_update_full",
    "capture.scope": "deployment",
    "snapshot.mode": "initial",
    "database.include.list": "test_cdc",
    "collection.include.list": "test_cdc.orders,test_cdc.customers,test_cdc.products",
    "collection.excluded.list": "test_cdc.audit_log",
    "snapshot.include.collection.list": "test_cdc.orders,test_cdc.customers",
    "provide.transaction.metadata": "true",
    "tombstones.on.delete": "true"
 }
```
---

**What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?**

_E.g. on-premises, with a specific cloud provider, etc._

Was Using MongoDB Atlas on AWS us-west-2

---

**What behavior do you expect?**

When a non-transactional event arrives while a transaction is marked as "in progress", the TransactionMonitor should emit an END record to close the transaction.

---

**What behavior do you see?**

When a non-transactional event arrives while a transaction is marked as "in progress", the TransactionMonitor correctly emits an END record to close the transaction. 
However, it failed to reset the transaction state by calling transactionContext.endTransaction().
This caused isTransactionInProgress() to remain true, resulting in duplicate END records being emitted for every subsequent non-transactional event.

---

**Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?**

_Ideally, also verify with the latest Alpha/Beta/CR version._

Apologies, haven't checked this yet, will update whenever I will test, Most likely it will be the similar behaviour because I didn't find the code fix.

---

**Do you have the connector logs, ideally from start till finish?**

_You might be asked later to provide [DEBUG/TRACE](https://debezium.io/documentation/reference/stable/operations/logging.html) level log._

Nope, Let me know if needed.

---

**How to reproduce the issue using our [tutorial](https://github.com/debezium/debezium-examples/tree/main/tutorial) deployment?**

I am new to Debezium, haven't tested this way. But can provide reproduction steps:
1. Insert documents in a MongoDB transaction (e.g., 2 inserts)
2. Insert a non-transactional document → END record emitted (correct)
3. Insert another non-transactional document → Duplicate END record emitted (bug)
author: sonagaras

project_fields.Title: Duplicate END records of a transaction

title: When using ROWID pseudocolumn as surrogate-key sort by primary key of table
body:
## Feature request or enhancement

**Which use case/requirement will be addressed by the proposed feature?**
We have found that when using the rowid as the surrogate-key during an incremental snapshot, the ORDER BY ROWID operation becomes very expensive and leads to significantly degraded performance.

I would like to instead decouple this such that ROWID is used in the projection and the PK of the table is used in the order by.

Relates to this discussion [here]([#community-oracle > Unable to transfer a large table @ 💬](https://debezium.zulipchat.com/#narrow/channel/348250-community-oracle/topic/Unable.20to.20transfer.20a.20large.20table/near/579705339))

---
