title: Oracle Savepoint Partial Rollback not handled when LOB column changed
body: ## Bug report Sourced from https://redhat.atlassian.net/browse/DBZ-9615 and https://github.com/debezium/dbz/issues/1422. This is a follow-up where a user inserts a non-null vaule into a LOB field within a SAVEPOINT operation and then the transaction rolls back the SAVEPOINT, seen here: In this specific use case, the `DELETE` operation ROWID will not directly map to the `UPDATE` event's ROWID, and this is problematic because the connector cannot make the connection between the two operations, and successfully roll the update event back. Delete ```sql delete from "DEBEZIUM"."DBZ915" where ROWID = 'AAAS2MAAMAAAACUAAB'; ``` Update ```sql update "DEBEZIUM"."DBZ9615" set "DATA" = NULL where "ID" = '1' AND ROWID = 'AAAS2MAAMAAAACUAAA`; ```
author: Naros

project_fields.Title: Oracle Savepoint Partial Rollback not handled when LOB column changed

title: MongoDbAuthProvider and MongoDbClientFactory lack close() lifecycle for resource cleanup
body:
# Feature request

## Description

The `MongoDbAuthProvider` and `MongoDbClientFactory` interfaces have no `close()` method, so implementations that hold closeable resources (e.g. AWS STS clients, HTTP connection pools) have no way to release them when the connector task stops.

Currently, `MongoDbConnectorTask.doStop()` closes the schema but does not close the `MongoDbConnectionContext`, which holds the `MongoDbClientFactory`, which in turn holds the `MongoDbAuthProvider`. Any resources acquired during `init()` are never explicitly released.

For the default `DefaultMongoDbAuthProvider` this is harmless (it holds no closeable resources). However, custom `MongoDbAuthProvider` implementations — such as those using AWS IAM authentication with STS clients — would benefit from a proper cleanup path.

## Proposed change

1. Add `default void close() {}` to `MongoDbAuthProvider` interface
2. Add `default void close() {}` to `MongoDbClientFactory` interface
3. Override `close()` in `DefaultMongoDbClientFactory` to delegate to `authProvider.close()`
4. Add `close()` to `MongoDbConnectionContext` to delegate to `clientFactory.close()`
5. Call `connectionContext.close()` in `MongoDbConnectorTask.doStop()`

All default implementations are no-op, so this is fully backward compatible. Custom auth providers can override `close()` to release their resources.

## Affected files

- `MongoDbAuthProvider.java`
- `MongoDbClientFactory.java`
- `DefaultMongoDbClientFactory.java`
- `MongoDbConnectionContext.java`
- `MongoDbConnectorTask.java`
author: sharanrudresh

project_fields.Title: MongoDbAuthProvider and MongoDbClientFactory lack close() lifecycle for resource cleanup

title: Debezium Server - Allow WebIdentityTokens / IRSA roles for Kinesis sink
body:
## Feature request or enhancement

**Which use case/requirement will be addressed by the proposed feature?**

When using the Kinesis sink with a Kubernetes ServiceAccount and AWS STS assuming the target role fails with this error message:

```
{
    ...
    "loggerClassName":"org.slf4j.impl.Slf4jLogger",
    "loggerName":"software.amazon.awssdk.auth.credentials.internal.WebIdentityCredentialsUtils",
    "level":"WARN",
    "message":"To use web identity tokens, the 'sts' service module must be on the class path.",
    ...
}
```

My DebeziumServer spec looks similar to this:

```
apiVersion: debezium.io/v1alpha1
kind: DebeziumServer
metadata:
    name: {{someName}}
    namespace: debezium
spec:
    image: 271813978393.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/quay/debezium/server:3.3.0
    quarkus:
        config:
            kubernetes-config.enabled: true
            kubernetes-config.secrets: {{postgresSecret}}
    runtime:
        serviceAccount: debezium-sa
    sink:
        type: kinesis
        config:
            region: {{awsRegion}}
    source:
       {{postgres source config}}
```

with the service account mapping into a AWS role like this:
```
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: debezium-sa
  namespace: debezium
  annotations:
    eks.amazonaws.com/role-arn: {{roleARN}}
```


---

**Implementation ideas (optional)**

I understand this happens due to a missing dependency. 
```
<dependency>
    <groupId>software.amazon.awssdk</groupId>
    <artifactId>sts</artifactId>
</dependency>
```

See the documentation here:
https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sdk-for-java/latest/developer-guide/troubleshooting.html#faq-credentials-provider-chain

It is also mentioned on StackOverflow here: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/60481017/unable-to-load-aws-credentials-from-any-provider-in-the-chain-webidentitytokenc

title: Add shutdown strategies in Debezium Engine
body:
## Context

The Debezium Engine does not currently provide a way to shut down gracefully. In addition, there is no built‑in mechanism to stop the engine based on specific conditions, such as:

- when a snapshot has completed
- after processing a fixed number of events
- when the event queue has been fully drained


## Changes

We propose introducing a new new `shutdownStrategy` API to the `EmbeddedEngine`:

```java
EmbeddedEngine.create()
                .shutdownStrategy(event -> {
                    return false;
                })
                .using(config)
                .using(this.getClass().getClassLoader())
                .using(Clock.SYSTEM)
                .notifying(this::sendRecord)
                .build();
```
author: kmos

project_fields.Title: Add shutdown strategies in Debezium Engine

title: Debezium kafka images with Kafka version 4.1.1 fails to start in KRaft mode
body:
## Summary

The Debezium Kafka Docker image versions 3.4 and 3.5 (using Kafka 4.1.1) fails to start when using KRaft mode with the `KAFKA_CONTROLLER_QUORUM_VOTERS` environment variable. 
This is a regression from Kafka 4.1.0.

## Current Behavior

The Kafka container exits with code 1 during the storage formatting phase with the following error:

```
You cannot specify controller.quorum.voters and format the node with --initial-controllers or --standalone.
If you want to use dynamic quorum, please remove controller.quorum.voters and specify
controller.quorum.bootstrap.servers instead.
```

**Reproduction:**

Using the following docker-compose configuration:

```yaml
services:
  kafka:
    image: quay.io/debezium/kafka:3.5.0-cr1
    ports:
      - 9092:9092
      - 9093:9093
    environment:
      - CLUSTER_ID=oh-sxaDRTcyAr6pFRbXyzA
      - NODE_ID=1
      - NODE_ROLE=combined
      - KAFKA_CONTROLLER_QUORUM_VOTERS=1@kafka:9093
      - KAFKA_LISTENERS=PLAINTEXT://kafka:9092,CONTROLLER://kafka:9093
      - KAFKA_ADVERTISED_LISTENERS=PLAINTEXT://kafka:9092
```

The container fails to start and exits immediately.

## Expected Behavior

Kafka should start successfully in KRaft mode with the combined broker+controller role, as it did with Kafka 4.1.0 and earlier versions.

The container should:
1. Format the storage directory successfully
2. Start the Kafka broker
3. Accept connections on port 9092
4. Accept controller connections on port 9093
author: vsantonastaso

project_fields.Title: Debezium kafka images with Kafka version 4.1.1 fails to start in KRaft mode

title: Oracle column reselection does not quote all key columns - fails with ORA-00904
body:
## Bug report

**What Debezium connector do you use and what version?**

Debezium Oracle Connector `3.4.2.Final`

---

**What is the connector configuration?**

```json
{
  "connector.class": "io.debezium.connector.oracle.OracleConnector",
  "post.processors": "reselector",
  "post.processors.reselector.type": "io.debezium.processors.reselect.ReselectColumnsPostProcessor",
  "post.processors.reselector.reselect.columns.include.list": "APP_SCHEMA.SampleTable:LargeTextField",
  "post.processors.reselector.reselect.unavailable.values": "true",
  "post.processors.reselector.reselect.null.values": "false",
  "post.processors.reselector.reselect.use.event.key": "false",
  "post.processors.reselector.reselect.error.handling.mode": "WARN"
}
```

---

**What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?**

Oracle Database (on-premises)

---

**What behavior do you expect?**

When using `ReselectColumnsPostProcessor` for a table with a `CLOB` column:

- If the CLOB value is not present in the redo log (`__debezium_unavailable_value`),  
- Debezium should re-select the column value from Oracle and populate it in the emitted event.

This should work regardless of whether the Oracle table uses quoted camelCase identifiers.

---

**What behavior do you see?**

The reselector fails when the Oracle table uses quoted camelCase identifiers.

Example table:
- "Id" (PK)
- "LargeTextField" (CLOB)

When an update occurs without modifying the CLOB:
- The emitted event still contains `__debezium_unavailable_value`
- The reselector does not replace it

Connector logs show:

Failed to re-select columns for table APP_SCHEMA.SampleTable and key [12345]

java.sql.SQLSyntaxErrorException: ORA-00904: "ID": invalid identifier

---

**Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?**

Yes, reproduced with `3.4.2.Final`.

---

**Do you have the connector logs, ideally from start till finish?**

Relevant excerpt:

2026-03-26T15:00:33,358 - WARN  [ReselectColumnsPostProcessor]
Failed to re-select columns for table APP_SCHEMA.SampleTable and key [12345]

java.sql.SQLSyntaxErrorException: ORA-00904: "ID": invalid identifier

at io.debezium.jdbc.JdbcConnection.reselectColumns
at io.debezium.connector.oracle.OracleConnection.reselectColumns
at io.debezium.processors.reselect.ReselectColumnsPostProcessor.apply

---

**How to reproduce the issue using our tutorial deployment?**

1. Create an Oracle table with quoted camelCase identifiers:

CREATE TABLE APP_SCHEMA."SampleTable" (
  "Id" NUMBER(9,0) PRIMARY KEY,
  "LargeTextField" CLOB
);

2. Configure Debezium Oracle connector with ReselectColumnsPostProcessor

3. Insert a row with a CLOB value

4. Update the row without modifying the CLOB column

5. Observe:
   - Event contains `__debezium_unavailable_value`
   - Logs contain ORA-00904
author: Shad107

project_fields.Title: Oracle column reselection does not quote all key columns - fails with ORA-00904

title: Improve core components testing
body:
### Description

Following the recent module renaming and restructuring (dbz#1616, dbz#1617), some modules currently have tests that are not consistently executed in CI.

In particular:
- `debezium-connect-plugins` (including transforms)
- `debezium-connector-common`
- `debezium-config`
- `debezium-util`

These modules were previously part of `debezium-core`, and their tests were skipped during core dependency builds. The same behavior persists after the refactoring. At the moment, some of these tests are only executed indirectly (e.g., via the `ai` module build), which is not explicit or reliable.

### Problem

- Tests for several core/shared modules are not explicitly run in CI.
- Failures in these modules could propagate downstream to connectors, wasting CI resources.
- There is no clear guardrail to ensure core modules are validated before dependent modules are built.

### Proposed Solution

- Introduce a dedicated CI step to explicitly build and run tests for:
  - `debezium-connect-plugins`
  - `debezium-connector-common`
  - `debezium-config`
  - `debezium-util`
- Configure dependent modules (e.g., connectors) to depend on this step.
  - This acts as a guardrail: if core/shared modules fail, downstream builds are skipped.

### Expected Outcome

- Ensure all relevant module tests are executed at least once in CI.
- Improve build reliability and faster feedback on core changes.
- Reduce unnecessary CI resource consumption by failing fast on shared module issues.
author: mfvitale

project_fields.Title: Improve core components testing

title: Signal-based binlog position adjustment for MariaDB connector
body:
## Description

Add signal-based binlog position adjustment support for the MariaDB connector, extending the functionality already implemented for MySQL in DBZ-3829 (#1156).

This allows users to send a signal to the MariaDB connector to adjust the binlog file and position it reads from, enabling re-reading of events from a specific point in the binlog.

## Implementation

The MariaDB implementation follows the same pattern as the MySQL connector:
- Implements `SignalActionProvider` for MariaDB
- Registers the provider via `META-INF/services/`
- Supports setting binlog file/position and GTID via signal payload
- Uses async stop pattern to avoid deadlocks when restarting from new position

## Related

- Original MySQL implementation: #1156 (DBZ-3829)
- MySQL PR: debezium/debezium#6552 (merged)
- MariaDB PR: debezium/debezium#7096
author: kebab-mai-haddi

title: Improve `LogFileCollector` logging and `LogFile` state
body:
## Feature request or enhancement

_For feature requests or enhancements, please provide the following information:_

**Which use case/requirement will be addressed by the proposed feature?**

Currently, the `LogFile` class does not track the file size and dictionary start/end markers. This information is useful when we want to determine the size of the redo or archive logs, but do not have the full logs. In addition, the dictionary markers are useful when the data dictionary is written to the logs as part of the `redo_log_catalog` strategy.

In addition, the `LogFileCollector` 's log entries during log collection can be improved by:
* Specifying the SCN delta range between the first/next boundaries on archive logs.
* Specifying the archive/redo log file sizes
* Specifying the dictionary start/end markers for archive logs

Lastly, when the redo log files are inconsistent, rather than output a `DebeziumException`, when logging is at INFO, only the inconsistent INFO message will be written, without the exception. If DEBUG is enabled, the current behavior will be used, including the INFO message and stack trace details.

Example improvements
```
Archive log logfile1 Seq# 1 Thread# 1 SCN [103300 - 103400 (delta 100)] Size 1,073,741,824 bytes Dictionary N/N in destination LOG_ARCHIVE_DEST_1 to be added.   [io.debezium.connector.oracle.logminer.LogFileCollector]
Archive log logfile3 Seq# 2 Thread# 1 SCN [103400 - 103700 (delta 300)] Size 1,073,741,824 bytes Dictionary N/N in destination LOG_ARCHIVE_DEST_1 to be added.   [io.debezium.connector.oracle.logminer.LogFileCollector]
Online log logfile2 Seq# 3 Thread# 1 SCN [103700 - 18446744073709551615] Size 1,073,741,824 bytes Status CURRENT to be added.   [io.debezium.connector.oracle.logminer.LogFileCollector]
```

title: SQS integration tests fail on CI due to LocalStack authentication requirements
body:
### Problem
  The `SqsIT` integration test fails on CI with the following error:
  The license activation failed for the following reason:

```text
2026-03-27 15:13:51,410 ERROR [tc.mir.gcr.io/localstack/localstack:latest] (pool-3-thread-1) Log output from the failed container:

LocalStack version: 2026.3.1.dev52
LocalStack build date: 2026-03-27
LocalStack build git hash: 817219635

Localstack returning with exit code 55. Reason: 
===============================================
The license activation failed for the following reason:

No credentials were found in the environment. Please make sure to either set the LOCALSTACK_AUTH_TOKEN variable to a valid auth token. If you are using the CLI, you can also run `localstack auth set-token`.

LocalStack requires an account to run.

==> Have an account? Learn how to set LOCALSTACK_AUTH_TOKEN: https://app.localstack.cloud/settings/auth-tokens
==> Need an account? Get started: https://www.localstack.cloud/pricing
==> Want more time? Snooze until April 6, 2026 by setting LOCALSTACK_ACKNOWLEDGE_ACCOUNT_REQUIREMENT=1
``` 

  ### Root Cause
  **LocalStack licensing model changed (March 2026)** - as documented in their [blog post](https://blog.localstack.cloud/the-road-ahead-for-localstack/#future-journey-localstack-for-aws-remains-free-for-students-hobbyists-and-open-source-projects) :

 
  1. **Authentication now required**: The unified `localstack/localstack:latest` image requires account-based authentication via `LOCALSTACK_AUTH_TOKEN`
  2. **Free tier available**: LocalStack offers a free tier for non-commercial use that includes CI credits (as of their recent pricing update)
  3. **Prior versions still work without auth**: Legacy version tags remain available without authentication requirements, though they won't receive
   future service/security updates

  ### Options for Resolution

  According to LocalStack's migration guide, we can either:

  1. **Sign up for free tier** and configure `LOCALSTACK_AUTH_TOKEN` in CI (free tier is for non-commercial use and now includes CI credits)
  2. **Pin to a prior version** which doesn't require authentication but won't receive future updates

  ### Current Configuration
  In `debezium-server-core/src/test/java/io/debezium/server/Images.java`:

  ```java
  public static final String LOCALSTACK_IMAGE = "mirror.gcr.io/localstack/localstack";  // No version pinned - pulls latest
```
  CI pulls the latest LocalStack image which now requires authentication.

  ### Proposed Solution

  Pin to the last stable community version to avoid authentication requirements:

```text
mirror.gcr.io/localstack/localstack:4.13.1";
```
author: mfvitale

project_fields.Title: SQS integration tests fail on CI due to LocalStack authentication requirements

title: CockroachDB connector: Remove broken permission check, fix hardcoded values and changefeed detection
body:
## Summary

Production-readiness fixes for the CockroachDB connector addressing multiple bugs found during a full connector audit.

## Changes

### 1. Remove broken `checkChangefeedPermissions()` (primary bug)

The `checkChangefeedPermissions()` method in `CockroachDBConnection.java` runs `SHOW GRANTS ON TABLE public.*` and only matches `privilege_type == "CHANGEFEED"`. This rejects legitimate users:

- **`ALL` privilege**: CockroachDB v25.x reports `ALL` as a single row, not expanded. Users with `ALL` (including `root`) fail the check.
- **`admin` role members**: The check does not query role membership.
- **Role-inherited privileges**: Only matches the literal username as grantee.
- **`CONTROLCHANGEFEED` role option**: Deprecated but still functional; not checked.

**Fix**: Removed `checkChangefeedPermissions()` entirely. CockroachDB validates permissions at `CREATE CHANGEFEED` time with accurate, version-specific errors. Replaced with `checkRangefeedEnabled()` that only checks the `kv.rangefeed.enabled` cluster setting. Removed `SKIP_PERMISSION_CHECK` config field (incubating connector, no backward compat needed).

### 2. Fix hardcoded `"cockroachdb"` topic prefix fallback

`buildSinkChangefeedQuery()` and `buildTopicName()` fell back to hardcoded `"cockroachdb"` when `cockroachdb.changefeed.sink.topic.prefix` was empty. Now falls back to `topic.prefix` (the standard Debezium config) for consistency.

### 3. Fix `changefeedExists()` fragile substring matching

`changefeedExists()` used `description.contains(tableName)` which matches substrings. A table named `users` would match a changefeed description containing `admin_users`. Now matches the fully-qualified table name AND the sink URI topic prefix, preventing false positives from changefeeds created by other connector instances.

### 4. Fix `CockroachDBPartition` hardcoded partition key

The partition key was hardcoded to `"cockroachdb"`, meaning ALL connector instances shared the same offset partition. Multiple connectors targeting different databases would have colliding offsets. Now uses `topic.prefix` as the partition key.

### 5. Fix Kafka URI protocol stripping

Only `kafka://` and `PLAINTEXT://` URI prefixes were stripped when deriving bootstrap servers from the sink URI. Added support for `SSL://`, `SASL_PLAINTEXT://`, and `SASL_SSL://`.

### 6. Extract cursor sentinel constants

Replaced scattered `"initial"` and `"now"` magic strings with `CockroachDBOffsetContext.CURSOR_INITIAL` and `CURSOR_NOW` constants.

### 7. Fix consumer group description

Config description falsely claimed group ID format was `{prefix}-{table_name}`. Updated to reflect actual behavior (single consumer for all tables).

## Files changed

- `CockroachDBConnection.java`: Replaced `checkChangefeedPermissions()` with `checkRangefeedEnabled()`
- `CockroachDBStreamingChangeEventSource.java`: Updated caller, fixed topic prefix fallback, fixed changefeedExists(), fixed URI stripping, use cursor constants
- `CockroachDBConnectorConfig.java`: Removed `SKIP_PERMISSION_CHECK`, updated descriptions
- `CockroachDBPartition.java`: Accept serverName parameter, use topic.prefix
- `CockroachDBPartitionProvider.java`: Pass serverName from config
- `CockroachDBConnectorTask.java`: Pass logical name to partition provider
- `CockroachDBOffsetContext.java`: Added CURSOR_INITIAL/CURSOR_NOW constants
- `CockroachDBMultiTableTest.java`: Updated test assertions for topic.prefix fallback
- 6 integration test files: Removed `skip.permission.check` config lines
- `README.md`: Added Required Privileges section, removed skip.permission.check, updated docs

## How to reproduce the primary bug

1. Start CockroachDB v25.x with default `root` user (which has `ALL` privileges)
2. Configure the connector with `database.user=root`
3. Start the connector -- fails with: `User 'root' lacks CHANGEFEED privilege`
4. Adding `cockroachdb.skip.permission.check=true` worked around the issue

## Version

- Debezium CockroachDB connector: 3.5.0-SNAPSHOT
- CockroachDB: v25.4.6
author: viragtripathi

project_fields.Title: CockroachDB connector: Remove broken permission check, fix hardcoded values and changefeed detection

title: Add OpenTelemetry tracing support to debezium-connector-cassandra
body:
## Feature request or enhancement

_For feature requests or enhancements, please provide the following information:_

**Which use case/requirement will be addressed by the proposed feature?**

The Cassandra connector currently has no tracing support, making it impossible to correlate CDC events with distributed traces across the pipeline. Other Debezium connectors support tracing via the ActivateTracingSpan SMT, but since the Cassandra connector does not use the Kafka Connect framework, that is unavailable.

Users running Cassandra CDC pipelines need end-to-end observability: knowing when a Cassandra mutation occurred, when it was processed by Debezium, and being able to continue the trace downstream (e.g. into an Elasticsearch Sink or other consumers). 

---

**Implementation ideas (optional)**

Instrument `debezium-connector-cassandra` at the Kafka Emitter level, following the same OTEL span conventions used in `debezium-core`. Two nested OTEL spans would be created per emitted event:                                                                                         
  - `db-log-write`: backdated to the Cassandra mutation timestamp (`tsMicro`), with source attributes: `keyspace, table, cluster, commitlog file/position, snapshot flag, connector version`.    
  - `debezium-read`: timestamped at Debezium processing time, with `op` and `ts_ms`.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            

A trace context is injected into the Kafka ProducerRecord headers (`traceparent`) so downstream consumers can continue the distributed trace.

A new config property `tracing.enabled` (default: false) would gate the feature. If tracing is enabled but no OTEL jar is present on the classpath, the connector no-ops rather than failing (same as `debezium-core`).
author: jogomez97

project_fields.Title: Add OpenTelemetry tracing support to debezium-connector-cassandra

title: Support STS AssumeRole for cross-account IAM authentication (PostgreSQL / MySQL)
body:
Hello

## Feature request or enhancement

_For feature requests or enhancements, please provide the following information:_

**Which use case/requirement will be addressed by the proposed feature?**

I'm running Kafka Connect (MSK Connect) in AWS account A and need to capture changes from RDS PostgreSQL databases in AWS account B using IAM authentication (database.authmode = aws-iam).

The connector generates the RDS IAM auth token using the credentials available to the process (the MSK Connect execution role in account A). RDS in account B rejects this token because the principal isn't in the same account.

The standard AWS pattern here is to assume a role in account B via STS AssumeRole, then generate the token with those temporary credentials. The AWS Advanced JDBC Wrapper supports this through StsAssumeRoleCredentialsProvider (see https://github.com/aws/aws-advanced-jdbc-wrapper/discussions/1239), but it requires programmatic configuration. I couldn't find a way to pass a role ARN through Debezium connector properties.

A property like database.iam.role.arn that tells the connection factory to assume a given role before generating the IAM auth token would make cross-account CDC possible without falling back to static credentials.

I might have missed an existing way to do this though, happy to be corrected

Have a good day !

---

**Implementation ideas (optional)**

`<Your answer>`
author: gh-jean-stephane-thibault

project_fields.Title: Support STS AssumeRole for cross-account IAM authentication (PostgreSQL / MySQL)

title: update jbang templates to `3.5.0.Final` release
author: kmos

project_fields.Title: update jbang templates to `3.5.0.Final` release

title: Improve Incremental Snapshot docs with database requirement
body:
Currently in the signaling documentation, we have a note in the Kafka signal channel about requiring database write access & the source channel for incremental snapshots, that reads:
> To use Kafka signaling to trigger ad hoc incremental snapshots for most connectors, you must first [enable a source signaling channel](https://debezium.io/documentation/reference/3.5/configuration/signalling.html#debezium-signaling-enabling-source-signaling-channel) in the connector configuration. The source channel implements a watermarking mechanism to deduplicate events that might be captured by an incremental snapshot and then captured again after streaming resumes. Enabling the source channel is not required when using a signaling channel to trigger an incremental snapshot of a read-only MySQL database that has [GTIDs enabled](https://debezium.io/documentation/reference/3.5/connectors/mysql.html#enable-mysql-gtids). For more information, see [MySQL read only incremental snapshot](https://debezium.io/documentation/reference/3.5/connectors/mysql.html#mysql-read-only-incremental-snapshots)

However, this is confusing because we do not have the same admonition in the other signal channels for JMX and File signals, which can be confusing to users, not realizing this is necessary regardless of channel for Incremental Snapshots.

We should either move the admonition into the `Signal actions > Incremental snapshots` or duplicate it to avoid confusion.
author: Naros

project_fields.Title: Improve Incremental Snapshot docs with database requirement

title: Row archival tests fail for Oracle XStream
body: In #1676 we introduced support to handle `ORA_ARCHIVE_STATE` columns, and while this worked for LogMiner, we did not account for this as part of the XStream adapter. This column should be skipped
author: Naros

project_fields.Title: Row archival tests fail for Oracle XStream

title: XStream does not decode all XML encodings
body:
Oracle may serialize XML data in various encodings, including UTF16BE, UTF16LE, and UTF8. The XStream adapter currently only supports UTF8, and can lead to emitting character-based XML with the wrong byte encoding format.

* Follow-up to https://github.com/debezium/dbz/issues/1373.
author: Naros

project_fields.Title: XStream does not decode all XML encodings

title: Offsets are not flushed periodically [DBZ-4664]
body:
Migrated from [DBZ-4664](https://issues.redhat.com/browse/DBZ-4664)

If there are no change records received, and the `debezium.source.offset.flush.interval.ms` has already passed, it seems there is no background thread that flushes the offsets.

It appears as though the offset flush interval is only used to debounce repeated calls (in `maybeFlush` method of `RecordCommitter` in the EmbeddedEngine). I could not see a mechanism to periodically flush. As a result, if I set the flush interval, for example, to 60s - and the initial sync takes a few seconds, no offset will be written.
author: jpechane

project_fields.Title: Offsets are not flushed periodically [DBZ-4664]

title: Add default value property in component descriptors
body: Default value is something we have in the `Field` class but is not exposed in the generated descriptors. It is useful for the UI to show a default value to the user.
author: mfvitale

title: Blocking snapshot race conditions cause connector deadlock on multiple snapshot signals
body:
## Bug report

**What Debezium connector do you use and what version?**

SQL Server connector, tested on main branch. The issue is in `ChangeEventSourceCoordinator` so it affects all connectors that use blocking snapshots.

---

**What is the connector configuration?**

Any configuration with a signal table configured for blocking snapshots.

---

**What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?**

SQL Server, on-premises. Not specific to any version or deployment — the issue is in the connector framework code.

---

**What behavior do you expect?**

When multiple blocking snapshot signals are submitted (e.g., multiple inserts into the signal table), each snapshot should run one after another and streaming should resume between them and there should be no deadlock.

---

**What behavior do you see?**

The connector deadlocks permanently. Streaming waits for the snapshot to finish and the snapshot waits for streaming to pause.

There are three race conditions in `ChangeEventSourceCoordinator.ChangeEventSourceContextImpl`:

**1. streaming flag set by wrong thread
The `streaming=true` in the blocking flow can interfere with the streaming thread, leaving the streaming thread waiting for snapshot to complete and the blocking-snapshot thread waiting for streaming to pause.     
This created a race condition where:

- Blocking snapshot sets pause=true
- Streaming thread checks isPaused() and sees paused=true
- Streaming thread calls streamingPaused() which sets streaming=false
- Blocking snapshot sets streaming=true
- Blocking snapshot thread waits for streaming=false (already satisfied)
- Both threads proceed, but streaming thread is now waiting for snapshot completion, while blocking snapshot finds streaming to still be running

**2. Back-to-back snapshots deadlock** — When the first snapshot completes, `resumeStreaming()` sets `paused = false` and signals the streaming thread. But before the streaming thread can exit the `while(paused)` loop, the next queued snapshot sets `paused = true` again. The streaming thread wakes, re-checks, sees `paused` is true, and goes back to waiting. No one signals it again — deadlock.

**3. Shutdown does not unblock waiting threads** — If `stop()` is called while the blocking snapshot thread is waiting on a condition variable, the streaming thread may terminate without signaling. The wait loops in `waitStreamingPaused()` and `waitSnapshotCompletion()` don't check if the task is still running, so the blocking snapshot thread waits forever.

**Suggested fix:**
- Remove `streaming = true` from `doBlockingSnapshot()` — let the streaming thread own its flag
- Add a condition variable so the blocking snapshot thread waits for streaming to actually resume before processing the next request
- Add `!running` checks in the wait loops to break out during shutdown

---

**Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?**

Yes, the code on main still has these issues.

---

**Do you have the connector logs, ideally from start till finish?**

TRACE logs showing the deadlock:
```
[18:13:55,438] INFO  Snapshot ended with SnapshotResult [status=COMPLETED, ...]
[18:13:55,439] TRACE Streaming will now resume.
[18:13:55,439] TRACE Requested a blocking snapshot. Waiting for streaming to be paused.
[18:13:55,440] TRACE Waiting for snapshot to be completed.
// connector is stuck from here — SignalProcessor keeps polling but no progress
[18:13:58,597] TRACE SignalProcessor processing
[18:14:03,599] TRACE SignalProcessor processing
[18:14:08,599] TRACE SignalProcessor processing
```

---

**How to reproduce the issue using our [tutorial](https://github.com/debezium/debezium-examples/tree/main/tutorial) deployment?**

1. Start a SQL Server connector with a signal table configured
2. Insert multiple blocking snapshot signals into the signal table in quick succession
3. Connector deadlocks after the first snapshot completes
author: b-goyal

project_fields.Title: Blocking snapshot race conditions cause connector deadlock on multiple snapshot signals

title: Release support lifecycle must be documented
body:
There is currently a discussion about whether upstream foundation projects should formally communicate their lifecycle release process, either directly on the website, in documentation, or both. The section must:

* Disclose clearly which versions are currently End-of-Life (EoL)
* Disclose clearly which versions are expected to reach EoL within the next six (6) months.

On the website, I believe a great place for this information would be on the documentation and release pages. Given that we need to link back to an upcoming page on the foundation's website, this would be a great place to state our EoL support policy or to link to it in our documentation.

In addition, we use 3 identifiers: "Development", "Latest Stable", and "Stable" on both pages to identify the version type. I propose we consider introducing a fourth label called "End-Of-Life" to apply to releases that fall outside our support criteria, and potentially rename the button "Hide older series" to "Hide unsupported series". We could potentially automate the switch from "Stable" to "EoL" based on the release data and our algorithm, but a simple flag in the yaml also works and fits with the current process.

If we all agree with that, the main question is whether the EoL policy description should be included on those two website pages, or whether we should document it officially in the docs and link to it from the website.
author: Naros

project_fields.Title: Release support lifecycle must be documented

title: Update to latest Infinispan 16.x
body:
We are currently on Infinispan 15.2.1.Final and the latest is now 16.1.3.

Several of the more interesting features include:
* In-place upgrades of clusters without restarts (16.0)
* Virtual thread support (16.0)
* Container-based eviction (16.1)
* Improved security (weak/vulnerability authentication disabled by default) (16.1)

The one limitation to bump to 16.1 would be that the Infinispan Server has a hard restriction of Java 25, whereas up to Infinispan 16.0, that requirement is still Java 17, which aligns with our connectors. 

This update also would affect Debezium Server, so we should decide whether 16.0.x is best or if going to 16.1.x is okay. @vjuranek any input from your side wrt the Sink and interoperability with HotRod, where the server may be 15.x or 16.x but we use 16.1 client?
author: Naros

title: Add exception details in replication slot WARN log
body:
## Feature request or enhancement

**Which use case/requirement will be addressed by the proposed feature?**

While operating the Debezium PostgreSQL Connector, we encountered the following WARN message:

> unable to load info of replication slot, Debezium will try to create the slot

However, this log does not include the exception details, making it difficult to understand why the call to retrieve the replication slot failed.

In our case, we attached a debugger to identify the root cause. It turned out to be an EOFException, which strongly suggested a network-level issue. Simply including the exception object in the log output would significantly improve the ability to narrow down the cause.

---

**Implementation ideas (optional)**

Include the exception object in the log message.
I'm going to open a PR shortly with this change.
author: atorik

project_fields.Title: Add exception details in replication slot WARN log

title: Cassandra connector FileOffsetWriter unbounded task queue causes heap exhaustion
body:
FileOffsetWriter.markOffset() submits a lambda to a single-threaded executor with an unbounded LinkedBlockingQueue for every record produced to Kafka. With the default offset.flush.interval.ms=0 (AlwaysFlushPolicy), each task performs disk I/O. At high throughput the executor cannot keep up, causing millions of FutureTask objects to accumulate in the queue and eventually exhausting the heap.

Symptoms: after ~2 hours of operation, Kafka producer latency degrades from 2ms to 800ms, throughput drops, CDC logs pile up, and restart is required.
author: nirnx

project_fields.Title: Cassandra connector FileOffsetWriter unbounded task queue causes heap exhaustion

title: Cassandra connector NoSuchFileException race condition in CDC directory size calculation
body: FileUtils.sizeOfDirectory() iterates directory entries and reads file sizes via PathUtils.sizeOfDirectory() through Uncheck.get(). If the commit log relocation thread moves a file between the directory listing and the size read, NoSuchFileException is thrown wrapped in UncheckedIOException, killing the processor thread and halting all CDC processing until restart.
author: nirnx

project_fields.Title: Cassandra connector NoSuchFileException race condition in CDC directory size calculation

title: Add OAuth2 authentication and batch mode support for HTTP Client sink
body:
Which use case/requirement will be addressed by the proposed feature?                                                                                                                                                                             
   
The Debezium Server HTTP Client sink currently supports JWT and Standard Webhooks authentication, but many HTTP endpoints require OAuth2 client credentials authentication (RFC 6749 Section 4.4). Additionally, the HTTP sink sends each change event as an individual HTTP request, which is inefficient for high-throughput scenarios. This feature adds:
 1. OAuth2 client credentials authentication for the HTTP Client sink, enabling Debezium Server to stream changes to OAuth2-protected HTTP endpoints.
 2. Batch mode for aggregating multiple change events into a single HTTP POST request, reducing network overhead.
author: danastott

project_fields.Title: Add OAuth2 authentication and batch mode support for HTTP Client sink

title: IBMi Connector: Support retrieving RRN from journal entries
body:
## Feature request or enhancement

_For feature requests or enhancements, please provide the following information:_

**Which use case/requirement will be addressed by the proposed feature?**

Currently, the IBMi connector does not expose the Relative Record Number (RRN) from journal entries. Having access to the RRN would be useful for identifying and tracking changes at the record level, especially for tables without primary keys or with duplicate data scenarios.

---

**Implementation ideas (optional)**
Use  `new BinaryFieldDescription(new AS400UnsignedBin8(), "10 count/relative record number"),`
to extract the Relative Record Number (RRN) from the journal entry. Then, propagate this value by adding an `"rrn"` field to `SourceInfo`, and set it appropriately within the `updateSourceInfo` method of the `AS400OffsetContext`  class.
author: j88753452-lab

title: Oracle LogMiner HEXTORAW string decoding hardcodes UTF-8, corrupts non-ASCII characters on non-UTF8 databases
body:
## Bug report

**What Debezium connector do you use and what version?**

Oracle connector, version 3.2.2

---

**What is the connector configuration?**

  connector.class: io.debezium.connector.oracle.OracleConnector
  database.connection.adapter: LogMiner
  log.mining.strategy: hybrid
  - Oracle NLS_CHARACTERSET: WE8ISO8859P1
  - Oracle NLS_NCHAR_CHARACTERSET: AL16UTF16

---

**What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?**

Oracle on-premises, character set WE8ISO8859P1 (Latin-1). Supplemental logging enabled (ALL COLUMNS).

---

**What behavior do you expect?**

Non-ASCII characters (e.g. Nordic characters ÅÄÖ) in VARCHAR2 columns should be captured correctly during LogMiner streaming, just as they are during snapshot.

---

**What behavior do you see?**

Non-ASCII characters are intermittently replaced with U+FFFD (Unicode replacement character) in Kafka events. For example, "KESKUKSEN LISÄTARVIKE" becomes "KESKUKSEN LIS�TARVIKE". Snapshot events for the same rows are always correct.

Oracle DUMP confirms the data is correctly stored as Latin-1 (c4 = Ä):
Typ=1 Len=21 CharacterSet=WE8ISO8859P1: 4b,45,53,4b,55,4b,53,45,4e,20,4c,49,53,c4,54,41,52,56,49,4b,45

Root cause in the code:
OracleValueConverters.convertHexToRawFunctionToString() (line 899) hardcodes StandardCharsets.UTF_8 when decoding HEXTORAW values for non-NCHAR columns:

default:
    return new String(RAW.hexString2Bytes(getHexToRawHexString(function)), StandardCharsets.UTF_8);

This path is reached when LogMiner returns STATUS=2 (SQL reconstruction failure) and the hybrid strategy falls back to LogMinerColumnResolverDmlParser, which receives SQL_REDO in COL x = HEXTORAW('...') format. The raw bytes are in the database's character set (Latin-1), but are decoded as UTF-8. Byte 0xC4 (Ä in Latin-1) is an incomplete UTF-8 sequence and becomes U+FFFD. OracleConnection only queries NLS_NCHAR_CHARACTERSET — the main NLS_CHARACTERSET is never fetched or used.

Why it is intermittent: The HEXTORAW path only fires when LogMiner returns STATUS=2 with a non-blank INFO column (AbstractLogMinerStreamingChangeEventSource.java, line 1487). Under normal operation, LogMiner returns plain string literals and the JDBC thin driver handles charset conversion correctly. We can see that during service windows the bug is prevalent and we believe that database restarts during these windows likely cause the online catalog to become temporarily unavailable, forcing LogMiner into the fallback COL x / HEXTORAW format.

---

**Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?**

The code at OracleValueConverters.java line 899 is unchanged on main (3.6.0-SNAPSHOT), so the bug is still present.

---

**Do you have the connector logs, ideally from start till finish?**

The affected redo logs have rotated past the 2-week retention window. The issue is reproducible whenever LogMiner returns STATUS=2 for rows containing non-ASCII characters on a non-UTF8 database.

---

**How to reproduce the issue using our [tutorial](https://github.com/debezium/debezium-examples/tree/main/tutorial) deployment?**

The tutorial uses an AL32UTF8 database, so it won't trigger this bug. To reproduce:

  1. Use an Oracle database with NLS_CHARACTERSET = WE8ISO8859P1
  2. Create a table with a VARCHAR2 column containing non-ASCII characters (e.g. ÅÄÖ)
  3. Configure the connector with log.mining.strategy: hybrid
  4. Trigger a LogMiner STATUS=2 event (SQL reconstruction failure) for that table — this can happen during a database restart while the
  connector is streaming
  5. Observe that the HEXTORAW bytes are decoded as UTF-8 instead of Latin-1

  Proposed fix with unit tests: https://github.com/debezium/debezium/pull/7309

  The fix adds getDatabaseCharacterSet() to OracleConnection (querying NLS_CHARACTERSET via NLS_CHARSET_ID()) and uses new CHAR(bytes,
  databaseCharacterSet).toString() instead of new String(bytes, StandardCharsets.UTF_8) — matching the pattern already used for
  NCHAR/NVARCHAR on the line above.
author: Aangbaeck

project_fields.Title: Oracle LogMiner HEXTORAW string decoding hardcodes UTF-8, corrupts non-ASCII characters on non-UTF8 databases

title: TypeRegistry initialization triggers pg_type query twice at startup
body:
## Bug report

While investigating issues such as #1683, I noticed something that seems unnecessary.

At Debezium startup, it appears that the process which loads all entries from pg_type is executed twice. I think this could be done only once.

**What Debezium connector do you use and what version?**

3.5.0

**What is the connector configuration?**

This behavior does not seem to depend on the configuration, but for example, it occurs with the following:

```
{
  "name": "twice-type-register-connector",
  "config": {
    "connector.class": "io.debezium.connector.postgresql.PostgresConnector",
    "database.hostname": "localhost",
    "database.port": "5432",
    "database.user": "atorik",
    "database.password": "xxx",
    "database.dbname": "db1",
    "topic.prefix": "example",
    "slot.name": "twice_type_register",
    "plugin.name": "pgoutput",
    "publication.autocreate.mode": "disabled",
    "publication.name": "p1"
  }
}
```
---

**What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?**

_E.g. on-premises, with a specific cloud provider, etc._

On-premises.

---

**What behavior do you expect?**

The SQL query that loads all entries from pg_type should be executed only once.

---

**What behavior do you see?**

The SQL query that loads all entries from pg_type is executed twice.

When PostgreSQL is configured with log_statement = all, the logs clearly show that the query defined as [SQL_TYPES](https://github.com/debezium/debezium/blob/0c40827d4fbf62f716dda1849bcf920ddd3a8f3a/debezium-connector-postgres/src/main/java/io/debezium/connector/postgresql/TypeRegistry.java#L385) in TypeRegistry is executed twice.

For example:

```
rg "n.nspname != \'pg_toast\'$" data/log/postgresql-2026-04-08.log
23:2026-04-08 22:46:45.712 JST [50915] LOG:  00000: execute <unnamed>: SELECT t.oid AS oid, t.typname AS name, t.typelem AS element, t.typbasetype AS parentoid, t.typtypmod as modifiers, t.typcategory as category, e.values as enum_values FROM pg_catalog.pg_type t JOIN pg_catalog.pg_namespace n ON (t.typnamespace = n.oid) LEFT JOIN (SELECT t.enumtypid as id, array_agg(t.enumlabel) as values FROM pg_catalog.pg_enum t GROUP BY id) e ON (t.oid = e.id) WHERE n.nspname != 'pg_toast'
42:2026-04-08 22:46:45.750 JST [50917] LOG:  00000: execute <unnamed>: SELECT t.oid AS oid, t.typname AS name, t.typelem AS element, t.typbasetype AS parentoid, t.typtypmod as modifiers, t.typcategory as category, e.values as enum_values FROM pg_catalog.pg_type t JOIN pg_catalog.pg_namespace n ON (t.typnamespace = n.oid) LEFT JOIN (SELECT t.enumtypid as id, array_agg(t.enumlabel) as values FROM pg_catalog.pg_enum t GROUP BY id) e ON (t.oid = e.id) WHERE n.nspname != 'pg_toast'
```

---

**Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?**

Yes.

---

**Do you have the connector logs, ideally from start till finish?**

_You might be asked later to provide [DEBUG/TRACE](https://debezium.io/documentation/reference/stable/operations/logging.html) level log._

Yes. I can provide them if needed.

---

**How to reproduce the issue using our [tutorial](https://github.com/debezium/debezium-examples/tree/main/tutorial) deployment?**

Simply starting the PostgreSQL source connector reproduces the issue.

---

**Cause**

In addition to the connection created by connectionFactory, another connection is created for Bean registration. The constructor of TypeRegistry is invoked for both connections:

```java
102:    public ChangeEventSourceCoordinator<PostgresPartition, PostgresOffsetContext> start(Configuration config) {
...
121:        MainConnectionProvidingConnectionFactory<PostgresConnection> connectionFactory = new DefaultMainConnectionProvidingConnectionFactory<>(
                () -> new PostgresConnection(connectorConfig.getJdbcConfig(), valueConverterBuilder, PostgresConnection.CONNECTION_GENERAL));
...
150:        // Manual Bean Registration
151:        beanRegistryJdbcConnection = connectionFactory.newConnection();
```

I placed a breakpoint in the TypeRegistry constructor and captured the backtraces for both invocations.

First invocation:

```
TypeRegistry.<init>(PostgresConnection) (/Users/atorik/debezium/debezium-connector-postgres/src/main/java/io/debezium/connector/postgresql/TypeRegistry.java:134)
PostgresConnection.<init>(JdbcConfiguration,PostgresConnection$PostgresValueConverterBuilder,String) (/Users/atorik/debezium/debezium-connector-postgres/src/main/java/io/debezium/connector/postgresql/connection/PostgresConnection.java:119)
PostgresConnectorTask.lambda$start$1(PostgresConnectorConfig,PostgresConnection$PostgresValueConverterBuilder) (/Users/atorik/debezium/debezium-connector-postgres/src/main/java/io/debezium/connector/postgresql/PostgresConnectorTask.java:122)
0x00007fe0014d5aa8.newConnection() (Unknown Source:0)
DefaultMainConnectionProvidingConnectionFactory.<init>(ConnectionFactory) (/Users/atorik/debezium/debezium-connector-common/src/main/java/io/debezium/jdbc/DefaultMainConnectionProvidingConnectionFactory.java:16)
PostgresConnectorTask.start(Configuration) (/Users/atorik/debezium/debezium-connector-postgres/src/main/java/io/debezium/connector/postgresql/PostgresConnectorTask.java:121)
BaseSourceTask.start(Map) (/Users/atorik/debezium/debezium-connector-common/src/main/java/io/debezium/connector/common/BaseSourceTask.java:281)
AbstractWorkerSourceTask.initializeAndStart() (/connect-runtime-4.1.1.jar/org.apache.kafka.connect.runtime/AbstractWorkerSourceTask.class:288)
WorkerTask.doStart() (/connect-runtime-4.1.1.jar/org.apache.kafka.connect.runtime/WorkerTask.class:191)
WorkerTask.doRun() (/connect-runtime-4.1.1.jar/org.apache.kafka.connect.runtime/WorkerTask.class:242)
WorkerTask.run() (/connect-runtime-4.1.1.jar/org.apache.kafka.connect.runtime/WorkerTask.class:298)
AbstractWorkerSourceTask.run() (/connect-runtime-4.1.1.jar/org.apache.kafka.connect.runtime/AbstractWorkerSourceTask.class:83)
Plugins.lambda$withClassLoader$1(ClassLoader,Runnable) (/connect-runtime-4.1.1.jar/org.apache.kafka.connect.runtime.isolation/Plugins.class:254)
0x00007fe0014a8238.run() (Unknown Source:0)
Executors$RunnableAdapter.call() (/java.base/java.util.concurrent/Executors.class:545)
FutureTask.run() (/java.base/java.util.concurrent/FutureTask.class:328)
ThreadPoolExecutor.runWorker(ThreadPoolExecutor$Worker) (/java.base/java.util.concurrent/ThreadPoolExecutor.class:1095)
ThreadPoolExecutor$Worker.run() (/java.base/java.util.concurrent/ThreadPoolExecutor.class:619)
Thread.runWith(Object,Runnable) (/java.base/java.lang/Thread.class:1460)
Thread.run() (/java.base/java.lang/Thread.class:1447)
```

2nd invocation:


```
TypeRegistry.<init>(PostgresConnection) (/Users/atorik/debezium/debezium-connector-postgres/src/main/java/io/debezium/connector/postgresql/TypeRegistry.java:134)
PostgresConnection.<init>(JdbcConfiguration,PostgresConnection$PostgresValueConverterBuilder,String) (/Users/atorik/debezium/debezium-connector-postgres/src/main/java/io/debezium/connector/postgresql/connection/PostgresConnection.java:119)
PostgresConnectorTask.lambda$start$1(PostgresConnectorConfig,PostgresConnection$PostgresValueConverterBuilder) (/Users/atorik/debezium/debezium-connector-postgres/src/main/java/io/debezium/connector/postgresql/PostgresConnectorTask.java:122)
0x00007fe0014d5aa8.newConnection() (Unknown Source:0)
DefaultMainConnectionProvidingConnectionFactory.newConnection() (/Users/atorik/debezium/debezium-connector-common/src/main/java/io/debezium/jdbc/DefaultMainConnectionProvidingConnectionFactory.java:26)
PostgresConnectorTask.start(Configuration) (/Users/atorik/debezium/debezium-connector-postgres/src/main/java/io/debezium/connector/postgresql/PostgresConnectorTask.java:151)
BaseSourceTask.start(Map) (/Users/atorik/debezium/debezium-connector-common/src/main/java/io/debezium/connector/common/BaseSourceTask.java:281)
AbstractWorkerSourceTask.initializeAndStart() (/connect-runtime-4.1.1.jar/org.apache.kafka.connect.runtime/AbstractWorkerSourceTask.class:288)
WorkerTask.doStart() (/connect-runtime-4.1.1.jar/org.apache.kafka.connect.runtime/WorkerTask.class:191)
WorkerTask.doRun() (/connect-runtime-4.1.1.jar/org.apache.kafka.connect.runtime/WorkerTask.class:242)
WorkerTask.run() (/connect-runtime-4.1.1.jar/org.apache.kafka.connect.runtime/WorkerTask.class:298)
AbstractWorkerSourceTask.run() (/connect-runtime-4.1.1.jar/org.apache.kafka.connect.runtime/AbstractWorkerSourceTask.class:83)
Plugins.lambda$withClassLoader$1(ClassLoader,Runnable) (/connect-runtime-4.1.1.jar/org.apache.kafka.connect.runtime.isolation/Plugins.class:254)
0x00007fe0014a8238.run() (Unknown Source:0)
Executors$RunnableAdapter.call() (/java.base/java.util.concurrent/Executors.class:545)
FutureTask.run() (/java.base/java.util.concurrent/FutureTask.class:328)
ThreadPoolExecutor.runWorker(ThreadPoolExecutor$Worker) (/java.base/java.util.concurrent/ThreadPoolExecutor.class:1095)
ThreadPoolExecutor$Worker.run() (/java.base/java.util.concurrent/ThreadPoolExecutor.class:619)
Thread.runWith(Object,Runnable) (/java.base/java.lang/Thread.class:1460)
Thread.run() (/java.base/java.lang/Thread.class:1447)
```

The difference in backtraces shows that they originate from different locations in PostgresConnectorTask (lines 121 and 151):

```
< DefaultMainConnectionProvidingConnectionFactory.<init>(ConnectionFactory) (/Users/atorik/debezium/debezium-core/src/main/java/io/debezium/jdbc/DefaultMainConnectionProvidingConnectionFactory.java:16)
< PostgresConnectorTask.start(Configuration) (/Users/atorik/debezium/debezium-connector-postgres/src/main/java/io/debezium/connector/postgresql/PostgresConnectorTask.java:121)
---
> DefaultMainConnectionProvidingConnectionFactory.newConnection() (/Users/atorik/debezium/debezium-core/src/main/java/io/debezium/jdbc/DefaultMainConnectionProvidingConnectionFactory.java:26)
> PostgresConnectorTask.start(Configuration) (/Users/atorik/debezium/debezium-connector-postgres/src/main/java/io/debezium/connector/postgresql/PostgresConnectorTask.java:151)
```
---

**Consideration/Proposal**

In my environment, after the fix for #1683, the initialization time of TypeRegistry has been significantly reduced, so the performance impact of this issue is currently small. However, if this twice registration is unnecessary, it would be better to avoid it:

- It removes redundant processing
- It would reduce the potential performance impact by roughly half if TypeRegistry initialization becomes expensive because of another reasons.

From what I observed, the connection registered for the Bean registry seems to be used only for ReselectColumns, and TypeRegistry does not appear to be required for that purpose.

Unless there are objections to fix this issue, I am planning to open a PR.
author: atorik

title: Oracle RAC: LogFileNotFoundException due to archive log dedup ignoring redo thread
body:
## Bug report

**What Debezium connector do you use and what version?**

Oracle connector, 3.5.0.Beta1. Also confirmed on `main` (3.6.0-SNAPSHOT) — the code is unchanged.

---

**What is the connector configuration?**

```properties
connector.class=io.debezium.connector.oracle.OracleConnector
database.connection.adapter=logminer
log.mining.strategy=online_catalog
lob.enabled=true
snapshot.mode=no_data
```

---

**What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?**

Oracle 23ai Enterprise Edition, 2-node RAC (on-premises).

---

**What behavior do you expect?**

On Oracle RAC, archive logs from all redo threads should be included in the LogMiner session. Each thread maintains its own independent sequence counter, so thread 1 seq 29 and thread 2 seq 29 are different log files that must both be mined.

---

**What behavior do you see?**

`LogFileCollector.mergeLogsByPrecedence()` deduplicates archive logs using only the sequence number as the key. On RAC where threads share the same sequence numbers, this drops all archive logs for threads after the first.

The connector then fails with `LogFileNotFoundException` because the dropped thread has no archive log covering the offset SCN:

```
Redo thread 2 is inconsistent; does not have a log that contains scn 2661984.
```

After 6 retries the connector crashes.

**Root cause** — line 289 of `LogFileCollector.java`:

```java
final Set<BigInteger> sequencesSeen = new HashSet<>();  // keyed by sequence only
...
if (!sequencesSeen.contains(logFile.getSequence())) {   // ignores thread
```

Thread 1 seq 29 is added, then thread 2 seq 29 is skipped because `sequencesSeen` already contains 29.

**Fix** — use `(thread, sequence)` as the composite key:

```java
record ThreadSequence(int thread, BigInteger sequence) {}
final Set<ThreadSequence> seen = new HashSet<>();
...
var key = new ThreadSequence(logFile.getThread(), logFile.getSequence());
if (!seen.contains(key)) {
```

---

**Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?**

Yes. The code in `mergeLogsByPrecedence()` on `upstream/main` (3.6.0-SNAPSHOT) is identical — still uses `Set<BigInteger>` keyed by sequence only.

---

**Do you have the connector logs, ideally from start till finish?**

DEBUG-level log showing the archive log collection and consistency check failure:

```
Archive log 1_29_*.arc Seq# 29 Thread# 1 ... to be added.
Archive log 2_29_*.arc Seq# 29 Thread# 2 ... to be added.
Archive log 1_30_*.arc Seq# 30 Thread# 1 ... to be added.
Archive log 2_30_*.arc Seq# 30 Thread# 2 ... to be added.
...
Performing consistency check on the following collected logs:
  Log: 1_29_*.arc thread=1
  Log: 1_30_*.arc thread=1
  Log: 1_31_*.arc thread=1
  Log: redo_t1_g18.log thread=1 CURRENT
  Log: redo_t2_g20.log thread=2 CURRENT    <-- thread 2 has ONLY the current online redo
Redo Thread 1 is consistent.
Redo thread 2 is inconsistent; does not have a log that contains scn 2661984.
```

Thread 2's archive logs (seq 29, 30, 31) were dropped by `mergeLogsByPrecedence()` because thread 1 already claimed those sequence numbers.

---

**How to reproduce the issue using our tutorial deployment?**

Requires Oracle RAC (2+ nodes). Does not reproduce on single-instance Oracle because there is only one redo thread with unique sequence numbers.

No RAC is needed to verify the fix — the unit test demonstrates the bug:

```java
@Test
public void testMergeLogsByPrecedenceWithRacOverlappingSequences() {
    // Thread 1 and thread 2 both have seq 29, 30, 31
    files.add(createArchiveLog("1_29.arc", ..., 29, 1));
    files.add(createArchiveLog("2_29.arc", ..., 29, 2));
    files.add(createArchiveLog("1_30.arc", ..., 30, 1));
    files.add(createArchiveLog("2_30.arc", ..., 30, 2));
    files.add(createArchiveLog("1_31.arc", ..., 31, 1));
    files.add(createArchiveLog("2_31.arc", ..., 31, 2));

    List<LogFile> results = mergeLogsByPrecedence(logs, destinations);
    assertThat(results).hasSize(6);  // FAILS without fix: returns 3
}
```

**Conditions to trigger:**

1. Oracle RAC — multiple redo threads with overlapping sequence numbers
2. Offset SCN in archived logs — connector restarts or falls behind current redo
3. Archive logs exist for multiple threads

This does NOT trigger when the offset SCN is within the current online redo, because only online redo logs are returned (no archive dedup needed).
author: rophy

project_fields.Title: Oracle RAC: LogFileNotFoundException due to archive log dedup ignoring redo thread

title: Configure Avro serialization automatically when detecting link to a schema registry [DBZ-59]
body:
Migrated from [DBZ-59](https://issues.redhat.com/browse/DBZ-59)

'-It'd probably be nice if our Docker images and tutorial could use the Confluent Schema Registry. In fact, it'd be awesome if this were automatically configured when the Connect service is started with a link to the Schema Registry container, or do the current behavior if no such link is specified.-

Update: the Avro converter has already been added to our image and is described in the documention. So we're re-purposing this issue for a usability improvement. Change the start-up script so that the Avro serializer is automatically used when detecting a link to the schema registry image ({{SCHEMA_REGISTRY}} env var is present).

**How to reproduce the issue using our [tutorial](https://github.com/debezium/debezium-examples/tree/main/tutorial) deployment?**

Not prepared

---

The [MongoDB Context](https://github.com/debezium/debezium/blob/b0817be92e98ce42b0b82d997373178eff153880/debezium-connector-mongodb/src/main/java/io/debezium/connector/mongodb/snapshot/MongoDbIncrementalSnapshotContext.java#L210-L222) on restart (or interruption) of the Connector does not seem to restore the additional conditions (`DATA_COLLECTIONS_TO_SNAPSHOT_KEY_ADDITIONAL_CONDITION`).

title: Standardize Java versions and adopt Debezium BOM for dependency management in debezium-examples
body:
#### Current Behaviour 

Currently, each example in the repository uses different Java versions and manages Debezium dependencies individually. This creates several issues:

  1. **Inconsistent Java versions** - Examples use different `maven.compiler.source`/`target` versions (e.g., some use Java 11, others may use different
  versions)
  2. **Manual dependency management** - Each example specifies Debezium dependency versions independently, making upgrades error-prone
  3. **Version conflicts** - During version updates (like the recent 3.5 upgrade), incompatibility issues arise due to inconsistent dependency
  versions
4. **Maintenance burden** - Every Debezium version upgrade requires updating multiple POMs across all examples

#### Expected Behaviour 

  1. **Standardize Java version**: Choose a single Java version (e.g., Java 17 or 21) for all examples
     - Update all `pom.xml` files to use consistent `maven.compiler.source` and `maven.compiler.target` properties
     - Document the minimum Java version requirement in the main README

  2. **Adopt Debezium BOM**: Use Debezium's Bill of Materials (BOM) for dependency management
     - Import `io.debezium:debezium-bom` in the `<dependencyManagement>` section
     - Remove explicit version declarations from Debezium dependencies in individual examples
     - This ensures all Debezium components use compatible versions automatically

####  Related

  - debezium/dbz#1165 PR discussion
author: vsantonastaso

project_fields.Title: Standardize Java versions and adopt Debezium BOM for dependency management in debezium-examples

title: Implement automated testing framework for debezium-examples
body:
#### Current Behaviour

  Most examples in the repository lack automated tests, making it difficult to:
  - Verify examples work correctly after Debezium version upgrades
  - Catch breaking changes before they reach users
  - Ensure examples continue to work as Docker images and dependencies evolve
  - Maintain confidence in example code quality

  Currently, only the `postgres-failover-slots` example has automated testing via GitHub Actions.

  #### Expected Behaviour

  Implement a test framework similar to the `postgres-failover-slots` example for all applicable examples.

  **Reference Implementation:**

  The `postgres-failover-slots` example demonstrates a working approach:
  - **Test script**: `scripts/run-example-test.py` - Python-based test runner
  - **Test manifest**: `postgres-failover-slots/test.yaml` - Declarative YAML DSL for test steps
  - **GitHub Actions**: `.github/workflows/postgres-failover-slots-workflow.yml` - CI integration

  The test framework supports:
  - Docker Compose service orchestration
  - HTTP endpoint polling (for service readiness)
  - Database operations via `docker compose exec`
  - Kafka message consumption and verification
  - Flexible wait conditions and timeouts


  Related

  - debezium/dbz#1165 PR discussion
author: vsantonastaso

project_fields.Title: Implement automated testing framework for debezium-examples

title: Unify Xstream behavior with LogMiner when using DBMS_LOB methods [DBZ-4741]
body:
Migrated from [DBZ-4741](https://issues.redhat.com/browse/DBZ-4741)

In DBZ-4366, LogMiner adapter was adjusted to emit multiple {{DBSM_LOB}} method calls to methods such as {{APPEND}} as a single event rather than individual events.  This allows the consumer to receive the data for LOB package methods as a single event.

Unfortunately, Xstream emits these as separate events.  We've documented that this is a nuanced behavior of Xstream currently but would like to look into how to unify the behavior and avoid this nuance if possible.
author: jpechane

title: Schema history: optimize memory usage
body:
## Feature request or enhancement

_For feature requests or enhancements, please provide the following information:_

**Which use case/requirement will be addressed by the proposed feature?**

`We can greatly improve memory usage when the database schema contains identical tables. This is a typical situation for e.g. multi-tenant databases where each tenant has its own schema with the same structure as the other tenants.`

---

**Implementation ideas (optional)**

`By "interning" (i.e. similarly to String.intern() API) schema history objects (basically tables, columns, attributes), we keep only one copy for each of them, thus reducing drastically memory usage.`
author: ochedru

title: Triggering a blocking snapshot can occur before the streaming loop is ready
body:
After a recent change to `ChangeEventSourceCoordinator` in debezium/dbz#1778, the `ChangeEventSourceCoordinator` does not initialize the `streaming` member variable when streaming actually begins. This previously used to be triggered when `doBlockingSnapshot` was called, but was removed.

As a result, when `doBlockingSnapshot` is triggered by a signal, it starts the blocking snapshot without waiting for the streaming loop to indicate it is in a state for the blocking snapshot to execute. This is because the `streaming` member variable is never initialized to `true`, and when the context waits for streaming to pause, the condition wait is skipped as a result.

For Oracle, this yields a ResultSet exception as the Snapshot and Streaming reuse the same JDBC connection, and the triggering on the blocking snapshot invalidates the ResultSet that the streaming loop is still using.
author: Naros

project_fields.Title: Triggering a blocking snapshot can occur before the streaming loop is ready

title: Move `TOPIC_PREFIX` configuration from `CONNECTION` group to `CONNECTOR` one
body: Currently the `TOPIC_PREFIX` field is inside the `CONNECTION` that does not fit correctly the meaning of the property. Moving to `CONNECTOR` group also facilitate the Debezium Platform UI to put all related `CONNECTION` properties on the `Connection` form.
author: mfvitale

project_fields.Title: Move `TOPIC_PREFIX` configuration from `CONNECTION` group to `CONNECTOR` one

title: Update google cloud BOM to 26.79.0
body:
## Feature request or enhancement

Currently in debezium server we use 26.17.0 for the Google Cloud Libraries BOM to support the pubsub sink.  I would like to update this to 26.79.0 which is the latest stable release here:  https://mvnrepository.com/artifact/com.google.cloud/libraries-bom/26.79.0

This is a pretty big jump so would need to check the impact of this.  I'm happy to make the change and do some testing to make sure nothing breaks.

The main underlying reason I would like to update is to hopefully improve resilience when there are issues with the underlying network while we attempt to push messages to pubsub.
author: nathan-smit-1

project_fields.Title: Update google cloud BOM to 26.79.0

title: Add imagePullPolicy support to Helm chart
body:
Currently, the Helm chart does not expose `imagePullPolicy` for the `stage` and `conductor` deployments, making it impossible to control image pull behaviour without modifying the chart directly.

Add `imagePullPolicy` as a configurable value for both deployments, defaulting to `IfNotPresent`.
author: pxcamus

project_fields.Title: Add imagePullPolicy support to Helm chart

title: Debezium Connector for MongDB cannot handle AVRO Schemas for collections starting with numbers [DBZ-2046]
body:
Migrated from [DBZ-2046](https://issues.redhat.com/browse/DBZ-2046)

I am trying to deploy a new Debezium Connector for MongoDB with Transforms. The configuration looks like this:
{code}
{"name": "mongo_source_connector_autostate",
    "config": {
    "connector.class": "io.debezium.connector.mongodb.MongoDbConnector", 
    "tasks.max":1,
    "initial.sync.max.threads":4,
    "mongodb.hosts": "rs0/FE0VMC1980:27017", 
    "mongodb.name": "mongo", 
    "collection.whitelist": "DASMongoDB.*_AutoState",
    "transforms": "unwrap",
    "transforms.unwrap.type" : "io.debezium.connector.mongodb.transforms.UnwrapFromMongoDbEnvelope",
    "transforms.sanitize.field.names" : true
    }}
{code}

Incidentally the collection names starts with number and I face the following error:
{code}
org.apache.kafka.connect.errors.ConnectException: Tolerance exceeded in error handler
        at org.apache.kafka.connect.runtime.errors.RetryWithToleranceOperator.execAndHandleError(RetryWithToleranceOperator.java:178)
        at org.apache.kafka.connect.runtime.errors.RetryWithToleranceOperator.execute(RetryWithToleranceOperator.java:104)
        at org.apache.kafka.connect.runtime.WorkerSourceTask.convertTransformedRecord(WorkerSourceTask.java:290)
        at org.apache.kafka.connect.runtime.WorkerSourceTask.sendRecords(WorkerSourceTask.java:316)
        at org.apache.kafka.connect.runtime.WorkerSourceTask.execute(WorkerSourceTask.java:240)
        at org.apache.kafka.connect.runtime.WorkerTask.doRun(WorkerTask.java:177)
        at org.apache.kafka.connect.runtime.WorkerTask.run(WorkerTask.java:227)
        at java.util.concurrent.Executors$RunnableAdapter.call(Unknown Source)
        at java.util.concurrent.FutureTask.run(Unknown Source)
        at java.util.concurrent.ThreadPoolExecutor.runWorker(Unknown Source)
        at java.util.concurrent.ThreadPoolExecutor$Worker.run(Unknown Source)
        at java.lang.Thread.run(Unknown Source)
Caused by: org.apache.avro.SchemaParseException: Illegal initial character: 10019_AutoState
        at org.apache.avro.Schema.validateName(Schema.java:1528)
        at org.apache.avro.Schema.access$400(Schema.java:87)
        at org.apache.avro.Schema$Name.<init>(Schema.java:675)
        at org.apache.avro.Schema.createRecord(Schema.java:212)
        at io.confluent.connect.avro.AvroData.fromConnectSchema(AvroData.java:893)
        at io.confluent.connect.avro.AvroData.fromConnectSchema(AvroData.java:732)
        at io.confluent.connect.avro.AvroData.fromConnectSchema(AvroData.java:726)
        at io.confluent.connect.avro.AvroData.fromConnectData(AvroData.java:365)
        at io.confluent.connect.avro.AvroConverter.fromConnectData(AvroConverter.java:80)
        at org.apache.kafka.connect.storage.Converter.fromConnectData(Converter.java:62)
        at org.apache.kafka.connect.runtime.WorkerSourceTask.lambda$convertTransformedRecord$2(WorkerSourceTask.java:290)
        at org.apache.kafka.connect.runtime.errors.RetryWithToleranceOperator.execAndRetry(RetryWithToleranceOperator.java:128)
        at org.apache.kafka.connect.runtime.errors.RetryWithToleranceOperator.execAndHandleError(RetryWithToleranceOperator.java:162)
        ... 11 more
{code}

Therefore I can not apply any transforms.
author: jpechane

project_fields.Title: BlockingSnapshotIT randomly fails for MySQL 9.1 Percona and MariaDB 11.4.3 w/GTIDs

title: Add condition to Debezium Platform Chart to conditionally disable operator dependency
body:
Currently the Debezium Platform Helm Chart declares the operator chart has dependency but there is no way to disable it as done for the `database`. 

We should add a `condition: debezium-operator.enabled` to permit to skip the operator installation for those already has it installed.
author: mfvitale

project_fields.Title: Add condition to Debezium Platform Chart to conditionally disable operator dependency

title: Debezium Platform should permit to specify Debezium Server image to be used in the CRD
body:
Currently when a CRD is created based on the pipeline definition, the [image](https://debezium.io/documentation/reference/3.5/operations/debezium-operator.html#debezium-operator-schema-reference-debeziumserverspec-image) is not specified. So the operator will determine the image to use with [ServerImageProvider](https://github.com/debezium/debezium-operator/blob/main/debezium-operator-core/src/main/java/io/debezium/operator/core/ServerImageProvider.java). 

We should add an application property to the `conductor` so the DS image could be specified at deployment time. A default value should be added to `values.yaml` as for the `conductor` and `stage` images.
author: mfvitale

project_fields.Title: Debezium Platform should permit to specify Debezium Server image to be used in the CRD

title: AWS RDS Failover takes long time or requires manual intervention
body:
## Bug report

**What Debezium connector do you use and what version?**

`2.7.4`

---

**What is the connector configuration?**

```
  "connector.class": "io.debezium.connector.postgresql.PostgresConnector",
  "errors.log.include.messages": "true",
  "topic.creation.default.partitions": "-1",
  "schema.refresh.mode": "columns_diff_exclude_unchanged_toast",
  "producer.override.interceptor.classes": "io.debezium.tracing.DebeziumTracingProducerInterceptor",
  "heartbeat.action.query": "CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS heartbeat (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, ts TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE);\nINSERT INTO heartbeat (id, ts) VALUES (1, NOW()) ON CONFLICT(id) DO UPDATE SET ts=EXCLUDED.ts;\n",
  "topic.creation.default.replication.factor": "-1",
  "interval.handling.mode": "string",
  "errors.log.enable": "true",
  "key.converter": "org.apache.kafka.connect.storage.StringConverter",
  "database.dbname": "...",
  "database.user": "...",
  "heartbeat.interval.ms": "60000",
  "plugin.name": "pgoutput",
  "topic.creation.enable": "true",
  "key.converter.schemas.enable": "false",
  "database.password": "...",
  "value.converter.schemas.enable": "true",
  "errors.tolerance": "all",
  "slot.name": "...",
  "tasks.max": "1",
  "decimal.handling.mode": "string",
  "value.converter": "org.apache.kafka.connect.json.JsonConverter",
  "publication.autocreate.mode": "filtered",
  "producer.override.compression.type": "snappy",
  "database.port": "...",
  "database.hostname": "..."
```

---

**What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?**

_E.g. on-premises, with a specific cloud provider, etc._

`AWS RDS Postgres 17.5 with Multi A-Z`

---

**What behavior do you expect?**

During a database failover, the Debezium connector experiences a prolonged recovery period, typically taking around 20 minutes to resume normal operation. In some cases, manual intervention is required, and the Kafka Connect connector must be restarted to successfully reattach to the replication slots.

Notably, this behavior occurs without any visible errors or failure states reported by the connector, making it difficult to detect and diagnose automatically.

---

**What behavior do you see?**

It is expected that the failover is reliable and takes much less time, only a few minutes to fully recover.

---

**Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?**

_Ideally, also verify with the latest Alpha/Beta/CR version._

We also tried other versions such as `2.5.1`

---

**Do you have the connector logs, ideally from start till finish?**

_You might be asked later to provide [DEBUG/TRACE](https://debezium.io/documentation/reference/stable/operations/logging.html) level log._

```
Starting Kafka Connect with configuration:
##############################
##############################
# This file is automatically generated by the Strimzi Cluster Operator
# Any changes to this file will be ignored and overwritten!
##############################
##############################

##########
# Bootstrap servers
##########
bootstrap.servers=<REDACTED_BOOTSTRAP>

##########
# Config providers
##########
# Configuration providers configured by the user and by Strimzi
config.providers=secrets,strimzienv,strimzifile,strimzidir
config.providers.strimzienv.class=org.apache.kafka.common.config.provider.EnvVarConfigProvider
config.providers.strimzienv.param.allowlist.pattern=.*
config.providers.strimzifile.class=org.apache.kafka.common.config.provider.FileConfigProvider
config.providers.strimzidir.class=org.apache.kafka.common.config.provider.DirectoryConfigProvider
config.providers.strimzidir.param.allowed.paths=/opt/kafka

##########
# Provided configurations
##########
offset.storage.topic=kafka-connect-offsets
value.converter=org.apache.kafka.connect.json.JsonConverter
config.storage.topic=kafka-connect-configs
key.converter=org.apache.kafka.connect.json.JsonConverter
group.id=kafka-connect
status.storage.topic=kafka-connect-status
config.providers.secrets.class=io.strimzi.kafka.KubernetesSecretConfigProvider
config.storage.replication.factor=-1
offset.storage.replication.factor=-1
status.storage.replication.factor=-1
consumer.interceptor.classes=io.opentelemetry.instrumentation.kafkaclients.v2_6.TracingConsumerInterceptor
producer.interceptor.classes=io.opentelemetry.instrumentation.kafkaclients.v2_6.TracingProducerInterceptor


##########
# REST Listeners
##########
rest.advertised.host.name = <REDACTED>
rest.advertised.port = <REDACTED>

##########
# Plugins
##########
plugin.path=/opt/kafka/plugins

##########
# Additional information
##########
consumer.client.rack=${strimzidir:/opt/kafka/init:rack.id}

##########
# Kafka Security protocol
##########
security.protocol=PLAINTEXT
producer.security.protocol=PLAINTEXT
consumer.security.protocol=PLAINTEXT
admin.security.protocol=PLAINTEXT
