title: PostgreSQL connector: WARN spam from TypeRegistry in multi-schema deployments
body:
## Bug report

**What Debezium connector do you use and what version?**

`io.debezium.connector.postgresql.PostgresConnector`, version `3.4.3.Final` (also reproduced on `3.5.0.Final`)

---

**What is the connector configuration?**

```json
{
  "connector.class": "io.debezium.connector.postgresql.PostgresConnector",
  "plugin.name": "pgoutput",
  "publication.autocreate.mode": "disabled",
  "publication.name": "cdc",
  "table.include.list": ".+\\.(table_a|table_b|table_c|table_d|...)$",
  "tasks.max": "1",
  "snapshot.mode": "no_data",
  "heartbeat.interval.ms": "5000"
}
```

Note: no `schema.include.list` — we capture from all tenant schemas by design.

---

**What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?**

Amazon Aurora PostgreSQL, managed (AWS RDS), MSK Connect.

---

**What behavior do you expect?**

The "Type is already mapped" condition should not log at WARN level when the duplicate is simply a same-named type in a different schema. This is expected in any multi-tenant or multi-schema PostgreSQL deployment and is not indicative of a problem.

---

**What behavior do you see?**

~17,000 WARN-level log lines per connector startup:

```
WARN  Type [oid:278431, name:accounts] is already mapped   [io.debezium.connector.postgresql.TypeRegistry]
WARN  Type [oid:278432, name:users] is already mapped       [io.debezium.connector.postgresql.TypeRegistry]
WARN  Type [oid:278433, name:enrollments] is already mapped [io.debezium.connector.postgresql.TypeRegistry]
... (repeated for every type × every tenant schema)
```

On MSK Connect, there is no per-class log level configuration, so we cannot suppress these via logging config. The volume drowns out genuine warnings from the connector.

---

**Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?**

Yes — the `addType()` method is unchanged in `3.5.0.Final`. Not verified against any Alpha/Beta/CR builds, but the code path has not changed on `main`.

---

**Do you have the connector logs, ideally from start till finish?**

The relevant logs are the WARN lines themselves — samples are shown above.

---

**How to reproduce the issue using our [[tutorial](https://github.com/debezium/debezium-examples/tree/main/tutorial)](https://github.com/debezium/debezium-examples/tree/main/tutorial) deployment?**

1. Create a PostgreSQL database with multiple schemas sharing the same table definitions:

```sql
CREATE SCHEMA tenant_1;
CREATE SCHEMA tenant_2;
CREATE SCHEMA tenant_3;
-- repeat for N schemas

CREATE TABLE tenant_1.table_a (id bigint primary key, name text);
CREATE TABLE tenant_2.table_a (id bigint primary key, name text);
CREATE TABLE tenant_3.table_a (id bigint primary key, name text);
-- repeat for each schema
```

2. Configure the connector with `table.include.list` matching all schemas (no `schema.include.list`):

```json
{
  "table.include.list": ".+\\.table_a$"
}
```

3. Start the connector. Observe WARN lines for every `table_a` composite type after the first schema.

The number of warnings scales as `(num_schemas - 1) × num_types_per_schema`.

---

### Cause

In [`[TypeRegistry.addType()](https://github.com/debezium/debezium/blob/v3.5.0.Final/debezium-connector-postgres/src/main/java/io/debezium/connector/postgresql/TypeRegistry.java#L143-L147)`](https://github.com/debezium/debezium/blob/v3.5.0.Final/debezium-connector-postgres/src/main/java/io/debezium/connector/postgresql/TypeRegistry.java#L143-L147), the `nameToType` map is keyed solely by type name:

```java
if (!nameToType.containsKey(type.getName())) {
    nameToType.put(type.getName(), type);
}
else {
    LOGGER.warn("Type [oid:{}, name:{}] is already mapped", type.getOid(), type.getName());
}
```

The underlying `SQL_TYPES` query joins `pg_namespace` but the namespace is never propagated into the map key. When schema `tenant_1` and schema `tenant_2` both define a composite type `accounts`, the second one triggers a WARN.

In our environment (Canvas LMS — a multi-tenant PostgreSQL application), a single database has hundreds of schemas sharing identical table/type definitions. This produces **~17,000 WARN lines per connector startup**, repeated on every restart or rebalance.

### Why existing fixes don't help

- **PR #7150 (schema-filtered type loading)**: Filters `pg_type` to `schema.include.list` schemas. Our connector captures tables from _all_ schemas (`table.include.list: ".+\\.(table_names...)$"`) — we have no `schema.include.list` because every tenant schema is needed. Even if we enumerated all schemas, the duplicates across them would still trigger warnings.
- **DBZ-5182 (ascending OID order)**: Addressed type _collisions_ from extension schemas overriding built-in types, but did not change the log level or namespace handling.
- **#1683 / #1800 (performance fixes)**: Reduced startup time from OID lookups and double initialization, but the WARN-level logging remains.

### Relationship to [DBZ-8519](https://github.com/debezium/dbz/issues/1323)

[DBZ-8519](https://github.com/debezium/dbz/issues/1323) reports the same symptom but from a different angle — the reporter has many schemas and expected `schema.include.list` to suppress the warnings. Our case is distinct: we intentionally capture all schemas, so schema filtering is not a viable workaround. The underlying code issue (name-only keying, WARN log level) is the same.

### Suggested fix

Downgrade the log level from `LOGGER.warn` to `LOGGER.debug` in `TypeRegistry.addType()`. The "already mapped" condition is a normal consequence of PostgreSQL's type system when multiple schemas define same-named types. It is not a correctness issue — the first-registered type wins, and `oidToType` still maps every OID correctly. The `nameToType` map is used for well-known type name lookups (e.g., `hstore`, `geometry`) that don't carry schema context, so the first-wins behavior is the right semantics here.
author: mark-major

project_fields.Title: PostgreSQL connector: WARN spam from TypeRegistry in multi-schema deployments

title: Debezium Sql Server connector stuck after connection idle-killed during schema history recovery
body:
When schema history recovery on a connector with a large history topic exceeds an intermediate LB / firewall idle timeout, the JDBC connections are silently closed during the replay and the first SQL call after recovery throws `SQLServerException: The connection is broken and recovery is not possible`. The error is retriable, so `BaseSourceTask` enters its retry loop, but every retry re-runs schema history recovery on fresh-but-equally-doomed connections and never makes forward progress.

## Bug report

**What Debezium connector do you use and what version?**

SQL Server. Reproduced on `3.2.x` and `3.5.0`. Vulnerable code path is also present on main.

---

**What is the connector configuration?**

```json
{
  "connector.class": "io.debezium.connector.sqlserver.SqlServerConnector",
  "database.hostname": "<lb-host>",
  "database.port": "1433",
  "database.user": "<user>",
  "database.password": "<pwd>",
  "database.names": "testDB",
  "topic.prefix": "server1",
  "schema.history.internal.kafka.bootstrap.servers": "broker:9092",
  "schema.history.internal.kafka.topic": "schema-changes.testdb",
  "snapshot.mode": "initial"
}
```

---

**What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?**

_E.g. on-premises, with a specific cloud provider, etc._

SQL Server 2019, behind an HAProxy TCP proxy with `timeout server` shorter than the schema history recovery duration. Equivalent to any deployment with an LB / firewall on the JDBC path that has an application-level idle timeout.

---

**What behavior do you expect?**

If the JDBC connections are killed by an LB / firewall during schema history recovery, the connector should detect the broken connection on the first SQL call of `executeIteration()`, refresh it, and resume streaming.

---

**What behavior do you see?**

The connector enters a permanent retry loop. Sequence on every retry:

1. `BaseSourceTask.start(config)` opens fresh `dataConnection` and `metadataConnection`.
2. `coordinator.start()` runs `schema.recover(previousOffsets)` - reads the schema history topic from Kafka. JDBC connections sit idle the entire time.
3. The LB's application-level idle timer fires and closes the backend connection.
4. Recovery completes, snapshot is skipped (offset records prior completion), streaming starts.
5. First SQL call (`commitTransaction()` or `getToLsn()` at the top of `executeIteration()`) throws:
```
com.microsoft.sqlserver.jdbc.SQLServerException: The connection is broken and recovery is not possible.
The connection is marked by the server as unrecoverable. No attempt was made to restore the connection.
    at com.microsoft.sqlserver.jdbc.SQLServerException.makeFromDriverError(SQLServerException.java:231)
    at io.debezium.connector.sqlserver.SqlServerConnection.getNthTransactionLsnFromLast(SqlServerConnection.java:313)
    at io.debezium.connector.sqlserver.SqlServerStreamingChangeEventSource.getToLsn(...)
    at io.debezium.connector.sqlserver.SqlServerStreamingChangeEventSource.executeIteration(...)
```
6. `SqlServerErrorHandler` classifies it retriable and restarts the connector looping back to step 1.

**Why the existing mechanisms don't recover this:**

1. **TCP keepalives are already on by default.** The driver hardcodes `TCP_KEEPIDLE=30s` / `TCP_KEEPINTERVAL=1s` on every socket via `Socket.setOption()`.
2. **OS-level keepalive doesn't help** The driver's per-socket `setOption(TCP_KEEPIDLE, 30)` overrides any `net.ipv4.tcp_keepalive_*` on the host, and there is no driver-level configuration to disable or tune the per-socket setting.
3. **Connection Resiliency does not engage.** (`connectRetryCount / connectRetryInterval`) - empirically the driver flags the session state as non-recoverable and skips its own retry.

**Suggested fix:**

At the top of `executeIteration()`, wrap `commitTransaction()` + `endTransaction()` + `getToLsn()` in a `try` / `catch (SQLException)`. In the catch, probe both `dataConnection` and `metadataConnection` with `isValid()`. If either is invalid, refresh only the invalid side(s) (`close()` + `connect()` on the JdbcConnection - clears the prepared-statement cache and re-runs initial setup on the fresh connection), then re-read `toLsn` once. If both connections are valid, rethrow - the SQLException is a real SQL error, not a connection issue.

Recovery is intentionally placed at this code site because `streamingExecutionContexts` (per-partition state, e.g. `lastProcessedPosition`) is mutated later in the iteration. Recovering at the top is safe because no iteration state has been touched yet; mid-iteration recovery would need explicit retry plumbing to keep state consistent with committed offsets.

Approach was discussed [here](https://debezium.zulipchat.com/#narrow/channel/348252-community-sqlserver/topic/Stuck.20after.20conn.20idle-killed.20during.20schema.20history.20recovery/with/591750238).

---

**Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?**

_Ideally, also verify with the latest Alpha/Beta/CR version._

Yes — the unwrapped call site is on main. Tested with connector version `3.5.0` - seeing the same behaviour and trace.

---

**Do you have the connector logs, ideally from start till finish?**

_You might be asked later to provide [DEBUG/TRACE](https://debezium.io/documentation/reference/stable/operations/logging.html) level log._

One full retry cycle (loop repeats):

```
[15:18:43,196] INFO  Started database schema history recovery
[15:21:58,713] INFO  Finished database schema history recovery of 7500500 change(s) in 195397 ms
[15:21:58,912] ERROR Producer failure
  com.microsoft.sqlserver.jdbc.SQLServerException: The connection is broken and recovery is not possible.
    The connection is marked by the server as unrecoverable. No attempt was made to restore the connection.
[15:21:58,920] WARN  Retry 1 of unlimited retries will be attempted
[15:21:59,256] WARN  Going to restart connector after 10 sec. after a retriable exception
[15:22:09,566] INFO  Successfully restarted task
[15:22:09,590] INFO  Started database schema history recovery
[15:25:21,262] INFO  Finished database schema history recovery of 7500500 change(s) in 191670 ms
[15:25:21,565] WARN  Going to restart connector after 10 sec. after a retriable exception
... loops indefinitely ...
```
---

**How to reproduce the issue using our [tutorial](https://github.com/debezium/debezium-examples/tree/main/tutorial) deployment?**

1. Start SQL Server with CDC enabled on enough tables that schema history recovery takes longer than the LB idle timeout (in our test: 500 tables with the schema history topic duplicated 15,000× to ~7.5M entries, recovery time ~2 minutes).
2. Put HAProxy in TCP mode in front of SQL Server with `timeout server` of e.g. 60s.
3. Start the Debezium SQL Server connector pointing at the HAProxy frontend. Wait for the initial snapshot to complete and offset to flush.
4. Restart the connector. The new task instance enters the retry loop described above.
author: 100maygupta

project_fields.Title: Debezium Sql Server connector stuck after connection idle-killed during schema history recovery

title: Use Adaptive polling in the Platform Stage UI in the resources listing pages.

## Feature request or enhancement

**Which use case/requirement will be addressed by the proposed feature?**

Currently, different listing pages poll different sets of data at a fixed 5-second interval:

Pipelines → pipelines
Sources → pipelines, sources
Transforms → pipelines, transforms
Destinations → pipelines, destinations
Connections → destinations, sources, connections

title: Auto truncate columns to adhere to a max message size
body:
## Feature request or enhancement

_For feature requests or enhancements, please provide the following information:_

**Which use case/requirement will be addressed by the proposed feature?**

We spend a decent bit of toil resizing and updating column truncation settings to ensure our kafka max message size is not breached. We have some tooling around auto detecting and validating this, but it would be ideal if Debezium had a config for a max message size or max length that it could use to calculate truncation thresholds and dynamically apply across all columns (eg as schema evolutions occur). This way it's fully handled by Debezium. Although not all users run kafka, there is often some restriction for output message size in bytes on whichever platform is used.

---

**Implementation ideas (optional)**

I am drafting a PR in the debezium vitess connector. If we believe it is useful enough for core I am happy to move it there.
author: twthorn

title: Document ORA-26913 RAC instance pinning issue for XStream adapter
body:
The current XStream documentation does not mention that ORA-26913 can occur in Oracle RAC environments when the JDBC connection is load-balanced to a different RAC instance than the one where the XStream outbound server is running.

When database.hostname and database.port are used, the Oracle listener can route the connection to any available RAC instance. If it lands on the wrong instance, XStream attach fails with:

oracle.streams.StreamsException: ORA-26913: must connect to instance X where XStream Outbound server is running

Additionally, the documentation does not mention that a PDB service name must not be used, as the XStream outbound server is defined at CDB level and connecting via a PDB service results in ORA-26701: Streams process does not exist.

The fix is to use database.url with a full TNS descriptor specifying an instance-pinned SERVICE_NAME and ENABLE=broken for TCP keepalive.

Add a FAQ entry to documentation/modules/ROOT/pages/connectors/oracle.adoc documenting the root cause and fix.
author: aqhil07

project_fields.Title: Document ORA-26913 RAC instance pinning issue for XStream adapter

title: Log test class/test name on each invocation
body:
One of the harder things with our tests on certain CI/CD platforms is that the failsafe/surefire output isn't preserved, so we don't have a solid way to identify in the raw stdout logs where a test begins or ends. This requires us to step through large classes to understand if a test starts/stops the connector N times, and try to map that cleanly to the output.

It would be useful if at the start of each test method, the test were to log
```
Running test io.debezium.connector.MyConnector : myFancySuperFunTest()
```

This can be done with a simple JUnit extension that is automatically registered by the ServiceLoader.

This follows-up on #1881.

title: Oracle OSON values not parsed correctly due to missing "/* JSON */" prefix in HEXTORAW detection (3.4.3.Final)
body:
## Bug report

**What Debezium connector do you use and what version?**  
`Debezium Oracle Connector 3.4.3.Final`

---
**What is the connector configuration?**
```json
{
  "connector.class": "io.debezium.connector.oracle.OracleConnector",
  "database.hostname": "<host>",
  "database.port": "1521",
  "database.user": "<user>",
  "database.password": "<password>",
  "database.dbname": "<dbname>",
  "database.pdb.name": "<pdb>",
  "database.server.name": "test",
  "table.include.list": "DEBEZIUM_TEST.TEST_TRANSACTIONS",
  "include.schema.changes": "false",
  "database.connection.adapter": "logminer"
}
```

---
**What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?**  
Oracle Database 21c (on-premises deployment)

---
**What behavior do you expect?**  
When capturing updates on columns of type `OSON`, Debezium should correctly extract and deserialize the JSON value from the redo log entry.

For example, when executing:

```sql
UPDATE DEBEZIUM_TEST.TEST_TRANSACTIONS t
SET t.EXTRA_DATA = '{"customerId":10111,"method":"CASH","status":"REFUND"}'
WHERE t.ID = '2';
```

I expect Debezium to correctly capture and emit the JSON value.

---
**What behavior do you see?**  

In the redo log, Oracle records the update in the following format:

```sql
update "DEBEZIUM_TEST"."TEST_TRANSACTIONS"
set "EXTRA_DATA" = /* JSON */ HEXTORAW('ff4a5a012106030019001c000030effc000b001200000a637573746f6d65724964066d6574686f64067374617475738403030102000b0010001523c302020c044341534806524546554e44')
where "ID" = '2'
```

However, in `OracleValueConverters.java`, the following constants are defined:

```java
public static final String HEXTORAW_FUNCTION_START = "HEXTORAW('";
public static final String HEXTORAW_FUNCTION_END = "')";
```

Since the actual redo log entry contains:

```sql
/* JSON */ HEXTORAW('...')
```

the connector fails to properly detect and extract the hex value for OSON fields.

It appears that `HEXTORAW_FUNCTION_START` should instead be:

```java
public static final String HEXTORAW_FUNCTION_START = "/* JSON */ HEXTORAW('";
```

so that OSON values can be correctly parsed from the redo SQL statement.

---
**Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?**  
This issue is reproducible with `3.4.3.Final`.  
Yes. It hasn't been modified yet.

---
**Do you have the connector logs, ideally from start till finish?**  
I can provide logs if needed. Please let me know if DEBUG or TRACE level logs are required.

---
**How to reproduce the issue using our tutorial deployment?**

1. Deploy Oracle and Debezium using the tutorial setup.
2. Create a table with an `OSON` column.
3. Insert and update a row with JSON content.
4. Observe the redo log representation via LogMiner:
   ```sql
   /* JSON */ HEXTORAW('...')
   ```
5. Verify that Debezium does not correctly extract the OSON value due to mismatch with `HEXTORAW_FUNCTION_START`.

---

Additionally, I would greatly appreciate it if you could provide a patched build (e.g., 3.4.3.Final with this fix), as this issue currently blocks our production usage.

Thank you!
author: SHSMJ200

project_fields.Title: Oracle OSON values not parsed correctly due to missing "/* JSON */" prefix in HEXTORAW detection (3.4.3.Final)

title: [Oracle] Add database.service.name configuration property
body:
I am working with Oracle XStream in a production RAC/CDB environment and have 
identified a gap in the Oracle connector configuration.

When using database.hostname and database.port, Debezium constructs the JDBC URL 
using database.dbname as the service name. In enterprise Oracle environments, the 
listener service name often differs from the database name, which results in:

  org.apache.kafka.connect.errors.RetriableException: Failed to resolve Oracle database version
  Caused by: java.sql.SQLRecoverableException: ORA-12514: Cannot connect to database.
  Service *** is not registered with the listener at host 10.1****5 port 1**7.

Changing database.dbname to the service name is not possible as it is used 
internally for CDC metadata and offset tracking. Changing it would break existing 
offsets stored in the offset storage topic.

The only current workaround is database.url with a full JDBC connection string, 
which bypasses database.hostname/database.port entirely.

**Proposal**: add an optional database.service.name property that, when set, is used 
as the service name in the JDBC URL instead of database.dbname.

I would like to check whether this is acceptable before investing time in 
implementation. If the team is open to it, I can implement and test this locally 
against my XStream environment and raise a PR as new feature.
author: aqhil07

project_fields.Title: [Oracle] Add database.service.name configuration property

title: Snapshot failing in finally, trying to close already-closed connections in pool
body:
## Bug report

**What Debezium connector do you use and what version?**

IBMi Source - 3.5.0.Final
** Issue is not specific to the connector; the bug is in the base Debezium code

---

**What is the connector configuration?**

Not particularly relevant, but can supply on demand.

---

**What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?**

_E.g. on-premises, with a specific cloud provider, etc._

IBMi DB2 AS/400 07.05.0000 V7R5m0
Bare metal Kafka, non-Docker

---

**What behavior do you expect?**

Snapshot to complete successfully

---

**What behavior do you see?**

At the end of the snapshot process, in the finally { } block an attempt is made to close the JDBC connections in the connection pool, but tries to close an already-closed connection.  This throws an exception and causes the entire snapshot to be considered failed.

---

**Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?**

_Ideally, also verify with the latest Alpha/Beta/CR version._

Experienced with the latest Debezium release, 3.5.0.Final.  The code is unchanged in 3.6.0.Alpha1.

---

**Do you have the connector logs, ideally from start till finish?**

_You might be asked later to provide [DEBUG/TRACE](https://debezium.io/documentation/reference/stable/operations/logging.html) level log._

Yes, but the vast majority is unnecessary.  The relevant part is:
```
[2026-05-05 05:55:13,087] INFO Finished chunk snapshot of 69 tables (1292 chunks); duration '00:00:00.0' (io.debezium.relational.RelationalSnapshotChangeEventSource:637)
[2026-05-05 05:55:13,619] INFO Creating thread debezium-jdbcconnection-JdbcConnection-jdbc-connection-close (io.debezium.util.Threads:311)
[2026-05-05 05:55:13,651] INFO Connection gracefully closed (io.debezium.jdbc.JdbcConnection:980)

[ ... repeated for each connection in the pool ... ]

[2026-05-05 05:55:13,651] ERROR Error in finally block (io.debezium.relational.RelationalSnapshotChangeEventSource:227)       
java.lang.RuntimeException: java.sql.SQLNonTransientConnectionException: The connection does not exist.
        at io.debezium.relational.RelationalSnapshotChangeEventSource.rollbackTransaction(RelationalSnapshotChangeEventSource.java:1234)
        at io.debezium.relational.RelationalSnapshotChangeEventSource.doExecute(RelationalSnapshotChangeEventSource.java:224) 
        at io.debezium.pipeline.source.AbstractSnapshotChangeEventSource.execute(AbstractSnapshotChangeEventSource.java:102)  
        at io.debezium.connector.db2as400.As400SnapshotChangeEventSource.execute(As400SnapshotChangeEventSource.java:70)      
        at io.debezium.connector.db2as400.As400SnapshotChangeEventSource.execute(As400SnapshotChangeEventSource.java:38)      
        at io.debezium.pipeline.ChangeEventSourceCoordinator.doSnapshot(ChangeEventSourceCoordinator.java:311)
        at io.debezium.pipeline.ChangeEventSourceCoordinator.doSnapshot(ChangeEventSourceCoordinator.java:295)
        at io.debezium.pipeline.ChangeEventSourceCoordinator.executeChangeEventSources(ChangeEventSourceCoordinator.java:206) 
        at io.debezium.pipeline.ChangeEventSourceCoordinator.lambda$start$0(ChangeEventSourceCoordinator.java:148)
        at java.base/java.util.concurrent.Executors$RunnableAdapter.call(Executors.java:572)
        at java.base/java.util.concurrent.FutureTask.run(FutureTask.java:317)
        at java.base/java.util.concurrent.ThreadPoolExecutor.runWorker(ThreadPoolExecutor.java:1144)
        at java.base/java.util.concurrent.ThreadPoolExecutor$Worker.run(ThreadPoolExecutor.java:642)
        at io.debezium.util.Threads$3.lambda$newThread$0(Threads.java:314)
        at java.base/java.lang.Thread.run(Thread.java:1583)
Caused by: java.sql.SQLNonTransientConnectionException: The connection does not exist.
        at com.ibm.as400.access.JDError.createSQLExceptionSubClass(JDError.java:877)
        at com.ibm.as400.access.JDError.throwSQLException(JDError.java:431)
        at com.ibm.as400.access.AS400JDBCConnectionImpl.checkOpen(AS400JDBCConnectionImpl.java:504)
        at com.ibm.as400.access.AS400JDBCConnectionImpl.rollback(AS400JDBCConnectionImpl.java:2732)
        at io.debezium.relational.RelationalSnapshotChangeEventSource.rollbackTransaction(RelationalSnapshotChangeEventSource.java:1231)
        ... 14 more
[2026-05-05 05:55:13,655] INFO Snapshot - Final stage (io.debezium.pipeline.source.AbstractSnapshotChangeEventSource:114)     
[2026-05-05 05:55:13,655] WARN Snapshot was not completed successfully , it will be re-executed upon connector restart (io.debezium.pipeline.source.AbstractSnapshotChangeEventSource:135)
```

The code in question is [RelationalSnapshotChangeEventSource:220](https://github.com/debezium/debezium/blob/b5dc01f5abf40362c3c193c9be760f307171d6f0/debezium-connector-common/src/main/java/io/debezium/relational/RelationalSnapshotChangeEventSource.java#L220)
```
        finally {
            try {
                if (connectionPool != null) {
                    for (JdbcConnection conn : connectionPool) {
                        if (!jdbcConnection.equals(conn)) {
==>                         conn.close();
                        }
                    }
                }
                rollbackTransaction(connection);
            }
            catch (final Exception e) {
                LOGGER.error("Error in finally block", e);
                if (exceptionWhileSnapshot != null) {
                    e.addSuppressed(exceptionWhileSnapshot);
                }
                throw e;
            }
        }
```

The .close() call should be caught, logged, and swallowed.
---

**How to reproduce the issue using our [tutorial](https://github.com/debezium/debezium-examples/tree/main/tutorial) deployment?**

This has only been experienced once out of ~25 full replication tests.  Unfortunately, it was during our production release, and 14 hours of replication had just finished.
author: battmush

title: [Oracle] Document uppercase schema/table name behaviour causing topic creation failures
body:
When using the Debezium Oracle connector, Oracle returns all schema and table names 
in uppercase (e.g., INVENTORY.CUSTOMERS). This applies to all connector adapters 
(XStream, LogMiner). With the default topic.delimiter of "-", Debezium generates 
topic names like server1-INVENTORY-CUSTOMERS.

Users who are happy to work with uppercase topic names are not affected. However, 
for users who require lowercase topic names (e.g., due to naming conventions or 
pre-created topics), the connector fails with:

  Task failed to create new topic (name=server1-INVENTORY-CUSTOMERS, 
  numPartitions=1, replicationFactor=1).
  Ensure that the task is authorized to create topics or that the topic exists 
  and restart the task.

This is expected Oracle behaviour but is not documented in oracle.adoc.

Fix: apply a LowerCaseTopic SMT to convert topic names to lowercase:

  transforms=LowerCaseTopicName
  transforms.LowerCaseTopicName.type=com.github.jcustenborder.kafka.connect.transform.common.LowerCaseTopic

I would like to add a note to oracle.adoc documenting this behaviour and the 
workaround.
author: aqhil07

project_fields.Title: [Oracle] Document uppercase schema/table name behaviour causing topic creation failures

title: OracleConnectorIT#shouldFailWhenConverterThrowsExceptionForValue fails randomly
body:
Fails randomly with
```
11:38:23,224 [ERROR] io.debezium.connector.oracle.OracleConnectorIT.shouldFailWhenConverterThrowsExceptionForValue -- Time elapsed: 11.19 s <<< ERROR!
java.lang.IllegalStateException: Engine is already being shutting down.
	at io.debezium.embedded.async.AsyncEmbeddedEngine.close(AsyncEmbeddedEngine.java:264)
	at io.debezium.embedded.async.TestingAsyncEmbeddedEngine.close(TestingAsyncEmbeddedEngine.java:34)
	at io.debezium.embedded.AbstractConnectorTest.stopConnector(AbstractConnectorTest.java:162)
	at io.debezium.embedded.AbstractConnectorTest.stopConnector(AbstractConnectorTest.java:147)
	at java.base/java.lang.reflect.Method.invoke(Method.java:580)
	at java.base/java.util.ArrayList.forEach(ArrayList.java:1596)
	at java.base/java.util.ArrayList.forEach(ArrayList.java:1596)
```

because exception (intentionally) thrown in the test starts engine shutdown and and engine shutdown is also run after each test. If those two overlap, it can result into this exception. We should wait in the test until engine shutdown is finished.
author: vjuranek

project_fields.Title: OracleConnectorIT#shouldFailWhenConverterThrowsExceptionForValue fails randomly

title: Add OpenTelemetry Java Agent support to Debezium Server distribution
body:
This issue tracks the integration of the OpenTelemetry Java Agent into the Debezium Server distribution module. The agent will collect metrics from Debezium JMX MBeans and export them via OTLP protocol to the OpenTelemetry Collector, establishing the foundation for observability in the Debezium Platform.

## Tasks

- [x] **Move OpenTelemetry Java Agent JAR to lib_metrics directory**
  - Relocate the Otel Java Agent JAR from the image to `debezium-server-dist/src/main/resources/distro/lib_metrics/`
  - Follow the same pattern used for the JMX Prometheus exporter

- [x] **Create enable_otel_agent.sh script**
  - Implement a script similar to existing `enable_exporter.sh`
  - Script should check for `OTEL_AGENT_ENABLED` environment variable
  - Configure `JAVA_OPTS` with Otel agent JAR path and configuration
  - Set default OTLP endpoint if not provided

- [x] **Create debezium-jmx-config.yaml configuration file**
  - Define JMX metric collection rules for Debezium MBeans
  - Map JMX attributes to OpenTelemetry metric names (snake_case convention)
  - Include rules for streaming metrics (events, lag, connection status)
  - Include rules for snapshot metrics (table count, progress, status)

- [x] **Update Debezium Server startup scripts**
  - Integrate `enable_otel_agent.sh` into the server startup process
  - Ensure conditional loading based on environment variable
  - Maintain compatibility with existing JMX exporter setup
author: mfvitale

The operator will inject necessary environment variables and resource attributes, allowing users to enable OpenTelemetry-based monitoring through the `DebeziumServer` custom resource. This provides an alternative to the existing JMX Exporter configuration.

## Tasks

body:
This issue adds OpenTelemetry Collector and Prometheus deployment capabilities to the Debezium Platform Helm chart. The collector aggregates metrics from all Debezium Server instances and exposes them in Prometheus format. The implementation supports three deployment scenarios: installing Prometheus Operator, using existing Prometheus Operator, or connecting to vanilla Prometheus.

## Tasks

- [x] **Add opentelemetry-operator Helm dependency**

- [x] **Configure processors in collector**

  - Add resource processor to inject platform-level attributes

  - Make processor settings configurable via Helm values

- [x] **Configure Prometheus exporter in collector**

  - Enable resource-to-telemetry attribute conversion
  - Add constant labels (e.g., `platform: debezium-platform`)

- [x] **Create ServiceMonitor for OpenTelemetry Collector**
  - Create Helm template for ServiceMonitor resource
  - Configure selector to match Otel Collector service (label: `app: otel-collector`)

title: Implement Monitoring REST API in Conductor
body:
This issue implements the Monitoring REST API in Conductor that serves pipeline metrics to the Stage UI. The API queries Prometheus for time-series data, transforms responses to a compact format, and implements efficient caching. The API provides two endpoints: one to list available monitoring panels and another to query panel data for specific pipelines.

## Tasks

- [x] **Create MonitoringResource REST controller**
  - Implement `GET /api/v1/monitoring/panels` endpoint to list available panels
  - Implement `GET /api/v1/monitoring/panels/{id}/query` endpoint with query parameters (pipeline_id, start, end, step)
  - Add OpenAPI/Swagger annotations
  - Implement proper error handling and validation

- [x] **Implement MonitoringService business logic**
  - Create service layer for monitoring operations
  - Implement query orchestration (call Prometheus, transform response)
  - Implement variable substitution (replace `{{pipeline_id}}` in PromQL queries)
  - Add security to prevent PromQL injection

- [x] **Create PrometheusClient REST client**
  - Use MicroProfile REST Client to query Prometheus `/api/v1/query_range`
  - Configure connection timeout (30s)
  - Implement retry logic for transient failures
  - Handle Prometheus error responses

- [x] **Define panel configurations in YAML**
  - Create YAML configuration file with panel definitions
  - Include panels: events filtered, replication lag, time since last event, event count, connection status
  - Include snapshot panels: table count, remaining tables, snapshot running/completed/aborted, rows scanned
  - Define PromQL queries with `{{pipeline_id}}` variable placeholders
  - Specify visualization hints (chart type, suggested step)

- [x] **Implement compact time-series response format**
  - Transform Prometheus response to compact array format: `[[timestamp, value], ...]`
  - Use Unix epoch timestamps (seconds)
  - Reduce JSON payload size (~53% smaller than verbose format)
  - Include metadata (cached flag, query duration)

- [x] **Configure Prometheus endpoint URL**
  - Add application property for Prometheus base URL
  - Support different URLs per deployment scenario
  - Default to installed Prometheus service URL
  - Make configurable via environment variable
author: mfvitale

project_fields.Title: Implement Monitoring REST API in Conductor

title: Implement caching layer for Monitoring API in Conductor
body:
This issue implements a multi-layer caching strategy for the Monitoring API to reduce load on Prometheus and improve response times. The implementation includes an in-memory cache with dynamic TTL based on data freshness, HTTP caching headers for browser-side caching, and cache invalidation on pipeline deletion events.

## Tasks

- [ ] **Implement MonitoringCache with Caffeine**
  - Create in-memory cache with Caffeine library
  - Implement dynamic TTL based on data freshness (historical: 24h, live: 10s)
  - Configure max cache size (1000 entries)
  - Implement cache key generation from query parameters

- [ ] **Implement HTTP caching headers**
  - Add Cache-Control headers with dynamic TTL based on data freshness
  - Generate ETag based on query parameters and result hash
  - Support If-None-Match header for conditional requests
  - Return 304 Not Modified when appropriate

- [ ] **Implement cache invalidation on pipeline deletion**
  - Listen for pipeline deletion events
  - Invalidate all cache entries for deleted pipeline_id
  - Prevent stale data from being served
  - Consider using event-driven approach or integration with pipeline lifecycle
author: mfvitale

project_fields.Title: Implement caching layer for Monitoring API in Conductor

title: Align LogMiner LOB operation handling with XStream (LOB_WRITE / LOB_TRIM / LOB_ERASE)
body:
### Background

debezium/debezium#7374 changed the XStream adapter so that standalone `DBMS_LOB.WRITE` / `DBMS_LOB.TRIM` / `DBMS_LOB.ERASE` operations are emitted as `op=u` with the full reselected post-op LOB value, plus selective reselect (only LOB-mutating LCRs trigger reselection).

The LogMiner adapter does not currently produce the same observable wire output for the same Oracle operations — the existing IT `shouldNotStreamAnyChangesWhenLobEraseIsDetected` is now LogMiner-only because XStream emits a record for `LOB_ERASE` post-PR-7374, while LogMiner does not.

### Acceptance criteria

Both `LOGMINER_BUFFERED` and `LOGMINER_UNBUFFERED` paths should:

- [ ] Emit `op=u` (UPDATE) for standalone `DBMS_LOB.WRITE` / `DBMS_LOB.TRIM` / `DBMS_LOB.ERASE`
- [ ] Carry the full reselected post-op LOB value in `after.<lobCol>`, not chunk-level fragments
- [ ] Selective reselect: reselection fires only on LOB-mutating ops, not every DML
- [ ] IT coverage via the LogMiner variants of `OracleClobDataTypeIT.shouldStreamUpdateWithReselectedValueForLogMinerLob*` and `OracleBlobDataTypeIT.shouldStreamUpdateWithReselectedValueForLogMinerLob*` (mirroring the XStream variants added in #7374)

### Out of scope

- Same-transaction `INSERT + LOB_WRITE` merging (XStream emits two records since #7374 — this is documented behavior on both adapters via `ReselectColumnsPostProcessor`)

### Tracking

Follow-up to debezium/debezium#7374. Will be picked up after that PR merges and after the dbz#1867 PR (null-guard on `XStreamChangeRecordEmitter` column iteration) since #1867 shares the same code area as #7374.
author: pyaroslav

project_fields.Title: Align LogMiner LOB operation handling with XStream (LOB_WRITE / LOB_TRIM / LOB_ERASE)

    at io.debezium.transforms.AbstractExtractNewRecordState.apply(AbstractExtractNewRecordState.java:104)

---

**How to reproduce the issue using our [tutorial](https://github.com/debezium/debezium-examples/tree/main/tutorial) deployment?**

To reproduce the issue:

Insert documents into MongoDB containing array fields.
Add records where array elements have different keys/structures.
Configure the connector with transforms.unwrap.array.encoding=document.
Start the connector and observe the failure while processing the records.

title: Support OTel as the one of the main monitoring frameworks
body:
This is a high-level issue for reworking Debezium monitoring and provide 1st-class support for OTel framework.

It should follow these requirements/design:
* Both OTel metrics and JMX metrics should be supported 
* Metrics should be refactored – the MBean interfaces removed from the metrics classes 
* Introduce a facade for metrics 
* New adapter modules will be introduced – debezium-metrics-jmx (with quantiles) and debezium-metrics-otel 
* OTel metrics will be implemented in the code, no transition step via JMX 
* OTel metrics will be the way to expose metrics in Debezium Server/Engine native mode 
* Logs and traces facades will follow eventually (for OTel) 
* Micrometer will be delayed in favour of OTel and implemented as another module if there is a demand
author: vjuranek

project_fields.Title: Support OTel as the one of the main monitoring frameworks

title: InformixConnector hangs permanently after Windows host restart — no TCP FIN, SocketDispatcher.read0 blocked indefinitely
body:
## Bug report

**What Debezium connector do you use and what version?**

`debezium-connector-informix` — confirmed on **3.2.4.Final**, **3.4.3.Final**, and **3.5.0.Final** (latest stable).

---

**What is the connector configuration?**

```properties
connector.class=io.debezium.connector.informix.InformixConnector
topic.prefix=ifxdebug
database.hostname=<informix-host>
database.port=9088
database.user=informix
database.dbname=<dbname>
table.include.list=<dbname>\\.informix\\.<table>
snapshot.mode=no_data
errors.max.retries=5
```

---

**What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?**

IBM Informix 14.10.FC9W1DE, deployed on-premises on a **Windows Server virtual machine**. The connector runs on a separate Windows host via the Embedded Engine.

---

**What behavior do you expect?**

When the Informix server becomes unreachable (e.g. VM restart, network loss), the connector should detect the dead connection within a reasonable time, log an error, and either reconnect or stop cleanly so the orchestrator can restart it.

---

**What behavior do you see?**

The connector thread blocks **forever** inside `CDCEngine.getRecord()` and never recovers. No error is ever logged. `errors.max.retries` has no effect because no exception is ever thrown — the thread simply never returns from the native socket read.

**Root cause:** Windows shuts down services with a 5-second grace period (`WaitToKillServiceTimeout`, default 5000 ms). Informix does not register `SERVICE_ACCEPT_PRESHUTDOWN` and is killed before it can complete TCP teardown. The CDC streaming TCP socket remains `ESTABLISHED` on the client side indefinitely — the OS has no indication the remote endpoint is gone.

The hang is inside `IfxDataInputStream.readFully()`, which waits for bytes that will never arrive:

```
"debezium-informixconnector-...-change-event-source-coordinator" RUNNABLE
    sun.nio.ch.SocketDispatcher.read0(SocketDispatcher.java:-1)
    ...
    java.io.BufferedInputStream.read(BufferedInputStream.java:343)  ← locked
    com.informix.asf.IfxDataInputStream.readFully(...)
    com.informix.asf.IfxDataInputStream.readSmallInt(...)
    com.informix.jdbc.IfxSqli.receiveMessage / doLoData / executeReadSmBlob ...
    com.informix.jdbc.IfxSmartBlob.IfxLoRead(...)
    com.informix.jdbc.stream.cdc.CDCEngine.getRecord(CDCEngine.java:335)
    io.debezium.connector.informix.InformixCdcTransactionEngine.getRecord(...)
    io.debezium.connector.informix.InformixStreamingChangeEventSource.execute(...)
```

There are two hang sites depending on the connector's state at the time of failure:

| Scenario | v3.2.4.Final | v3.4.3.Final | v3.5.0.Final |
|---|:---:|:---:|:---:|
| **Case A** — server dies during initial catch-up / no prior events | `:132` | `:132` | `:131` |
| **Case B** — server dies during normal streaming | `:186` | `:190` | `:182` |

In both cases `Thread.interrupt()` cannot unblock a native socket read. The `context.isRunning()` guard is never re-evaluated again.

**Note on `cdc.timeout`:** The `cdc.timeout` config (default 5 s) tells the Informix **server** to send a `TIMEOUT` record every N seconds when idle. It is not a client-side socket read deadline (`SO_TIMEOUT`). When the server is dead, no bytes of any kind can be delivered, so this parameter provides no protection against a silently dead TCP connection.

**Network evidence (v3.5.0.Final, `capture_informix_windows_restart_with_initial_event_v3.5.0.Final.pcapng`):**

Informix opens 4 TCP connections to port 9088 at startup. On Windows restart with `WaitToKillServiceTimeout=5000` (default):

| Client port | Role | Last server activity | Server FIN? |
|---|---|---|---|
| :61112 | Setup/init | frame 26915, t=12.185s | ✅ frame 26917 |
| :61113 | Admin | frame 40541, t=66.738s | ✅ clean teardown |
| :61115 | Admin | frame 40539, t=66.738s | ✅ clean teardown |
| **:61114** | **CDC streaming** | **frame 40537, t=66.722s (10 bytes)** | **❌ none** |

The two admin connections receive FINs at t≈66.738s and close cleanly. The CDC streaming connection (:61114) receives its last 10 bytes at t=66.722s and then — silence. No FIN, no RST, no ICMP from the server. The client eventually sends a RST at frame 43880 (t=105.346s), approximately 38 seconds later, triggered by the JVM/watchdog shutdown rather than any server-side signal.

When `WaitToKillServiceTimeout` is raised to 30 000 ms, Informix flushes all connections cleanly, FINs are received, and the connector reconnects normally. The hang is exclusive to the no-FIN teardown scenario.

---

**Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?**

Yes. Confirmed on **3.5.0.Final**. The CDC engine class was renamed from `com.informix.stream.cdc.IfxCDCEngine` (JDBC 4.x) to `com.informix.jdbc.stream.cdc.CDCEngine` (JDBC 15.x), but the blocking behaviour inside `readFully()` is identical. Thread dump from a live stuck process on v3.5.0.Final:

```
"debezium-informixconnector-ifxdebug-change-event-source-coordinator@4369" prio=5 tid=0x1b nid=NA runnable
  java.lang.Thread.State: RUNNABLE
    at sun.nio.ch.SocketDispatcher.read0(SocketDispatcher.java:-1)
    at sun.nio.ch.SocketDispatcher.read(SocketDispatcher.java:46)
    at sun.nio.ch.NioSocketImpl.tryRead(NioSocketImpl.java:266)
    at sun.nio.ch.NioSocketImpl.implRead(NioSocketImpl.java:317)
    at sun.nio.ch.NioSocketImpl.read(NioSocketImpl.java:355)
    at sun.nio.ch.NioSocketImpl$1.read(NioSocketImpl.java:808)
    at java.net.Socket$SocketInputStream.read(Socket.java:966)
    at java.io.BufferedInputStream.fill(BufferedInputStream.java:244)
    at java.io.BufferedInputStream.read1(BufferedInputStream.java:284)
    at java.io.BufferedInputStream.read(BufferedInputStream.java:343)
      - locked <0x12fc> (a java.io.BufferedInputStream)
    at com.informix.asf.IfxDataInputStream.readFully(IfxDataInputStream.java:135)
    at com.informix.asf.IfxDataInputStream.readSmallInt(IfxDataInputStream.java:391)
    at com.informix.jdbc.IfxSqli.receiveMessage(IfxSqli.java:1804)
    at com.informix.jdbc.IfxSqli.doLoData(IfxSqli.java:4842)
    at com.informix.jdbc.IfxSqli.executeReadSmBlob(IfxSqli.java:4790)
    at com.informix.jdbc.IfxSmartBlob.IfxLoRead(IfxSmartBlob.java:705)
    at com.informix.jdbc.stream.cdc.CDCEngine.getRecord(CDCEngine.java:335)
    at io.debezium.connector.informix.InformixCdcTransactionEngine.getRecord(InformixCdcTransactionEngine.java:79)
    at io.debezium.connector.informix.InformixStreamingChangeEventSource.execute(InformixStreamingChangeEventSource.java:182)
    at io.debezium.connector.informix.InformixStreamingChangeEventSource.execute(InformixStreamingChangeEventSource.java:36)
    at io.debezium.pipeline.ChangeEventSourceCoordinator.streamEvents(ChangeEventSourceCoordinator.java:339)
    ...
```

---

**Do you have the connector logs, ideally from start till finish?**

Yes. The connector logs normally up to the last CDC event before the VM restart, then goes completely silent — no error, no warning, no further output of any kind:

```
[watchdog] events=47 since-last=10042ms
[watchdog] events=47 since-last=20051ms
[watchdog] events=47 since-last=30060ms
... (grows forever; no exception ever logged)
```

Full DEBUG logs are available on request. Port-filtered Wireshark captures (`.pcapng`, filtered to `tcp.port == 9088` only) can be provided for the stuck case and working baselines (`net stop`, `docker kill`, `docker stop -t 30`, `onmode -ky`, VM restart with `WaitToKillServiceTimeout=30000`). Note: raw full-interface captures are not attached as they may contain unrelated sensitive traffic.

---

**How to reproduce the issue using our [tutorial](https://github.com/debezium/debezium-examples/tree/main/tutorial) deployment?**

> The tutorial uses Docker on Linux; this bug requires Windows-hosted Informix. Steps below use the Embedded Engine directly.

**Prerequisites:**
- Informix 14.10 or 15.x on a Windows Server VM
- `WaitToKillServiceTimeout` at its default (5000 ms) — verify with:
  ```
  reg query "HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control" /v WaitToKillServiceTimeout
  ```
- Debezium connector running via Embedded Engine with `snapshot.mode=no_data`

**Case A — hang during initial catch-up (no prior events):**
1. Start with a clean offset store (no previously captured events)
2. Start the connector and restart the Informix VM during the catch-up phase (watchdog shows `events=0`)
3. Observe: thread frozen at `InformixStreamingChangeEventSource.execute(:131)` in v3.5 — the recovery loop
4. The connector never recovers regardless of how long you wait

**Case B — hang during normal streaming (events were flowing):**
1. Start the connector and confirm CDC events are flowing (insert a row)
2. Restart the Informix VM: `shutdown /r /t 0`
3. Observe: connector logs go silent after the last CDC event; `since-last` grows without bound
4. `jstack <pid>` shows thread frozen at `InformixStreamingChangeEventSource.execute(:182)` in v3.5
5. The connector never recovers

**Does NOT reproduce when:**
- Using `net stop InformixServer` — graceful FIN sent, connector reconnects normally
- Restarting the VM with `WaitToKillServiceTimeout=30000` — Informix flushes connections before kill
- Running Informix in Linux Docker with `docker kill` — Linux kernel sends FIN on behalf of the container

**Proposed fix directions:**

- **Option A — `SO_TIMEOUT` on the CDC socket (preferred):** A client-side read deadline on the socket underlying `CDCEngine` would cause `readFully()` to throw `SocketTimeoutException` after N seconds regardless of server state, allowing the connector to treat it as a retriable error. Requires the `CDCEngine.Builder` to expose a socket timeout option.
- **Option B — Watchdog thread:** A background thread monitors time-since-last-event and calls `engine.close()` when the threshold is exceeded with no error. Implementable today without driver changes.
- **Option C — TCP keepalives:** Enable `SO_KEEPALIVE` with a short `TCP_KEEPIDLE`. The kernel would detect the dead connection and unblock `read0`. Requires OS-level tuning or JVM socket options.

[connector.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/27479330/connector.log)
[thread_dump_windows_restart_with_initial_event_v3.5.0.Final.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/27479334/thread_dump_windows_restart_with_initial_event_v3.5.0.Final.txt)
author: regi-c

project_fields.Title: InformixConnector hangs permanently after Windows host restart — no TCP FIN, SocketDispatcher.read0 blocked indefinitely

title: Customizable zero-date fallback value (per type) for MySQL/MariaDB connectors
body:
## Feature request or enhancement
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  
  **Which use case/requirement will be addressed by the proposed feature?**
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  
  The MySQL and MariaDB connectors currently coerce zero dates (`0000-00-00` for `DATE`, `0000-00-00 00:00:00` for `DATETIME` and `TIMESTAMP`) into a hardcoded `1970-01-01` sentinel for `NOT NULL` columns. This makes
  legitimate `1970-01-01` events indistinguishable from coerced zero-dates downstream — a real source of data quality bugs in analytics pipelines (Iceberg, ClickHouse, StarRocks, JDBC sinks, etc.).

  **Use case.** We ingest legacy MySQL tables that contain millions of zero-date rows. Downstream we use Iceberg + Spark + JDBC sink, and we want zero-dates to land as a clearly identifiable sentinel like `9999-12-31` so      
  analytics queries can filter them with `WHERE created_at < DATE '9000-01-01'`. The current hardcoded `1970-01-01` coercion silently corrupts time-series and SCD2 queries.
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  
  **Why per-type overrides?** MySQL `TIMESTAMP` is bounded to `1970-01-01..2038-01-19`, so any sentinel outside that range is rejected by MySQL `TIMESTAMP` sinks — but `DATETIME`/`DATE` accept the much wider `1000–9999` range.
   A common requirement is "DATETIME = `1001-01-01`, TIMESTAMP unchanged at `1970-01-01` for sink compatibility".
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  
  **Proposed config (four keys, all optional):**                                                                                                                                                                                  
                                          
  | Property | Default | Purpose |                                                                                                                                                                                                
  |---|---|---|                                                                                                                                                                                                                   
  | `zero.date.fallback.value` | `1970-01-01` | General default for all 3 temporal types |
  | `zero.date.fallback.value.date` | (unset → inherit) | Override for `DATE` columns |                                                                                                                                           
  | `zero.date.fallback.value.datetime` | (unset → inherit) | Override for `DATETIME` columns |
  | `zero.date.fallback.value.timestamp` | (unset → inherit) | Override for `TIMESTAMP` columns |
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  
  Cascading lookup: type-specific override → general default → hardcoded `LocalDate.EPOCH`. Validator: ISO-8601 `yyyy-MM-dd`, range `0001-01-01..9999-12-31`, fail-fast on invalid input.                                         
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  
  **Example.** With `zero.date.fallback.value.datetime=1001-01-01` and a `NOT NULL` row receiving `0000-00-00 00:00:00`:                                                                                                          
  - `DATETIME` column → `1001-01-01T00:00:00Z` (epoch millis `-30578688000000`)                                                                                                                                                   
  - `TIMESTAMP` column → `1970-01-01T00:00:00Z` (general default; MySQL `TIMESTAMP` sink-safe)
  - `DATE` column → `1970-01-01` (general default)                                                                                                                                                                                
  **Backward compatibility.** All four properties are optional; with no config, output is byte-for-byte identical to today. No schema changes (sentinel only affects the value, not the Avro/Protobuf logical type). Other
  Debezium connectors (`postgres`, `oracle`, `sqlserver`) are unaffected — the change in `JdbcValueConverters` is additive.                                                                                                       
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  
  **Out of scope (potential follow-up).** A `zero.date.handling.mode=null` mode that promotes the schema to `optional` and emits Kafka `null` instead of a sentinel for zero-dates. Schema-breaking; warrants a separate proposal.
   The cascading getter introduced here is shaped to support it.                                                                                                                                                                  
                                                                  
  ---                                                                                                                                                                                                                             
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  
  **Implementation ideas (optional)**
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  
  Two paths in the binlog connector hardcode `1970-01-01` and need to be wired to a single per-type fallback source:
                                                                                                                    
  1. **DDL parsing → schema `default`** in `BinlogDefaultValueConverter` (constants `EPOCH_DATE`, `EPOCH_TIMESTAMP`).
  2. **Row event conversion → payload value** in `JdbcValueConverters` (12 fallback sites: `convertDateToEpochDays`, `convertTimestampToEpochMillis`, `convertTimestampToUtcIsoString`, etc.).
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  
  Both should read from a new cascading getter on `BinlogConnectorConfig`:
  ```java                                                                                                                                                                                                                         
  public LocalDate getZeroDateFallback(int jdbcType) {            
      // type-specific override → general default → LocalDate.EPOCH                                                                                                                                                               
  }                                                                                                                                                                                                                               
  ```                                                                                                                                                                                                                             
  so schema `default` and row payload stay consistent for any chosen sentinel.                                                                                                                                                    
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  
  To avoid signature explosion as more per-type config (potentially mode in the follow-up) is added, I bundle the three sentinels into a small immutable parameter object `io.debezium.jdbc.ZeroDateFallback` and have
  `JdbcValueConverters` accept that single object. The existing `JdbcValueConverters` constructor used by `postgres`/`oracle`/`sqlserver` delegates with `ZeroDateFallback.defaultEpoch()`, so those connectors are unaffected.   
                                                                                                                                                                                                                               
  **Bundled fix — streaming `TIMESTAMP` zero-date detection.** While implementing the feature I confirmed end-to-end that the streaming path doesn't apply the configured sentinel to `TIMESTAMP` columns. In                     
  `RowDeserializers.java`:                                                                                                                                                                                                        
  - `deserializeDate` (line 305), `deserializeDatetime` (404), `deserializeDatetimeV2` (453) already return `null` when `year/month/day == 0`.                                                                                    
  - `deserializeTimestamp` (474) and `deserializeTimestampV2` (490) read `epochSecond` and build a `ZonedDateTime` directly — no zero-date check. A wire value of `0000-00-00 00:00:00` (4 zero bytes) is treated as              
  `1970-01-01T00:00:00 UTC` and bypasses the fallback path.                                                                                                                                                                       
                                                           
  Because MySQL `TIMESTAMP`'s legal range starts at `1970-01-01 00:00:01 UTC`, `epochSecond == 0` unambiguously signals the zero-date sentinel. Adding a four-line guard to mirror the `DATE/DATETIME` pattern restores symmetry  
  between snapshot and streaming. I'd like to bundle this in the same PR — otherwise the new `*.timestamp` override is silently inert during streaming.                                                                           
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  
  **Verification.** Locally validated end-to-end with Docker (MySQL source → Kafka Avro + Schema Registry → JDBC sink to MySQL) across three scenarios (default, uniform `9999-12-31`, per-type `.datetime=1001-01-01`), both     
  snapshot and streaming paths. Unit tests added for `BinlogConnectorConfig` cascading + validator, `MySqlDefaultValueConverter` zero-date sentinel, `RowDeserializers` `TIMESTAMP(V2)` zero-detection. Integration test extended 
  in `BinlogDefaultValueAllZeroTimeIT` to cover both schema defaults and streaming row payloads.             




[#community-mysql-mariadb > Support Configurable Fallback Value for MySQL "Zero Dates"](https://debezium.zulipchat.com/#narrow/channel/348104-community-mysql-mariadb/topic/Support.20Configurable.20Fallback.20Value.20for.20MySQL.20.22Zero.20Dates.22/with/593410951)

@vjuranek
author: minleejae

project_fields.Title: Customizable zero-date fallback value (per type) for MySQL/MariaDB connectors

title: Replace fn_cdc_map_lsn_to_time scalar function with lsn_time_mapping JOIN
body:
## Problem

The current SQL Server CDC query uses the scalar function 
`fn_cdc_map_lsn_to_time()` to map LSN to timestamp. This function 
is called once per row, which causes significant performance degradation 
on high-volume tables due to repeated per-row function invocations.

## Solution

Replace the per-row scalar function call with a `LEFT JOIN` on the 
`cdc.lsn_time_mapping` table. This allows SQL Server to resolve 
timestamps in a single set-based operation instead of row-by-row.

## Changes

- Replace `fn_cdc_map_lsn_to_time([__$start_lsn])` with 
  `LEFT JOIN cdc.lsn_time_mapping ltm ON ltm.start_lsn = cdc_data.[__$start_lsn]`
- Add `cdc_data` alias to CDC function result to avoid ambiguous column names
- Update `WHERE` and `ORDER BY` clauses to use `cdc_data` prefix
- Fix `skippedOperations` filter to use `cdc_data.[__$operation]`
- Use `ltm.tran_begin_time` instead of scalar function result for timestamp

## Impact

- Improves CDC query performance on high-volume tables
- No change in connector behavior or output format
- Only affects `FUNCTION` data query mode
author: caobahuong

project_fields.Title: Replace fn_cdc_map_lsn_to_time scalar function with lsn_time_mapping JOIN

title: Negative timestamp in Postgres not being handled correctly during snapshot
body:
## Bug report

**What Debezium connector do you use and what version?**
3.6.0.Alpha1

---

**What is the connector configuration?**
Default

---

**What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?**

_E.g. on-premises, with a specific cloud provider, etc._

Postgres 18.3, local deployment using Docker.

The setup is basically the same as the one explained in the [tutorial](https://github.com/debezium/debezium-examples/tree/main/tutorial#using-postgres).

Exact steps are given down below.

---

**What behavior do you expect?**
When I insert a negative timestamp for a `timestamptz` column (< 1970-01-01), output should be as is.
e.g. `0001-01-31T00:00:00Z::timestamp` should produce `0001-01-31T00:00:00.000000Z`.

---

**What behavior do you see?**
When the connector do initial snapshot, the output is actually incorrect.
e.g.
Expected: `0001-01-31T00:00:00Z::timestamp`
Actual: `0001-01-29T00:00:00.000000Z`

---

**Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?**

_Ideally, also verify with the latest Alpha/Beta/CR version._

I'm using the latest version, so yes.

---

**Do you have the connector logs, ideally from start till finish?**

_You might be asked later to provide [DEBUG/TRACE](https://debezium.io/documentation/reference/stable/operations/logging.html) level log._

Yes.

---

**How to reproduce the issue using our [tutorial](https://github.com/debezium/debezium-examples/tree/main/tutorial) deployment?**
Pretty much based on the [tutorial](https://github.com/debezium/debezium-examples/tree/main/tutorial#using-postgres). Here's the detail:
1. Run Postgres first
```bash
docker compose --env-file ../.env -f docker-compose-postgres.yaml up -d postgres
```
2. Run this SQL
```sql
BEGIN;

CREATE TABLE inventory.test_ts (
  id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
  dt TIMESTAMPTZ
);

INSERT INTO inventory.test_ts VALUES
(1, '0001-01-31T00:00:00Z'),
(2, '0001-01-30T23:59:59Z');

COMMIT;
```
3. Run the rest (Kafka, Debezium connector)
```bash
docker compose --env-file ../.env -f docker-compose-postgres.yaml up -d
```
The rest should follow the tutorial.

The output:
* `0001-01-31T00:00:00Z` becomes `0001-01-29T00:00:00.000000Z`
* `0001-01-30T23:59:59Z` becomes `0001-01-28T23:59:59.000000Z`

---

**Additional references**
Related to this [zulipchat](https://debezium.zulipchat.com/#narrow/channel/348249-community-postgresql/topic/Postgres.20timestamptz.20negative.20UNIX.20ts.20converted.20incorrectly).

title: CockroachDB connector pom.xml uses wrong Java target property -- produces Java 21 bytecode instead of Java 17
body:
## Bug report

**What Debezium connector do you use and what version?**

`debezium-connector-cockroachdb` 3.5.0.Final, 3.6.0.Alpha1, and current 3.6.0-SNAPSHOT.

**What is the connector configuration?**

Any. The problem is at build time, not runtime.

**What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?**

Not applicable -- the connector cannot be loaded by Kafka Connect when Kafka Connect is running on Java 17. Currently only loadable on Java 21+.

**What behavior do you expect?**

The CockroachDB connector should be compiled to Java 17 bytecode (class file version 61), matching the rest of the Debezium connectors. The parent build POM defines two separate Java targets:

```xml
<!-- Java version used for connectors and core libraries -->
<debezium.java.connector.target>17</debezium.java.connector.target>

<!-- Java version used for Debezium specific components - Server, Operator, Outbox -->
<debezium.java.specific.target>21</debezium.java.specific.target>
```

All connectors are expected to target Java 17.

**What behavior do you see?**

The connector is compiled to Java 21 bytecode (class file version 65). Kafka Connect deployments running on Java 17 (the most common LTS version for Kafka Connect today) fail to load the connector with:

```
java.lang.UnsupportedClassVersionError: io/debezium/connector/cockroachdb/CockroachDBConnector
  has been compiled by a more recent version of the Java Runtime
  (class file version 65.0), this version of the Java Runtime only recognizes
  class file versions up to 61.0
```

**Root cause**

`debezium-connector-cockroachdb/pom.xml` overrides the parent compiler properties using the wrong property name -- it references `${debezium.java.specific.target}` (= 21, intended for Server/Operator/Outbox) instead of inheriting the parent default of `${debezium.java.connector.target}` (= 17):

```xml
<properties>
    <!-- JDK version is controlled by Debezium Parent, do not change! -->
    <maven.compiler.source>${debezium.java.source}</maven.compiler.source>
    <maven.compiler.target>${debezium.java.specific.target}</maven.compiler.target>
    <maven.compiler.release>${debezium.java.specific.target}</maven.compiler.release>
    <maven.compiler.testSource>${debezium.java.source}</maven.compiler.testSource>
    <maven.compiler.testTarget>${debezium.java.specific.target}</maven.compiler.testTarget>
    <maven.compiler.testRelease>${debezium.java.specific.target}</maven.compiler.testRelease>
```

The other out-of-tree Debezium connectors (`debezium-connector-vitess`, `debezium-connector-cassandra`, etc.) do not override these properties and correctly inherit the parent's `maven.compiler.target=${debezium.java.connector.target}` (= 17).

**Proposed fix**

Remove the property overrides from `debezium-connector-cockroachdb/pom.xml` so the connector inherits the parent's Java 17 connector target, matching all other Debezium connectors.

**Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?**

Yes. This affects every released version of the CockroachDB connector to date.

**How to reproduce the issue**

```
git clone https://github.com/debezium/debezium-connector-cockroachdb
cd debezium-connector-cockroachdb
./mvnw clean package -DskipITs
javap -v target/classes/io/debezium/connector/cockroachdb/CockroachDBConnector.class | grep 'major version'
# Observed: major version: 65 (Java 21)
# Expected: major version: 61 (Java 17)
```
author: viragtripathi

project_fields.Title: CockroachDB connector pom.xml uses wrong Java target property -- produces Java 21 bytecode instead of Java 17

title: SQL Server:  IllegalArgumentException when CDC capture pollinginterval is 0
body:
## Bug report

**What Debezium connector do you use and what version?**
 SQL Server connector, Debezium 3.5.0.Final

---

**What is the connector configuration?**


Any standard SQL Server connector configuration targeting Azure SQL Database. The specific configuration values are not relevant — the bug triggers regardless of connector settings as long as the target is an Azure SQL Database (or any SQL Server instance where sys.sp_cdc_help_jobs reports pollinginterval = 0 for the capture job).

---

**What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?**

 Azure SQL Database (managed service). The bug affects any managed SQL Server environment where CDC capture is handled by the platform rather than SQL Server Agent, including:

   - Azure SQL Database
   - Azure SQL Managed Instance
   - AWS RDS SQL Server (likely)
   - Google Cloud SQL for SQL Server (likely)

---

**What behavior do you expect?**

The connector should start streaming CDC changes normally, even when sys.sp_cdc_help_jobs reports pollinginterval = 0 for the capture job. A non-positive pollinginterval should be treated as the default of 5 seconds.

---

**What behavior do you see?**

The connector task crashes with an unrecoverable IllegalArgumentException the moment it processes its first CDC change event:

 java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: Initial delay must be positive
     at io.debezium.util.ElapsedTimeStrategy.constant(ElapsedTimeStrategy.java:35)
     at io.debezium.connector.sqlserver.SqlServerStreamingChangeEventSource
         .resetEndTransactionTimer(SqlServerStreamingChangeEventSource.java:578)

This is wrapped in ConnectException("This connector will be stopped.") — the Kafka Connect task is permanently dead and cannot recover without a restart (which will fail again).

The failure rate is ~60–90% (not 100%) because resetEndTransactionTimer() is only reached after executeIteration detects a real LSN advance. Tasks that are deleted or paused before any CDC change arrives never trigger the bug.

---

**Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?**

_Ideally, also verify with the latest Alpha/Beta/CR version._

Yes. The bug was introduced in 3.5.0.Final by PR #7078 and is present in the current main branch.


---

**Do you have the connector logs, ideally from start till finish?**

_You might be asked later to provide [DEBUG/TRACE](https://debezium.io/documentation/reference/stable/operations/logging.html) level log._

`<Your answer>`

---

**How to reproduce the issue using our [tutorial](https://github.com/debezium/debezium-examples/tree/main/tutorial) deployment?**

`<Your answer>`
author: nkokitkar

project_fields.Title: SQL Server:  IllegalArgumentException when CDC capture pollinginterval is 0

title: IBMi Connector: Support for Multi-member Physical Files and Journal Resolution via SQL Alias
body:
Hi Debezium team!

While investigating Debezium capabilities for legacy IBM i environments, we've identified a behavior regarding DDS-defined Physical Files with multiple members that prevents CDC. We are almost certain that this is not a direct limitation of the Debezium connector itself, but rather a long-standing historical constraint originating from the underlying JDBC JT400 driver's design and its interaction with the IBM i database engine.

**Current Behavior:**

**Member Access:** The connector defaults to the first member (*FIRST). There is currently no documented way to iterate or specify additional members in the configuration.

**Naming Convention**: Attempting to use standard IBM i member syntax (e.g., LIBRARY/FILE(MEMBER)) in the table.include.list results in a lookup failure. We already tried changing the naming convention to system and other related parameters without success.

**SQL Alias Workaround**: As a workaround, we created a SQL ALIAS pointing to a specific member name.  The streaming phase (CDC) fails immediately when the connector attempts to locate the journal associated with the object.

**Error Log observed:**

java.lang.IllegalStateException: unable to retrieve journal details
Caused by: java.lang.IllegalStateException: Journal not found for SCHEMA.ALIAS_NAME error File ALIAS_NAME not allowed with SYSTEM(*LCL).

**Technical Observation:**
In our humble opinion,  the error File ALIAS_NAME not allowed with SYSTEM(*LCL) suggests that the connector's internal mechanism for retrieving journal entries is incompatible with SQL ALIAS objects. Since an Alias seems to be a DDM file pointing to a local member, the underlying system API used by the connector appears to reject it, expecting a Physical (*FILE PF) or Logical (*FILE LF) file instead.

**Questions & Request**:
Is there a recommended workaround to capture changes (CDC) from specific members of a multi-member Physical File?

If it is not possible to support member declaration via regex/syntax in the configuration, would you please enhance the journal resolution logic to support SQL ALIAS tables

**Environment**:
Debezium version: 3.5.0.Final
Driver: jt400.jar (JTOpen) version 21.0.6
Platform: IBM i V7R5
author: kansoit

project_fields.Title: IBMi Connector: Support for Multi-member Physical Files and Journal Resolution via SQL Alias

title: Support AWS RDS Oracle with CDB deployments
body:
## Feature request or enhancement

_For feature requests or enhancements, please provide the following information:_

**Which use case/requirement will be addressed by the proposed feature?**

When deploying Oracle on AWS RDS using a CDB deployment strategy, AWS enforces a strict security model that does not permit direct access to the database's container root. The `CDB$ROOT` (container root) is required for Debezium to perform very specific operations, such as streaming changes using LogMiner, as well as accessing specific performance views (`V$`) that are not available in the PDB.

---

**Implementation ideas (optional)**

In RDS, when using CDB deployments, custom PL/SQL packages are provided to interact with LogMiner. We might be able to create a strategy-based approach that can be used with RDS+CDB to leverage these custom packages.

In addition, the documentation would need to be updated to reflect the CDB-specific steps to operate in AWS RDS.

As a side note, this work, if structured well, could also serve as the basis for support for OCI cloud deployments of Autonomous and others, where we specifically need to mine differently for the same reasons.
author: Naros

project_fields.Title: Support AWS RDS Oracle with CDB deployments
