title: Add YDB sink (debezium-server-ydb)
body:
## Feature request or enhancement

_For feature requests or enhancements, please provide the following information:_

**Which use case/requirement will be addressed by the proposed feature?**

`  Debezium Server has no sink for https://ydb.tech — an open-source distributed SQL database with a Kafka-compatible Topics API. Today users who want CDC into YDB must either bridge through Kafka or hand-roll an embedded-engine app, re-inventing the offset/retry/commit  
  machinery debezium-server already provides for other sinks.                                                                                                                                                                                                                  
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               
  A first-class debezium-server-ydb sink would put YDB on par with the existing Pulsar / Kinesis / Pub/Sub / RabbitMQ / NATS sinks and pair naturally with the YDB-backed OffsetBackingStore and SchemaHistory proposed separately, enabling a Kafka-free, fully YDB-resident  
  Debezium deployment.`

---

**Implementation ideas (optional)**

`- New module debezium-server-ydb, wired into the parent pom.xml, BOM, and debezium-server-dist assembly.
  - YdbChangeConsumer extends BaseChangeConsumer, @Named("ydb") @Dependent. @PostConstruct opens GrpcTransport + TopicClient; @PreDestroy drains async writers.                                                                                                                
  - YdbChangeConsumerConfig exposing the usual Field.Set under debezium.sink.ydb.*.                                                                                                                                                                                            
  - handleBatch keeps a pool of AsyncWriters keyed by producer_id (connector + instance + message group, group defaulting to record.destination()), awaits all WriteAcks, then calls markProcessed / markBatchFinished.                                                                                                                                                                                                                         
  - YdbIT using a testcontainer-based TestResourceLifecycleManager.                                                                                                                                                                                                            
                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               
  A working prototype of the consumer logic already exists out-of-tree and will be ported into the canonical CDI shape for the PR. `
author: vyrexd

project_fields.Title: Add YDB sink (debezium-server-ydb)

title: YDB offset backing store and schema history (debezium-storage/debezium-storage-ydb)
body:
## Feature request or enhancement

_For feature requests or enhancements, please provide the following information:_

**Which use case/requirement will be addressed by the proposed feature?**

`  Debezium has no offset backing store or schema history implementation for https://ydb.tech — an open-source distributed SQL database with strong ACID guarantees. Users running YDB as their primary datastore currently have to provision a separate backend (Kafka / JDBC /
   Redis / file) just to hold Debezium's offsets and schema history, which adds an operational dependency that the rest of their stack does not need.                                                                                                                          
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               
  YDB-backed implementations would put it on par with the existing kafka / jdbc / redis / file storage modules and enable a fully YDB-resident Debezium deployment (paired with the proposed debezium-server-ydb sink in #). YDB's transactional guarantees also make it a     
  natural fit for offset storage in exactly-once pipelines that already terminate in YDB.`

---

**Implementation ideas (optional)**

`  Follow debezium-storage-jdbc / debezium-storage-redis as templates:
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               
  - New module debezium-storage-ydb under debezium-storage/, wired into the parent pom.xml.
  - YdbOffsetBackingStore extends MemoryOffsetBackingStore — flushes to a YDB table via QueryClient with serializable_read_write tx isolation; load on start(), upsert on save().                                                                                              
  - YdbSchemaHistory extends AbstractFileBasedSchemaHistory (or AbstractSchemaHistory) — append-only table keyed by monotonic sequence, full scan on recover().                                                                                                                
  - Config classes YdbOffsetBackingStoreConfig / YdbSchemaHistoryConfig exposing endpoint, database, auth mode, TLS, and table names via Field.Set.                                                                                                                            
  - Shared YdbCommonConfig + YdbTransports helper for connection setup, reused by both stores.                                                                                                                                                                                 
  - Integration tests using the YDB testcontainer, matching the layout of debezium-storage-jdbc's ITs.                                                                                                                                                                         
  - SDK: tech.ydb:ydb-sdk-core and tech.ydb:ydb-sdk-query.                                                                                                                                                                                                                     
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               
  A working prototype of both stores already exists out-of-tree and will be submitted as the PR for this issue.  `
author: vyrexd

project_fields.Title: YDB offset backing store and schema history (debezium-storage/debezium-storage-ydb)

title: Flaky chunked snapshot test due to incorrect random row-count generation
body:
### Problem

`SqlServerChunkedSnapshotIT.shouldSnapshotMultipleTablesChunkedWithVaryingRowCounts` can fail intermittently due to the random row-count generation in `AbstractChunkedSnapshotTest.java`, line 249:

```java
final int tableRowCount = (int) (Math.random() * (10_000 - 2_500 + 1));
```

The lower-bound offset (`+ 2_500`) is missing.

As a result, the formula produces values in the range `[0, 7500]` instead of the intended `[2500, 10000]`, making `tableRowCount == 1` statistically possible (~0.013% probability per table).

This may cause the downstream assertion at line 690 to fail intermittently:

```java
assertThat(records).hasSizeGreaterThan(1);
```

---
### Evidence

In the failing CI run, `tableRowCount` was effectively generated as `1`, causing table `dbz1220c` to contain only a single row.
The assertion at line 276 passed:

```java
assertThat(records).hasSize(tableRowCount); // PASS: size = 1
```

However, the subsequent snapshot marker assertion failed because only one snapshot record existed.

The CI logs showed:

```text
topic='server1.testDB1.dbo.dbz1220c'
key=Struct{id=0}
value=Struct{after=Struct{id=0, data=0}, snapshot=last_in_data_collection, ...}
```

---

### Suggested fix

```java
final int tableRowCount = (int) (Math.random() * (10_000 - 2_500 + 1)) + 2_500;
```

---
### Notes

I encountered this while working on PR #5977 (tracking issue DBZ-1915), where all connector tests passed except this flaky test.
I'm happy to submit a small follow-up PR for the fix if maintainers agree.
author: caobahuong

project_fields.Title: Flaky chunked snapshot test due to incorrect random row-count generation

title: Connector context MDC tag is missing in logs produced in ChangeEventSourceCoordinator thread
body:
## Bug report

**What Debezium connector do you use and what version?**

PostgresConnector-3.5.0.Final

---

**What is the connector configuration?**

Not relevant to this bug report.

---

**What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?**

_E.g. on-premises, with a specific cloud provider, etc._

PostgreSQL 18, Kafka 4.2.0, Kubernetes deployment.

---

**What behavior do you expect?**

All logs produced by Debezium connector should include [connector.context](https://github.com/apache/kafka/blob/4.2.0/connect/runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/connect/util/LoggingContext.java#L50) MDC tag.

---

**What behavior do you see?**

The error logs produced in `ErrorHandler` class by change-event-source-coordinator thread does not include `connector.context` MDC tag. 

There are Debezium specific MDC tags present, including:
- `dbz.connectorContext`
- `dbz.connectorLogicalName`
- `dbz.connectorType`
- `dbz.databaseName`
- `dbz.taskId`

But those tags although helpful, are not sufficient to directly identity the name of affected Kafka connector. The `dbz.connectorContext` in this case is set to `streaming` value so its not alias of `connector.context` tag, whereas `dbz.connectorLogicalName` is set to `topic.prefix` configuration.

My expectation is that all logs produced by Kafka connector, will include logging context set in [WorkerTask](https://github.com/apache/kafka/blob/trunk/connect/runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/connect/runtime/WorkerTask.java#L294).

I will admit, this is not a critical issue. It is still possible to identify the connector based on Debezium tags, but not in automated way, which is needed for issue reporting.

---

**Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?**

I tested it only with version 3.5.0.Final.

---

**Do you have the connector logs, ideally from start till finish?**

The error itself is *not the reported bug*.

```json
{
    "mdc": {
        "dbz.connectorContext": "streaming",
        "dbz.connectorLogicalName": "<redacted>",
        "dbz.connectorType": "Postgres",
        "dbz.databaseName": "<redacted>",
        "dbz.taskId": "0"
    },
    "exception": {
        "exception_class": "io.debezium.DebeziumException",
        "exception_message": "Failed to start replication stream at LSN{1F/2F19A168}",
        "stacktrace": "io.debezium.DebeziumException: Failed to start replication stream at LSN{1F/2F19A168}\n\tat io.debezium.connector.postgresql.connection.PostgresReplicationConnection.startStreaming(PostgresReplicationConnection.java:705)\n\tat io.debezium.connector.postgresql.PostgresStreamingChangeEventSource.execute(PostgresStreamingChangeEventSource.java:175)\n\tat io.debezium.connector.postgresql.PostgresStreamingChangeEventSource.execute(PostgresStreamingChangeEventSource.java:51)\n\tat io.debezium.pipeline.ChangeEventSourceCoordinator.streamEvents(ChangeEventSourceCoordinator.java:339)\n\tat io.debezium.pipeline.ChangeEventSourceCoordinator.executeChangeEventSources(ChangeEventSourceCoordinator.java:217)\n\tat io.debezium.pipeline.ChangeEventSourceCoordinator.lambda$start$0(ChangeEventSourceCoordinator.java:148)\n\tat java.base/java.util.concurrent.Executors$RunnableAdapter.call(Executors.java:572)\n\tat java.base/java.util.concurrent.FutureTask.run(FutureTask.java:317)\n\tat java.base/java.util.concurrent.ThreadPoolExecutor.runWorker(ThreadPoolExecutor.java:1144)\n\tat java.base/java.util.concurrent.ThreadPoolExecutor$Worker.run(ThreadPoolExecutor.java:642)\n\tat io.debezium.util.Threads$3.lambda$newThread$0(Threads.java:314)\n\tat java.base/java.lang.Thread.run(Thread.java:1583)\nCaused by: org.postgresql.util.PSQLException: ERROR: can no longer get changes from replication slot \"debezium\"\n  Detail: This slot has been invalidated because it was conflicting with recovery.\n\tat org.postgresql.core.v3.QueryExecutorImpl.receiveErrorResponse(QueryExecutorImpl.java:2875)\n\tat org.postgresql.core.v3.QueryExecutorImpl.processCopyResults(QueryExecutorImpl.java:1414)\n\tat org.postgresql.core.v3.QueryExecutorImpl.startCopy(QueryExecutorImpl.java:1085)\n\tat org.postgresql.core.v3.replication.V3ReplicationProtocol.initializeReplication(V3ReplicationProtocol.java:62)\n\tat org.postgresql.core.v3.replication.V3ReplicationProtocol.startLogical(V3ReplicationProtocol.java:45)\n\tat org.postgresql.replication.fluent.ReplicationStreamBuilder$1.start(ReplicationStreamBuilder.java:38)\n\tat org.postgresql.replication.fluent.logical.LogicalStreamBuilder.start(LogicalStreamBuilder.java:41)\n\tat io.debezium.connector.postgresql.connection.PostgresReplicationConnection.startPgReplicationStream(PostgresReplicationConnection.java:1087)\n\tat io.debezium.connector.postgresql.connection.PostgresReplicationConnection.createReplicationStream(PostgresReplicationConnection.java:916)\n\tat io.debezium.connector.postgresql.connection.PostgresReplicationConnection.startStreaming(PostgresReplicationConnection.java:697)\n\t... 11 more\n"
    },
    "@version": 1,
    "source_host": "kafka-connect-0",
    "message": "Producer failure",
    "thread_name": "debezium-postgresconnector-<redacted>-change-event-source-coordinator",
    "@timestamp": "2026-05-12T12:04:21.319+0000",
```

title: Fix intermittent failure in TransactionMetadataIT: add waitForStreamingRunning before CDC operations
body:
# Description
`TransactionMetadataIT.transactionMetadata` fails intermittently with:
```text
AssertionError: Expecting actual not to be null
```

# Specifically:
```java
records.recordsForTopic("testdb.DB2INST1.TABLEA")
```
sometimes returns `null`.

# Root Cause
After:
```java
consumeRecordsByTopic(1)
```
completes, the test immediately enables CDC and executes inserts for both `tablea` and `tableb`.
At this point, the connector may still be transitioning from snapshot mode to streaming mode. Because of this race condition, CDC log entries for `TABLEA` can be missed before streaming has fully started.

This explains why the failure is intermittent.
`TABLEB` is typically unaffected because the test later performs an additional standalone insert after `refreshAndWait`, when streaming mode has already stabilized.

# Proposed Fix
Add:
```java
waitForStreamingRunning("db2_server", TestHelper.TEST_DATABASE);
```

immediately after:
```java
consumeRecordsByTopic(1);
```

This ensures the connector has fully entered streaming mode before CDC is enabled and DML operations are executed.

# Patch

```java
consumeRecordsByTopic(1);

waitForStreamingRunning("db2_server", TestHelper.TEST_DATABASE);

TestHelper.enableDbCdc(connection, "TABLEA");
TestHelper.enableDbCdc(connection, "TABLEB");
```

# Additional Notes
Other DB2 integration tests already use `waitForStreamingRunning(...)` before performing streaming-dependent operations, including:
* `AbstractDb2DefaultValueIT`
* `Db2ChunkedSnapshotIT`
* `Db2OfflineDefaultValueIT`
* `Db2ReselectColumnsProcessorIT`
`TransactionMetadataIT` appears to be missing this synchronization step.
author: caobahuong

project_fields.Title: Fix intermittent failure in TransactionMetadataIT: add waitForStreamingRunning before CDC operations

title: Introduce a ChronicleQueue-based ChangeEventQueue implementation
body:
## Feature request or enhancement

_For feature requests or enhancements, please provide the following information:_

**Which use case/requirement will be addressed by the proposed feature?**

As a follow-up to DDD-36, we determined that the vast majority of the issues with Oracle occur during large commits, where there can easily be back pressure due to the overflooding of the `ChangeEventQueue` when we buffer entire transactions and emit them all at once.

Currently, users can optimize the default `ChangeEventQueue` implementation by raising the `max.queue.size`, but if they are already using off-heap stores like Ehcache or Infinipsan, they begin to trade off-heap storage for heap storage, which is still finite. So there should be solutions that can bridge this gap.

We should implement a new `debezium-storage-chronicle-queue` module that provides custom `ChangeEventQueue` implementations that can be wired in as a replacement for the default.

---

**Implementation ideas (optional)**

There should ideally be two implementations in this module, both that support ChronicleQueue.

1. An implementation that relies solely on `ChronicleQueue`, flushing to disk and reading from disk with no JVM heap store.
2. A hybrid implementation that uses heap up to `max.queue.size` and spills to `ChronicleQueue`.
author: Naros

project_fields.Title: Introduce a ChronicleQueue-based ChangeEventQueue implementation

title: Provide the default value(if available) for the fields in schema descriptor response
body:
Currently, the schema descriptor API doesn't provide the default value for a field (even if a default is available), but it's offered in the field description.

Current field structure
```
{
    "name": "string",
    "type": "string/boolean/integer",
    "display": {
        "label": "string",
        "description": "string",
        "group": "string",
        "groupOrder": integer,
        "width": "string",
        "importance": "string"
    },
    "validation": [],
    "valueDependants": []
}
```

Once these default values are available its should be correctly reflected in the UI and should be pre-filled on the connector forms on loading.
author: indraraj

project_fields.Title: Provide the default value(if available) for the fields in schema descriptor response

title: SQLServer - Full database snapshot when snapshot mode is 'when_needed'
body:
## Bug report

**What Debezium connector do you use and what version?**

SQLServer connector >= 2.7

---

**What is the connector configuration?**

`snapshot.mode: when_needed`

---

**What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?**

_E.g. on-premises, with a specific cloud provider, etc._

On-premise Kafka Connect deployment.

---

**What behavior do you expect?**

According to the [documentation](https://debezium.io/documentation/reference/3.5/connectors/sqlserver.html)
`when_needed` snapshot mode should perform a full snapshot under certain circumstances.

---

**What behavior do you see?**

When the circumstances specified in the documentation are not met, the connector does a snapshot.

---

**Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?**

_Ideally, also verify with the latest Alpha/Beta/CR version._

Unfortunately, we can't test with the latest version easily but we got the exact same issue with the PostgreSQL connector. and we found it was fixed here: https://github.com/debezium/debezium/pull/6514/changes#diff-f2d4d5bd7e1a3d93fe13e699b7ec7ba0fb138468c6c35b1592e08449142e87a7
(file `io/debezium/connector/postgresql/connection/PostgresConnection.java`

The file `io/debezium/connector/sqlserver/SqlServerConnection.java` should implement the same algorithm but the condition is not exactly the same as `PostgresConnection`.
The comparison of the LSNs is `<= 0` for Postgres and `< 0` for SQLServer.

---

**Do you have the connector logs, ideally from start till finish?**

_You might be asked later to provide [DEBUG/TRACE](https://debezium.io/documentation/reference/stable/operations/logging.html) level log._

There is not log in the version 2.7 which is used. `main` has logs.

---

**How to reproduce the issue using our [tutorial](https://github.com/debezium/debezium-examples/tree/main/tutorial) deployment?**

Configure the connector with `snapshot.mode: when_needed` and restart it two or three times. The connector performs a full snapshot.
author: stondini

project_fields.Title: SQLServer - Full database snapshot when snapshot mode is 'when_needed'

title: [Spanner] NullPointerException in ApproximateStructSizeCalculator when finished-partition cleanup dispatches a null-offset heartbeat (max.queue.size.in.bytes > 0)
body:
## Bug report

**What Debezium connector do you use and what version?**

Debezium Spanner connector (incubating), `io.debezium.connector.spanner.SpannerConnector`, **v3.5.0.Final**. The same code path also exists in `v2.6.1.Final` (confirmed identical for the relevant files; see *Affected versions* below).

---

**What is the connector configuration?**

The relevant subset (full Avro / SASL / reroute config elided for brevity):

```json
{
  "connector.class": "io.debezium.connector.spanner.SpannerConnector",
  "tasks.max": "1",
  "gcp.spanner.project.id": "<my-project>",
  "gcp.spanner.instance.id": "<my-instance>",
  "gcp.spanner.database.id": "<my-database>",
  "gcp.spanner.change.stream": "<my-change-stream>",
  "connector.spanner.finished.partition.deletion.delay": "3600",

  // ← this is what flips the latent bug from silent to fatal:
  "max.queue.size.in.bytes": "268435456"
}
```

`max.queue.size.in.bytes` was set to `256 MiB` after a wide-table backfill OOM, to back-pressure heap pressure earlier than the default 8192-record count limit. Once the byte limit is on, the latent bug below fires every time the Spanner connector cleans up a finished partition.

---

**What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?**

- Source: Google Cloud Spanner (change streams), regional instance.
- Connect runtime: Strimzi Kafka Operator v0.51.0 / Apache Kafka 4.2.0 on Kubernetes.
- Kafka backend: Confluent Cloud (managed), SASL_SSL / PLAIN.

---

**What behavior do you expect?**

When the Spanner connector clears a finished partition by dispatching a heartbeat `SourceRecord` with `sourceOffset = null` from `RemoveFinishedPartitionOperation.removeFinishedPartitions`, `ChangeEventQueue.doEnqueue` should still be able to enqueue it without aborting the task. Either:

- `ApproximateStructSizeCalculator.getApproximateRecordSize` should handle a null `sourceOffset()` gracefully (count it as 0 bytes), or
- The Spanner connector should not emit a `SourceRecord` whose `sourceOffset()` is null.

The connector should not enter a permanent task-restart loop in either case.

---

**What behavior do you see?**

With `max.queue.size.in.bytes > 0`, the source task throws a NullPointerException the moment the Spanner partition-cleanup heartbeat is enqueued:

```text
java.lang.NullPointerException: Cannot invoke "java.util.Map.size()"
  because the return value of "org.apache.kafka.connect.source.SourceRecord.sourceOffset()" is null
    at io.debezium.util.ApproximateStructSizeCalculator.getApproximateRecordSize(ApproximateStructSizeCalculator.java:30)
    at io.debezium.pipeline.DataChangeEvent.objectSize(DataChangeEvent.java:31)
    at io.debezium.connector.base.ChangeEventQueue.doEnqueue(ChangeEventQueue.java:254)
    at io.debezium.connector.spanner.processor.heartbeat.SpannerHeartbeat.emit(SpannerHeartbeat.java:69)
    at io.debezium.pipeline.EventDispatcher.alwaysDispatchHeartbeatEvent(EventDispatcher.java:448)
    at io.debezium.connector.spanner.task.operation.RemoveFinishedPartitionOperation
        .lambda$removeFinishedPartitions$0(RemoveFinishedPartitionOperation.java:81)
    ...
```

`SpannerErrorHandler.setProducerThrowable` wraps it as a retriable exception, the task restarts, and the next `RemoveFinishedPartitionOperation` tick re-fires the NPE. The connector-level state remains `RUNNING` so external dashboards miss it; per-pod logs show roughly 5–10 NPE-driven task restarts per minute and offsets stop advancing.

### Root cause analysis

The Spanner connector intentionally builds a heartbeat `SourceRecord` with `sourceOffset = null` when removing a finished partition. See `RemoveFinishedPartitionOperation` (v3.5.0.Final and v2.6.1.Final are byte-identical here):

```java
// debezium-connector-spanner / src/main/java/io/debezium/connector/spanner/task/operation/RemoveFinishedPartitionOperation.java
LOGGER.info("Task {}, Dispatching null offset for partition {} because it is removed", ...);
PartitionOffset partitionOffset = new PartitionOffset();   // empty ctor → offset=null
SpannerOffsetContext ctx = new SpannerOffsetContext(partitionOffset, new TransactionContext());
spannerEventDispatcher.alwaysDispatchHeartbeatEvent(partition, ctx);
```

`PartitionOffset.getOffset()` returns `null` (not an empty map) for the empty-ctor case:

```java
// debezium-connector-spanner / .../context/offset/PartitionOffset.java
public Map<String, String> getOffset() {
    if (this.offset == null) {
        return null;            // ← intentional
    }
    return Map.of(...);
}
```

`SpannerHeartbeat.emit` propagates that null straight into a new `SourceRecord`:

```java
// debezium-connector-spanner / .../processor/heartbeat/SpannerHeartbeat.java
this.queue.enqueue(new DataChangeEvent(
    heartbeatRecord((Map<String, String>) partition, offset.getOffset())));   // offset.getOffset() == null

private SourceRecord heartbeatRecord(Map<String, String> sourcePartition, Map<String, ?> sourceOffset) {
    return new SourceRecord(sourcePartition, sourceOffset, topicName, 0, ...);
}
```

Then in Debezium core's size calculator the null is dereferenced unconditionally:

```java
// debezium-core (3.x: debezium-connector-common) / src/main/java/io/debezium/util/ApproximateStructSizeCalculator.java
public static long getApproximateRecordSize(SourceRecord changeEvent) {
    long value = changeEvent.sourcePartition().size() * 100L
               + changeEvent.sourceOffset().size() * 100L        // ← NPE when sourceOffset == null
               + changeEvent.headers().size() * 100L;
    ...
}
```

…and the call site is gated by the byte-limit feature switch:

```java
// debezium-core (3.x: debezium-connector-common) / .../connector/base/ChangeEventQueue.java doEnqueue
queue.add(record);
if (maxQueueSizeInBytes > 0) {            // ← byte-level queue tracking on?
    long messageSize = record.objectSize();   // calls the calculator above
    sizeInBytesQueue.add(messageSize);
    currentQueueSizeInBytes += messageSize;
}
```

So the bug stays latent as long as `max.queue.size.in.bytes = 0` (Debezium default) but lights up the moment byte-level queue accounting is enabled.

### Why only Spanner triggers this

`SourceRecord(... sourceOffset = null ...)` is permitted by the Kafka Connect API. Other Debezium connectors (MySQL, Postgres, MongoDB, Vitess, …) never construct one in practice — their offsets (binlog position, LSN, GTID, resume token) are always populated. The Spanner connector is unique in deliberately using `sourceOffset = null` as a signaling convention for the partition-lifecycle "this partition is being removed" event. Future connectors that adopt a similar partition-lifecycle pattern will hit the same NPE.

### Proposed fix (core; one-line, defensive)

Make `ApproximateStructSizeCalculator.getApproximateRecordSize` null-safe so the Kafka Connect API contract (which permits null offsets) is honoured at the framework layer rather than relying on every connector to never emit one:

```diff
 public static long getApproximateRecordSize(SourceRecord changeEvent) {
-    long value = changeEvent.sourcePartition().size() * 100L
-               + changeEvent.sourceOffset().size() * 100L
-               + changeEvent.headers().size() * 100L;
+    final Map<String, ?> partition = changeEvent.sourcePartition();
+    final Map<String, ?> offset    = changeEvent.sourceOffset();
+    long value = (partition == null ? 0 : partition.size()) * 100L
+               + (offset    == null ? 0 : offset.size())    * 100L
+               + changeEvent.headers().size() * 100L;
     value += 8; // timestamp
     return value + getStructSize((Struct) changeEvent.key()) + getStructSize((Struct) changeEvent.value())
             + changeEvent.topic().getBytes().length;
 }
```

Happy to send a PR with this change plus a unit test covering both `sourcePartition == null` and `sourceOffset == null` cases. The Spanner connector side can stay as-is (the null-offset dispatch is by design).

### Affected versions

The relevant files are byte-identical between Debezium 2.6.1.Final and 3.5.0.Final:

- `debezium-connector-spanner/.../RemoveFinishedPartitionOperation.java` — diffs only in unrelated code
- `debezium-connector-spanner/.../PartitionOffset.java` — identical
- `debezium-connector-spanner/.../SpannerHeartbeat.java` — identical
- `debezium-core` (2.6.1) / `debezium-connector-common` (3.x) `ApproximateStructSizeCalculator.java` — identical
- `ChangeEventQueue.doEnqueue` byte-size gate — identical

So this is **not** a 2.x ↔ 3.x regression; it's a latent bug across the whole 2.x/3.x line. Any 2.x or 3.x Spanner connector configured with `max.queue.size.in.bytes > 0` will hit it the first time a partition reaches the finished-deletion-delay.

---

**Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?**

Yes — the code on `main` and `v3.5.1.Final` is identical to `v3.5.0.Final` for these files. The NPE reproduces on `v3.5.0.Final` today, and we have verified the proposed null-safe patch eliminates it end-to-end on the same image with `max.queue.size.in.bytes = 256 MiB`.

---

**Do you have the connector logs, ideally from start till finish?**

Representative segment after the connector reaches `RUNNING` and starts dispatching cleanup heartbeats (NPE count: ~42 in 10 minutes over a ~100 minute observation window):

```text
NullPointerException: Cannot invoke "java.util.Map.size()" because the return value of "SourceRecord.sourceOffset()" is null
    at io.debezium.util.ApproximateStructSizeCalculator.getApproximateRecordSize(ApproximateStructSizeCalculator.java:30)
    at io.debezium.pipeline.DataChangeEvent.objectSize(DataChangeEvent.java:31)
    at io.debezium.connector.base.ChangeEventQueue.doEnqueue(ChangeEventQueue.java:254)
    at io.debezium.connector.spanner.processor.heartbeat.SpannerHeartbeat.emit(SpannerHeartbeat.java:69)
    at io.debezium.pipeline.EventDispatcher.alwaysDispatchHeartbeatEvent(EventDispatcher.java:448)
    at io.debezium.connector.spanner.task.operation.RemoveFinishedPartitionOperation.lambda$removeFinishedPartitions$0(RemoveFinishedPartitionOperation.java:81)
    ...

# SpannerErrorHandler converts to RetriableException, task restarts:
WARN  AbstractWorkerSourceTask: WorkerSourceTask{id=...} failed to poll records from SourceTask. Will retry operation.
org.apache.kafka.connect.errors.RetriableException: An exception occurred in the change event producer. This connector will be restarted.
    at io.debezium.connector.spanner.SpannerErrorHandler.setProducerThrowable(SpannerErrorHandler.java:58)
    ...
Caused by: java.lang.NullPointerException

# Loops at ~5-10 restarts per minute, offsets stop advancing
```

Happy to attach a full DEBUG-level log on request.

---

**How to reproduce the issue using our [tutorial](https://github.com/debezium/debezium-examples/tree/main/tutorial) deployment?**

The tutorial doesn't ship a Spanner setup, but the bug only needs three things, all confirmable from the source:

1. Run any Debezium Spanner connector (2.x or 3.x) with `max.queue.size.in.bytes > 0` in its config.
2. Wait until at least one change-stream partition transitions to FINISHED and the `connector.spanner.finished.partition.deletion.delay` window elapses, so `RemoveFinishedPartitionOperation.removeFinishedPartitions` dispatches the null-offset heartbeat.
3. The first such heartbeat enqueue throws the NPE shown above.

A unit test against `ApproximateStructSizeCalculator.getApproximateRecordSize(new SourceRecord(Map.of(), null, "topic", null, null))` reproduces the underlying defect deterministically without any source DB.

---

### Workaround

Until a null-safe core lands, two viable workarounds, both confirmed:

- Set `max.queue.size.in.bytes = 0` on the Spanner connector (loses byte-level queue back-pressure).
- Build Debezium core with the one-line null-safe fix above (the NPE is gone end-to-end with `max.queue.size.in.bytes = 256 MiB`).

I will open a PR with the fix and a unit test as a follow-up; happy to incorporate any feedback first.
author: laughingman7743

project_fields.Title: [Spanner] NullPointerException in ApproximateStructSizeCalculator when finished-partition cleanup dispatches a null-offset heartbeat (max.queue.size.in.bytes > 0)

title: mongodb tcmalloc kernel issue for MongoDB 8.0+ images
body:
## Description

An update to Linux kernel version 6.19 may cause startup issues with MongoDB 8.0+. This kind of issue can prevent the correct execution of test container inside an host machine with a linux kernel 6.19+


## Workaround

the current workaround is to add an environment variable to the Docker image:

`GLIBC_TUNABLES=glibc.pthread.rseq=1`


## References

https://jira.mongodb.org/browse/SERVER-121912
author: kmos

_For feature requests or enhancements, please provide the following information:_

**Which use case/requirement will be addressed by the proposed feature?**

In some cases users do not want to truncate their columns and want the full value. However, some downstream systems enforce a maximum message size. Extend the EnforceRecordSize SMT to have a strategy for this case.

title: Incorrect pg_stat_replication.reply_time calculation
body:
When a replication connection is created by the Debezium connector for PostgreSQL,
`pg_stat_replication.reply_time` is reported as a timestamp more than 20 years in the past.


## Bug report

**What Debezium connector do you use and what version?**

main(HEAD)

---

**What is the connector configuration?**

Although I believe this issue occurs any replication connection using Debezium connector for PostgreSQL, below configuration can reproduce it:
```
% cat pg_stat_replication.reply_time.json
{
  "name": "test-connector",
  "config": {
    "connector.class": "io.debezium.connector.postgresql.PostgresConnector",
    "database.hostname": "localhost",
    "database.port": "5432",
    "database.user": "postgres",
    "database.password": "postgres",
    "database.dbname": "postgres",
    "topic.prefix": "example",
    "slot.name": "testslot",
    "plugin.name": "pgoutput",
    "publication.name": "p1"
  }
}
```


---

**What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?**

_E.g. on-premises, with a specific cloud provider, etc._

on-premise

---

**What behavior do you expect?**

An accurate current timestamp should be shown in `pg_stat_replication.reply_time`.

---

**What behavior do you see?**

An incorrect timestamp is shown. Specifically, a value more than 20 years in the past:

```
=# SELECT application_name, reply_time FROM pg_stat_replication;
  application_name  |          reply_time
--------------------+-------------------------------
 Debezium Streaming | 1999-12-21 10:02:23.396147+09
(1 row)
```

---

**Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?**

Yes.

---

**Do you have the connector logs, ideally from start till finish?**

_You might be asked later to provide [DEBUG/TRACE](https://debezium.io/documentation/reference/stable/operations/logging.html) level log._

Yes. I can provide them if needed.

---

**How to reproduce the issue using our [tutorial](https://github.com/debezium/debezium-examples/tree/main/tutorial) deployment?**

Create the publication:

```
postgres=# CREATE PUBLICATION p1;
```

Start the PostgreSQL connector:

```
$ curl -i -X POST -H "Accept:application/json" -H  "Content-Type:application/json" http://localhost:8083/connectors/ -d @pg_stat_replication.reply_time.json
```

Check pg_stat_replication:

```
postgres=# SELECT * FROM pg_stat_replication;
```

You will see output similar to:

```
reply_time | 1999-12-21 01:01:56.467807+00
```

---

**Cause**
Based on my investigation, this issue is caused by a bug in PostgreSQL JDBC driver.
I have already fixed it on the JDBC driver side:
https://github.com/pgjdbc/pgjdbc/pull/3906/changes/0272f7c1a464c1bc622f21eb3f0efed3d8222dc5

I plan to submit a PR to Debezium to update the JDBC driver dependency to a version that includes this fix.
author: atorik

project_fields.Title: Incorrect pg_stat_replication.reply_time calculation

title: Debezium CLI
body:
## Feature request or enhancement

Today, Debezium offers powerful CDC capabilities, but managing connectors, configurations, state, and observability is available only under the Debezium Platform.


---

A unified Debezium CLI would provide developers and operators with a streamlined way to create, manage, and monitor CDC pipelines across heterogeneous data systems.

This project proposes a dedicated CLI that simplifies pipeline lifecycle operations, exposes monitoring insights, and enables consistent workflows for both local development and production environments:
Build & prepare Debezium Server

- Debezium Platform
- Manage pipelines
- Monitor pipeline

## Reference

proposal: https://github.com/debezium/debezium-design-documents/pull/24

title: JDBC Connection for offset storage validation issues
body:
## Bug report

**What Debezium connector do you use and what version?**

Debezium Server 3.5. The main source uses the SQL Server connector, but the issue is with the JDBC connector for offset storage.

---

**What is the connector configuration?**

```

database.hostname=<my-source-db>.database.windows.net
database.port=1433

database.password=<redacted>

# Offset storage

# JDBC offset storage

# Schema history

schema.history.internal.jdbc.table.name=dbo.debezium_database_history

schema.history.internal.jdbc.table.ddl=CREATE TABLE dbo.debezium_database_history (
  id VARCHAR(36) NOT NULL,
  history_data VARCHAR(MAX),
  history_data_seq INTEGER,
  record_insert_ts DATETIME2 NOT NULL,
  record_insert_seq INTEGER NOT NULL,
  PRIMARY KEY (id)
)

# Sink config

sink.kafka.producer.bootstrap.servers=<my-namespace-name>.servicebus.windows.net:9093
sink.kafka.producer.security.protocol=SASL_SSL
sink.kafka.producer.sasl.mechanism=PLAIN

sink.kafka.producer.key.serializer=org.apache.kafka.common.serialization.StringSerializer

```

---

**What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?**

_E.g. on-premises, with a specific cloud provider, etc._

```
Source database: Microsoft SQL Server on Azure SQL Database
Offset/schema history database: Azure SQL Database
Deployment mode: Debezium Server running in Azure Container Apps
Authentication method for JDBC offset/schema history storage: ActiveDirectoryMSI (Managed Identity) using Microsoft SQL Server JDBC driver
```
---

**What behavior do you expect?**

```
Debezium JDBC offset storage and JDBC schema history storage should support Azure SQL Managed Identity authentication using a JDBC URL such as:

jdbc:sqlserver://<server>:1433;databaseName=<db>;encrypt=true;authentication=ActiveDirectoryMSI

without requiring jdbc.user/jdbc.password configuration properties.
Alternatively, if the properties are mandatory for validation purposes, empty values should be accepted and should not be forwarded to the JDBC driver.
```
---

**What behavior do you see?**

```
Debezium validates the JDBC offset storage configuration and requires offset.storage.jdbc.password to be present.
If the property is omitted, Debezium fails validation with:

Validation error for property with prefix 'offset.storage.':
The 'jdbc.password' value is invalid: A value is required

If a password value is supplied to satisfy validation, the Microsoft SQL Server JDBC driver rejects the connection because Managed Identity authentication does not allow password usage:

Cannot use "Authentication=ActiveDirectoryManagedIdentity"
with "Password" connection string keyword.

This creates a configuration deadlock where:
- Debezium requires the password property
- The JDBC driver forbids the password property 
making Azure Managed Identity authentication unusable for JdbcOffsetBackingStore and JdbcSchemaHistory.
```
---

**Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?**

_Ideally, also verify with the latest Alpha/Beta/CR version._

`Is see it in 3.5, haven't verified it with 3.6 but none of the release notes mention anything similar`

---

**How to reproduce the issue using our [tutorial](https://github.com/debezium/debezium-examples/tree/main/tutorial) deployment?**

```
1. Deploy Debezium Server with JDBC offset storage and JDBC schema history enabled.

2. Configure Microsoft SQL Server JDBC URLs using Azure Managed Identity authentication:

   offset.storage=io.debezium.storage.jdbc.offset.JdbcOffsetBackingStore

   offset.storage.jdbc.url=jdbc:sqlserver://<server>:1433;databaseName=<db>;encrypt=true;authentication=ActiveDirectoryMSI

   schema.history.internal=io.debezium.storage.jdbc.history.JdbcSchemaHistory

   schema.history.internal.jdbc.connection.url=jdbc:sqlserver://<server>:1433;databaseName=<db>;encrypt=true;authentication=ActiveDirectoryMSI

3. Omit:
   
   offset.storage.jdbc.password

   because Managed Identity authentication does not use passwords.

4. Start Debezium Server.

5. Observe startup failure:

   Validation error for property with prefix 'offset.storage.':
   The 'jdbc.password' value is invalid: A value is required

6. Add a dummy password value:

   offset.storage.jdbc.password=dummy

7. Restart Debezium Server.

8. Observe JDBC driver failure:

   Cannot use "Authentication=ActiveDirectoryManagedIdentity"
   with "Password" connection string keyword.

This reproduces the issue consistently.

The issue appears independent of Azure Container Apps specifically and should reproduce anywhere the Microsoft SQL Server JDBC driver is used with ActiveDirectoryMSI authentication.
```
author: ADBlom

title: Simplify the Oracle LogMiner buffered implementation event logic
body:
Over the last several months, the transaction buffer's approach to managing events that should be partially rolled back has changed. Before these changes, if a constraint violation was observed or if an event participated in a sub-transaction that was discarded due to a save point rollback, those events were never buffered in the transaction cache. This meant that the cache only ever stored events that needed to be emitted, or, if they were LOB operations, folded into one another to create a single logical event.

Since then, we've introduced several new changes

* #1422 
* #1914 
* #1917 

The most notable point is that these PRs have changed how we handle partial rollbacks. Previously, these were evicted from the buffer immediately, but now they're retained until commit time, where they're filtered out. This requires us to maintain specific data that we stitch together across multiple mining passes. There's nothing inherently wrong with the approach, but it calls into question whether there's value in decoupling processing from commit-dispatch semantics.

### Motivation

There is often insufficient data available when we process a given event because we have not yet ingested the entire transaction. In other situations, we need to perform ad hoc cache traversal, such as during partial rollbacks, which can be costly.

### Proposal

Given that Ehcache and Infinispan are used as ephemeral stores today, we should consider using this to our advantage. The biggest heap cost for transaction buffering is the column data itself, not the per-event metadata we track. So, for Ehcache/Infinispan, we reduce the number of caches to store only the cached column data; everything else is retained in the heap.

With all the metadata in the heap, we can use this to do one or two forward passes over a transaction at commit time. These forward passes are designed to identify a few key pieces of information:

1. Track what events should be folded into what events. Here, we can iterate the full transaction operation list, compare row ids, and other metadata, and identify what event keys should be folded into what parent event keys. We use this information to build a dispatch map that looks something like `Map<EventKey, List<EventKey>>`, where each key maps to a list of event keys to fold into a target event. 
2. Track what events should be discarded because they've been rolled back, e.g. `Set<EventKey>`.

Once we have these two pieces of information, we can iterate over the entire event cache for a given transaction and begin to dispatch the events. The rules we'd apply would amount to things like:

* If the event's key exists in the discard `Set<EventKey>`, we skip the event. This would include the events marked with the rollback flag, as well as the event(s) referenced by the partial rollback. 
* When we encounter an `EventKey` with an entry in the `Map`, we retrieve all the events that should be folded, fold them, and emit a single event. Any event that was marked to be folded would be skipped as we iterate forward.

This would:

* Replace `TransactionCommitConsumer` likely with two independent yet dedicated pieces of code, which will make the overall understanding of LogMiner event folding and discarding easier.
* Improve Ehcache/Infinispan cache performance as we will be doing significantly fewer cache writes/reads.
* Simplifies the logic handlers during event processing, keeping them thin & light.

### Future Use cases

This approach plays well into the idea of using other cache/queue strategies like ChronicleQueue. In this case the processing layer is just writing to the cache/queue. Then only during dispatch, are there reads, which lends itself well to potentially favoring a separation between commit handling and read/event process handling.
author: Naros

project_fields.Title: Simplify the Oracle LogMiner buffered implementation event logic

title: Harden EhcacheCacheProvider XML parser against XXE and extend cache config validation
body:
## Bug report

**What Debezium connector do you use and what version?**

Oracle connector >= 3.2.0.Beta1

---

**What is the connector configuration?**

log.mining.buffer.type=ehcache

---

**What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?**

N/A - affects connector configuration handling, not database-specific

---

**What behavior do you expect?**

1. The DocumentBuilderFactory used to parse Ehcache XML configuration
should reject DOCTYPE declarations and external entity resolution.

2. The five per-cache Ehcache config fields (transactions, 
processedtransactions, schemachanges, events, rollbacks) should reject
<cache/>, <key-type/>, and <value-type/> tags, consistent with their
field descriptions and the existing global config validator.

---

**What behavior do you see?**

1. DocumentBuilderFactory.newInstance() is used without disabling
external entity resolution, leaving the parser open to XXE on
environments where Oracle XML JARs (xdb.jar, xmlparserv2.jar) are
on the classpath.

2. Only the global.config field has tag validation. The five
per-cache config fields have no validation, allowing users to
accidentally or intentionally override <key-type/> and <value-type/>
which are managed exclusively by Debezium.

---

**Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?**

Yes, reproduced on 3.2.0.Final and 3.6.0-SNAPSHOT.

---

**How to reproduce the issue using our tutorial deployment?**

Reported via security advisory GHSA-9fr8-jrc3-vmqj.
Fix discussed and approved by @Naros in the advisory thread.
author: caobahuong

project_fields.Title: Harden EhcacheCacheProvider XML parser against XXE and extend cache config validation

title: Prevent file contents from appearing in logs and add security notice for FileSignalChannel
body:
## Bug report

**What Debezium connector do you use and what version?**

All connectors using FileSignalChannel >= 1.6.0

---

**What is the connector configuration?**

signal.enabled.channels=file

---

**What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?**

N/A - affects signal channel logging behavior

---

**What behavior do you expect?**

1. When a signal line fails to parse, the log should record the parse
error only - not the raw content of the signal line.

2. When FileSignalChannel is initialized, an INFO log should inform
operators that the file signal channel is active and that the Kafka
Connect REST API and filesystem permissions should be properly secured.

---

**What behavior do you see?**

1. On parse failure, the raw signal line content is logged at WARN level
via `LOGGER.warn("Skipped signal due to an error '{}'", signalLine, e)`.
If signal.file points to a sensitive file, its contents are exposed in
connect.log and any configured log aggregator.

2. The current startup log only records the file path with no security
guidance for operators.

---

**Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?**

Yes, reproduced on 3.2.0.Final and 3.6.0-SNAPSHOT.

---

**How to reproduce the issue using our tutorial deployment?**

Reported via security advisory GHSA-j7fv-8c5f-48pv.
Fix approach discussed and approved by @Naros and @jpechane in the
advisory thread.

title: Add propagation of "skipped.operations" into publications for the Postgres connector
body:
## Feature request or enhancement

**Which use case/requirement will be addressed by the proposed feature?**

Currently, the PostgreSQL connector supports the `skipped.operations` configuration property to filter out specific operation types (`c`, `u`, `d`, `t`) at the Debezium processing layer.

However, when Debezium automatically creates or updates PostgreSQL publications, the generated publication always publishes all operation types. This means PostgreSQL still produces WAL entries for operations that Debezium is configured to skip, resulting in unnecessary replication traffic and processing overhead.

It would be useful if the connector propagated the configured `skipped.operations` setting into publication creation/update statements by configuring the PostgreSQL publication `publish` option accordingly.

For example:

- If `skipped.operations=d`
- Then the generated publication should omit delete operations:

```sql
CREATE PUBLICATION <publication_name>
FOR TABLE <table_name>, <table_name>, ...
WITH (publish = 'insert,update');
```

This would improve efficiency and align PostgreSQL logical replication behavior with Debezium connector configuration.

---

**Implementation ideas (optional)**

The publication creation/update logic currently exists in:

`PostgresReplicationConnection.java`

https://github.com/debezium/debezium/blob/main/debezium-connector-postgres/src/main/java/io/debezium/connector/postgresql/connection/PostgresReplicationConnection.java#L471-L476

A possible implementation could:

1. Map Debezium operation codes:
   - `c` → `insert`
   - `u` → `update`
   - `d` → `delete`
   - `t` → `truncate`

2. Derive the allowed publication operations by excluding configured `skipped.operations`

3. Append the PostgreSQL publication clause:

```sql
WITH (publish = '<operations>')
```

Example:

```sql
CREATE PUBLICATION <publication_name>
FOR TABLE <table_name>, <table_name>, ...
WITH (publish = 'insert,update');
```

The same logic could also be applied when updating existing publications.
author: rigadon

project_fields.Title: Add propagation of "skipped.operations" into publications for the Postgres connector

title: CockroachDB connector: table discovery only scans `cockroachdb.schema.name`, ignores `schema.include.list` and drops tables in `table.include.list` from other schemas
body:
## Bug report

**What Debezium connector do you use and what version?**

`debezium-connector-cockroachdb` 3.5.0.Final and current 3.6.0-SNAPSHOT.

**What is the connector configuration?**

Any config whose `table.include.list` references tables in more than one schema, or any single-schema config where the target schema is not `public`. Example trigger: `table.include.list=public.orders,reporting.daily_summary` with default `cockroachdb.schema.name`.

**What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?**

CockroachDB v25.4.10 LTS, both self-hosted and Cockroach Cloud. The bug is in the discovery code path so the deployment shape doesn't matter.

**What behavior do you expect?**

Match the PostgreSQL connector idiom. `schema.include.list` / `schema.exclude.list` (already inherited from `RelationalDatabaseConnectorConfig`) should restrict which schemas are scanned during table discovery; when neither is set, all non-system schemas should be scanned and `table.include.list` should then narrow the set, as `JdbcConnection.readSchema()` already does for every other JDBC-based Debezium connector.

**What behavior do you see?**

Only tables in the single schema named by `cockroachdb.schema.name` (default `public`) are discovered. `schema.include.list` is silently ignored; tables in `table.include.list` that live outside the configured schema are never registered, so they cannot match the include filter.

**Root cause**

`CockroachDBSchema.loadAndRegisterTables` (lines 82–87) hardcodes a single-schema scan:

```java
String sql = "SELECT table_schema, table_name FROM information_schema.tables " +
        "WHERE table_schema = ? AND table_type = 'BASE TABLE'";
try (var pstmt = connection.connection().prepareStatement(sql)) {
    pstmt.setString(1, config.getSchemaName());   // single value, default "public"
```

The connector reimplemented JDBC discovery instead of calling `JdbcConnection.readSchema(...)`, which already honors `RelationalTableFilters` (the predicate built from `schema.include.list`, `schema.exclude.list`, and `table.include.list`). Additionally, `CockroachDBConnectorConfig.SystemTablesPredicate` (line 867) omits the CRDB-specific system schemas `crdb_internal` and `pg_extension`, so they would surface once discovery is broadened.

**Proposed fix**

Replace the custom SQL in `CockroachDBSchema.loadAndRegisterTables` with `JdbcConnection.readSchema(tables(), catalog, null, dataCollectionFilter, columnFilter, false)` and add `crdb_internal` and `pg_extension` to `SystemTablesPredicate`. Deprecate `cockroachdb.schema.name` in favor of the standard `schema.include.list`.

**Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?**

Yes. The offending code has been in `CockroachDBSchema` since the initial connector commit and is present in every released version.

**How to reproduce the issue**

```sql
CREATE DATABASE demo;
USE demo;
CREATE SCHEMA reporting;
CREATE TABLE public.orders (id INT PRIMARY KEY);
CREATE TABLE reporting.daily (id INT PRIMARY KEY);
```

Deploy the connector with `table.include.list=public.orders,reporting.daily`. Insert into both tables. Only `public.orders` events are emitted; the `reporting.daily` topic is never created and no WARN is logged.
author: viragtripathi

project_fields.Title: CockroachDB connector: table discovery only scans `cockroachdb.schema.name`, ignores `schema.include.list` and drops tables in `table.include.list` from other schemas

title: CockroachDB connector: accept file paths for Kafka sink TLS certs and inject them into the changefeed sink URI
body:
## Summary

Add config options that let users point at PEM files on disk for the Kafka changefeed sink's TLS material, instead of having to base64-encode the certs and inline them into `cockroachdb.changefeed.sink.uri`. The connector reads each file, base64-encodes the contents, and assembles the final `kafka://...?ca_cert=...&client_cert=...&client_key=...` URI before passing it to `CREATE CHANGEFEED`.

## Motivation

CockroachDB's Kafka sink URI expects TLS material inline as base64-encoded PEM in query parameters (`ca_cert`, `client_cert`, `client_key`). Users typically run something like `cat ca.pem | base64 -w0` and paste the result into the connector config. This is error-prone:

- Line breaks dropped or added by editors silently corrupt the cert; the TLS handshake then fails with opaque errors.
- Long base64 strings get truncated when copy-pasted through chat/JIRA/email.
- Cert contents end up in connector config JSON, which is harder to rotate than swapping a file on disk.
- It is inconsistent with every other Kafka Connect connector that takes paths to truststores/keystores.

## Proposed Config

| Option | Default | Description |
|--------|---------|-------------|
| `cockroachdb.changefeed.sink.kafka.ca.cert.file` | _(none)_ | Path to a PEM-encoded CA certificate file. When set, the connector reads the file, base64-encodes it, and adds `ca_cert=…` to the sink URI. |
| `cockroachdb.changefeed.sink.kafka.client.cert.file` | _(none)_ | Path to a PEM-encoded client certificate file. When set, adds `client_cert=…` to the sink URI. |
| `cockroachdb.changefeed.sink.kafka.client.key.file` | _(none)_ | Path to a PEM-encoded client private key file. When set, adds `client_key=…` to the sink URI. |
| `cockroachdb.changefeed.sink.kafka.tls.enabled` | `false` | When `true`, adds `tls_enabled=true` to the sink URI. Implied automatically if any of the three file options above is set. |

Existing inline `ca_cert` / `client_cert` / `client_key` query params in `cockroachdb.changefeed.sink.uri` continue to work; the file-based options take precedence and overwrite any same-named query param to keep the resulting URI unambiguous.

## Implementation Notes

- New helper in `CockroachDBStreamingChangeEventSource.buildSinkChangefeedQuery()` that, when any file option is set, reads the file with `Files.readAllBytes(Path.of(...))`, base64-encodes with `Base64.getEncoder().withoutPadding().encodeToString(...)`, and appends the param to the URI before the existing `topic_prefix` append.
- Validation in `CockroachDBConnectorConfig` rejects file options whose path is unreadable or empty at startup, with a clear error pointing at the bad config key.
- Field declarations live alongside the other `CHANGEFEED_*` fields in `CockroachDBConnectorConfig`.

## Related

- CockroachDB Kafka sink URI reference: https://www.cockroachlabs.com/docs/stable/changefeed-sinks#kafka
author: viragtripathi

project_fields.Title: CockroachDB connector: accept file paths for Kafka sink TLS certs and inject them into the changefeed sink URI

title: CockroachDB connector: typed column DEFAULT values rejected because DefaultValueConverter is null
body:
## Bug report

**What Debezium connector do you use and what version?**

`debezium-connector-cockroachdb` 3.5.0.Final and current 3.6.0-SNAPSHOT.

**What is the connector configuration?**

Any config where a captured table has a non-string column DEFAULT — for example `quantity INT DEFAULT 0`, `is_active BOOL DEFAULT false`, `created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT current_timestamp()`. Surfaced while validating the fix for debezium/dbz#1973.

**What is the actual behavior?**

The connector task fails to start with:

```
io.debezium.DebeziumException: Failed to set field default value for
'<db>.<schema>.<table>.<column>' of type int8, the default value is 0 of type class java.lang.String
  at io.debezium.relational.TableSchemaBuilder.addField(TableSchemaBuilder.java:443)
  ...
Caused by: org.apache.kafka.connect.errors.DataException:
  Invalid Java object for schema with type INT64: class java.lang.String for value
```

`CockroachDBSchema` constructs `TableSchemaBuilder` with a `null` `DefaultValueConverter`, so the raw string from JDBC metadata is handed to `SchemaBuilder.defaultValue(...)` without being parsed to the column's Java type.

**What is the expected behavior?**

DEFAULT values should be parsed into the schema's Java type (e.g. `"0"` → `0L` for `INT8`), matching `PostgresSchema`'s behavior. `PostgresSchema` wires a `PostgresDefaultValueConverter` into `TableSchemaBuilder` for exactly this reason.

**How to reproduce the issue using our tutorials deployment?**

Create a table with any typed DEFAULT, point a connector at it:

```sql
CREATE TABLE inventory.warehouse_items (
    sku INT PRIMARY KEY,
    description STRING NOT NULL,
    quantity INT DEFAULT 0
);
```

```json
"table.include.list": "inventory.warehouse_items"
```

The task fails on `schema.initialize(...)` during the first `refreshSchema(tableId)` for `warehouse_items`.

**Feature request or improvement**

Implement `CockroachDBDefaultValueConverter` (mirroring `PostgresDefaultValueConverter`) and pass it as the second argument to `TableSchemaBuilder` in `CockroachDBSchema`'s constructor. Strip CockroachDB's `:::<type>` annotation that JDBC metadata appends to DEFAULTs, handle the standard scalar types (INT, BOOL, FLOAT, DECIMAL, STRING, BYTES, DATE, TIME, TIMESTAMP, TIMESTAMPTZ, INTERVAL, UUID), and forward function defaults like `current_timestamp()` / `gen_random_uuid()` as `null` so they fall through to runtime evaluation.
author: viragtripathi

project_fields.Title: CockroachDB connector: typed column DEFAULT values rejected because DefaultValueConverter is null

title: MySQL connector: snapshot fails at step 7 with "Cannot execute without committing"
body:
## Bug report

When running the MySQL connector with snapshot.locking.mode=none the connector crashes at snapshot step 7 with:

```
io.debezium.DebeziumException: Cannot execute without committing because auto-commit is enabled
    at io.debezium.jdbc.JdbcConnection.executeWithoutCommitting(JdbcConnection.java:1539)
    at io.debezium.connector.binlog.jdbc.BinlogConnectorConnection.getEstimatedTableSize(BinlogConnectorConnection.java:210)
    at io.debezium.connector.binlog.BinlogSnapshotChangeEventSource.rowCountForTable(BinlogSnapshotChangeEventSource.java:558)
    at io.debezium.relational.RelationalSnapshotChangeEventSource.createDataEvents(RelationalSnapshotChangeEventSource.java:500)

```
---

**Root cause**

getEstimatedTableSize() calls executeWithoutCommitting("USE db") which throws DebeziumException (a RuntimeException) if auto-commit is enabled on the connection. The catch block in getEstimatedTableSize() only handles SQLException, so DebeziumException propagates uncaught and kills the snapshot.

Auto-commit becomes enabled when the JDBC connection is re-established between steps 6 and 7 — new connections default to auto-commit=true. With snapshot.locking.mode=none the connection holds no locks and can go fully idle during the schema history write in step 6 (which can take minutes for large schemas), making reconnection likely on RDS/Aurora.

The USE statement has no transactional semantics and does not need to run inside a transaction — executeWithoutCommitting is the wrong method here.

---

**Affected versions**

Confirmed: 3.3.2.Final, 3.5.0.Final, main

Not affected: 3.1.1.Final (the auto-commit guard in executeWithoutCommitting did not exist yet)

---



**How to reproduce**

The bug requires the main JDBC connection to drop and reconnect between snapshot step 6 and step 7. The new connection defaults to autoCommit=true, which executeWithoutCommitting guards against — but getEstimatedTableSize only catches SQLException, not the DebeziumException that guard throws.

To force this reliably in a test environment:

1. Configure the connector against a large dataset (multiple databases, to make step 6 take 20+ seconds) with autoReconnect removed from database.connection.properties so Debezium's own reconnect path fires instead of the driver's transparent reconnect
2. Start the connector and watch for Snapshot step 6 - Persisting schema history in the logs
3. While the MySQL connection is idle (Debezium is writing schema history to Kafka, not querying MySQL), close the MySQL socket. I did this with:
```
import ctypes
libc = ctypes.CDLL("libc.so.6")
# fd = the file descriptor of the MySQL socket in the JVM process
# found via /proc/1/fd cross-referenced with /proc/net/tcp port 3306
libc.shutdown(fd, 2)  # SHUT_RDWR
```
4. Step 7 starts. getEstimatedTableSize → executeWithoutCommitting("USE db") → connection() sees isClosed()=true → establishConnection() creates a new connection with autoCommit=true → conn.getAutoCommit() returns true → throws DebeziumException
5. catch (SQLException e) does not catch it → exception propagates → snapshot crashes
author: ptqa

project_fields.Title: MySQL connector: snapshot fails at step 7 with "Cannot execute without committing"

title: MySQL connector:  Snapshot fails with Failed reading CURRENT_TIMESTAMP when connections go stale during stage 6
body:
## Bug report

**Summary**

Stage 5 (read structures) and stage 7 (export data) use JDBC connections. Stage 6 (persist DDL to Kafka) uses none. All connections sit idle for the duration of stage 6 with no validation, keepalive, or ping. With large schemas, stage 6 can take hours.

When stage 7 starts, getSnapshotSourceTimestamp() executes SELECT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP on a dead connection and throws with no retry.
tcpKeepAlive=true (MySQL CJ default) does not help — TCP keepalive probes do not reset MySQL's wait_timeout, and the default Linux probe interval (2h) is too long to prevent intermediate network devices (NAT Gateway: 350s) from dropping the session.

The snapshot pool (ConcurrentLinkedQueue<JdbcConnection>) lacks validate-on-borrow — a standard feature in any mainstream JDBC pool.

---

**What Debezium connector do you use and what version?**

 3.3.2


---

**What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?**

_E.g. on-premises, with a specific cloud provider, etc._

Aurora RDS MySQL 3.12

---

**What behavior do you expect?**

Complete snapshot

---

**What behavior do you see?**

ConnectException: Failed reading CURRENT_TIMESTAMP from source database

---

**Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?**

_Ideally, also verify with the latest Alpha/Beta/CR version._

`<Your answer>`

**Do you have the connector logs, ideally from start till finish?**

_You might be asked later to provide [DEBUG/TRACE](https://debezium.io/documentation/reference/stable/operations/logging.html) level log._

```
        at io.debezium.pipeline.ChangeEventSourceCoordinator.doSnapshot(ChangeEventSourceCoordinator.java:285) ~[debezium-core-3.3.2.Final.jar:3.3.2.Final]

        at java.base/java.util.concurrent.FutureTask.run(FutureTask.java:317) ~[?:?]
        ... 5 more
Caused by: com.mysql.cj.jdbc.exceptions.CommunicationsException: Communications link failure
```

---

**How to reproduce the issue using our [tutorial](https://github.com/debezium/debezium-examples/tree/main/tutorial) deployment?**

Similar to https://github.com/debezium/dbz/issues/1976

The bug requires a JDBC connection to become stale while idle during snapshot stage 6 (schema history persistence). In production this happens naturally when stage 6 exceeds the MySQL wait_timeout or an intermediate network device's idle timeout. In a test environment it can be forced:
1. Configure the connector against a schema with many databases/tables (to make stage 6 take 20+ seconds writing DDL to Kafka). 
2. Start the connector and watch for Snapshot stage 6 - Persisting schema history in the logs.
3. While the MySQL connection is idle (Debezium is writing schema history to Kafka, not querying MySQL), kill the TCP connection. From inside the JVM container:
```
import ctypes
libc = ctypes.CDLL("libc.so.6")
# fd = the file descriptor of the MySQL socket in the JVM process
# found via /proc/1/fd cross-referenced with /proc/net/tcp port 3306 (0x0CEA)
libc.shutdown(fd, 2)  # SHUT_RDWR
```
4. Stage 7 starts. The first pool worker calls getSnapshotSourceTimestamp() -> getCurrentTimestamp() -> SELECT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP on the dead connection. The driver attempts to read the response, gets 0 bytes (EOFException), wraps it as CommunicationsException, which propagates as ConnectException("Failed reading CURRENT_TIMESTAMP"). There is no validation, retry, or reconnect attempt. The snapshot crashes.


--
author: ptqa

project_fields.Title: MySQL connector:  Snapshot fails with Failed reading CURRENT_TIMESTAMP when connections go stale during stage 6

title: Keep order of events in batch processing
body:
## Context

Actually the Quarkus Extension offers batch processing which internally order the events by destination. This ordering can be harmful in some condition and makes the handler unaware of the previous order.

## Change

- deprecate in `CapturingEvents` the destination field
- add in `BatchEvent` a destination field
- remove the order logic in the default `GeneralChangeConsumer`
author: kmos

project_fields.Title: Keep order of events in batch processing

title: Avoid Overriding Configuration with Agroal
body:
## Context

Quarkus Extensions are built with compatibility with quarkus agroal conventions. In some cases, the agroal configuration can override values like `quarkus.debezium.name` which can result in unwanted situation.

## Change

We want to give priority to user configuration values and in case of empty value, it's possibile an override from agroal
author: kmos

title: Disable compatibility mode
body:
## Context

Compatibility mode permits to run inside the Quarkus Debezium Engine a connector that doesn't support native mode or all the feature inside the quarkus extensions. In some cases, it's too aggressive to have it by default enabled like for tests.

## Changes

add a configuration option to disable compatibility mode

title: rowCountForTableChunked generates unquoted SQL identifiers, causing SQLSyntaxErrorException on hyphenated database names
body:
## Bug report

**What Debezium connector do you use and what version?**

`Debezium MySQL Connector 3.5.1.Final`

---

**What is the connector configuration?**

 ```json
  {
    "connector.class": "io.debezium.connector.mysql.MySqlConnector",
    "snapshot.mode": "when_needed",
    "snapshot.max.threads": "2",
    "internal.legacy.snapshot.max.threads": "false"
  }
```
  The key combination is snapshot.max.threads > 1 with internal.legacy.snapshot.max.threads=false
  (the default). Any connector config that triggers the chunked snapshot path against a database
  whose name contains a hyphen will reproduce this.

---

**What is the captured database version and mode of deployment?**

_E.g. on-premises, with a specific cloud provider, etc._

`MySQL 8.0, AWS RDS (also reproducible with any MySQL deployment). The database name contains
  a hyphen, e.g. my-database.`

---

**What behavior do you expect?**

The connector should successfully complete a snapshot of all tables in the database, regardless
  of whether the database name contains hyphens or other special characters.

---

**What behavior do you see?**

The connector crashes during snapshot with a SQLSyntaxErrorException. MySQL interprets the
  unquoted hyphenated database name as arithmetic subtraction:
```
  org.apache.kafka.connect.errors.ConnectException: An exception occurred in the change event producer. This connector will be stopped.
      at io.debezium.pipeline.ErrorHandler.setProducerThrowable(ErrorHandler.java:67)
      ...
  Caused by: io.debezium.DebeziumException: java.sql.SQLSyntaxErrorException: You have an error in your SQL syntax; check the manual that corresponds to your MySQL server version for the right syntax to use near
  '-database.my_table' at line 1
      at io.debezium.pipeline.source.AbstractSnapshotChangeEventSource.execute(AbstractSnapshotChangeEventSource.java:111)
      at io.debezium.pipeline.ChangeEventSourceCoordinator.doSnapshot(ChangeEventSourceCoordinator.java:311)
      ...
  Caused by: java.sql.SQLSyntaxErrorException: You have an error in your SQL syntax; check the manual that corresponds to your MySQL server version for the right syntax to use near '-database.my_table' at line 1
      at io.debezium.relational.RelationalSnapshotChangeEventSource.rowCountForTableChunked(RelationalSnapshotChangeEventSource.java:1103)
      at io.debezium.relational.RelationalSnapshotChangeEventSource.lambda$createChunkedDataEvents$7(RelationalSnapshotChangeEventSource.java:572)
      at io.debezium.relational.RelationalSnapshotChangeEventSource.prepareTables(RelationalSnapshotChangeEventSource.java:1364)
      at io.debezium.relational.RelationalSnapshotChangeEventSource.createChunkedDataEvents(RelationalSnapshotChangeEventSource.java:570)
      at io.debezium.relational.RelationalSnapshotChangeEventSource.doExecute(RelationalSnapshotChangeEventSource.java:197)
```
  Root cause: rowCountForTableChunked builds its COUNT query using
  jdbcConnection.getQualifiedTableName(tableId), which returns an unquoted
  catalog.table string. BinlogConnectorConnection.getQualifiedTableName is
  implemented as tableId.catalog() + "." + tableId.table() — no quoting. When
  the catalog (database) name contains a hyphen, MySQL sees my - database.table
  and raises a syntax error.

  Every other method in BinlogSnapshotChangeEventSource that constructs SQL table
  references correctly uses quotedTableIdString() (which returns `catalog`.`table`
  with backtick quoting). rowCountForTableChunked is the only outlier.

  Workaround: set internal.legacy.snapshot.max.threads=true to bypass the chunked
  code path entirely.`

---

**Do you see the same behaviour using the latest released Debezium version?**

_Ideally, also verify with the latest Alpha/Beta/CR version._

Confirmed on 3.5.1.Final. Not yet verified on a newer version — the bug is in
  RelationalSnapshotChangeEventSource.rowCountForTableChunked which was introduced
  with the chunked snapshot feature and the fix has not been observed in any changelog.

---

**Do you have the connector logs, ideally from start till finish?**

_You might be asked later to provide [DEBUG/TRACE](https://debezium.io/documentation/reference/stable/operations/logging.html) level log._

The relevant stack trace is included above.

---

**How to reproduce the issue using our [tutorial](https://github.com/debezium/debezium-examples/tree/main/tutorial) deployment?**

1. Start MySQL and create a database with a hyphen in its name:
 ```
CREATE DATABASE `my-test-db`;
CREATE TABLE `my-test-db`.`items` (id INT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(50));
INSERT INTO `my-test-db`.`items` VALUES (1, 'a'), (2, 'b'), (3, 'c');
```
  2. Configure the Debezium MySQL connector with:
  ```
{
    "connector.class": "io.debezium.connector.mysql.MySqlConnector",
    "database.include.list": "my-test-db",
    "snapshot.max.threads": "2",
    "internal.legacy.snapshot.max.threads": "false"
}
```
  3. Start the connector. It will crash immediately during snapshot with the
  SQLSyntaxErrorException shown above.

  Proposed fix: In RelationalSnapshotChangeEventSource.rowCountForTableChunked
  (line 1103), replace jdbcConnection.getQualifiedTableName(tableId) with
  jdbcConnection.quotedTableIdString(tableId). A PR with this fix and a
  regression test is available at:
  https://github.com/ryandsouzaappfolio/debezium/tree/fix/DBZ-hyphenated-db-name-chunked-snapshot
author: ryandsouzaappfolio

project_fields.Title: rowCountForTableChunked generates unquoted SQL identifiers, causing SQLSyntaxErrorException on hyphenated database names

title: Debezium Extensions for Quarkus: filter on column changes - Single Event Capturing
body:
Migrated from [DBZ-9400](https://issues.redhat.com/browse/DBZ-9400)

h2. Context

By default, Debezium captures and emits change events for any row-level modification in the database, regardless of which columns were affected. In certain use cases, consumers are only interested in changes to a subset of columns, while updates to other fields are irrelevant.

h2. Decision

We will add support in the Debezium Quarkus extension to define a filter on specific columns. The extension will check which columns have changed in each event and suppress those that do not include changes.
author: jpechane

project_fields.Title: Debezium Extensions for Quarkus: filter on column changes - Single Event Capturing

title: Spanner connector resolves partition offsets one-by-one without timeout protection on batch path
body:
## Summary

When partitions are scheduled for streaming in `TakePartitionForStreamingOperation`, each partition's offset is resolved individually via `PartitionFactory.getPartition()` → `PartitionOffsetProvider.getOffset()`. This results in N sequential calls to the Kafka Connect offset storage for N partitions. During rebalancing scenarios with many partitions, this significantly slows down partition scheduling.

Additionally, the existing batch method `PartitionOffsetProvider.getOffsets()` calls `offsetStorageReader.offsets()` directly without any timeout protection. The single-partition path has a 5-second timeout via `Future.get()`, but the batch path can block indefinitely if the offset storage is slow or unresponsive.

## Impact

- Partition scheduling latency scales linearly with partition count during rebalances
- A stuck offset storage reader can hang the task indefinitely on the batch path

## Proposed Fix

- Add `PartitionFactory.getPartitions()` to resolve all partition offsets in a single batch call
- Wire `TakePartitionForStreamingOperation` to use the batch path instead of per-partition lookups
- Add 30-second timeout protection on the batch `offsetStorageReader.offsets()` call
- Remove dead `getOffsetMap()` method from `PartitionOffsetProvider`
- Return empty map instead of null from `PartitionOffset.getOffset()` to prevent downstream NPEs
author: sauravkumarrr

project_fields.Title: Spanner connector resolves partition offsets one-by-one without timeout protection on batch path
